She explained that she was born at an unlucky time, her husband gambled and lost heavily yesterday, and he intended to sell her to pay off his gambling debts. She found it difficult to leave her mother-in-law and could not abandon her young child, so she considered death as the only solution. He asked her how much she was worth, and she said that the agreed price was twenty thousand cash. He reassured her that this was a small matter and suggested she take her child home while he would bring the money the next morning to pay off her husband's debt. She hesitated to believe him, but Mr. Ni swore by the river and asked for her name and place of residence in detail. She told him everything, wiped her tears, and thanked him before leaving. He returned to his boat and instructed the boatman not to untie the rope immediately. The next morning, he carried several dozen gold pieces and went to the woman's house.

    84

    妇正盼望，见公至，大喜，顾谓姑曰：“此即江干所遇善人也。”公急命其夫遍招博徒来，为偿其资，且戒以后勿再与其夫同局，免致夫妻分离。佥诺诺，连声称叹而去。公又出银三十两付其夫曰：“此给汝，聊为生计。汝好为之，一家数口庶不致冻馁。汝妇贤孝，予爱而敬之，不揣冒昧，愿寄为吾女。予岁常上下往来，过此，必来问讯，有无尚可相通也。”一家闻之，环拜地下，叩公姓名，以便尸祝。公笑曰：“久自知之。”后，公过芜湖，必往探之，举家奉公如神明。其夫已戒赌，善权子母，居然小阜矣。越岁，公再如金陵，访星者，诘其言何不验。星者见之惊曰：“公阴骘纹满面，不惟延寿，后福且不可量。”问：“别后作何善事？”公殊茫然，默思：“岂即芜湖救妇事乎？”再十二年乃终，年已将八十矣。次郊大令为予总角交，尝历历言之最详。

    Explanation

    84

    The wife was anxiously waiting when she saw the gentleman arrive, and she was greatly pleased. She turned to her mother-in-law and said, "This is the good man I met by the riverbank." The gentleman quickly ordered his wife to summon all the gamblers, to repay their stakes, and warned them not to play with his husband again, to prevent the separation of husband and wife. They all agreed, repeatedly praising, and left. The gentleman then gave him thirty taels of silver and said, "This is for you, to help with your livelihood. You should use it well, so that your family of several mouths will not suffer from cold and hunger. Your wife is virtuous and filial, I love and respect her, without considering my boldness, I wish to adopt her as my daughter. I often travel up and down, when I pass by, I will definitely come to inquire, we can still communicate about needs." The whole family heard this and prostrated themselves on the ground, asking for his name to perform sacrifices in his honor. The gentleman smiled and said, "I have known it for a long time." Later, whenever the gentleman passed through Wuhu, he would definitely go to visit them, and the whole family treated him like a god. His husband had stopped gambling and became good at managing his finances, and he had become moderately prosperous. The next year, the gentleman went to Jinling again and visited an astrologer, questioning why his predictions were not accurate. The astrologer saw him and was surprised to say, "The gentleman's face is covered with benevolent marks, not only extending his life but also promising immense future blessings." He asked, "What good deeds have you done since then?" The gentleman was very puzzled, silently thinking, "Could it be the rescue of the woman in Wuhu?" Twelve years later, he passed away at the age of nearly eighty. Mr. Cì Jiāo, who was my childhood friend, once told me about it in great detail.

    85

    里乘子曰：淮阴侯谓：公，小人也，为德不卒。观倪封翁，既为偿博资，又给银使为生计。然则为德能卒，斯真君子矣。越岁，星者再见公，惊谓阴骘纹满面，不惟延寿，后福且不可量。谚云：“相随心变。”其然乎！

    Explanation

    85

    Li Chengzi said: "The Marquis of Huaiyin said: 'You, sir, are a petty person, for your virtue does not reach its end.' When observing Master Ni, he not only repaid the gambling debts but also gave silver to help with livelihood. Therefore, if one's virtue can be completed, then one is truly a gentleman. A year later, the astrologer saw him again and was astonished to see that the lines of fate were all over his face. Not only did it extend his life, but his future blessings were also immeasurable. As the saying goes, 'Appearance follows the heart's transformation.' Is that the case?"

    86

    李封公
    　　
    　　吾皖合肥李玉泉封翁文安，道光戊戌进士。生平笃于天性，躬行君子也。官刑部提牢时，例各囚每饭一勺。公散饭，必期满勺，生熟必亲尝之。又自捐米煎粥，以济晚饭。后收到人犯，狱中瘟疫易作，公恳切为文祷于神，囚病俱起，又预制药材以济急。夏，则捐颁蒲扇；每秋，各司捐棉衣，公于每所更添棉被十二件，以备病犯发汗养病之用，种种善事，不可枚举。公著《愚荃》、《敝帚》二种，上卷《贯垣纪事》，下卷《村居杂景》，每事各纪七绝诗一首，予多采入诗话。而《贯垣纪事》一卷，不惟可备掌故，而后之人踵而行之，功德真非浅鲜。所谓哀矜勿喜，好生之德，洽于民心，封翁有焉。
    　　
    　　里乘子曰：闻之萧山汤敦甫相国云，凡事实事求是，即是为圣、为贤学问。观李封翁官提牢时所为，真可谓实事求是矣。由是推之，其平日居心行事可知，似续显贵，方兴未艾，其所由来者，不信而有征乎？

    Explanation

    86

    The Duke Li of Wuhu

    My esteemed ancestor, Duke Li of Wuhu, known as Wen'an, was a jinshi (imperial scholar) during the Daoguang era's Wuxu year (1828). Throughout his life, he was deeply committed to his innate virtues, embodying the qualities of a true gentleman. When he served as the director of the prison affairs office in the Ministry of Punishment, the regulation stipulated that each prisoner received only one spoonful of food per meal. The duke ensured that each prisoner received a full spoonful, and he personally tasted both raw and cooked food to ensure its quality. Additionally, he donated rice to cook porridge for the prisoners' evening meals. Later, when prisoners were received into the prison, epidemics were easily triggered due to the poor living conditions. The duke earnestly wrote prayers to the deities, and the prisoners' illnesses were alleviated. He also prepared medicines in advance to help those in urgent need. During the summer, he distributed fans as a gift; every autumn, the various departments donated cotton clothing, and the duke added twelve extra blankets to each prison to provide warmth for prisoners who needed to sweat to recover from their illnesses. There are countless other good deeds he performed, too numerous to list. The duke wrote two books, The Foolish Orchid and The Worn Brush, with the upper volume titled Chronicles Through the Ages and the lower volume titled Scenes of Rural Life. Each event was recorded in a seven-character poem, and I have often included these poems in my discussions of poetry. The Chronicles Through the Ages volume is not only valuable for its historical anecdotes but also serves as a model for future generations to follow, truly demonstrating profound virtue. The duke exemplified the Confucian virtue of cherishing life and showing compassion, which resonated deeply with the people.

    The Village Traveler said: I have heard from Premier Tang Dunpu of Xiaoshan that the essence of being a sage or a virtuous scholar lies in seeking truth from facts. Observing the duke's actions while serving as the director of prison affairs, we can truly say that he embodied this principle. From this, we can infer his character and actions in his daily life. His descendants' continued prominence and success are a testament to the enduring impact of his virtuous deeds.

    87

    叶封翁
    　　
    　　吾邑叶封翁凤奎，生性孝友，慷慨好施。母抱痼疾，手奉汤药者，五年如一日。弟客塞外，乃短衣匹马，出关觅之，手足把晤，欢喜过望。兄宦蜀，无嗣，仅一女，卒后，所遗宦囊颇充，一以付女。或谓宜少留作归柩资，公谢曰：“女为兄钟爱，何忍较锱铢，伤骨肉情耶？”遇亲友贫者，不吝推解。有商缓急者，必展转称贷以应。坐此，负累积千金，箧券盈寸，人甘心负约，辄焚去。方壮年，以事泊镇江，见邻舟一少年色惨变，手持碗饮泣。诘之，哭曰：“家姑苏。从亲故丐数金，被盗路绝，只合仰药死。”公夺掷江中，招之同归，为措资使还。生平好善不倦，多类此。今公之长子树南，官湖北知县；次毓桐，己未进士，官吏部文选司主事；次树棠，即选教谕；次湛元，辛酉副举人；次毓荣，乙丑进士，官工部屯田司郎中。予昔游蜀，公视如子侄。予又与诸郎君交如兄弟，故得备闻公懿行云尔。

    Explanation

    87

    The Elder Ye Fengkui

    　　In my hometown, there was an elder named Ye Fengkui, who was naturally filial and friendly, generous and fond of giving. His mother suffered from a chronic illness, and he personally administered her medicine for five years without fail. When his younger brother was away in the frontier regions, Ye Fengkui set out on horseback, dressed simply, to find him. Upon their reunion, they embraced with great joy, exceeding all expectations. His elder brother served in Shu and had no male heir, leaving only a daughter. After his death, the remaining assets were substantial, and Ye Fengkui gave everything to his sister-in-law. Someone suggested that he should keep some for the cost of returning the coffin, but he declined, saying, "My sister-in-law is my brother's beloved, how could I begrudge a few coins and hurt our familial bond?" He was generous to poor relatives and friends, never hesitating to lend money when needed. Because of this, he accumulated debts amounting to thousands of gold pieces, with promissory notes filling his boxes. People were willing to break their promises, and he would burn the notes. When he was still in his prime, while on business in Zhenjiang, he saw a young man on a neighboring boat who looked distressed, holding a bowl and weeping. He asked the youth, who cried, "I am from Suzhou. I asked relatives for several gold pieces, but they were stolen, and now I have no way back. I might as well commit suicide by taking poison." Ye Fengkui took the bowl and threw it into the river, inviting the youth to return home with him and provided funds for his journey back. Throughout his life, he never tired of doing good, often acting in similar ways. Today, his eldest son, Ye Shunan, serves as the magistrate of Hubei; his second son, Ye Yutong, passed the imperial examination in 1629 and serves in the Ministry of Personnel; his third son, Ye Shutang, was appointed as a school supervisor; his fourth son, Ye Zhan yuan, passed the provincial examination as a runner-up in 1631; and his fifth son, Ye Yurong, passed the imperial examination in 1635 and serves in the Ministry of Works. I once traveled to Shu, where Ye Fengkui treated me as if I were his own children. I also became close friends with his sons, so I have fully heard about his virtuous deeds.

    88

    里乘子曰：叶封翁存心忠厚，见人之急，不惜转为称贷以济之。箧中借券恒满，负约者甚多，亦不与较，以故，寄居蜀中数十年，受累不少。予昔游蜀，翁日就谈乡前辈事，娓娓不倦，真不愧先正典型。此摘挺生吏部所选翁行略中数事，其居心待人，亦可概见矣。
    　　
    　　黄勤敏公
    　　
    　　乾隆某科，当涂黄勤敏公名田钺，入闱，坐某字第一号。薄暮，见号门外一女郎频来窥觑，讶之，以文场那得有女子至此？试危坐以觇其异。更柝初报，女来益数，似欲进号而不敢者。公素有胆略，迫而察之，果一女郎，乱头粗服，而姿色妖丽，颇带怒容。心知非人，因大声叱问：“何处妖魅！到此何事？”女歛衽频蹙，曰：“妾抱沉冤，请命于帝，特来寻第几号某生索命。尚书公请赐垂悯，毋阻妾路幸甚！”公念某生为同乡社友，倘放女去，性命休矣。

    Explanation

    88

    The village scholar said: Old Gentleman Ye had a sincere and kind-hearted nature. When he saw someone in urgent need, he would not hesitate to borrow money on their behalf to help them. His box of loan contracts was always full, and there were many who failed to fulfill their agreements, but he did not insist or argue with them. Therefore, he lived in Shu for several decades, suffering a great deal. I once traveled to Shu, and the old gentleman would talk about the affairs of his village's predecessors every day, speaking in a continuous and engaging manner, truly living up to the standards of the virtuous predecessors. This selection of several stories from the biographical notes of the old gentleman chosen by the Ministry of Personnel shows his character and how he treated others, which can be generally seen.

    During the Qianlong era, the diligent and sensitive official Huang Tianyue from Dangtu, named Huang, entered the examination hall and was seated in the first seat with a certain character. Late in the evening, he saw a young woman frequently looking through the gate, which surprised him, as how could there be a woman in the examination hall? He tried to sit upright to observe any unusual behavior. When the night watchman first reported the time, the woman came even more frequently, seeming to want to enter the hall but was hesitant. The official, known for his courage and strategy, approached and observed her, and indeed, it was a young woman with disheveled hair and coarse clothing, yet her beauty was striking and she wore a look of anger. Realizing that she was not an ordinary person, he loudly asked, "What kind of spirit or demon are you? What brings you here?" The woman bowed and sighed, saying, "I carry a deep grudge and have appealed to the emperor for justice. I have come specifically to seek the life of the student in the first seat. Minister Huang, please show mercy and do not obstruct my path!" The official thought that the student was a fellow townsman and friend, and if he allowed the woman to leave, the student's life would be in danger.

    89

    又以女称己尚书公，胆益壮。遂谓女曰：“某生系我故人，有何负汝？盍为我言之。”女腼然曰：“妾某氏，父佃生田，征租尝至妾家，屡以游语挑妾。会生失偶，指天信誓，聘妾为继室，决不相负。妾信为真，苟且从之，来往年余。屡促通媒妁，但漫应之。妾既体孕，又力促之，生遂绝迹不来，且论婚某氏，置妾不齿。无何，妾将分娩，父诘知其由，往告，生坚不肯承。父归诃责，妾力疾自踵生门，将面诘之。生预戒门者，拒勿为通。妾进退无归，乃投缳死。

    Explanation

    89

    He called himself the female's Shangshu Gong and his courage grew even stronger. He then said to the woman, "I am a close friend of your husband. What wrong have I done to you? Why not tell me about it?" The woman, blushing, replied, "I am a woman of the surname Meng. My father rented land to my husband, and the rent collector would often come to my home, flirting with me with casual words. When my husband lost his wife, he swore by heaven to marry me as his second wife, promising he would never betray me. I believed him and yielded to him out of necessity, and we lived together for over a year. He repeatedly urged the matchmaker, but he only answered carelessly. After I became pregnant, I pushed him hard, but he disappeared and did not return. Instead, he arranged a marriage with another family and treated me with disdain. Soon, I was about to give birth, and my father inquired about the reason. He went to inform him, but my husband firmly refused to admit anything. My father scolded him severely, and I struggled to my feet and went to his door, determined to confront him directly. My husband instructed the gatekeeper to refuse entry. With nowhere to turn, I hanged myself to death.

    90

    ”女且泣且诉，并曰：“人孰无情？似此薄情郎，誓必报之！”公曰：“汝言固是，然冤宜解不宜结。论生负心，不特汝衔恨九原，即闻者亦无不发指。但系我友，又不忍坐视不救。我今善筹一调停之法，必使服汝心，汝肯从否？”女曰：“公试言之。”曰：“汝与生以怨终，固以恩始。生固难宥，汝须念当初恩好，姑宽一线。当令生对汝书券，约定场后负荆，诣汝父请罪，仍定翁婿，并请汝骨归葬祖茔，册为继配。所娶某氏，生子先祧为汝子；某生倘贵显，诰典当先及汝。

    Explanation

    90

    The woman wept and spoke, saying, "Who is without feeling? Such a heartless man, I swear I will avenge you!" The gentleman replied, "Your words are true, but grievances should be resolved rather than allowed to accumulate. The man's unfaithfulness not only makes you harbor resentment in the afterlife, but even those who hear of it cannot help but feel anger. However, he is my friend, and I cannot stand idly by. I have devised a well-thought-out method of mediation, which will surely satisfy your heart. Will you agree to it?" The woman said, "Please tell me." He said, "Your relationship with the man ended in resentment, but it began in kindness. The man is indeed difficult to forgive, but you must remember the initial kindness and give him a small chance. I will make him write a contract, agreeing to carry thorns on his back after the event, and go to your father to ask for forgiveness. We will also confirm the relationship between father-in-law and son-in-law, and request that your remains be buried in the ancestral grave, recorded as a subsequent wife. The woman he married, who bore a son, will be adopted as your son; if the man becomes prominent, the imperial edict should first be bestowed upon you."

    91

    并请高行僧道，讽经超度。似此，庶可稍纾汝恨，汝意云何？”女俯首沉思良久，曰：“妾当一遵公命，但未免侥幸薄情郎矣！”公乃呼生至。骤见女，翼公肘下，骇欲死。公先数其罪，次具道其调停之法，问生允否，生击齿诺诺，连声应曰：“谨如公命。”并向女叩首乞恕。女麾令起，曰：“君休矣！非遇黄公，妾与君一重公案，不知几世方能了结也！”场后，公恐负女，督生往农家订翁婿，其余一如所约。是秋，公与生俱捷。后公官大宗伯；某生官至河帅，女封夫人。

    Explanation

    91

    The gentleman then invited highly virtuous monks and Taoist priests to recite scriptures for the deceased's liberation. In this way, it may slightly alleviate your grudge. What do you think?" The woman bowed her head and deeply contemplated for a long time, saying, "I will strictly follow your command, but it's hard to avoid being a bit lucky with a heartless man!" The gentleman then called the young man over. Upon suddenly seeing the woman, he was startled almost to death, standing by the gentleman's side. The gentleman first enumerated his sins, then fully explained the method of reconciliation, asking the young man if he agreed. The young man nodded and agreed, repeatedly responding, "I will strictly follow your command." He also knelt to the woman to beg for forgiveness. The woman waved her hand to signal him to stand up, saying, "You may rest now! If not for Mr. Huang, my case with you would remain unresolved for countless generations!" Afterwards, fearing to disappoint the woman, the gentleman urged the young man to go to the farmer's house to arrange the marriage, with the rest as agreed upon. That autumn, both the gentleman and the young man passed the exams. Later, the gentleman became the Grand Master of Rites; the young man became the Governor of the Yellow River, and the woman was granted the title of Lady.

    92

    里乘子曰：某生薄情已甚，鬼之临场索命，原是应得之罪。幸遇黄公为之调停，竟叨宥恕，鬼始终可谓多情矣。或曰：“某生福命好，所以能遇黄公力为调停，虽鬼极厉，亦无如何？”予曰：此究是某生徼幸而免，向使不遇黄公，又将奈何？即遇黄公，而袖手不为调停，又将奈何？福命虽好，可恃乎哉？况福命有不能尽如某生者乎？文人薄幸，今古甚多，每届临场，无不获报。少年闻此，亦当有以自警矣。

    Explanation

    92

    Li Chengzi said: The student was so lacking in affection that when the ghost came to claim his life, it was a punishment he deserved. Fortunately, he met Mr. Huang who mediated on his behalf, and he was ultimately forgiven. The ghost can truly be called full of emotion. Someone said, "The student had good fortune and destiny, which allowed him to meet Mr. Huang who helped mediate, even though the ghost was extremely fierce, there was nothing to be done about it." I replied, "This was ultimately a matter of the student escaping by luck. If he had not met Mr. Huang, what would have happened? Even if he had met Mr. Huang, and Mr. Huang had chosen not to mediate, what would have happened? Good fortune and destiny may be favorable, but can they be relied upon? Moreover, not everyone has the same level of good fortune as the student." Scholars are often fickle, both in ancient and modern times. Whenever they find themselves in critical situations, they inevitably face consequences. Young people who hear this should also take it as a warning to themselves.

    93

    张叔未先生
    　　
    　　嘉兴张叔未先生廷济，乾隆丙午秋试，侨居武林。故事试期，多有携杂物踵各寓求售者。某甲至张寓卖货，去时遗布帕一，内裹番镪十饼，先生见为同寓某生拾去，默识不言。少选，某甲踉跄来寻，意甚仓皇，询之某生，怒唾其面，以为诬己，将纠众缚而鞭之。先生急出排解，且谓甲曰：“而物固吾所拾，业将去市物，而勿妄诬人也。”以好言劝慰某生勿与甲较。复自解囊，如数付甲。甲大喜，感激叩谢而去。先是，甲父以株连逮系，得番镪廿饼便可释归。甲称贷戚友，仅得其半，馀俟售物以足其数。自计失镪，父罪莫释，则己亦不欲生，盖将问诸水滨矣。乃是科先生发解。越六十年道光丙午，其子庆容字稚村，亦登贤书第一。士林荣之。

    Explanation

    93

    Mr. Zhang Shuwei

    Mr. Zhang Shuwei of Jiaxing, courtesy name Tingji, resided temporarily in Wulin during the year Qianlong Bǐng Wǔ (1776) when he took the local-level examination. As was customary during the exam period, many people came to various residences to sell their wares. A certain man, Jia, came to Zhang’s residence to sell goods, and as he left, he left behind a cloth pouch containing ten pieces of foreign silver. Mr. Zhang saw that a fellow resident, a certain student, had picked it up and silently noted this without saying anything. Shortly afterward, Jia arrived in a hurried and unsteady manner, clearly in a state of panic. He questioned the student, who then spat in his face in anger, thinking he was falsely accused. Jia was about to gather people to bind and whip him. Mr. Zhang rushed out to intervene and said to Jia, “That item was indeed picked up by me, and I have already used it to buy goods. Do not recklessly accuse others.” He comforted the student with kind words, telling him not to quarrel with Jia. Then, he took out his own money and gave Jia the full amount. Jia was greatly pleased and, deeply grateful, bowed and thanked him before leaving.

    Previously, Jia’s father had been arrested due to being implicated in a case, and if twenty pieces of foreign silver could be obtained, he would be released. Jia had borrowed from relatives and friends and managed to get only half of what was needed, with the remainder to be made up by selling goods. Calculating that if he lost the silver, his father’s crime could not be absolved, and thus he would not wish to live, he had even considered taking his own life by seeking the waters.

    In that year, Mr. Zhang passed the examination. Sixty years later, in the year Daoguang Bǐng Wǔ (1836), his son Qingrong, courtesy name Zhi Cun, also ranked first in the list of successful candidates. The scholarly community greatly honored him.

    94

    里乘子曰：叔未先生博学能文，仪征相国阮文达公极所赏识。予弱冠游浙，犹及见之，时先生年已逾七十，身颀而长，白髯垂胸，语言和婉，望而知为有道之士。即此一事，亦可知其胸次超拓，迥异恒流。夫以番镪十饼得两解元，似此便益，谁不愿作？但苦临时吝财难舍耳。或曰：“造物弄人甚巧，安知是科非同寓生解首，暗中即坐此事，竟为先生夺去乎？”予抚掌笑曰：“信如君言，以天道断之，固应若是。”

    Explanation

    94

    It is said: Shuwei Master was broadly learned and skilled in writing, greatly admired by Grand Secretary and scholar-official Yuan Wendada from Yizheng. When I was a young man in my early twenties, traveling to Zhejiang, I still had the opportunity to meet him. At that time, Master Shuwei was over seventy years old, tall and slender, with a white beard hanging down to his chest, and his speech was gentle and courteous. Seeing him, one could immediately recognize him as a person of virtue and learning. Even from this single event, one can see that his mind was broad and open-minded, greatly different from the ordinary. To win two 解元 with ten pieces of foreign silver, such a benefit, who would not want to do it? But the difficulty lies in being reluctant to part with money at the time. Someone might say: "The gods play with people very cleverly. How can one know that this examination was not won by a fellow student, who secretly engaged in this matter, ultimately taken away by the master?" I clapped my hands and laughed, saying: "If what you say is true, judging by the principles of heaven, it should indeed be so."

    95

    姚孝廉
    　　
    　　吾邑姚孝廉某，耿介醇正，诚笃不欺。某科秋试，苦无资斧，试期既迫，无所为计。忽梦父责其何不赴试，姚以无资对。父曰：“上帝以汝为人不苟，今科应予一第。我三日内于东门外紫来桥下，赐汝元宝一枚。汝勿惮劳苦，连日昧爽，自往觅取，勿使旁人拾去也。汝须谨识勿忘。”姚寤后，初以为妄。甫交睫，又梦父来，责其违训不孝，词色甚怒，醒而异之。计姑往觅，即不得，何害？有粮胥某甲，素善掊克，家于东门，苦热早起，窥姚连日经过其门，蹀躞徬徨，不解何故。试叩之，姚附耳遂以梦告，且曰：“今已二日矣，明晨尚须一往，知君无须此戋戋，故敢相告。得否不可知，但须秘之，勿贻外人笑。”抑亦虑其攘夺也，坚嘱再三而别。

    Explanation

    95

    Yao the Scholar-Official

    In our county, there was a scholar-official named Yao, who was upright, sincere, and trustworthy. For a certain provincial examination, he was in dire need of funds, and the exam date was approaching, leaving him with no plan. Suddenly, he dreamed that his father scolded him for not taking the exam, and Yao replied that he lacked the funds. His father said, "The Heavenly Deity has seen that you are not careless in your conduct, and this year you should be granted a degree. Within three days, I will give you a gold ingot at the Purple Arrival Bridge outside the eastern gate. Do not fear hard work; every morning before dawn, go and retrieve it yourself, and do not let others pick it up. You must remember and not forget." After waking, Yao initially thought it was a dream. Just after closing his eyes, he dreamed again that his father came and scolded him for disobeying and being unfilial, his words and expressions very angry. Upon waking, Yao found it strange. He decided to go look for it anyway, thinking that even if he did not find it, there would be no harm. There was a tax collector named Jia, who was usually good at squeezing people for money, living near the eastern gate. On a hot morning, Jia saw Yao passing by his door every day, stumbling and hesitating, and did not understand why. He tried to ask Yao, who whispered the dream to him and said, "It has already been two days, and I still need to go tomorrow morning. Knowing that you do not need this small amount, I dare to tell you. Whether or not I will get it is unknown, but you must keep it secret and do not give outsiders reason to laugh." Yao was also worried that Jia might take it, so he repeatedly urged him and then parted.

    96

    甲窃腹嗤其迂，遂以锡镕元宝一枚，将送置桥下绐姚，以供一噱。适有友来访，猝藏匵中。友去，匆匆携置桥下。诘旦，又瞰姚踽踽然出东门去，须臾而返，喜容可掬。甲戏低声迎问曰：“今晨可得宝否？”姚点首笑曰：“得之矣。”甲戏相贺，益腹嗤之，因密遣人，伺姚归作何部署。少选，复命曰：“姚不知何处得一元宝，业兑钱若干，半作家用，半作川资，定于明日买舟赴试矣。”甲大骇，试启匵觇之，则锡宝依然尚在也。急诘纪纲，则曰：“昨曾收某户纳粮元宝一枚，置匵中，公未见也耶？”甲瞪目顿足叱曰：“竖子误我矣！奈何！”盖甲启匵取赝鼎时，初不虞内有真鼎，而误携以去也。急往姚处细告其由，且索返璧。姚让之曰：“我以公长者，故敢质言相告。今乃饰词诈索，何无赖也！”甲语塞。姚是科果领乡荐。

    Explanation

    96

    Jia privately mocked Yao's old-fashionedness and then made a silver ingot out of tin, planning to send it to the bridge to deceive Yao for a laugh. Just as a friend came to visit, he hastily hid it in a box. After the friend left, he hurriedly took it and placed it under the bridge. The next morning, he saw Yao walking alone out of the east gate again, returned shortly, and was full of joy. Jia jokingly asked in a low voice, "Did you get the treasure this morning?" Yao nodded and smiled, "I got it." Jia congratulated him jokingly but laughed at him even more. Then, he secretly sent someone to find out what Yao had done upon his return. Shortly after, the messenger reported, "Yao didn't know where he got the silver ingot, had exchanged it for some money, half for household expenses and half for travel expenses, and decided to take a boat the next day to take the exam." Jia was very surprised and tried to open the box to see, only to find that the tin ingot was still there. He quickly asked the steward, who said, "Yesterday, he received a silver ingot from a household as tax payment, put it in the box, didn't you see it?" Jia glared and stomped his feet, scolding, "This little brat has ruined me! What can be done?" Because when Jia opened the box to take the fake ingot, he hadn't expected there to be a real ingot inside and mistakenly took it away. He hastened to Yao's place to explain the situation in detail and demanded the return of the treasure. Yao rebuked him, "I thought you were a gentleman, so I dared to tell you frankly. Now you're using flattery to cheat. How unscrupulous." Jia was speechless. Yao indeed received a recommendation for the provincial examination this time.

    97

    里乘子曰：姚孝廉平日为人，只“耿介醇正、诚笃不欺”八字，天固已尝佑之矣。观其对糈吏附耳密告所梦，亦可见其坦率。乃某甲戏弄腐儒，方自喜其得计，不知己方戏弄人，而天即藉之以戏弄己乎？吾邑当承平极盛之时，凡糈吏之居室衣食，务极奢华，享用过于王侯，挥掷阿堵物如泥沙然，不甚爱惜。将此等不甚爱惜之财以济寒酸，最为得当。吾尤笑某甲误真为赝之后，公然往索返璧，呆蠢极矣。嗟乎！造化默相正人，骗罚狡吏，亦何巧哉！

    Explanation

    97

    The Village Scholar said: Yao, the Filial and Virtuous, was a person of uprightness and purity, sincerity and honesty, without deceit. Heaven had surely already favored him. His frankness can be seen when he whispered his dream to the official in charge of grain. Yet, there was someone who mocked a pedantic scholar, thinking he had succeeded in his scheme, not knowing that he was being mocked by Heaven in return. During our county's period of peace and prosperity, all officials in charge of grain lived in extreme luxury, enjoying themselves more than kings and princes, spending money as if it were sand, showing no regard for it. Using such carelessly spent wealth to help the poor would be most appropriate. I especially laugh at someone who, after mistaking something for a fake, boldly demanded its return, showing foolishness to the extreme. Alas! Heaven silently aids the virtuous, deceiving and punishing the cunning officials, how ingenious!

    98

    某公分校
    　　
    　　某公分校礼闱，夜阅一卷，文甚平庸，方将弃置，忽闻窗外诵云：“火楼火，裸妇躲，狐裘裹，秉烛达旦，尔与我。”声甚宏朗，怪之。复取前卷细玩，真无佳处，又弃置之，窗外诵如前。三置三诵，异而伏窗窥之，见一伟丈夫，赤面长髯，类世所塑汉寿亭侯，后随一女子，徘徊月下。知此卷必有盛德事，爰加评语呈荐，并将所闻所见，婉达主司。主司夜阅其文，颇不惬意，而窗外亦闻有朗诵前语者，窥之，果如某公言，遂拔殿一军。榜后，其人谒见房师，公谓：“吾之所以取君者，以德不以文。君究竟作何盛德事？”其人答言：“无有。”公因述所见闻，其人恍然叹曰：“是矣。

    Explanation

    98

    A certain official served as an examiner in the imperial examinations. One night, while reviewing a scroll, he found the writing to be quite ordinary and was about to discard it. Suddenly, he heard someone reciting outside the window: "Fire at the fire tower, a naked woman hides, wrapped in a fox fur coat, holding a candle until dawn, you and I." The voice was very loud and strange. Surprised, he took the scroll again and examined it carefully, but found no remarkable points. He was about to discard it again when the recitation outside the window continued as before. He did this three times, and each time the recitation followed. Intrigued, he leaned against the window to look out and saw a tall man with a red face and long beard, resembling the statue of Guan Yu, the Lord of Hanshou Pavilion, followed by a woman who was pacing under the moonlight. Knowing that this scroll must contain a great moral deed, he added a comment and recommendation, and gently conveyed his observations to the chief examiner. That night, the chief examiner read the scroll and was not particularly impressed by the writing, but he also heard the same recitation outside his window. He leaned out to see and indeed saw what the examiner had described. He then selected the candidate as the top one. After the results were announced, the candidate came to see his examiner. The examiner said, "I chose you not because of your writing, but because of your virtue. What great moral deed did you perform?" The candidate replied, "I did not do anything special." The examiner then recounted what he had seen and heard. The candidate suddenly realized and exclaimed, "That is it!"

    99

    先是计偕北上，舟泊村市。会市有火灾甚烈，时已夜半，闻有人踉跄上舟，启户视之，见一赤体妇人，蹲伏暗陬，羞慄堪怜，乃脱狐裘，掷使遮裹，呼入船房避寒。妇人固少艾，问知其夫亦系孝廉，益敬而矜之。秉烛达旦，呼舆送归。夫以迹涉可疑，颇相龃龉，且逼令大归。以试期既迫，匆匆解缆，后事不知如何？意者今科侥幸，其以是乎？”言毕，坐间一同年生，崩角在地，感且泣曰：“吾过矣！吾过矣！吾即夫夫是也。微君言，疑团莫释。今而后乃知世间真有狄梁公，而天道之果不爽也。”公闻之，抚掌称赞，谓：“非盛德君子，焉能若是？”乃命两生缔交，欢逾骨肉。后同年生旋里，喜迎妇归，遂为夫妇如初。

    Explanation

    99

    Previously, the scholar-official traveled north together for the purpose of taking the imperial examination, and his boat stopped at a village market. Just as there was a very intense fire in the market, it was already midnight, and he heard someone stumbling and staggering onto the boat. He opened the door and looked at her, and saw a naked woman crouching in a dark corner, ashamed and trembling, pitiable. He took off his fox fur coat and threw it to her to wrap herself, called her into the boat cabin to avoid the cold. The woman was indeed beautiful and young. He asked and found out that her husband was also a scholar-official, and respected and pitied her even more. He held the candle until dawn and called for a carriage to send her back. Her husband suspected her due to her strange appearance and they had many disagreements, and pressured her to return home. Because the examination date was approaching, he hurriedly untied the boat. What happened afterward is unknown. Perhaps she was fortunate this time, and it was because of this. The scholar-official knelt on the ground, said with emotion and tears, "I was wrong! I was wrong! Without your words, my doubts would not be resolved. Now I know that there is indeed a true Di Lianggong in the world, and that heaven's will is indeed just." The scholar-official applauded and praised, saying, "Only a person of great virtue could do such a thing." He ordered the two men to become friends, and their friendship was deeper than blood relations. The scholar-official returned home and happily welcomed his wife back, and they became husband and wife again.

    100

    里乘子曰：其人与夫夫出自同门，又同时谒师，足见造物暗中联络之巧，何尝愦愦？即味神所诵之语，质朴大似古繇词，断非凡人所可拟议。
    　　
    　　乡场显报
    　　
    　　道光乙未科湖南乡试，某生写七律一首于卷上，曰：“千里来观上国光，卷中潜被火焚伤。半生只为淫三女，七届谁怜贴五场。始信红颜为鬼蜮，悔从黑地结鸳鸯。而今敢告青云士，休道残花艳且香。”闻此生在闱得狂疾，寻卒。又浙江丁巳补行乙卯壬子两科乡试，一生入闱，在号壁题二绝句，掷笔大叫一声而绝。诗曰：“记否花阴立月时？倚栏偷赋定情诗。而今观试秋风冷，露湿罗鞋君未知。”“黄土无情玉骨眠，可怜欢意杳秋烟。何须更织登科记？修到鸳鸯便是仙。”此两生事，皆刘小馨司马亲见者，书以告予。少年子弟，当以为戒。

    Explanation

    100

    Lichengzi said: The person and the two men came from the same school and paid respects to the same teacher at the same time, clearly demonstrating the ingenious connections made by fate in the darkness. How could it be foolish? Even the taste of the divine words he recited was simple and similar to ancient incantations, certainly beyond the comprehension of ordinary people.

    Public Announcement of the Examination Results

    In the Daoguang era, during the year of the Rabbit (乙未), the provincial examination in Hunan province, a certain student wrote a seven-character regulated verse on his scroll, saying: "A thousand miles to witness the brilliance of the capital, secretly burned and injured in the scroll. Half a life spent seducing three women, in seven sessions, who pities me for five trials. Only now do I believe that beautiful women are like evil spirits, regretting having formed a bond of love in a dark place. Now, I dare to warn the high-ranking officials, do not speak of the faded flowers that were once beautiful." It is said that this student went mad in the examination hall and soon passed away. Another student from Zhejiang province, during the year of the Dragon (丁巳), participated in the supplementary examination for the years of the Rabbit (乙卯) and the Rat (壬子). He entered the examination hall, wrote two couplets on the wall of his examination room, threw down his brush, and shouted loudly before dying. The poems read: "Do you remember standing under the moon's shadow of flowers? Leaning on the railing, secretly composing love poems. Now, observing the exam in the cold autumn wind, the dew wets your silk shoes, and you do not know." "The yellow earth is无情 (without emotion), and the precious bones sleep. How pitiful that the joy of love has vanished like autumn smoke. Why bother to weave a register of successful candidates? To become a pair of mandarin ducks is like being a fairy." These two cases were personally witnessed by Commander Liu Xiaoxin, who wrote them down to inform me. Young people should take this as a warning.

    101

    里乘子曰：福州梁敬叔观察《劝戒续录》亦载前条，系湖南甲午科事。又后一条，林若衣大令云，浙江女鬼，系山阴张细娘。
    　　
    　　吾乡张生
    　　
    　　吾乡张生，巨族也。少美风仪，才华丰蔚。甫成童，入邑庠，长老均以远大期之。父为金匮县令，生随任读书，下帷攻苦，自谓科名可拾芥视之也。县绅有某公者，典学时，以赇败，令奉檄率弁役籍没其家，生亦偕往。绅有名园，擅花草竹木之盛，亭台池馆，接构极工，生乘间一游。正流览间，瞥见及笄女郎称娖入山洞去，意其身藏珠宝，亟蹑迹追之。既入洞，以袖拂女郎面，谛视之，盖绝色也。心动，迫与求合，女怒叱曰：“汝何人也？我乃某绅之女，汝敢犯也耶！”生冷笑曰：“汝某绅女，岂不畏我张公子耶？汝父有罪，我知汝身藏珠宝，得失在我掌握，汝敢不畏我耶！”女益怒曰：“我闻，罪人不孥，经之训也。

    Explanation

    101

    Li Chengzi said: The Persuasion and Admonition Continuation by Liang Jingshu of Fuzhou also records the earlier entry, which concerns an incident from the Jiawu examination in Hunan. Another later entry, contributed by Magistrate Lin Ruoyi, speaks of a female ghost from Zhejiang, identified as Zhang Xin娘 of Shanyin.

    My hometown's Zhang Sheng

    My hometown's Zhang Sheng was from a prominent family. As a youth, he was handsome and composed, with abundant talent and eloquence. Just as he came of age, he entered the county school, and the elders all expected great things of him. His father served as the magistrate of Jinkui County, and Zhang Sheng accompanied him to study, isolating himself in diligent study, to the point of regarding passing the imperial examinations as easy as picking up a single grain of rice. There was a certain official among the county gentry, who had fallen from office due to bribery while overseeing education. The magistrate received an official order and led officials and guards to seize the official's property, and Zhang Sheng went along with him. The official had a renowned garden, rich in flowers, grasses, bamboo, and trees, with pavilions, terraces, ponds, and halls connected in exquisite and elaborate fashion. Taking the opportunity, Zhang Sheng went for a tour. While admiring the scenery, he caught a glimpse of a maiden, just past her fifteenth year, walking with grace into a mountain cave. Suspecting that she carried jewels upon her person, he quickly followed her trail. Upon entering the cave, he brushed her face with his sleeve and looked closely at her — she was of peerless beauty. His heart stirred, and he pressed forward to seek union with her. The girl angrily rebuked him, saying, "Who are you? I am the daughter of a certain official; dare you commit a crime against me?" Zhang Sheng coldly replied, "You, daughter of a certain official, should you not fear me, Zhang's young master? Your father has committed a crime, and I know you carry jewels. The outcome is in my hands; dare you not fear me?" The girl became even more angry and said, "I have heard that the punishment of the guilty does not extend to their family — this is the teaching of the classics."

    102

    纵父有罪，何预我事？即身藏珠宝，一弱女子能藏几何？况身藏之物，法所不禁，汝又将奈何我耶！”生恨其语不逊，勃然怒曰：“汝谓我奈何汝不得，我偏要奈何汝！看汝又奈我何也！”乃叱众役褫去女裳衣，并缓内重私小结束，且褪及行缠，几至不留一缕，见女胸乳菽发，肌白如玉，竟体所藏缠臂金及他珠宝无算。生拊掌大笑，搜掠罄尽而去。女羞忿啜泣，遂投缳死。越岁秋试，生入闱，各艺草甫毕，将秉烛缮真。忽闻窸窣声，见一女子搴帘入，视之，即绅女也。大惊，携卷踉跄欲遁。女摇手，嫣然笑曰：“轻薄儿，何其怯也？妾非祸君者，何遁为？”生察其意不恶，心稍壮。叩其来意，女笑曰：“君畏妾，岂以前事未能去怀耶？以前事论之，君固爱妾，非有他意，可惜妾命薄耳。

    Explanation

    102

    Even if my father has committed a crime, how does it concern me? If I have hidden jewels, how many could a weak woman possibly hide? Moreover, the items hidden on the body are not prohibited by law, what can you do to me again?" The man was angered by her disrespectful words and suddenly became furious, saying, "You think you can't do anything to me, but I will do something to you! See what you can do to me now!" He then ordered the servants to remove the woman's outer clothing and inner garments, and even pulled down her stockings, leaving almost nothing on her. He saw her breasts and nipples, her skin as white as jade, and her entire body adorned with countless armlets and other jewels. The man laughed heartily, thoroughly searched, and took everything away. The woman, humiliated and angry, wept and then hanged herself. After a year, during the autumn examination, the man entered the examination hall, finished his preliminary drafts, and was about to light a candle to finalize his answers. Suddenly he heard a rustling sound and saw a woman enter by lifting the curtain; upon closer look, it was the daughter of the official. He was greatly surprised, grabbed his papers, and stumbled to flee. The woman waved her hand and smiled sweetly, saying, "You are so cowardly, light-hearted boy! I am not here to harm you, so why are you fleeing?" The man sensed that her intentions were not malicious and felt a bit more at ease. He asked her about her intentions, and the woman smiled and said, "Do you fear me because you still remember the past? Considering the past, you truly loved me, not with any other intention, but it's unfortunate that my fate was so poor."

    103

    妾不幸枉死，命也，于君何尤？且稽君冥籍，前程远大，感君爱妾私意，特来预贺，今科必中魁选。惟首艺中权遣词尚未妥适，必如此修饰，方为完璧。”生细思之，良然，果如女言，重加改订。女并为斟酌数字，始称尽美尽善。女笑曰：“得之矣。二三场好自为之，但求无弊，便可高枕坦卧，静听捷音。妾去矣。”歘然遽逝。生怅然神痴者久之，深感女意，又自悔前事孟浪，未免薄情太甚，然已无可如何矣。及揭晓，果中经魁。时生父已罢官家居，闻生闱中事，窃幸女不念旧恶；既得捷报，老怀愈增快慰。贺客阏门，乐不可支。献岁即趣子束装，计偕北上，以谓指日可作老封君矣。

    Explanation

    103

    I was unjustly killed, it is fate, what fault do I have with you? Moreover, consulting your records in the netherworld, your future is great. Moved by your private affection for me, I have come to congratulate in advance; you will definitely pass the top examination this year. Only the central part of your first essay is not yet properly phrased, and it must be revised in this way to be perfect. The man carefully considered it, and indeed, as she said, he revised it thoroughly. She carefully considered and adjusted the wording, and it was finally perfect. She smiled and said, "I have it now. Do well in the next few exams, just avoid any mistakes, and you can sleep soundly and peacefully, waiting quietly for good news. I am leaving now." She suddenly vanished. The man was deeply melancholic and lost in thought for a long time, deeply felt her intentions, and regretted his previous impulsive actions, feeling that he was too cold-hearted, but there was nothing to be done about it. When the results were announced, he indeed passed the top examination. At the time, his father had retired from office and lived at home. Hearing about his son's examination, he secretly hoped that the woman did not hold onto old grudges; having received the good news, the old man's heart was even more joyful. Guests came to congratulate at the door, and he could not contain his joy. At the beginning of the new year, he urged his son to pack his bags, planning to go north together, believing that he could soon become an honored father-in-law.

    104

    生去不数日，乃翁日晡假寐胡床，倏见女子披发伸舌来前，怒容满面，戟手指而詈之曰：“老贼尚在此梦梦耶！汝子乘人之危，无礼于妾，致死非命。汝初既失义方之教，后又无悔过之心，犹痴顽无耻，妄想作老封君耶！妾在闱中，非不能索汝子之命，然等闲措大，犹不足以大伤厥考心，必令其小有得意，使老贼快慰之馀，愈生奢望，然后妾乃得而甘心焉，则厥考之心伤矣。实告老贼，汝仅此子，妾稽其冥籍，秩居极品，寿臻期颐。妾请命于帝，已罚削殆尽，许妾于芦沟桥旅次报仇泄忿矣。汝尚梦梦，妄想作老封君耶？”遽前批其颊曰：“汝如不信，不日当有好音至矣！”其父惊觉，知其不祥，亟遣使兼程追生归。使至中途，见傔从已扶生柩南旋，果于芦沟桥旅次自经云。乃翁闻信，怨懊自挝，郁郁不乐，寻卒。其嗣以斩。

    Explanation

    104

    The son left home not many days later. One afternoon, his father, napping on a folding stool, suddenly saw a woman with disheveled hair and a protruding tongue approaching him. Her face was filled with anger, and she pointed her finger like a spear while scolding him: "Old thief, are you still dreaming? Your son took advantage of my misfortune and was disrespectful to me, causing my death by unnatural means. You failed to teach him right from wrong, and later had no remorse. Still foolish and shameless, you foolishly think you can become an old official? I in the inner chambers, am not powerless to take your son's life. But an ordinary scholar is not enough to deeply wound your father's heart. I must make him slightly successful, so that after your father's satisfaction, he grows even more ambitious. Only then can I take satisfaction, and your father's heart will be deeply wounded. I tell you, your son is your only child. I checked his fate in the afterlife records, and he is destined for the highest rank and to live to a hundred. I have petitioned the emperor, and his fate has been almost entirely erased. I have been allowed to take revenge at the inn on the Lugou Bridge. You still dream, foolishly thinking you can become an old official?" She suddenly slapped his cheek and said, "If you don't believe me, good news will come soon!" His father woke up in shock, realizing the bad omen, and immediately sent a messenger to return home at top speed. The messenger reached halfway and saw the servants carrying the coffin southward. Indeed, he had hanged himself at the inn on the Lugou Bridge. Upon hearing the news, the father was filled with regret and self-blame, and he became depressed and unhappy, eventually dying soon after. His descendant was executed.

    105

    里乘子曰：初女与生抵牾，其言理直气壮，生稍有天良，即当引咎自责，谢过不遑。乃恶其不逊，窘辱不留馀地，反拊掌大笑，自鸣得意，是诚纨恶少之所为，岂复成读书君子哉！致女饮恨枉死，其不能甘心于生也审矣。“乘人之危”四字，诛心之论；订生爰书，可谓铁案。顾女欲报怨于生，先乃显斥其父，良以生之轻薄，必其父平日骄纵怙恶，有以酿成。所谓“初失义方之教”，“后无悔过之心”二语，又是老吏断狱，以此归咎，乃翁虽百喙不能辞责。

    Explanation

    105

    里乘子曰：初女与生抵牾，其言理直气壮，生稍有天良，即当引咎自责，谢过不遑。乃恶其不逊，窘辱不留馀地，反拊掌大笑，自鸣得意，是诚纨恶少之所为，岂复成读书君子哉！致女饮恨枉死，其不能甘心于生也审矣。“乘人之危”四字，诛心之论；订生爰书，可谓铁案。顾女欲报怨于生，先乃显斥其父，良以生之轻薄，必其父平日骄纵怙恶，有以酿成。所谓“初失义方之教”，“后无悔过之心”二语，又是老吏断狱，以此归咎，乃翁虽百喙不能辞责。

    The author of Li Cheng said: At first, the woman clashed with the man, and her words were just and bold. If the man had any sense of right and wrong, he would have taken responsibility for his mistakes and apologized immediately. Instead, he disliked her rudeness, humiliated her without leaving any room for grace, and laughed heartily, feeling self-satisfied. This is truly the behavior of a spoiled and immoral young man; how can he still be considered a scholar or a gentleman? The woman died unjustly with regret, and she cannot rest easy with the man's actions. The phrase "taking advantage of someone's misfortune" is a severe criticism of his intentions; the legal proceedings against the man can be called an ironclad case. However, the woman wanted to seek revenge against the man, so she first openly criticized his father, because the man's behavior was surely the result of his father's daily indulgence and encouragement of evil. The phrases "initially lost the teaching of righteousness and proper conduct" and "later had no heart to repent" are the judgments of an experienced official, who attributed the blame to the man's father, who, even if he had a hundred mouths, could not avoid responsibility.

    106

    女言“汝仅此子”四字，最为刺心；且告以冥籍云云，更属恶极。意谓汝子前程远大，非寻常措大可比，姑令小有得意，以为明效；然后于乃翁妄存奢望时，下一辣手，使老怀快慰，乐不可支者，转而伤心怨懊，郁郁不乐以死，且斩其嗣，报亦惨矣！观女闱中绐生之言，玩弄孺子，直同儿戏，的是妙人；但不知芦沟桥旅次报仇泄忿时又作何状，想必另换一副面孔，不似前嫣然含笑矣。惜张生一去，不能起于九原而问之也！此事予儿时即习闻之，晤我友张南耕大令光甲，询之良然。大令与生同族，并言其领乡荐时，年才十有八耳。

    Explanation

    106

    The woman said, "You only have this one child," which was the most painful statement. She also mentioned the records of the afterlife, which was extremely wicked. She meant that your son's future was bright, not like an ordinary scholar, so she let him have a little success, as a clear example. Then, when your father foolishly harbored extravagant hopes, she gave a harsh blow, making his elderly heart's joy and comfort turn into sorrow and discontent, leading him to die in sorrow. Moreover, she cut off his descendants, a cruel punishment indeed! Looking at the deceptive words she used in the inner chambers of her home, playing with the child like a child's game, she was indeed a clever person. But I wonder how she would act when seeking revenge at the Luogou Bridge, likely changing her appearance and demeanor, not smiling sweetly as before. Unfortunately, Zhang San went away, and I cannot rise from the grave to ask about it. I heard this story since childhood, and when I met my friend Zhang Nangeng, the magistrate Guangjia, he confirmed it. He and Zhang San were of the same clan, and he said that Zhang San passed the local examination when he was only eighteen years old.

    107

    张玉常观察
    　　
    　　吾乡张玉常观察曾飏，大学士文端公曾孙，少司空讳廷之孙也。未达时，赴金陵秋试，舟次牛渚，见上流一女尸，赤体浮水面。观察恻然，亟命榜人援置岸上，以红氍毹裹之；又出钱市棺，殡瘗义山，树碣识之而去。是科，房官某公与典试官在闱，每夜阅卷毕就寝，甫交睫，即见一红衣女子立帐外，口诵“且士林有气节，而后朝廷有功名”二句。及阅观察卷，开讲恰此二语，首题乃“宪问耻，子曰：邦有道椸”也。房官、典试官俱窃异之。阅其文，通幅称是，意是名手，且系盛德君子，故一经呈荐，即拔冠多士。既揭晓，房官、典试官话及前梦，彼此相符，叹为奇事。及观察谒见，俱以所梦询之，观察沈思良久，意是葬女一事，因具以告，始各恍然。

    Explanation

    107

    Inspector Zhang Yuchang

    My hometown's Inspector Zhang Yuchang, Zeng Yang, is the great-grandson of Grand Councillor Wen Duan Gong and the grandson of Junior Minister of Works, whose name is Ting Xi. Before he achieved fame, he traveled to Jinling for the autumn examination. While his boat was moored at Niuzhu, he saw a female corpse floating in the water upstream, naked. The inspector felt compassion and immediately ordered the boatmen to pull her onto the shore, wrap her in a red carpet, and buy a coffin to bury her in a public cemetery, erecting a stone monument to mark the place before leaving. That year, during the examination, one of the examiners and the chief examiner were in the examination hall. Every night after they finished reading the papers and went to sleep, as soon as they closed their eyes, they would see a red-clothed woman standing outside their curtains, reciting the two lines, "Scholars must have integrity before they can achieve success in the court." When they read Inspector Zhang's paper, the opening lines were exactly these two lines, and the first question was "Xian asked about shame, the Master said: 'In a well-governed state.'" Both the examiner and the chief examiner found this strange. Upon reading his entire paper, they thought it was written by a master hand and that he was a gentleman of great virtue. Therefore, once they recommended him, he was selected as the top candidate among many. After the results were announced, the examiner and the chief examiner discussed their previous dream, and it matched what they had seen. They marveled at the strange event. When Inspector Zhang came to meet them, they all asked him about his dream. After deep thought, he realized it was related to the burial of the woman, and he explained it to them. Only then did they all understand.

    108

    里乘子曰：似此等善事，所谓易如折枝，人人能为，乃守钱奴视若无睹，诚为可叹耳。张南耕大令言，先是有怀宁县宿儒赵汝谐者，前科落第，以来岁有庆榜，决意不归，僦居兰若，下帷待试。徐夕，梦至佛殿，闻数人聚语，姑伏暗陬侦之。闻一人言：“明秋江南乡试，未审名数定否？”一人答曰：“定矣。”曰：“十八魁何人也？”曰：“第一名解元，桐城县张曾飏。”后历数至十八名，其第九名则系赵名，心窃喜之。又闻问曰：“闱艺何人所拟？”答曰：“拟者系方望溪先生及某某诸公。”曰：“颇识之否？”曰：“颇能识之。首艺题‘宪问耻，子曰：邦有道榖’。”乃历将所拟十八魁文一一朗诵毕。赵澄心定神，一一默识。忽闻寺中钟鼓声，惊觉，见东方已白。

    Explanation

    108

    Li Chengzi said: Such good deeds are as easy as breaking a branch, something everyone can do, yet miserly people treat them as if they do not see them, truly lamentable. Zhang Nangeng, the magistrate, said that previously there was an elderly scholar named Zhao Ruhai from Huaining County who had failed the imperial examination. Because the next year had a special list for candidates who were descendants of officials, he decided not to return home and rented a place in a temple to study diligently in preparation for the exam. One evening, he dreamed of reaching the Buddha hall and heard several people talking. He hid in a dark corner to eavesdrop. He heard one person say, "Next autumn's provincial examination in Jiangnan, is the number of successful candidates determined yet?" Another replied, "It is determined." "Who are the top 18 candidates?" "The first place is the jieyuan, Zhang Zengyang from Tongcheng County." After listing the names up to the 18th, the ninth name was Zhao's own, and he secretly rejoiced. He then heard someone ask, "Who prepared the examination essays?" The answer was, "They were prepared by Mr. Fang Bangxi and several other scholars." "Do you recognize them?" "I can recognize them. The first essay topic is 'When Xian asked about shame, the Master said: In a well-governed state, one should hold a position.'" He then recited all the prepared essays for the top 18 candidates one by one. Zhao calmed his heart and mind, memorizing them word for word. Suddenly, he heard the sound of bells and drums from the temple, woke up in a start, and saw that the east was already bright.

    109

    诧为奇梦，亟披衣起，濡笔默书第一名文，却一字不复记忆，惟记得第九名一篇，姑录藏行箧，以观后验。及入闱，果是此题，赵大喜，爰走笔录就，时号门未开，不能交卷，偶至第几号，见其人正缮首艺，以后二比有出无对，思索殊苦。赵觇卷面籍贯姓名，恰是桐城张某，又窥其文，前幅果即梦中所闻第一名之作。以有所触，后幅遂复记忆。因抚其人肩曰：“公何思之苦耶？”其人怒曰：“我自苦思，干卿甚事！何相嬲也？”赵笑曰：“公请息怒，此文小子仿佛识之。”索笔一挥而就。张读之，愕然曰：“公何由知我心也？顷构思正复如是，但苦未就耳。”赵附耳密以梦告，并戒勿泄，张亦窃喜。揭晓，果然。两人由是订交，较他同年，情谊倍觉亲厚云。由此观之，足征科名自有定数，岂人力所能为耶？

    Explanation

    109

    He was startled by the strangeness of the dream and quickly got up, putting on his clothes. He moistened his brush and silently wrote down the first-ranked essay he had heard in the dream, but he could not remember a single word of it. However, he only remembered one article of the ninth rank, so he temporarily recorded and stored it in his travel box, to observe the outcome later. When he entered the examination hall, it turned out to be this question, and Zhao was greatly pleased. Then he quickly wrote it down, but the examination hall doors were not yet open, so he could not submit the paper. He happened to go to room number, and saw the person just finishing the first part of the essay. The next two parts had something to say but no answers, and the person was thinking very hard. Zhao peeked at the name, hometown, and name on the paper, and it happened to be Zhang from Tongcheng. He also looked at the text, and the first part was indeed the first-ranked work heard in the dream. Because of some inspiration, the second part was then remembered. Then he patted the person's shoulder and said, "Why are you thinking so hard?" The person angrily said, "I am thinking hard on my own, what business is it of yours! Why bother me?" Zhao smiled and said, "Please calm down, I vaguely recognize this text." He took the pen and finished it in one stroke. Zhang read it and said in surprise, "How did you know my thoughts? Just now I was thinking like this, but it was difficult to finish." Zhao whispered the dream in his ear and warned not to reveal it. Zhang was also secretly happy. When the results were announced, as expected. The two people then became friends, and their friendship felt even closer and thicker than with others of the same year. From this, it is sufficient proof that success in exams has its own destiny; how could it be achieved by human effort?

    110

    余镜湖太史
    　　
    　　咸丰己未秋，粤寇尚窃据金陵，暂借浙闱，举行江南乡试。婺源余镜湖太史鉴，时为诸生，寄居如皋，资斧无措，几不能应试，赖各友醵助，始克束装就道。将至浙省，停车河畔待渡，见前舟渡人甚多，半是赴试者，中流大风骤起，溜疾舟重，遂致覆溺。太史恻然，亟以手指车大声呼曰：“我带路资甚丰，有能援一人上岸者，酬白金百镒。”濒河居民多习泅水，时方秋获，农人贪财，咸辍业从田中趋至，争脱衣下河，将所溺之人尽拯置岸上，俱庆再生。众向太史索酬，太史又笑指曰：“我资具在车上，尔曹自取可也。

    Explanation

    110

    In the autumn of 1859, during the reign of Emperor Xianfeng, the Taiping rebels still occupied Nanjing. The Jiangnan provincial examination was temporarily held in the Zhejiang examination hall. Yu Jinghu, a scholar-official from Wuyuan, Anhui, was then a student and was staying in Rugao, Jiangsu. He had no means to pay for his travel expenses and was almost unable to take the exam. Relying on the help of his friends, who pooled their resources, he finally managed to pack and set out on his journey. As he approached Zhejiang, he stopped by the riverbank to wait for a ferry. He saw that many people had already crossed in earlier boats, half of whom were going to take the exam. Suddenly, a strong wind arose in the middle of the river, the current was swift, and the boat was heavy, causing it to capsize and resulting in people drowning. The scholar-official felt pity and immediately pointed at his carriage and shouted loudly, "I have a lot of travel money. Whoever can rescue one person to the shore will be rewarded with a hundred taels of silver." The residents near the river were skilled swimmers. It was the time of autumn harvest, and the farmers were greedy for money. All left their work in the fields and rushed to help. They competed to take off their clothes and jump into the river, rescuing all the people who had fallen into the water and placing them on the shore. All were overjoyed to be saved. The crowd asked the scholar-official for the reward. The scholar-official smiled and pointed to his carriage, saying, "My money is in the carriage; you may take it yourselves."

    111

    ”众展其襆被，惟铜钱十余千文；又启视箱中，则旧衣数袭、破书数本而已。众索然失望。问曰：“君资何在？”曰：“我资具在此，不汝欺也。”曰：“然则一人百金之说何谓也？”太史笑曰：“我姑妄言之耳。尔曹如谓我食言，所拯诸人具在，生死惟君，既援之上岸，再推之下水如何？”众哗然曰：“君傎也耶？天下只有救命，那有戕命之理！”太史笑曰：“若然，尔曹即当行一善事，请不必再较锱铢矣。”遂取钱十千给众曰：“戋戋薄敬，聊以塞责。”众相视无可如何，瓜分其钱而去。是科，太史登贤书第一名。此亦张南耕大令为予言者。

    Explanation

    111

    The crowd unfolded his bedding, finding only several thousand copper coins within; then they opened the box, discovering only several old clothes and some worn-out books. The crowd was disappointed and disheartened. They asked, "Where is your wealth?" He replied, "My wealth is here, and I am not deceiving you." They said, "Then what does the claim of a hundred gold coins per person mean?" The historian laughed and said, "I was just joking. If you think I am breaking my word, all the people I saved are here, their lives and deaths depend on you. How about after rescuing them to the shore, pushing them back into the water?" The crowd exclaimed, "Are you crazy? In the world, there is only saving lives, how could there be a reason to harm lives!" The historian laughed again and said, "If so, you should do a good deed, please don't argue over small amounts anymore." He then took ten thousand coins and gave them to the crowd, saying, "A small token of respect, just to fulfill my duty." The crowd looked at each other, unable to do anything, and divided the money among themselves and left. In that year, the historian was the first to be listed in the imperial examination. This is also what the magistrate Zhang Nangeng told me.

    112

    里乘子曰：大令并言，太史素抱不羁之才，兄升，字见龙，幼举武孝廉；其封翁正行先生，精越人术，侨居如皋四十余年，为人治疾，遇贫穷者不惟不责谢，且施药饵，历久不懈。即此，阴德不小，彼苍能不默有以报之哉！观太史仓猝应变，指车一呼，众人云集，似此急智，亦人所难及；况倾资分贶，毫无吝色，谓非豪杰之士乎？宜其弁冕贤书而簪毫词馆也。

    Explanation

    112

    Li Cheng said: The great decree speaks together, Tai Shi has always possessed unrestrained talent. His brother, Xian Long, was young when he was selected as a scholar-official excelling in both military and filial piety. His father, Zhengxing, was a retired official and expert in the medical practices of the Yue people. He resided as a guest in Rugao for more than forty years, treating others' illnesses. When encountering poor people, he not only did not demand payment, but he even provided medicine and food, continuing this practice for a long time without tiring. Thus, his hidden virtue is not small. Could heaven not secretly reward him? Observing Tai Shi's ability to respond to sudden changes, pointing to a carriage and calling out, a crowd gathers. Such quick intelligence is also hard for people to match. Moreover, spending all his resources to distribute gifts and favors, without any sign of reluctance, can such a person not be called a heroic or outstanding individual? It is fitting that he achieves high status in both government and literature.

    113

    甲乙偕试
    　　
    　　甲与乙偕赴秋试，襆被同车。日暮失路，见前有茅屋数椽，因往叩门，闻内哭声甚哀。久之，哭声甫辍，一老妇篝灯开门，问：“客胡为者？”甲乙以失路借宿告。老妇曰：“我家无男子，屋又湫隘，奈何？”甲乙恳曰：“但假容膝地，得免露宿，受惠不浅，他无求也。”老妇曰：“既不嫌亵，敢不如命。”甲乙大喜，命仆解装，同随老妇入，展被于地，将坐以待旦。乃问老妇：“姆家男子何往？顷闻哭声，何哀之深也？”老妇叹曰：“亡夫亡儿，俱为诸生，颇负微名。前岁大疫，不幸相继而逝。今将服满，孀妇难以存活，况亡人去世时，殡殓绌资，不无债负，不得已将儿妇改蘸。

    Explanation

    113

    A and B Took the Provincial Exam Together

    A and B went together to take the provincial exam, wrapping their bedding in the same carriage. At dusk, they lost their way and saw a few thatched cottages ahead. They went to knock on the door and heard a very sad crying sound inside. After a while, the crying sound finally stopped. An old woman lit a lamp and opened the door, asking, "What are you guests doing?" A and B explained that they had lost their way and were asking to stay for the night. The old woman said, "My house has no men, and it is small and cramped. What can I do?" A and B pleaded, "Just give us a place to sit. We can avoid sleeping out in the open, which would be a great favor. We ask for nothing else." The old woman replied, "If you do not mind, I dare not disobey your request." A and B were very happy and told their servant to unpack their belongings. They followed the old woman into the house, spread their bedding on the ground, and prepared to sit and wait until dawn. They then asked the old woman, "Where did the men of your family go? I just heard the crying. How deep was the sorrow?" The old woman sighed, "My deceased husband and son were both scholars and had some reputation. Last year, there was a great epidemic, and unfortunately, they died one after another. Now, the mourning period is almost over, and a widow finds it hard to survive. Especially since the deceased passed away without enough funds for burial, there is no debt. Unavoidably, I had to remarry my daughter-in-law."

    114

    昨甫有成说，姑妇难分，是以哀耳！”甲乙闻之恻然，问：“身价几何？”曰：“已说定白金四十两，择日人财两交矣。”曰：“汝妇毕竟愿守愿去？”老妇泣曰：“我妇素贤孝，如愿去，不待服满矣！今实逼处此，难再迁延，那得不悲！”甲乙又问其父子姓名，知无虚妄，不胜太息。因附耳筹议，谓老妇曰：“我二人敬汝妇贤孝，又怜汝姑妇难分，愿赠白金四十两，免汝妇改蘸，何如？”老妇闻言，裣衽伏地，泣谢曰：“若此，恩同再造，结草莫报。”甲乙急掖老妇起，启箧出金，如数付讫，天明兴辞而去。时七月中旬，新秋残暑，晴雨不时。一日晡后，暴雨如注，薄暮始霁，泥淖，车艰于行。方踌躇间，月出东山，皎若晶镜，路傍有一小屋，门外二人徘徊，若有所待。

    Explanation

    114

    Yesterday, I had just made up my mind, and it's hard to distinguish between a daughter-in-law and a mother-in-law, which is why I'm sad!" Upon hearing this, Jia and Yi were moved with compassion and asked, "How much is her value?" The old woman replied, "It has been agreed upon that forty taels of silver is the amount, and a date will be chosen for the exchange of people and property." Jia and Yi then asked, "Will your wife ultimately stay or leave?" The old woman wept and said, "My wife has always been virtuous and filial. If she wishes to leave, she won't wait until the mourning period is over! But now she is compelled to stay here, and it's difficult to delay further. How can one not be sad!" Jia and Yi then asked the names of her husband and son, and upon confirming that there was no deception, they sighed deeply. They then leaned close to discuss, and said to the old woman, "The two of us respect your wife for her virtue and filial piety, and we also pity the strained relationship between you and your daughter-in-law. We are willing to give forty taels of silver to prevent your wife from remarrying. What do you think?" Upon hearing this, the old woman knelt and bowed her head, weeping with gratitude, and said, "If so, the kindness is like being reborn, and even tying grass to repay the debt is impossible." Jia and Yi quickly helped the old woman up, opened their box, and handed over the silver as agreed. At dawn, they bid farewell and departed. It was mid-July, the late summer of early autumn, with the weather alternating between clear and rainy. After noon on one day, heavy rain poured down, and it cleared up at dusk, leaving the terrain muddy and difficult. While hesitating, the moon rose over Mount Dong, bright and clear like a crystal mirror. By the roadside, there was a small house, and two people were pacing outside the door, as if waiting for something.

    115

    见甲乙至，拱手前迎曰：“两先生辛苦哉！如不嫌蜗居，敢请税驾。”甲乙就视，前一老者，苍髯垂胸，年可五十许，后一少者，年约三十以来。亟与为礼，曰：“吾侪正苦栖止无所，荷长者高谊挽留，感甚！惟仓猝干扰，未免太率。”老者曰：“二公休得谦让，蜗居狭陋，勿罪为幸。”遂肃客入。屋止一楹，东西对设二榻，余无长物。命仆解装宿西榻，请甲乙展被东榻。宾主席地环坐，老者命少者出酒肴列地上，笑曰：“执炊无人，暮夜不能举火，有屈先生冷啜，殊愧人也。”甲乙谢曰：“天气尚暖，冷啜何碍？”彼此酬酢，谈笑甚欢。甲乙叩问主人姓名，笑而不答。

    Explanation

    115

    When the two gentlemen arrived, the elder bowed and stepped forward to greet them, saying, "You two have worked hard! If you do not mind our humble abode, we dare to ask you to stay with us." The two gentlemen accepted the invitation and looked around. In front was an elderly man with a long white beard hanging down to his chest, about fifty years old, and behind him was a younger man, about thirty years old. They quickly bowed in greeting, and the younger man said, "We are currently struggling to find a place to stay, and we are deeply grateful for your kindness in inviting us to stay. We apologize for any inconvenience we may cause, but we are too hasty in our arrival." The elder replied, "The two of you need not be so modest. Our humble dwelling is narrow and shabby, but please do not blame us for it." He then led the guests inside. The house had only one room, with two beds set up facing each other, and no other valuables. He ordered the servant to unpack and sleep in the west bed, and asked the two gentlemen to spread their bedding on the east bed. The guests sat on the floor in a circle, and the elder instructed the younger man to bring out the wine and food and place them on the ground. With a smile, the elder said, "There is no one to cook, so we cannot light a fire at night. It is truly shameful to trouble you to drink cold, but please do not mind." The two gentlemen thanked him, saying, "The weather is still warm, so there is no harm in drinking cold." They then engaged in friendly conversation and laughter. The two gentlemen inquired about the elder's name, but he smiled and did not answer.

    116

    少选，月至中天，明河横练，老者笑曰：“良夜逢嘉客，闷饮殊属无趣，两先生志切观光，何妨各拟三艺，以消良夜？”少者曰：“诺。”爰出纸笔，拈四书题三，强甲乙同作。甲乙构思颇苦，见老者少者走笔风驰，顷刻三艺各就。老者汇付甲乙曰：“我二人遁迹荒郊，不谈此调久矣。草草急就，敢求斧正。”甲乙读之，叹为杰作，自愧不及。老者曰：“夜将阑矣，两先生请少寐，以待明发。老夫亦倦欲眠，不能久陪矣。”言毕，与少者枕藉地上，倏已熟睡。甲乙以行路劳乏，一偃仰便入黑甜。无何，仆醒，见己身与甲乙各卧厝柩上，大惊，亟呼甲乙起，互相骇愕，拂视前和，书有姓名，盖即老妇之夫若子也。心知有异，喜夜作各艺俱在，分藏箧中。入闱，果此三题，录之，果同中式；春闱，复联捷成进士。甲乙不忘鬼德，又各馈老妇百金，并为其子妇请旌于朝，以表贤孝焉。

    Explanation

    116

    Shortly thereafter, the moon rose high in the sky, and the Milky Way stretched across like a silver ribbon. The older person smiled and said, "A good night meets a fine guest, but drinking alone is particularly boring. The two gentlemen are eager to visit and observe; why not each prepare three pieces of work to pass the good night?" The younger person replied, "Agreed." He then produced paper and ink and selected three questions from the Four Books for the two gentlemen to answer. The two gentlemen struggled with their work, while the older and younger persons quickly completed their pieces. The older person handed over the completed works to the two gentlemen and said, "We have been living in seclusion in the wilderness and haven't talked about this for a long time. We hastily and carelessly completed these, and we humbly ask for your corrections." The two gentlemen read the works and were amazed, feeling inferior. The older person said, "The night is nearly over. The two gentlemen please sleep a little to rest for the morning. I too am tired and want to sleep; I cannot stay longer." After saying this, he and the younger person lay down on the ground and suddenly fell into a deep sleep. The two gentlemen, tired from their journey, also fell into a deep sleep. Soon, one of them woke up and saw themselves and the two gentlemen lying on the coffins. They were greatly alarmed and hastily called the two gentlemen to wake up. They were mutually shocked and alarmed, brushing and reading the previous works, which had names written on them. It turned out to be the husband and son of the old woman. They understood something was amiss but were glad that the works from the night were all there, so they separately stored them in their cases. They entered the examination hall, and it turned out to be these three questions. They both passed the exam. In the spring imperial examination, they successively passed and became Jinshi. The two gentlemen did not forget the kindness of the spirits and gave the old woman a hundred gold pieces each, petitioning the court to honor the son and daughter-in-law to show their filial piety and virtue.

    117

    里乘子曰：鬼知报德，足征杜回结草之言不妄。此定远方芷春茂才为予言者。芷春并能历举其姓名，惜予不复记忆矣。

    Explanation

    117

    Lǐ Chéngzǐ said: Ghosts know how to repay kindness, which is sufficient to prove that the words of Du Hui tying grass are not false. This is definitely something that the talented scholar Zhi Chun from a distant place told me. Zhi Chun was able to list their names in detail, but I regret that I no longer remember them.

    118

    林妃雪
    　　
    　　熊生瑞缥，字凡葊，姑苏太湖厅人。性倜傥，容止甚都。读书邓尉山中，冬夜，漏二下，霜月满天，清辉皎洁，顾而乐之，徘徊忘寐。忽闻管弦声，抑扬盈耳，若远若近。信步迹之，数武，见深林中楼台窅霭，气象壮严，石兽当门，双扉未；堂皇灯烛辉煌，人影幢幢，往来蹀躞。潜蹑足，次且入内，伏窗窃窥，一美人宫装上坐，年可三十许；右侧坐一美人，齿亦相等，着淡黄绡衫，手弹箜篌；联肩坐一美人，年二十以来，着葱绿水云之裾，两腕约金玉条脱，手擫玉笛；对坐一美人，衣绛绡帔，年可十七八，鬓边贴翠钿，轻拍牙板，疾徐中节；其余满堂姝丽，年皆二十上下，列坐倾听，所衣各色不同，类皆轻绡软縠，更无一人着羔狐者。窃怪时方苦寒，何不畏冷？少选曲终，上坐美人赞曰：“南昌夫人古调独弹，一洗筝琶俗响，我辈何幸，闻所未闻。

    Explanation

    118

    A Woman Named Lin Feixue

    Xiong Sheng Ruipiao, courtesy name Fan'an, was from the Taihu Hall in Suzhou. He had a bold and unrestrained character, and his appearance and demeanor were very graceful. While studying in the mountains of Dingshu, one winter night, as the water clock struck two, the moon was full and bright, its clear light illuminating the sky. He looked back and found joy in it, wandering and forgetting to sleep. Suddenly, he heard the sound of music, rising and falling, filling his ears, as if it were both far away and near. He followed the sound, taking a few steps, and saw a deep forest with a mysterious and grand pavilion, the atmosphere imposing. Stone statues stood at the entrance, and the double doors were not open. Inside, the grand hall was brightly lit with candles, and people's shadows moved about, walking back and forth. He sneaked in quietly, cautiously entering the interior, and peeked through the window secretly. Sitting high was a woman in imperial attire, around thirty years old; to her right sat another woman of similar age, wearing a light yellow silk robe and playing a konghou; sitting side by side was a woman in her twenties, wearing a green and blue robe with water cloud patterns, with gold and jade bracelets on both wrists, holding a jade flute; facing her was a woman in a red silk cape, around seventeen or eighteen, with green jade hairpins on her temples, lightly tapping a wooden clapper, the rhythm appropriate. The rest of the beautiful women in the hall were all around twenty, sitting in rows and listening, dressed in various colors, all in light and soft fabrics, and no one was wearing fur. He secretly wondered why they did not feel cold in such bitterly cold weather? After a short while, the music ended, and the woman sitting high praised, "Madame Nanchang plays ancient melodies alone, washing away the vulgar sounds of the zither and pipa. How fortunate we are to hear something we have never heard before."

    119

    ”黄衫美人谦曰：“三日不弹，手生荆棘，蒙贤妃谬赞，更觉可羞。”东坐一美人，着藕色五铢之衣者，笑曰：“夫人曲奏雅，毫发无憾，惟罗夫人笛声入破，稍滞半板，赖贵主灵心妙腕，巧为偷声，不然几难合拍。意者心念羊生，神移手涩耶？”上坐者笑曰：“欲加之罪，何患无辞。似此吹毛索瘢，得勿令夫人齿冷？”着葱绿者叹曰：“妾当日不过念羊生尚有仙骨，不惜以一粒金丹度其出世，固无他事，一经慧业文人哓哓饶舌，遂不觉轻薄殆尽矣！”西坐一衣青绡褂者笑曰：“姊姊与羊生一段因缘，尚属形迹可疑；尤可笑者赵师雄小子，偶然醉寐，梦中便妄生幻想，若非翠羽唤醒，又要造出几许黑白矣。”东坐一衣紫罗襦者笑曰：“师雄仅托于梦，犹不敢公然唐突；惟有老逋无赖，判将一种清寒骨，老气横秋，硬呼我辈作妻，不尤令人喷饭耶！”满堂大笑。

    Explanation

    119

    The Yellow Robe Beauty modestly said, "If I do not play the instrument for three days, my hands feel as rough as thorns. To be praised by a noble lady, I feel even more ashamed." A beautiful woman sitting to the east, dressed in a light pink five-zhu robe, smiled and said, "Madam's music is so refined, without a single flaw. Only Luo Madam's flute playing slightly lags in the rhythm, but thanks to Your Highness's skillful touch, she cleverly adjusted the melody. Otherwise, it would have been difficult to keep in tune. Could it be that her thoughts were on Yang Sheng, causing her hands to tremble?" The person sitting above laughed and said, "If one wants to find fault, it is easy to find words. To nitpick like this, would it not make Madam feel embarrassed?" The one dressed in light green sighed, "I only thought Yang Sheng still had a divine quality, and did not hesitate to use a magical pill to transform him into a divine being. There was no other reason, but once a clever but talkative scholar started gossiping, I was thoroughly ridiculed!" A woman sitting to the west, dressed in a blue silk robe, laughed and said, "Sister's relationship with Yang Sheng is still somewhat suspicious; especially amusing is Zhao Shixiong, a young man who, after a drunken nap, had wild fantasies in his dream. If not for the magical bird waking him up, he would have created more confusion." A woman sitting to the east, dressed in a purple silk robe, laughed and said, "Shixiong's dreams were still private, so he did not dare to be openly forward; only this persistent and unscrupulous man, stubbornly displaying a cold and aloof demeanor, insistedently claimed us as his wives, which is not particularly amusing!" The entire hall burst into laughter.

    120

    上坐者曰：“卿等亦不必过于恶谑，我辈伏处山林，亦全赖好事文人品题渲染，聊为林下生色。况神仙眷属，自古尽多，固无足怪，但今夕快聆妙绪，闺阁情形，未免毕露，倘属垣有耳，不又传为谈柄耶？”乃命侍儿出户。即有丽婢三五辈持烛联袂出，瞥见生，惊呼：“有贼！”内问：“何在？”群婢前推后挽，将生拥跽堂下。上坐叱问：“何处狂生？夤夜偷觑人家内梱，罪该万死！复有何说？”生幸近群美，自贺死得其所，因从容自陈邦族，叩称实系误犯，罪知不赦，但求赐死。着淡黄衫者叱曰：“既系秀才，定知守礼，论汝罪在不赦，姑念书生无知，贤妃爱才，汝如能拟庾子山《咏画屏风》诗第一首，称妃旨，我辈当为缓颊。”生不假思索，即次元韵，口占以应曰：“仙境四时春，梅花堪结邻。顾影只自赏，索笑岂无人？绿萼镜中，红英醉后唇。

    Explanation

    120

    The person seated above said, "You need not go too far with your jesting. We who live in seclusion in the mountains and forests also rely on the praise and embellishments of talented writers to add a bit of color to our lives. After all, divine couples have been numerous throughout history, so there is nothing to be surprised about. However, tonight we have just heard such wonderful stories, and the circumstances of the inner chambers have been fully revealed. If there are eavesdroppers, won't this become a topic of conversation?"

    He then ordered the maidservants to open the door. Immediately, several beautiful maidservants holding candles came out in a line. They caught sight of the guest and exclaimed, "There's a thief!" One of them asked, "Where is he?" The maidservants pushed and pulled him, bringing him to his knees in the hall. The person seated above shouted, "Where is this mad scholar who sneaked into someone's private chambers at night? You deserve a thousand deaths!"

    The guest, fortunate to be near the beautiful women, congratulated himself on dying in such a place. He calmly introduced his clan and bowed, admitting that he had indeed made a mistake and that he knew his crime was unforgivable. He only asked for a swift death. The person wearing a light yellow robe scolded, "Since you are a scholar, you must know how to behave. Your crime is unforgivable, but considering your ignorance as a scholar and the noble lady's love for talent, if you can compose a poem in the style of Yu Zishan's 'Ode to Painted Screen,' the first poem, which pleases the lady, we will intercede on your behalf."

    Without hesitation, the guest composed a poem on the spot, reciting:

    "In the fairy realm, spring lasts all year round. The plum blossoms can be neighbors. Looking at one's reflection, one admires oneself. Who would not smile at this? The green calyx in the mirror, the red petals like lips after drinking."

    121

    碧天霜月净，辉映增精神。”群美同声赞其敏捷，且谓子山此诗，即唐人五律之祖，和诗雅近初唐，足以赎罪。上坐者笑曰：“始以君为风狂儿，不谓竟是风雅士，休怪孟浪。”乃命生起，赐坐。生三让，然后就位。上坐者曰：“既遇嘉客，不可无酒。”乃命设三席，独坐生于东席，中西两席，群美环坐。生默数共十有五人。遍添松明，照耀如昼。顷刻水陆杂陈，凡龙肝麟脯、水桃火枣之属毕具。行洒侍儿，靡不佳妙。唯肴酒皆冷，酒入口，寒沁齿牙，而甘芳异常，下咽顿觉肺腑清爽，肢体舒泰。生量素宏，连飞十数觥，众赏其豪。上坐者命各拈经史中“梅”字流觞，禁用唐以后诗词，生屡犯令受罚。又问：“古以梅为氏者谁先？”或曰：“梅宛陵。”或曰：“梅福。”或曰：“梅鋗。”生谓：“殷大夫梅柏谏纣炮烙死，当以此为最先。”众谓：“稗官不足信，应倍罚。

    Explanation

    121

    The clear night sky with frosty moonlight, enhancing the spirit. A group of skilled individuals praised his agility in unison, and said that Zishan's poem was the ancestor of the five-character regulated verse of the Tang dynasty, elegant and close to the early Tang style, sufficient to atone for a sin. The person seated above smiled and said, "Originally, I thought you were a playful fool, but I did not expect you to be a refined and cultured gentleman. Do not blame me for being careless." He then ordered the person to rise and bestowed a seat upon him. The person declined three times before finally taking his place. The person seated above said, "Since we have encountered such an excellent guest, we cannot do without wine." He then ordered the setup of three tables, seating the person alone on the eastern table, with the central and western tables occupied by the group of beauties. The person silently counted fifteen people in total. Pine torches were added everywhere, shining as brightly as day. In an instant, a mix of aquatic and terrestrial delicacies was presented, including dragon liver, unicorn meat, water peaches, and fire dates. The maids serving wine were all excellent. The dishes and wine were all cold; when the wine entered the mouth, the coldness penetrated the teeth, but the sweetness and fragrance were extraordinary. After swallowing, one suddenly felt the lungs and abdomen clear, and the limbs felt relaxed and comfortable. The person's capacity for drinking was usually large, and he continuously drank more than ten cups. Everyone admired his boldness. The person seated above ordered each person to pick up the character "梅" from the classics and histories and pass the wine, prohibiting the use of poems and verses from after the Tang dynasty. The person repeatedly violated the rules and was punished. They then asked, "Who was the earliest to use 'Mei' as a surname in ancient times?" Some said, "Mei Wanling." Others said, "Mei Fu." Still others said, "Mei Xuan." The person said, "Duke Mei Bo of the Yin dynasty, who advised King Zhou and died by the burning rack, should be considered the earliest." The group said, "Unreliable historical sources are not trustworthy and should be punished double."

    122

    ”生争见《路史》，众谓杜撰，因满引一大斗，促立饮，生不得已，一饮而罄，众益豪之，约轮流把盏。接坐一女郎，着淡白绡衫，年可十五六，齿最稚，时已微醺，笑靥双涡，目波剪水，色尤娇艳，最后把盏。生故辞让，久之接盏，阴捎其掌。女郎一笑，盏堕地，砰然有声。四座大哗，谓应罚十爵。生避席曰：“鲰生幸叨宠遇，得预盛筵，不觉饮已逾量。倘再贪杯，必致失仪，敢辞。”众不许。上坐命：“再尽三爵，而后罢席。”生如命饮讫。上坐者谓生曰：“妾自膺宝勅，管领花魁，伏处山林，久与尘世疏隔。不虞君忽涉玉趾，良非偶然。”因指白衫女郎，谓：“是儿与君固有夙缘，今夕良辰，合当遣奉裳衣，君其无辞。”生喜出非望，仓猝莫知所对，唯唯而已。寻命侍儿撤烛，送两人归寝，群美各亦告退。侍儿导生至东院，一月洞门，门内白石嵌地，其平如掌。

    Explanation

    122

    The protagonist struggled to see the Lu Shi (History of the Path), but the group dismissed it as fabricated. As a result, they filled a large cup and urged him to drink it quickly. Reluctantly, he drank it all at once, and the group became even more enthusiastic, agreeing to take turns drinking. A young woman, dressed in a light white silk robe, sat next to him. She was about fifteen or sixteen years old, the youngest in the group, and was already slightly tipsy. With a smile that revealed dimples and eyes like water cut by scissors, she was especially charming and beautiful. She took the last turn to drink. The protagonist politely refused, but after a while, he accepted the cup and secretly touched her hand. She smiled, and the cup fell to the ground with a loud noise. The entire gathering erupted in commotion, saying a penalty of ten cups was due. The protagonist stood up and said, "A humble scholar like me is fortunate to receive such favor, to be invited to this grand banquet, but I did not realize I had already drunk more than my share. If I drink more, I will surely lose decorum, and I dare not." The group would not allow it. The person in the highest position ordered, "Drink three more cups, and then end the banquet." The protagonist complied and finished the drinks. The person in the highest position said to him, "I have received a divine command to oversee the flower queens and have lived in seclusion in the mountains, long separated from the mundane world. I did not expect you to suddenly arrive, and this certainly is not by chance." She pointed to the young woman in the white silk robe and said, "This girl has an ancient affinity with you. This auspicious evening, she should be given to you as a gift, and you should not refuse." The protagonist was overjoyed beyond expectation, in a hurry and not knowing what to say, just nodding and agreeing. Soon, the maids were ordered to extinguish the candles, and the two were sent back to bed. The other beauties also left. The maid led the protagonist to the eastern courtyard, where a moon-shaped archway stood. Inside the gate, the ground was paved with white stone, smooth as a palm.

    123

    历阶而升，循廊左转，有屋十余，椽楣上榜“暖香精舍”四字，复室曲房，连犿窱窌。室内图画满架，鼎彝罗列，绝无俗玩。而青庐中，床奁衾枕，壹皆新制，一似咄嗟猝办者。几上烧桦烛如臂，壁悬吴道子所画《嫦娥窃药图》，两傍悬杨少师行楷一联，云：“绿水鸳鸯芙蓉池沼，青春鹦鹉杨柳楼台。”旁设杨妃榻，有围屏十二幅，前十幅系边鸾所画梅花，末两幅系花蕊夫人楷书宋广平《梅花赋》。时虽严冬，满室盎然有春气。侍儿拽扉既去，生叩女郎姓名，自言：“妃雪林氏，小字皑蕾。”问：“上坐美人为谁？”答：“为江妃采苹；弹箜篌者，乃神仙尉之夫人，即严陵外姑也；倚笛者萼绿华；拍板者寿阳公主。”其他一一称述。生听村鸡已鸣，因趣就寝，相将入帏，备极嬿婉。觉一种奇香出自女郎肌肤，汗气微带酒醺，异常扑鼻，因问：“顷所饮酒是何曲糵，如此甘芳？”妃雪笑曰：“君真俗物。

    Explanation

    123

    Ascending the steps and turning left along the corridor, there are more than ten rooms, with a signboard above the beams and rafters displaying the four characters "Warm Fragrance Hermitage." The rooms are interconnected and winding, forming a labyrinthine layout. Inside, the room is filled with paintings, ritual vessels are arranged, and there is not a single vulgar item. In the green tent, the bed, chest, quilt, and pillow are all newly made, as if hastily prepared. On the table, a birch candle as thick as an arm burns, and on the wall hangs Wu Daozi's painting of "Chang'e Stealing the Elixir." On both sides hang a couplet in clerical script by Yang Shao, which reads: "Green water, mandarin ducks, lotus ponds; youthful parrots, willow towers." Beside it is a couch for Yang Fei, with a screen of twelve panels; the first ten are paintings of plum blossoms by Bian Luan, and the last two are calligraphic copies of Song Guangping's "Plum Blossom Ode" by Huayi. Although it is mid-winter, the entire room is filled with the warmth of spring. The maidservant pulled the door shut, and the man asked the young lady's name, who replied: "I am Lin Feixue, also known as Ailei." He asked, "Who is the beautiful woman seated above?" She replied, "She is Jiang Fei Cai Ping; the one playing the konghou is the wife of the Immortal Wei, who is also the aunt of Yan Ling; the one leaning on the flute is E Luhua; and the one clapping the clappers is the Princess Shouyang." She went on to describe each of them in detail. The man heard the village rooster crow, and he urged her to go to bed. They entered the curtain together, and their union was filled with utmost tenderness. He noticed a peculiar fragrance emanating from the young lady's skin, and her sweat carried a faint scent of wine, which was unusually pleasant. He asked, "What kind of liquor did you drink just now, so sweet and fragrant?" Feixue smiled and said, "You are truly a vulgar person."

    124

    此酒乃采百花之精，以甘露酝酿而成。上者为沆精，次者为瀣髓。顷所饮者，尚是次等；君若饮沆精，更不知颠倒何若也。”生不禁称羡，叹曰：“安得一尝沆精，愿斯足矣！”妃雪笑曰：“君休妄想。沆精惟真仙可饮，俗人饮之，反伤脏腑，为害不浅。”生闻而大惊。固疑妃雪非人，为爱其美，又以其语言和婉，似非祸己者，遂亦不畏。因问：“卿所言江妃诸人，去今千百馀年，何得尚在？”曰：“江妃本黄姑之妹，上帝念其平日无过，且素爱梅，谪满后，乃命司掌梅花。若妾等则各有所司，要皆归其管领。以与君前世有缘，故蒙赐以逑好。”生笑曰：“信如卿言，是亦仙子无疑。小生幸近芗泽，乐而忘死，倘不及时细意领略，恐有日分离，悔之无及。”遂捧其颊而逐逐嗅之。妃雪低笑曰：“狂生啰唣矣。”晨起，问往朝江妃。妃谓生曰：“林妹妙龄慧质，妾所珍爱。

    Explanation

    124
