    This wine is made by collecting the essence of a hundred flowers and fermenting it with sweet dew. The finest is called Hàngjīng, and the next tier is called Xièsuǐ. What you drank before was still of the second grade; if you were to drink Hàngjīng, you would not even know how profoundly it would turn you upside down." The young man could not help but admire and sighed, saying, "How I wish I could taste Hàngjīng—that would be enough for me!" Princess Xue smiled and said, "Do not be foolish. Hàngjīng is meant only for true immortals; if ordinary people drink it, it will instead harm their internal organs and cause great damage." Upon hearing this, the young man was greatly startled. Though he was already suspicious that Princess Xue was not human, he was drawn to her beauty and, further, her gentle and harmonious speech seemed not to intend harm. Thus, he no longer feared. He then asked, "If what you say is true, and the Jiangfei and others you mention have been gone for more than a thousand years, how is it that they still remain?" She replied, "The Jiangfei was originally the younger sister of Huanggu. The Heavenly Emperor, considering her past sinless nature and her long-standing love for plum blossoms, after the completion of her period of exile, appointed her to oversee the plum blossoms. As for I and others like me, we each have our own duties, yet all fall under her jurisdiction. Because of a karmic connection with you from a previous life, I have been granted this union with you." The young man laughed and said, "If what you say is true, then you are indeed a celestial being without doubt. I, a humble scholar, am fortunate to be near such fragrance and beauty, so delighted that I have forgotten death. If I do not take the time now to fully appreciate this moment, one day we may be separated, and I will regret it endlessly." So he lifted her cheek and sniffed it repeatedly. Princess Xue softly laughed and said, "You wild scholar are being quite boisterous!" In the morning, he asked to go to pay homage to the Jiangfei. The princess said to him, "Linmei is young, gifted, and beautiful—she is one I deeply cherish."

    125

    今既遣事君子，可在此小住，俟梅花盛开，当召客为贺。”生顿首谢。生尝阅室内藏书，各帙皆有牙签编志甲乙，书名多目所未睹。内有百函，小篆署“天地心”三字，偶披览之，皆备载古今梅花故实，并历代诗词歌赋，卷末以高青邱诗终。方循玩间，妃雪适至，问：“古人言梅如‘盐梅’‘摽梅’，皆只言实而不言花。以梅花入诗，始自何人？”生曰：“卿忘也耶？前小生所拟庾子山诗，即咏梅花之始。”妃雪笑曰：“卿休矣。子山以前，不已有陆凯、鲍照耶？”生辨曰：“不然，咏梅花要以《葩经》‘山有嘉卉，侯栗侯梅’为始，所谓卉者，即花是也。”妃雪笑曰：“君如遇觞政，又当受罚。如以卉为花，则栗花固未见嘉也。”相与拊掌。生暇，从妃雪游，见屋之四围，纵横数里，尽是梅树，不下数万株。蓓蕾繁密，每低徊其下，盼其速放。瞬近上元，开者渐多，各色缤纷，迷离炫目，直如万顷晴霞。诘旦，传江妃命召客。

    Explanation

    125

    Now that I have sent you to attend to the gentleman, you may stay here for a short while, awaiting the plum blossoms to bloom, at which point I will invite guests to celebrate. The student bowed his head in thanks. He had once read the books stored in the room, each volume cataloged with toothpicks and arranged in order, with many titles being unfamiliar to him. Among them was a collection of a hundred volumes, inscribed in small seal script with the name "Tian Dixin" (Heavenly Heart). Occasionally, he would peruse these volumes, finding them to be comprehensive accounts of the history of plum blossoms, along with poems and songs from various dynasties, with the collection concluding with a poem by Gao Qingqiu. As he was carefully examining the books, Jiang Fei arrived and asked, "The ancients spoke of the plum as 'salt and plum' and 'plum in bloom,' but they only referred to the fruit, not the flower. Who was the first to write about the plum blossom in poetry?" The student replied, "Have you forgotten? The poem I composed in the style of Yu Zishan was the first to praise the plum blossom." Jiang Fei laughed and said, "You are mistaken. Before Yu Zishan, there were already Lu Kai and Bao Zhao who wrote about the plum blossom." The student countered, "That is not correct. The praise of the plum blossom should begin with the Shijing (Book of Songs), where it states, 'The mountain has good plants, the chestnut and the plum.' Here, 'good plants' refers to flowers." Jiang Fei laughed again and said, "If you were to encounter a drinking game, you would be penalized for this. If you consider 'good plants' to mean flowers, then the chestnut flower would not be considered good." They clapped hands together in amusement. In his leisure, the student accompanied Jiang Fei on a walk, seeing that the area around the house extended for several miles, all filled with plum trees, numbering no fewer than tens of thousands. The buds were dense, and he often lingered beneath them, hoping they would bloom quickly. As the Lantern Festival approached, more and more blossoms opened, displaying a colorful array that dazzled the eyes, like a vast expanse of clear sky. The next day, a message was conveyed to invite guests.

    126

    妃雪晨起浓妆，戒生勿出。日晡，喧言客至。妃雪携生登后一小楼，窗皆嵌以五色颇黎。生倚窗遥窥，则见美人数辈，从天而降。有骑龙者，有骑虎者，有骑鸾凤鹓鹤者，所乘奇禽异兽，类多不识。末一人，骑五色蝴蝶，翅如车轮，栩栩可爱，其衣裳钗舄，迥与世别。妃雪密告生以群仙之名：骑龙者，上元夫人；骑虎者，吴采鸾；骑蝶者，罗浮君；其余董双成、范成君、许飞琼、纪离容、李庆孙、郭密香、段安香、宛凌华、石公子、王子登、杜兰香、麻姑、毛女、嫦娥、织女、女几、弄玉、碧霞君、云和夫人等，不可胜记。江妃率众相迎，上元夫人问：“林婢何匿不出见客？岂贪恋新郎，寸刻不舍耶？”生闻之，急推妃雪出。罗浮君见之，携手先言曰：“林妹出落风流，天然可爱，腹中已有俗种，犹腼腆乔妆处子何为？”妃雪面发赤，一一稽首问讯。

    Explanation

    126

    Fēixuě woke up early and put on heavy makeup, warning her husband not to leave. In the late afternoon, she loudly announced that guests had arrived. Fēixuě took her husband to a small upper floor, where the windows were inlaid with multi-colored glass. The husband leaned against the window and looked far away, seeing many beautiful women descending from the sky. Some were riding dragons, others were riding tigers, and some were riding phoenixes, pheasants, and cranes. The strange birds and beasts they rode were mostly unknown. The last one, riding a five-colored butterfly, had wings like wheels, vivid and lovely. Their clothes, hairpins, and shoes were completely different from the world.

    Fēixuě secretly told her husband the names of the immortals: those riding dragons were Lady Shangyuan, those riding tigers were Wu Cailuan, and those riding butterflies were Lord Luofu. The rest included Dong Shuangcheng, Fan Chengjun, Xu Feiqiong, Ji Lirong, Li Qingsun, Guo Mìxiāng, Duan Anxiang, Wan Linghua, Shi Gongzi, Wang Zideng, Dulanxiang, Machu, Mao Nü, Chang'e, Zhinv, Nüji, Nongyu, Bixia Jun, Yunhe Fu Ren, etc., too many to count. Lady Jiang led the group to greet. Lady Shangyuan asked, "Why does Lin婢 hide and not come out to see the guests? How can she be so fond of the new husband that she doesn't leave for a moment?" The husband heard it and quickly pushed Fēixuě out. Lord Luofu saw it and held hands with her, speaking first and saying, "Lin妹 has become graceful and charming, naturally lovely, but already has a common seed in her belly. Still pretending to be a maiden, what for?" Fēixuě's face turned red, one by one, she bowed deeply and greeted.

    127

    上元夫人谓：“今夕元夜，我辈当趁良宵嘉会，为林婢添妆上鬟。佥应曰：“诺。”江妃肃客升堂，肆筵设席，八音迭奏，主客尽欢。薄暮，江妃命树间悉悬灯烛，作卜夜之游。俄而，皓月渐升，群仙游戏花间。月影灯辉，花光人面，互相照映，愈觉精神。未几，蟾魄西斜，群仙始各兴辞，翩跹花杪，缓缓凌空而去。妃雪招生下楼，生视群仙已为上鬟，较前更增娬媚。妃雪胪列群仙所赐木难、火齐、琅玕、珊瑚等物，皆世罕有。亡何，落英盖地，密叶成阴，生抚时感物，凄然有故乡之思。妃雪已知之，谓：“君欲归乎？”生曰：“诚如卿言。故土可怀，新人难舍，奈何？”妃雪叹曰：“人生悲欢离合，自有定数。如不令秋扇见捐，又何愁破镜不合！正无须琐琐作儿女子态也！”越日，即白江妃，为生祖饯。群美毕集，江妃自倚玉笛，命妃雪歌《梅花落》曲，以送生行。

    Explanation

    127

    The Lady of the First Night said, "Tonight is the first night of the first lunar month; we should take advantage of this auspicious gathering and adorn the lower-ranking maid, Lin Bi, with fine hairpins." All agreed, saying, "Yes." The Lady Jiang invited the guests into the hall, set out a feast, and had various musical instruments play in succession, delighting both host and guests. As dusk approached, the Lady Jiang ordered that lamps and candles be hung throughout the trees, and they began their night of revelry. Soon, the bright moon gradually rose, and the immortals frolicked and played among the flowers. The moonlight, lanterns, flowers, and faces of the immortals all reflected and enhanced each other, creating a vivid and enchanting atmosphere. Not long after, the moon's light waned in the west, and the immortals began to bid farewell, gracefully dancing among the flower tips, slowly ascending into the air. The Lady Feixue called the protagonist down from the upper chambers. He saw that the immortals had adorned themselves with fine hairpins, and they appeared even more charming than before. The Lady Feixue listed the rare treasures given by the immortals, including wood pearls, fire opals, lapis lazuli, and corals, all of which were rarely seen in the world. Soon, fallen blossoms covered the ground, and the dense leaves formed a canopy, and the protagonist felt a sense of nostalgia for his homeland. The Lady Feixue, sensing his thoughts, said, "Do you wish to return?" The protagonist replied, "Indeed, as you say. The homeland can be missed, but it is hard to part from new acquaintances. What can be done?" The Lady Feixue sighed and said, "The joys and sorrows of life, the partings and reunions, are all predestined. If we do not let the autumn fan be discarded, why should we worry about the broken mirror not being reunited? There is no need to act like a child or a woman!" The next day, she informed the Lady Jiang, and they prepared a farewell banquet for the protagonist. A gathering of beauties assembled, and the Lady Jiang played a jade flute herself, instructing the Lady Feixue to sing the Plum Blossom Falls melody to send the protagonist on his way.

    128

    妃雪低鬟歛容，曼音歌曰：“昨日梅花开，今日梅花落。明知花落时，何不早行乐？乐乐乐，送君懒劝白玉杓。”初阕甫毕，举座相视，皆有离别可怜之色。又命再歌后阕，妃雪以绡帕拭目，裣衽再歌曰：“今日梅花落，后日梅花开。花开厌孤赏，盼君早归来。来来来，待君满引黄金杯。”歌毕，众皆称妙。谓：“后会有期，此歌定征佳谶，足以破涕为笑。”生起作别。江妃赐明珠四双，南昌夫人以次各有所馈。妃雪又取前群仙所赐，并自脱金钗珠珥等物，以锦帕裹好，纳生怀内。招玄鹤一只，与生并坐，自送生行。嘱：“闭目勿视。”但闻鹤起空际，耳畔风声习习，约一炊许，妃雪呼曰：“止。”生启目视之，人鹤俱杳，身立郊外，距家门不过数武。急趋至家，妻见之，悲喜交集。先是，生夜出，逾日不归，馆主人疑其归家；既而，妻使人招生，始共诧异。

    Explanation

    128

    Lady Xue lowered her head and restrained her expression, singing softly: "Yesterday the plum blossoms bloomed, today they fall. Knowing the time of the flower's fall, why not enjoy life early? Joy, joy, joy, I lazily offer you the white jade ladle." As soon as the first verse ended, everyone present looked at each other, all showing a pitiful expression of parting. Lady Xue was asked to sing the second verse again, and she wiped her eyes with a silk handkerchief, then sang again: "Today the plum blossoms fall, tomorrow they will bloom again. Hating to admire the flowers alone, I hope you return soon. Come, come, come, wait for you to fill the golden cup." After the song ended, everyone praised it, saying, "There will be a reunion, this song surely foretells good omens, enough to turn tears into smiles." The protagonist stood up to bid farewell. Lady Xue gave him four pairs of precious pearls. The wife of the Nanchang Lady also gave him something. Lady Xue then took the gifts from the immortals and added her own gold hairpins and pearl earrings, wrapping them in a silk handkerchief and placing them in the protagonist's pocket. She summoned a black crane and sat with the protagonist, accompanying him on his journey. She instructed, "Close your eyes and do not look." The protagonist heard the crane take off into the air, with a gentle breeze in his ears, and after a short while, Lady Xue called out, "Stop." The protagonist opened his eyes and saw that both the person and the crane were gone, finding himself standing outside the outskirts, not far from his home. He hurried home, and his wife, upon seeing him, was filled with a mixture of sadness and joy. Earlier, when the protagonist left at night and did not return the next day, the innkeeper suspected he had gone home. Later, when the protagonist's wife sent someone to look for him, they were all surprised.

    129

    妻钟氏美而贤，检生衣物俱在，又以生好为狭斜游，疑有所昵，姑置之。至是，生具述所遇，屈指流连将两月。共猜遇仙，因绣江妃及群仙像祀之。生乃出所赆各物，拣鬻数事，已得资巨万，营田宅，蓄仆婢，居然大家。惟念妃雪不置，托故仍如旧馆，潜访其处，青山白云，茫无所有，惟老梅万本，接叶交柯；无数野鸟，回翔嘲于其间而已。叹息零涕而返。越岁，生方家居，忽有道士款关来访，自称苋陆山人。怀中绷一婴儿，解以授生，附书一缄。生折读之，云：“自阻光尘，瞚轮寒燠。计故人之无恙，思君子兮弗谖。非无缩地之方，尅期可至；惜少回天之力，夺命为难。诚以聚散靡常，悲欢难一，迟速固有定数，毫忽不可强求。果其白首有心，彼此静以待之，未必无合并时也。兹以正月初吉，一索得男，敬浼上仙，寄还嗣体。是儿福相，远过乃父。悬知夫人贤淑，腓字覆翼，实嘉赖之。

    Explanation

    129

    My wife, surnamed Zhong, is beautiful and virtuous. All of my clothes and belongings were still there, and because I enjoy visiting brothels, she suspected that I had someone I favored and thus ignored me. Now, I have described my encounter in detail, and we have spent two months lingering in each other's thoughts. We both suspect that we have encountered a fairy, so we worship an image of the Fairy of the Xiu River and other fairies. I then took out the gifts I had received and sold several items, which brought us a fortune of millions. We bought land and a house, hired servants and maids, and became a wealthy household. However, I could not forget the snow-white fairy, so I went to the old garden again, pretending to visit, to find her. But the green mountains and white clouds were vast and empty, with only thousands of wild plum trees, their leaves intertwined and branches overlapping, and countless wild birds flying and calling among them. I sighed and returned, tears streaming down my face.

    A year later, while I was at home, a Taoist priest knocked on the door to visit, claiming to be from the Xianlu Mountain. He carried a baby wrapped in a cloth and handed it to me, along with a letter. I unfolded and read the letter, which said:

    "Since we were separated, time has passed like the changing seasons. I calculated that my old friend is still well, and I have not forgotten the gentleman. Although I have the means to shorten distances and can come at any time, I lack the power to change fate and bring back the dead. It is true that meetings and partings are not constant, and joys and sorrows are hard to reconcile. The speed of events is determined by fate, and it is impossible to force them. If you still have a heart until old age, let us wait calmly, and there may still be a time when we reunite. On the first day of the first lunar month, I gave birth to a son. I respectfully entrust him to the high-ranking deity to return to you. This child has a fortunate appearance, surpassing even his father. I know well that your wife is virtuous, and she will protect and nurture him, benefiting from his presence."

    130

    嗟乎！碧云千里，皎日一心，倚竹有怀，飞蓬莫沐。谁能遣此，花浓蝶聚之天；无可奈何，月落乌啼之夜。伏惟保护动履，歛摄闲情。倘蒙念旧殷拳，则玩儿股掌，见子即如见其母可也。林氏妃雪笺上。并颂夫人奁祉。”生阅书大恸。款留道士，自抱子入内付妻。妻方苦不育，得子大喜，名曰毓仙。生出谢道士，并求偕访妃雪所在，道士不肯，坚求不已。生阶前故有红梅一株，道士袖出一玉杯授生，嘱日挹杯水浇之，俟红梅变白，自可与意中人相见。生再拜受杯，酬以黄金，不受而去。生果如言，日浇杯水，祝其速变。至七八年，红色渐杀；十年，花开全成白色，粉搓玉琢，一片晶莹。生顾而狂喜，时对花踯躅，以盼好音。一夜，明月方中，独立花阴，正有所思，忽有人拊其背曰：“故人别来无恙！良夜迢迢，得毋岑寂不？”生惊视之，乃是妃雪。大喜，携手至斋中，备诉相思之苦。

    Explanation

    130

    Alas! With clouds stretching a thousand miles and the sun shining brightly in my heart, leaning on bamboo with longing, the flying thistle is not washed. Who can bear this, in a world where flowers bloom and butterflies gather; what can be done, under the moon setting and crows crying at night? I humbly ask for your protection and guidance in my actions and thoughts, and to restrain my idle feelings. If you remember the past with affection, then handle me as if I were on your palm, seeing my child is as if seeing its mother. Lady Lin Fuxue writes this letter. I also wish you well-being and happiness. Upon reading the letter, the protagonist was deeply grieved. He respectfully asked the Daoist priest to stay and brought the child inside to give it to his wife. His wife, who had long struggled with infertility, was overjoyed to have a child and named it Yu Xian. The protagonist thanked the Daoist priest and asked to visit Lady Lin Fuxue together, but the priest refused, insisting that they would not go. Before the stairs, there was a red plum tree. The Daoist priest took out a jade cup from his sleeve and gave it to the protagonist, instructing him to pour water from the cup onto the red plum tree daily, waiting until the red plum blossoms turned white, at which point he could meet his beloved. The protagonist bowed twice and accepted the cup, offering gold as a reward, but the priest declined and left. The protagonist followed the instructions, pouring water and wishing for a quick transformation. After seven or eight years, the red color gradually faded; after ten years, the flowers turned completely white, like finely crafted jade, pure and brilliant. The protagonist looked at them in ecstasy, pacing in front of the flowers, waiting for good news. One night, the moon was at its highest point in the sky, and he stood alone in the shadow of the flowers, lost in thought. Suddenly, someone touched his back and said, "Old friend, have you been well? The night is long and quiet, are you not lonely?" The protagonist was startled and turned around, and it was Lady Lin Fuxue. Overjoyed, he took her hand and led her to the study, where they recounted their mutual longing and suffering.

    131

    妃雪笑曰：“君不言，妾已知之。江妃感绣像之祀，喜君志诚，又恐始终不能如一，故命道人授玉杯以试之。果蒙用情之专，历久不懈，不似寻常轻薄儿，始命妾来，从此可常聚首矣。”翌旦，生挈妃雪朝妻。妻疑为仙，齿序姊妹。时子已十岁，聪慧绝伦，自塾中唤归拜母。妃雪笑抚其顶曰：“儿有母覆育，忘所自出矣。”妃雪和婉娴静，生妻亦爱好之，不与争夕，而妃雪则每劝生就妻寝。其平居与人无异，惟偶食瓜果，绝不尝烟火物。临下谦而且惠，每遇失物，辄知盗自何人，藏于何处，即使其人自行献出，并戒生勿苛责人，以此奉如神明，敬且畏之。生尝问苋陆山人，知为羊真人权。因问：“真人至今尚与罗夫人相聚不？”曰：“仙人眷属与人世伉俪不同，大抵仙人相交以神不以迹；相接以气不以形。交以神者，千里不啻一室；即或有时相聚，则以气相接，而两情融洽，真极絪缊化醇之乐。

    Explanation

    131

    Fei Xue smiled and said, "You didn't say it, but I already know. The Lady of the River was moved by the worship of the embroidered image, pleased by your sincerity, and yet she feared that it might not remain consistent over time. Therefore, she ordered a Taoist priest to give you a jade cup to test you. Indeed, you have shown exclusive devotion, enduring and unwavering over time, unlike ordinary frivolous men. Only then did she send me, and from now on, we can meet often." The next day, the scholar took Fei Xue to meet his wife. His wife suspected her to be a fairy and treated her as a sister. At that time, their son was ten years old, extremely intelligent and wise, and was called back from school to pay respects to his mother. Fei Xue smiled and patted his head, saying, "The child has been nurtured by his mother and has forgotten his origins." Fei Xue was gentle, courteous, and calm, and the scholar's wife also loved her, not competing for the bed. However, Fei Xue often encouraged the scholar to sleep with his wife. In daily life, she was no different from others, except that she occasionally ate fruits and vegetables but never touched cooked food. She was humble and kind to those below her, and whenever something was lost, she knew who stole it and where it was hidden. She would have the person voluntarily return it and advise the scholar not to be harsh. Because of this, they revered her like a deity, both respecting and fearing her. The scholar once asked the hermit Shan Ren about this and learned that it was the real person, Yang. He then asked, "Does the real person still live with Luo's wife?" The hermit replied, "The relationships of immortals are different from those of ordinary people. Generally, immortals interact based on spirit rather than physical form; they connect through qi rather than physical form. Those who connect spiritually are as close as if they were in the same room, even if they are a thousand miles apart; even when they meet, they connect through qi, and their feelings are harmonious. This is the ultimate joy of the perfect blending of qi."

    132

    不比人世，必琴瑟而后谓静好，床笫而后谓恩爱也。”生闻之，恍然顿悟，因求授神交气接之方。妃雪笑曰：“汝根基浅薄，何遽欲作神仙功课耶？”生问：“神仙功课，当从何作起？”曰：“当从善事作起。凡人能行百善者，可登上寿；能行千善者，可作鬼仙；能行万善者，可作地仙；如能行十万善者，则可身超三界，而为大罗天仙矣。君其勉之。”生极为首肯。自是力行善事。时毓仙十七岁，已入翰林，弱冠典学楚南，奉勅迎养。生方求学道，不乐远行，遂与妃雪留家，惟钟夫人一人前往。妃雪寄金刀二柄，付毓仙藏之，以备不虞。后果遇盗，见空中有金甲神拥护，盗不敢犯；又尝过洞庭，大风掀天，覆舟甚多，金刀忽跃出匣，化二金龙，夹舟泊岸，刀仍自还匣中。毓仙知母仙人，焚香遥拜而珍藏之。生幼好学，著作甚富，垂老孜孜不倦。汇集全稿，将谋付梓。

    Explanation

    132

    Not comparable to the mundane world, one must have the zither and lute to be called peaceful and beautiful, and the bed and bedding to be called affectionate and loving." Upon hearing this, the student suddenly realized and asked to be taught the method of spiritual connection and qi exchange. The fairy Snow laughed and said, "Your foundation is shallow; why do you hastily wish to undertake the practices of the immortals?" The student asked, "What should I start with for the practices of the immortals?" She replied, "Start with good deeds. Any person who can perform a hundred good deeds can achieve a long life; one who can perform a thousand good deeds can become a ghostly immortal; one who can perform ten thousand good deeds can become a terrestrial immortal; and one who can perform ten thousand good deeds can transcend the three realms and become a great Lao Tian immortal. You must strive to do so." The student wholeheartedly agreed. From then on, he diligently practiced good deeds. At the time, Yuexian was seventeen years old, already a member of the Hanlin Academy, and at the age of twenty, he was in charge of education in Chu Nan, having received an imperial command to care for his parents. The student was just seeking the way of learning, unwilling to travel far, so he stayed home with Fairy Snow, leaving only Zhong's wife to go. Fairy Snow left two golden knives, giving them to Yuexian to keep, in case of unforeseen circumstances. Later, they encountered bandits, who saw a golden-armored deity protecting them from the sky and dared not attack; once, while passing through Dongting Lake, a great wind overturned many boats, but the golden knives suddenly leaped out of their sheaths, transforming into two golden dragons that guided the boat to the shore, and the knives returned to their sheaths. Yuexian knew his mother was a divine being, burned incense and bowed from afar, and treasured them. The student was fond of learning from a young age, producing a wealth of writings, and continued to work diligently until his old age. He compiled his complete manuscript and planned to publish it.

    133

    妃雪取而火之，笑谓生曰：“君一生徒务虚名，不知名为造物所最忌。古今享大名者，境多蹇塞；不如藏拙，为子孙造福。”生以毕生心血一旦焚弃，殊甚懊惜，而已无可如何，只合付之一笑。从此专心致志与妃雪讲求玄理。初教以按摩吐纳之术，久之渐能辟榖。年过八十，而貌居然少年。妃雪将七十，望之犹如处子。时毓仙长子鼎，年十八，已入词馆；次子彝，年十七，亦登贤书。毓仙皆遣回事亲，妃雪大喜，自为择妇，皆称嘉耦，逾年各举一子。祖父母出，与孙儿妇齿相若，不知者多以为昆季宛若焉。妃雪笑谓生曰：“古云‘人老成精’，若我辈久慁世间，虽不成精，亦难免人窃议，不如撤手为高。”生亦为然。乃作遗训付二孙，夫妇衣冠端坐，含笑并蜕。毓仙已晋卿贰，京邸闻讣，星夜奔丧。归葬，舁棺轻若无物，人多以为尸解。斋前白梅，自后花开并蒂，家每有喜庆事，结实愈多。子孙至今犹以之占休咎焉。

    Explanation

    133

    Fēixuě took and burned it, smiling at the author and saying, "Throughout your life, you have pursued only empty fame, not knowing that fame is most detested by the Creator. Those who enjoy great fame throughout history often face many obstacles; it is better to hide your abilities and benefit your descendants." The author, having burned away his lifetime of effort in an instant, was extremely regretful, but he could do nothing about it, so he could only laugh it off. From then on, he focused entirely on studying profound principles with Fēixuě. Initially, he was taught massage and breathing techniques, and after a long time, he gradually became able to abstain from food. At over eighty years old, his appearance remained that of a young man. Fēixuě, at nearly seventy, looked like a maiden. At that time, Mùxiān's eldest son Dǐng, at eighteen, had already entered the literary academy; his second son Yí, at seventeen, had also been listed among the talented. Mùxiān sent them back to take care of their parents, and Fēixuě was very happy, personally choosing wives for them, all of whom were considered a good match. After a year, each of them had a son. The grandparents, when they came out, were the same age as their grandchildren's wives, and those who didn't know thought they were brothers and sisters. Fēixuě smiled at the author and said, "As the ancients say, 'Old people become ghosts.' If we have long troubled the world, even if we don't become ghosts, we cannot avoid being secretly criticized by others; it is better to withdraw gracefully." The author agreed. They then wrote a will for their two grandsons, dressed in formal attire, and sat side by side, smiling as they departed. Mùxiān had already been promoted to a high official position, and the capital heard the news of their death, rushing to the funeral at night. When they were buried, the coffin was carried as if it weighed nothing, and many people thought it was a case of shiji (leaving the body behind). The white plum tree in front of the hall, from then on, bloomed with twin flowers. Whenever there was a happy or celebratory event in the family, the fruit bore more. The descendants still use it to predict good or bad fortune.

    134

    里乘子曰：神仙未有不多情者。观江妃之试熊生，以其用情之专，乃许永谐逑好，足见神仙眷属不能忘情。必谓七情俱绝，始可入道，吾不信也。至妃雪教生求仙之方，惟在力行善事，然则求仙并非甚难，特患人不肯行善耳。何物熊生，得此奇遇，朱颜不老，含笑同归，来去分明，得勿令刘、阮羡杀耶！

    Explanation

    134

    The author of Li Cheng Zi says: Deities and immortals are not devoid of emotion. Observe the story of the river goddess testing Xiong Sheng; because of his devoted affection, she agreed to a lifelong union, which clearly shows that even divine couples cannot forget their emotions. One might say that one must eliminate all seven emotions to achieve spiritual enlightenment, but I do not believe this. The river goddess taught Xiong Sheng the way to immortality, which lies solely in performing good deeds. Thus, seeking immortality is not particularly difficult; the main obstacle is that people are unwilling to do good. What kind of person is Xiong Sheng, to have such an extraordinary fortune? His eternal youth and beauty, his joyful return, his clear comings and goings, would surely make even Liu An and Ruan Ji envy him!

    135

    夜话
    　　
    　　予客滦州，冬夜与陈果堂参军、葛少莪司马、倪次郊刺史围炉瀹茗，各述先德。果堂言，其祖焕文公讳章，少而读律，每在幕府，办公惟谨。其祖母沈太君，内助行善，御下慈爱，臧获有过，亦不肯以疾言厉色相加。老年奉佛茹素，子孙进甘旨，悉却之，但偶食螺蛳数枚而已。邻有某甲走无常，尝至阴司，见有房屋一所，金碧焜耀，内设龙头几杖，各物陈设精洁，门上横额四字，甲故不识一丁，不知云何。惟见额四旁栖螺蛳数枚，蠕蠕然动，亦不喻其何意。忽一人来，甲试叩之，其人答曰：“此陈十八太娘之第也。君未知耶？”十八者，其祖行；太娘者，其土称也。后为沈太君述之，为之悚然，遂不复食螺。又言，其外大父清苑孙翁，少遭伯奇之厄，卒能以至诚感化。

    Explanation

    135

    Evening Conversation

    I was a guest in Luanzhou during the winter, and on a cold night, I gathered with General Chen Guotang, Marshal Ge Shaoye, and Prefect Ni Cixiao to sit around a fire and brew tea, each recounting the virtues of our ancestors. General Chen said that his ancestor, Huanwen Gong, whose name was Zhang, was young when he studied law and always handled affairs with utmost care while serving in the office. His grandmother, Lady Shen, assisted in doing good deeds from within and governed her subordinates with kindness. Even if servants made mistakes, she would not scold them with harsh words or stern looks. In her old age, she worshiped the Buddha and ate vegetarian food, refusing all delicacies offered by her descendants, only occasionally eating a few snails. There was a man named Jia who served as a messenger in the underworld and once visited the realm of the dead. He saw a house radiating with golden and jade-like brilliance, inside which were placed dragon-headed chairs and staffs, and all objects were neatly arranged. On the door hung a four-character horizontal inscription, which Jia did not know how to read. He only saw several snails nestled on the sides of the inscription, moving gently, and could not understand their meaning. Suddenly, a person came, and Jia asked him, "Is this the residence of Lady Chen the Eighteenth? Do you not know?" The Eighteenth refers to his ancestor's position in the family line, and "Lady" is a local honorific for a woman of high status. After Lady Shen heard this story, she was startled and decided never to eat snails again. He also mentioned that his maternal grandfather, Sun翁from Qingyuan, suffered a misfortune similar to that of Boqi when he was young but eventually moved by his sincere feelings.

    136

    后出而读律，每阅爰书，细意平反。尝夜祷告鬼神，倘有冤屈，乞于梦中相示。娶叶孺人，相助行善，惟恐不及，夫妻矜惜贫苦，老而逾笃。至今子孙科第相承弗替，人以为天之报施善人为不爽也。少莪言，其祖母吴太君，好行善事，愈老愈笃。丐者求食必热而给之。或叩其故，曰：“残羹冷炙，最易伤人，况若曹饥饱不时，肠胃疲败，若滞以冷物，必速其病，是非行善，适所以害之也。”闻者称叹。是可为给食丐人之法。次郊言，其本生祖母某太夫人，性慈善，平生矜孤恤寡，敬老怜贫，无微不至。冬月，遇穷媪寒冷，辄解身所着棉袄予之，己则恒自忍冻，虽子孙不告之。此所谓阴德耳鸣也。是与予曾祖妣殷太君绝相类。初，曾祖父石村公，由拔贡中顺天南元，大挑知县，改就教职，官高邮州学正，嘉惠士林，州人至今称颂。

    Explanation

    136

    After studying the laws, he would carefully re-examine legal documents, ensuring that every case was thoroughly reviewed. He would pray to the spirits at night, asking that if there were any wronged or unjustly treated, they might be shown to him in dreams. He married Lady Ye, who helped him do good deeds, and they were always afraid they could not do enough. The couple cherished the poor and needy, and their kindness became even more profound as they grew older. Until today, their descendants have continued to achieve academic success, and people believe that heaven's reward for good people is always just.

    As said by Shao E, his grandmother, Lady Wu, enjoyed doing good deeds, and the older she got, the more devoted she became. When beggars asked for food, she always gave it to them hot. Some asked why, and she replied, "Leftover food and cold dishes are most likely to harm people, especially since you eat irregularly. Your stomach and intestines become weak and damaged. If you consume cold food, it will surely hasten your illness. This is not truly doing good; instead, it harms them." Those who heard praised her, and this can be a method for feeding beggars.

    As said by Ci Xiao, his biological grandmother, Lady So-and-So, had a kind and charitable nature. Throughout her life, she pitied orphans and widows, respected the elderly, and pitied the poor, attending to every detail. In winter, when she encountered poor old women feeling cold, she would take off her own cotton jacket and give it to them, while she herself would always endure the cold, even though her descendants did not know. This is what is called the reward of hidden virtue, and it is very similar to what my great-grandmother, Lady Yin, did.

    Initially, my great-grandfather, Stone Village Gong, passed the imperial examination as a selected scholar and became the southern winner in the metropolitan examination. He was appointed as a county magistrate through a special selection process and later changed to a teaching position, serving as the head of the school in Gaoyou Prefecture. He benefited the scholarly community, and the people of the prefecture still praise him.

    137

    考终任所，宦橐萧条。时先王父问凫公尚未成童，曾祖妣殷太君扶榇携孤归里，亲自课读，日仅薄飥充饥而已。以家道零落，井臼躬操，并于荒山自锄地开垦，荷篠负锸，不惮劳苦。性极慈善，遇贫苦必设法赒济，虽忍饥耐冻而不辞也。既先王父捷秋闱，报至，适殷太君在圃种莱，就问居止。太君笑而指示之。或谓：“此即太夫人也。”报人相顾诧叹。自是日用差给。太君冬月皮衣，恒质以济人，先王父为之赎取，一至春暖，又付典库矣。平居训先王父居官为人之道，壹以廉正慈爱为本，故先王父官楚南，历宰安仁、安福、会同等县，不名一钱，见背后几不能归榇。同时邑中多显宦，归时类获重资，不几何而后嗣凌夷，华屋为墟，良田易主矣。而吾家寒素固如故也。

    Explanation

    137

    A Dignified End and a Life of Frugality

    At the end of his term in office, the official's belongings were meager and sparse. At that time, my father, who was still a child, was cared for by his great-grandmother, Lady Yin, who carried him back to their hometown after the death of his father. She personally taught him to read and write, and their daily meals consisted of nothing more than a thin porridge. Due to the decline of the family fortune, she took on all household chores herself, including drawing water from the well and grinding grain. She also cleared and cultivated land on a barren hill, carrying a bamboo basket and a spade, without fearing hard work. Her nature was extremely charitable, and she would always find ways to help those in need, even if it meant enduring hunger and cold herself. When my father passed the provincial imperial examinations and the news reached home, Lady Yin was tending her garden. He asked her about their living situation. She smiled and pointed out their home. Someone remarked, "This is the residence of the respected elder." Those who heard this looked at each other in surprise and admiration. From then on, their daily expenses were somewhat sufficient. Lady Yin would often pawn her fur coat in winter to help others, and my father would redeem it for her. As soon as spring came, she would pawn it again. In her daily life, she taught my father the principles of integrity, fairness, and love in his conduct as an official. Therefore, when my father served in the southern part of Chu, he governed several counties, including Anren, Anfu, and Huai, but he possessed no wealth. His belongings were so few that they could barely fill a coffin. At the same time, many prominent officials in the area returned home with substantial wealth, but not long after, their descendants declined, their grand houses became ruins, and their good lands changed hands. Yet, our family remained poor and simple as always.

    138

    殷太君殁，先祖妣程太君，踵其遗法，觏若画一。至本生先妣史太恭人，生性慈和，最喜隐恶扬善。平居自奉俭约，家大人好客，客至，烹饪必躬亲之，脍炙丰腆，不少吝啬。村妪为先妣执炊，间窃米盐等物，或以告先妣，先妣置若罔闻。问之，则曰：“此细事，我所失有限，若曹得少沾润，不无小补。一经道破，纵不致变生事外，倘他人闻之，相戒勿纳，是绝其给役之路，彼将何以自存耶！”乡党贤之，至今犹称道弗衰。昨予归京师，家大人训之云：“族中有扶鸾者，先妣与先伯母陈太君降坛，谓同膺上帝勅封，先妣为东隅老人，先伯母为西隅老人，同在北海镇守海怪，三年无过，例当迁擢。”今三年矣，又未知量移何若也。惟予小子不肖，因循苟且，不自奋励，少遂显扬，增光泉壤，以慰在天之灵，清夜自思，能勿愧乎！能勿勉乎！

    Explanation

    138

    Lady Yin passed away, and my grandmother, Lady Cheng, followed her legacy as if it were a unified standard. When it came to my late mother, Lady Shi, she was by nature kind and gentle, most pleased to conceal evil and promote good. In daily life, she was frugal in her own upkeep. My father loved to entertain guests, and when guests came, she personally prepared the food, serving delicate and rich dishes without any stinginess. Village women served as cooks for my late mother, and occasionally stole rice, salt, and other items. Someone reported this to my late mother, but she pretended not to hear about it. When asked, she said, "This is a minor matter; what I lose is limited. You all get a little benefit, which is not insignificant. Once revealed, even if it doesn't lead to external troubles, if others hear about it, they will warn each other not to accept, cutting off their opportunity to work for us. How will they survive?" The villagers respected her, and even now, they still praise her.

    Last time I returned to the capital, my father instructed me, saying, "There is a family member who practices fúluán (a form of spirit communication). My late mother and my late aunt Chen descended the altar, and it was said that they received an imperial decree from heaven. My late mother was the Eastern Elder, and my late aunt was the Western Elder, and they together guarded the sea monsters in the North Sea. For three years without fault, they should be promoted." Now it has been three years, and I do not know what further promotion they will receive. Only I, unworthy as I am, have procrastinated and made do, not striving and encouraging myself, and have not achieved some fame to add luster to the afterlife, to comfort their spirits in heaven. When I reflect at night, can I not feel ashamed? Can I not strive harder?

    139

    吴真人
    　　
    　　吾邑明季有吴某者，性至孝，曾两刲左右臂疗父母疾。家贫，贩鱼为业。父母所欲，必竭力奉之，甘旨无或缺。他日贩鱼归，见道左一兀丐，两足自膝以下、踝以上腐烂无完肤，脓血狼藉，臭不可迩。行路者掩鼻而过，吴独顾而叹息，心窃怜之。兀丐叱曰：“汝胡为者！顾吾足而叹，得勿憎厌我耶！”吴谢曰：“否否。吾叹子之足创甚，而怜其不良于行，恨无术可以疗之，何憎厌之与有！”丐笑曰：“如此甚善。顾吾患此数十年，经多医无少效。佥谓必得一义士跪而舐之，患当已。汝果惠而怜我，能如医者言，姑一试之，可乎？”吴曰：“此惠而不费，果能已子之患，快莫大焉。有何不可？”丐遽伸两足，顾谓吴曰：“来来，请尝试之。

    Explanation

    139

    Wu the True Man

    In the late Ming Dynasty, there was a man named Wu in my hometown. He was extremely filial, and once cut off his left and right arms to extract medicine for his parents' illness. He was poor, making a living by fishing. Whatever his parents desired, he would do his utmost to fulfill it, ensuring they had the best possible nourishment. One day, after returning from selling fish, he saw a beggar by the roadside with his feet from below the knees to above the ankles severely rotten and damaged, covered in pus and blood, and emitting an unbearable odor. Passersby covered their noses and hurried past, but Wu looked at the beggar and sighed, secretly feeling pity for him. The beggar scolded, "What are you doing here! You look at my feet and sigh, are you not disgusted by me?" Wu replied, "No, no. I am sighing because your feet are so severely injured, and I pity you for your inability to walk, wishing there was a way to heal you. How could I possibly be disgusted by you!" The beggar smiled and said, "That is very good. I have suffered from this for decades, and many doctors have had little effect. Everyone says that only a righteous person who kneels down and licks my feet can cure me. If you truly have compassion for me and can do as the doctors say, why not give it a try? Can you do it?" Wu said, "This is a kind act without cost. If it can cure your illness, it will be the greatest joy for me. What is there to object to?" The beggar quickly extended his feet and said to Wu, "Come here, please try it."

    140

    ”吴面无难色，果伏地次第跪舐，但觉芳香扑鼻，不闻其臭。舐讫，痂落盈掬，肤柔润如切肪，光可鉴影，心窃称异。丐叹曰：“汝真义士也！实告汝，我非他，乃拐仙也。夙耳汝好义，今果然，真仙材也！汝能从我游乎？”吴叩首谢白：“仙师不弃，幸甚！徒以有老亲在，事养需人，不敢奉教。”丐赞曰：“汝根器甚深，上帝鉴汝孝思，久已名注仙籍。我姑授以大道，汝归而时习之。至时，再来度汝出世。汝掬中痂，慎勿轻弃，一痂可活万鱼，多者汝自服之，可以却病延年，胜寻常丹药万万也。”吴自舐创后，心地光明，顿增智慧，跪受仙教，入耳即悟。归家试取片痂置鱼篮中，枯鱼无数尽活，味且鲜美。自是，价倍他贩。不数年，居然小阜。乃买田润屋，娶妻生子。

    Explanation

    140

    Wu showed no difficulty on his face, and indeed, he knelt down in sequence to lick, feeling only a pleasant fragrance, without any odor. After licking, scabs fell into a handful, and his skin became soft and smooth like cut fat, shiny enough to reflect his shadow, secretly marveling in his heart. The beggar exclaimed, "You are truly a man of righteousness! Let me tell you, I am not just an ordinary beggar; I am a guiding immortal. I have long heard of your righteousness, and now I see it is true; you are indeed material for immortality! Will you join me on my journey?" Wu knelt and thanked him, saying, "I am fortunate that the immortal teacher does not abandon me! However, I have elderly parents at home who need care, so I dare not follow your teachings." The beggar praised, "Your roots and nature are profound; the Heavenly Deity appreciates your filial thoughts, and your name has long been inscribed in the register of immortals. I will first impart to you the great way. You should return home and practice it regularly. When the time comes, I will come back to guide you to transcend the mortal world. The scabs in your hand, do not lightly discard them. One scab can revive ten thousand fish, and if you take more, you can consume them yourself to prevent illness and extend your life, far surpassing ordinary elixirs. After Wu licked the scabs, his heart became bright and pure, suddenly gaining wisdom. He knelt to receive the teachings from the immortal, understanding them immediately upon hearing. Returning home, he tried placing a piece of scab in a fish basket, and countless dried fish came back to life, their taste fresh and delicious. From then on, the price of his fish doubled compared to others. Within a few years, he unexpectedly became prosperous. He bought land to enrich his home, married, and had children.

    141

    亲殁，殡葬尽礼，庐墓三年。为子授室，即以家事付之，绝不过问。每遇旱涝，迫于乡人之请，祷晴祈雨，辄有验。乡人多受其福，群以“真人”呼之。邑有某公子者，素好客。吴尝出入其门，与之游戏。座上有崔道士，江西人，固天师府法官也。少业儒，嗜酒，父戒之不悛，杖之，怒逃至龙虎山；既为法官，颇得天师宠任。父殁，家人赴闻，怒不奔丧。天师恶而摈逐之。崔挟术游江湖，闻公子名，踵门求见，公子试其术，大喜，待以殊礼。邑城东有河，每春水新涨，潋滟满岸。公子偶以有事过河，欲渡无舟，崔呵片席成舴艋，登之倏达彼岸。归时，又临流觅舟，吴笑曰：“舟不如骑。”戏拾拳石呵之，果成骏马，鞍鞯具备。三人并跨，踏波如履平地。

    Explanation

    141

    After his parents passed away, he conducted their funeral rites according to proper etiquette and lived in a temporary hut near their graves for three years as a sign of filial piety. When he arranged a marriage for his son, he handed over all household affairs to him and never interfered. Every time there was drought or flood, under pressure from the requests of the villagers, he prayed for clear skies or rain, and his prayers always proved effective. The villagers often benefited from his blessings, and they all called him "True Person." In the county town, there was a young master who was naturally fond of entertaining guests. Wu used to come and go at his door, and they would play games together. At one of the gatherings, there was a Daoist priest named Cui from Jiangxi, who was originally a judge at the Celestial Master's court. When he was young, he studied Confucianism but loved drinking, and despite his father's warnings, he did not repent. His father beat him, and he angrily fled to Longhu Mountain. After becoming a judge, he gained the Celestial Master's favor. When his father died, his family reported it, but he was angry and did not go to the funeral. The Celestial Master disliked him and expelled him. Cui traveled the rivers and lakes with his magical skills, and upon hearing of the young master's name, he came to the door to seek an audience. The young master tested his magic and was very pleased, treating him with special courtesy. East of the county town was a river, and every spring, when the water rose, it spread across the banks. Once, the young master had to cross the river for an errand but had no boat. Cui waved a piece of matting, and it turned into a small boat. They climbed into it and suddenly reached the other side. On the way back, they again looked for a boat along the river. Wu laughed and said, "A boat is not as good as a horse." Playfully picking up a stone and waving it, it turned into a fine horse with saddle and bridle ready. The three of them mounted it and walked on the water as if it were solid ground.

    142

    达岸，衣履不濡，视所坐骑，依然拳石也。吾邑春日，儿童争放纸鸢，公子顾而乐之。崔戏摘柳叶吹之，幻一大鹏，盘旋霄汉，吴戏吹系马石，幻成一龙，鳞甲灿烂，夭矫天际。见者皆惊为真龙也。公子有别业在龙眠山，秋日，招吴与崔小酌，请各试其术。崔谓能倒四时，拂袖一挥，果见满山红叶变成桃李。吴笑谓崔曰：“汝颠倒四时，盍观我瞬息千里乎？”以公子尝夸西湖，亦拂袖一挥，果见六桥、三竺诸胜境，历历在目。崔笑谓吴曰：“君能瞬息千里，而谓我不能乎？”时京师某皇亲有花园名甲海内，公子幼曾从父游览，尝称道不置。崔谓：“在此小饮殊闷闷，请一访某皇亲花园何如？”公子白：“甚善。”崔擥公子祛：“试一举足。”果即至其园。

    Explanation

    142

    Reaching the shore, his clothes and shoes remained dry, and when he looked at the horse he was riding, it was still a fist-sized stone. During the spring in our county, children competed to fly kites, and the young lord watched with delight. Cui playfully picked a willow leaf and blew it, creating a large Peng bird that circled the sky, while Wu blew on the stone tied to the horse, transforming it into a dragon with shining scales and armor, gracefully twisting in the sky. Those who saw it were all amazed it was a real dragon. The young lord had a villa in Longmian Mountain, and on a autumn day, he invited Wu and Cui for a small drink, asking each to demonstrate their skills. Cui claimed he could reverse the four seasons, and with a wave of his sleeve, he indeed saw the red leaves on the mountain turn into peach and plum trees. Wu smiled at Cui and said, "You can reverse the four seasons, why don't you see me travel a thousand miles in an instant?" Since the young lord had once praised West Lake, Wu also waved his sleeve and indeed saw the six bridges, three Zhus, and other scenic spots clearly in sight. Cui smiled at Wu and said, "You can travel a thousand miles in an instant, and you say I can't?" At that time, a certain royal relative in the capital had a garden famous throughout the land, and the young lord had once accompanied his father on a tour and often praised it without stopping. Cui said, "Drinking here is quite boring, why don't we visit the garden of the royal relative?" The young lord replied, "Very good." Cui took the young lord's sleeve and said, "Just take a step." Indeed, they arrived at the garden immediately.

    143

    园中景物与曩时所见无异，公子不胜诧愕。吴笑曰：“是何足道。公子倘一游瑶池，更不知倾倒何若也！”公子喜曰：“瑶池亦有术可至乎？”吴曰：“可。”乃袖出布帕，方五尺许，挈公子与崔立其上，叱曰：“起！起！”帕果凌空而升，顷刻身登天界。但见仙山楼阁，金碧耀目，其中琪花瑶草，千态方状。蟠桃多树，时方着花，五色迷离，其大如盏，芳气沁骨，一空凡艳。既至一亭，上榜“俯视一切”四字，试侧身下视，五岳如粒，沧海盈勺，眼界陡扩，胸襟洞开。亭中几榻，皆设锦裀绣褥，吴拉公子少坐，觉柔软异常，竟体爽适。

    Explanation

    143

    The scenery in the garden was no different from what had been seen before, and the young master could not help being amazed and surprised. Wu smiled and said, "What is there to be surprised about? If the young master were to visit the Jade Pool, he would not know how to be overwhelmed!" The young master was delighted and asked, "Is there a way to reach the Jade Pool as well?" Wu replied, "Yes." He then pulled out a cloth handkerchief from his sleeve, about five feet square, and placed the young master and Cui upon it. He shouted, "Up! Up!" The handkerchief indeed rose into the air, and in an instant, they reached the heavenly realm. They saw immortal mountains and pavilions, golden and jade-like, dazzling to the eyes. Within were jade-like flowers and precious grass, in countless shapes and forms. There were many peach trees, their blossoms just beginning to open, variegated and bewildering, as large as cups, their fragrant aroma permeating the bones, leaving behind all mundane beauty. They arrived at a pavilion, where a sign read "Look Down Upon Everything." The young master tried to look down, and saw the Five Great Mountains like grains of rice, the vast ocean like a ladle. His field of vision suddenly expanded, and his heart and mind were wide open. In the pavilion were tables and chairs, all adorned with silk mats and embroidered cushions. Wu led the young master to sit down, and he felt the cushions unusually soft, feeling refreshingly comfortable throughout his body.

    144

    吴笑曰：“枯坐究属少趣，臣敢请阿母赏给清宴，派遣仙姬为劝一觞，可乎？”公子笑曰：“不敢请耳，固所愿也。”吴下坐，向内稽首者三，口中喃喃不知云何。少选，仙童数辈，果赍酒肴而至，麟脯龙肝、冰梨火枣之属，罗列满案。吴率公子再拜稽首，遥谢阿母，然后就坐。王母寻命侍儿范成君、婉凌华、许飞琼、董双成四仙姬前来劝觞。各起为礼毕，觥筹交错，酬献甚欢。酒酣，吴避席，拜手前致词曰：“臣等猥叨阿母赐宴，荣幸已属溢分；又蒙命仙姬劝觞，尤为旷典。惟久慕仙姬等雅擅音乐，敢请一奏天籁，俾洗俗耳，传之下界，允称佳话。区区之忱，未审见许否也？”诸仙姬俱笑而首肯。须臾，五灵之石，八琅之璈，湘阴之磬，云和之笙，丝竹铿锵，泠泠盈耳。范成君敛容正襟，倡歌曰：“二曜左行，苍苍右些；左之右之，人增寿些。

    Explanation

    144

    Wu smiled and said, "Sitting idly is truly uninteresting. I dare ask Mother to grant us a refined banquet, and to send celestial maidens to encourage us with a toast. Would that be acceptable?" The young nobleman smiled and replied, "I dare not ask, but it is indeed my wish." Wu sat down, bowed three times towards the inner chamber, and muttered something indistinctly. Shortly after, several celestial children arrived, bearing wine and delicacies such as deer meat, dragon liver, ice pears, and fire dates, which were arranged on the table. Wu and the young nobleman bowed twice in reverence, offered their thanks to Mother from afar, and then took their seats. Soon, the Queen Mother summoned her attendants, the celestial maidens Fan Chengjun, Wan Linghua, Xu Feiqiong, and Dong Shuangcheng, to come and encourage the toast. After they all stood up and performed their formal greetings, the cups and tokens were passed around, and the toasts were exchanged with great joy. When the wine was flowing freely, Wu stood up, bowed his hands, and addressed her, saying, "We are honored to be granted this banquet by Mother, and our gratitude is already overflowing. To be further honored with the celestial maidens encouraging us with a toast is indeed a rare and splendid occasion. We have long admired the celestial maidens' mastery of music and dare to ask for a performance of heavenly sounds to cleanse our vulgar ears, to be passed down to the lower realms, and to be a fine tale. This humble request, I wonder if it is acceptable?" The celestial maidens all smiled and nodded in agreement. Soon, the five-spirit stones, the eight-lacquer flutes, the Xiangyin bells, and the Yunhe flutes were played, with the strings and winds resonating and producing a clear, pure sound that filled the ears. Fan Chengjun composed herself, straightened her robe, and began to sing, "The two luminaries move to the left, while the vast sky moves to the right; moving left and right, one's lifespan increases."

    145

    ”婉凌华整鬟袂，继歌曰：“醯鸡其展翼兮，黍民其交捷兮，载幬其裈衵兮，蚩蚩其一虱兮。”许飞琼嘅然太息而续歌曰：“噫嘻！夏何为而必暄兮？噫嘻！冬何为而必寒兮？噫嘻！四时何为而必代谢兮？噫嘻！造化何为而必拘拘不化兮？”董双成掩口含睇而卒歌曰：“云油油而果车耶？风飘飘而果马耶？真耶？假耶？无可，无不可耶？”歌毕，四座称善。吴率公子等再拜兴辞。仍登帕，凌空冉冉而下。公子拭目而视，不禁诧，去时秋杪，归已暮春，树木杈枒，一瞬绿阴成幄矣。始知天上片刻，人间已逾半载。公子俗念顿空，知吴异人，坚请愿从学道。吴谓公子是富贵中人，执不可。崔以吴技出己右，衔之。尝以足戏蹴吴腹，隐痛下坠。

    Explanation

    145

    Wan Linghua arranged her hair and folded her sleeves, then continued to sing: "The fly spreads its wings, the common folk vie with each other, they wear cloaks and undergarments, foolishly like a single louse." Xu Feiqiong sighed deeply and continued to sing: "Ah! Why must summer be hot? Ah! Why must winter be cold? Ah! Why must the four seasons always change? Ah! Why must the natural order be so rigid and unchanging?" Dong Shuangcheng covered her mouth and looked at her before finishing her song: "Smooth as a chariot, light as a horse? Is it real? Is it false? Is it neither real nor false?" After the song ended, everyone in the audience praised it highly. Wu led the young nobleman and others to bow and take leave. He then ascended the silk cloth, slowly descending through the air. The young nobleman wiped his eyes and looked up in amazement, unable to believe what he saw. When he left, it was the end of autumn, but when he returned, it was already late spring, with trees branching out and a canopy of green shade forming instantly. He realized that a moment in heaven was more than half a year on earth. The young nobleman's worldly desires suddenly vanished, and he knew that Wu was an extraordinary person, so he firmly requested to follow him in learning the Dao. Wu said that the young nobleman was from a wealthy and noble family and thus refused. Cui, feeling that Wu's skills surpassed his own, harbored resentment. He once playfully kicked Wu's abdomen with his foot, causing a hidden pain and a descent.

    146

    吴知崔将以术杀己，闭门内视七日，腹中下一铁砧，重十余斤，恶之。后与崔遇，以掌拍其背曰：“君何恶作剧？然铁砧之惠，不可不报也。”崔心痛甚，知吴报己，急辞公子归家，亦将闭门炼气以祓除之，计过四十九日当无患。匝月后，吴使人探诸其家，崔子遵父命拒不使见。其人绐之曰：“汝何愚也？我知汝父闭门炼气，今已月馀矣，焉有人经月馀勺水不咽，而犹能存活者乎？”其子以为然，试往觇之。甫一推门，见崔趺坐榻上，背有五小虎，口各啣铜钉而力拔之，钉约长五寸许，拔出过半，见生人至，虎遁而钉仍入内。崔厉声长叹，而气遽绝。吴年至八十，腰脚健于少年。一日，有兀丐来访，与话良久。丐去，吴命于为治丧具，命毕，端坐无疾而逝。或谓拐仙来度出世。后舁棺轻若无物，盖尸解云。

    Explanation

    146

    Fú, knowing that Cuī intended to use a method to kill him, closed the door and isolated himself for seven days. During this time, a heavy iron anvil, weighing over ten pounds, descended into his abdomen, filling him with dread. Later, when he encountered Cuī, Fú patted his back and said, "What evil trickery is this? Yet the favor of the anvil cannot go unrepaid." Cuī, in great pain, realized that Fú was seeking retribution, and he hastily bid farewell to the nobleman, returning home to isolate himself and purify his energy. He planned to remain secluded for forty-nine days, believing that he would be free from harm. After a month, Fú sent someone to investigate Cuī's household. Cuī's son, following his father's instructions, refused to allow the visitor to see him. The visitor then said to him, "How foolish are you? I know that your father has been secluding himself to purify his energy. It has already been over a month, and how can anyone survive without even a sip of water for that long?" Cuī's son, convinced by these words, went to check on his father. As soon as he opened the door, he saw Cuī sitting cross-legged on the bed, with five small tigers on his back, each holding a copper nail in its mouth and trying to pull it out. The nails were about five inches long, and they had been pulled out halfway when they noticed a living person entering. The tigers retreated, and the nails re-entered Cuī's body. Cuī let out a long, mournful sigh, and his breath suddenly left him. When Wú reached the age of eighty, he still had the strength and vitality of a young man. One day, a beggar came to visit him, and they spoke for a long time. After the beggar left, Wú ordered preparations for his funeral. Once everything was arranged, he sat upright and passed away peacefully. Some say that a crutch spirit came to guide him to transcendence. After his death, his coffin was carried as if it were weightless, suggesting that he had undergone a spiritual transformation, or shiji.

    147

    里乘子曰：大罗天原无无父之国。吴之孝思早蒙上帝鉴察，名注仙籍，宜矣。若崔者，初逆父教，继不奔丧，欲以术杀吴，卒为吴所杀，是殆亦天假吴手以诛不孝者乎？吴之奇迹甚多，乡妇孺类能道之，予所闻者如此。并闻吴殁期年，有乡人游嵩山，见吴与一道士藉地饮酒，乡人愕然问曰：“久闻君已仙去，顾尚慁迹风尘耶？”吴笑谢曰：“仙去则吾岂敢，好事者为之也。”其人归，急访诸其家，告以所见，始知尸解之说不谬。

    Explanation

    147

    The Author says: The highest heaven, the Great Lao Heaven, originally has no country without a father. Wu's filial piety was early recognized by the Heavenly Deity, and his name was inscribed in the register of immortals, which is fitting. As for Cui, he initially defied his father's teachings, then failed to attend the funeral, and intended to use magic to kill Wu, but was eventually killed by Wu. Perhaps it was also heaven allowing Wu to kill the unrighteous. Wu performed many miracles, and even village women and children can recount them. What I have heard is as such. I also heard that a year after Wu's death, a villager visited Mount Song and saw Wu drinking with a Taoist seated on the ground. The villager was surprised and asked, "I have long heard that you have ascended to heaven, yet you still mingle in the mundane world?" Wu smiled and replied, "How dare I claim to have ascended to heaven; it was just a fabrication by those who enjoy spreading stories." The villager returned home and quickly visited Wu's family, telling them what he had seen. They then understood that the concept of shijie (spiritual transformation) is not false.

    148

    吴解元子
    　　
    　　江浦吴解元家楣。有女年十八，于归有日，忽下体肿痛，卧不能兴。翌晨，自以手扪之，俨然变为伟男也。庸通和尚飞锡沪渎，曾亲见之，时年已及壮，且娶妻生子多年矣。并言其两耳尚有钏孔，两足以纤瘦不能纳履，终年但着吉莫靴，中实以絮，大不过五寸许耳。予闻而异之，谓：“非作大善事，不能致此。”后闻解元家固临江，行人来往，恒苦无渡，乃倡捐于两岸，各制舟楫，创设义渡，永占利涉，万口交颂。时解元齿已垂老，殊苦无子。一旦化雌为雄，足见天报善人为不爽也。

    Explanation

    148

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    In all writing, it is like riding a swift steed — though it may possess a wild and unrestrained spirit, one must control it with bit and bridle, and must not let it run wild, straying from the proper path or indulging in aimless wanderings.

    From ancient times, wise kings and sage emperors still required diligent study; how much more so, then, for common people! This matter is found throughout the classics and histories. I cannot elaborate further, but I will briefly mention a few essential points of recent times in order to enlighten you.

    As for writings, their origin lies in the Five Classics: edicts, memorials, and military proclamations arise from the Shujing (Book of Documents); expository and argumentative writings arise from the Yijing (Book of Changes); songs, odes, and praises arise from the Shijing (Book of Poetry); ritual sacrifices, mourning, and elegies arise from the Liji (Book of Rites); memorials, admonitions, and inscriptions arise from the Chunqiu (Spring and Autumn Annals). Court regulations, military oaths and decrees, the proclamation of benevolence and righteousness, the elucidation of virtue and merit, governing the people and establishing the state—all are indispensable. As for cultivating the mind and spirit, offering gentle admonition in a relaxed manner, and savoring the flavor of such writings, these too are pleasant pursuits. When one has spare energy, then one may study them.

    The Fateful Transformation of Wu Jieyuan's Daughter

    In the household of Wu Jieyuan from Jiangpu, there was a daughter who was eighteen years old. On the day of her wedding, she suddenly experienced swelling and pain in her lower body, unable to rise from her bed. The next morning, she touched herself and found that she had clearly transformed into a great man. The monk Yongtong, flying his staff to Shanghai, had once seen her. At that time, she was already mature, and had been married and had children for many years. He also mentioned that her ears still had marks of bracelets, and her feet were so thin and slender that they could not fit into shoes. She wore only Jimo boots all year long, stuffed with cotton, no more than about five inches in size. I heard this and found it strange, saying: "Unless one does great good deeds, it cannot happen." Later, I learned that Wu Jieyuan's family was indeed by the river, and people traveling back and forth always suffered from the lack of a ferry. So, he initiated donations on both banks, each made boats and rafts, established a free ferry, and permanently enjoyed the benefit of crossing. The people praised him with one voice. At that time, Wu Jieyuan was already almost elderly, very much suffering from having no son. One day, he transformed from female to male, clearly showing that heaven's reward for good people is not wrong.

    149

    潘氏祖
    　　
    　　苏州吴县潘氏，其先累世巨富。虔奉大士，乐善不倦，凡求佽助者，皆能曲如其愿。日设米谷于门，使纪纲司之，以饷丐者，如是者有年矣。一日，晨兴，忽有老妪携筐来乞米，予之升，请益者再，而意犹未足。纪纲以其无厌，诃之，声闻于内。主人出询，知其故，问妪所欲。曰：“欲得谷一石。”诺之，令呼人来担。妪大喜称谢，置筐而去。待至日暮，不见人来，视所留之筐内给谷三升，已粒粒变成珍珠矣。始悟妪为大士化身，试其诚心为善否也。厥后科第蝉联，至文恭公世恩，以大魁为首辅，子孙至今显贵。予南游，闻吴人言之甚详。

    Explanation

    149

    The Ancestor of the Pan Family

    The Pan family of Wuxian, Suzhou, had been wealthy for many generations. They devoutly worshipped the Bodhisattva and took joy in doing good without tiring. They provided assistance to those who requested it, fulfilling their wishes completely. Every day, they placed rice and grain at the door, entrusted the head of the household to manage it, and distributed it to beggars. This practice continued for many years. One day, early in the morning, an old woman came to beg for rice. They gave her a measure, and she asked for more twice, but still seemed unsatisfied. The head of the household, thinking she was greedy, scolded her, and the sound was heard inside the house. The master came out to inquire, learned the reason, and asked the old woman what she wanted. She replied, "I want a stone of grain." The master agreed and ordered someone to bring it. The old woman was greatly pleased and expressed her gratitude, placing her basket and leaving. By evening, no one had come to deliver the grain. When they looked inside the basket, the three measures of grain had turned into pearls. They realized that the old woman was a Bodhisattva in disguise, testing their sincerity in doing good. Thereafter, the family achieved continuous success in the imperial examinations. The family of Wen Gong, who was the top scholar and served as the chief minister, remained prominent. The narrator traveled south and heard the details of this story from the people of Wu.

    150

    里乘卷三
    　　
    　　扎拉芬夫妇
    　　
    　　扎拉芬者，汉军百文敏公之冢嗣也，堕地授官。成童，娶妻某氏，年齿相同，伉俪綦笃。文敏薨后，公子荫袭，秩跻卿贰。十九岁甫生子，贺客阏闾，汤饼溢座。诘旦，公子忽起颒漱，具冠服，向北九拜毕，令左右请太夫人至，延诸上坐，自伏地稽颡曰：“昨夜先公命之矣。儿本上界星官，今既有子，合归旧班，不得久恋人世。但儿不能奉母终天年，且以此呱呱者一块肉累老人教育，儿罪实甚！此子骨相是富贵中人，异日定能代儿尽孝。凡此皆天意，母亦毋庸戚恸伤怀。”又起坐，遍谕侮甬人等：“善事太夫人，共抚孺子，好理家政，我去矣。”言讫，瞑目含笑而逝。

    Explanation

    150

    The Third Volume of Lǐchéng

    The Couple Zhalafen

    Zhalafen was the eldest son of Bai Wénmǐn, a Han military official. He was born with an official position. When he reached adulthood, he married a woman from a family of the same age, and their marriage was extremely devoted. After the death of his father, Zhalafen inherited his father's position and was promoted to a high-ranking official. At the age of nineteen, he had a son, and the celebration was filled with guests and overflowing with noodle dishes. The next day, Zhalafen suddenly got up, washed his face and mouth, put on formal attire, and faced north to perform nine bows. He then ordered his attendants to bring his mother to the upper seat and prostrated himself before her, saying: "Last night, my father commanded me. I was originally a celestial official, and now that I have a son, I should return to my former position and cannot remain long in the human world. But I cannot serve my mother until her natural end, and I burden the elderly with the education of this little one, which is truly my fault! This child's appearance suggests a destiny of wealth and nobility, and in the future, he will surely be able to fulfill my filial duties. All of this is the will of heaven; Mother should not need to grieve deeply." He then stood up and addressed the household: "Treat the grandmother well, take care of the child together, and manage the household affairs well. I am leaving." With these words, he closed his eyes and passed away with a smile.

    151

    初，公子以妻新娩，戒家人勿遽以凶耗告，太夫人痛子爱妇，恐伤厥心，遂如戒秘治丧事。及妻审公子，佥托入直为辞，三问三如之，乃不复问。儿既弥月，妻忽晨兴，命婢具汤沐，浓妆结束，珠冠霞帔，向北九拜毕，令左右请太夫人至，延诸上坐，自伏地裣衽曰：“曩夕，公子命之矣。妾本上界女星，夙与公子有缘，今既有子，合与公子各归旧班，不得久恋人世。但妾不能奉姑终天年，且以此呱呱者一块肉累老人教育，妾罪实甚！此子骨相是富贵中人，异日定能代妾夫妇尽孝。凡此皆天意，姑亦毋庸戚恸伤怀。”又起坐，遍谕侮甬人等：“善事太夫人，共抚孺子，好理家政。我去矣。”言讫，瞑目含笑而逝。

    Explanation

    151

    At first, the lord, because his wife had just given birth, warned the family not to hastily inform them of the bad news. The mother-in-law, grieving for her son and loving her daughter-in-law, feared it would hurt her heart, so she followed the advice and kept the funeral arrangements secret. When the wife realized the truth about her husband, everyone pretended he was on duty. She was asked three times and received the same answer each time, and finally stopped asking. The child was one month old when the wife suddenly got up in the morning, ordered the maid to prepare a bath, put on heavy makeup and dressed up, wore a pearl crown and a brocade robe, and bowed nine times facing north. She then ordered the attendants to bring the mother-in-law, invited her to sit in the upper seat, and kneeling down and clasping her hands, she said, "Last night, the lord commanded me. I am originally a star from the celestial realm, and I have had a connection with the lord for a long time. Now that there is a son, I should return to my previous position with the lord. I cannot stay long in this world. But I cannot serve my mother-in-law until her death, and I leave this crying infant, just a piece of flesh, to be educated by the elderly. My sin is indeed great! This child has the appearance of a person destined for wealth and nobility. In the future, he will certainly be able to fulfill the filial duties of my husband and me. All of this is the will of heaven. You also do not need to grieve and mourn." She then stood up, instructed the family members: "Take good care of the mother-in-law, together raise the child, manage the household well. I am leaving now." After saying this, she closed her eyes with a smile and passed away.

    152

    里乘子曰：观公子夫妇弥留时告母谕众之词，历历明晰，如出一口，此殆苏长公所谓“其生也有自来，其逝也有所为”者欤？降昴骑箕，足征载籍不谬。如公子夫妇者，一得嗣体，人事已尽，含笑偕逝，可告无罪矣。牛女两星之外，天上又添此一双嘉耦，传之千秋，真堪艳羡。

    Explanation

    152

    里乘子曰：观公子夫妇弥留时告母谕众之词，历历明晰，如出一口，此殆苏长公所谓“其生也有自来，其逝也有所为”者欤？降昴骑箕，足征载籍不谬。如公子夫妇者，一得嗣体，人事已尽，含笑偕逝，可告无罪矣。牛女两星之外，天上又添此一双嘉耦，传之千秋，真堪艳羡。

    The Master of the Village said: When the couple, just before their passing, spoke to their mother and the assembled people, their words were clear and distinct, as if spoken by one voice. This is likely what Su Shi, the Great Master, meant by "his life had its origin, and his death had its purpose." Descending from the stars, it is sufficient proof that the records are not mistaken. For a couple like this, once the succession is secured, all human affairs are fulfilled; they depart together with a smile, and can now be told that there is no fault. Beyond the stars Altair and Vega, the heavens have added this pair of virtuous couples, to be passed down through the ages, truly enviable.

    153

    仙露
    　　
    　　高阳某生，旧家子也，少孤。家业素丰，以委托非人，乾没折阅，销耗垂尽。及生长知事，年少负气，奋欲恢扩旧业，遂将存产尽行鬻变，作孤注，得二万金，服贾游吴，计反掌可与陶、猗埒富。时吴下有名姬仙露者，年甫破瓜，艳声隆起。生方丧耦，耳姬名，姑偕友具贽往访，一见目成，两情心许。酒阑，送客留髡，枕席绸缪，各吐衷曲，割臂缔盟，矢以嫁娶。自是，无日不往。未及一年，腰缠渐绌，难厌媪欲，顿遭白眼。生将质衣货马，以供缠头之资。姬微闻之，谓生曰：“君以寻常狭邪视儿耶则已；如以伉俪视儿，则宜早为之计。大抵平康生活，有资即事趋承，虽富有铜山，亦不能填无底之壑。

    Explanation

    153

    Xianlu

    A certain man from Gaoyang, once the scion of a wealthy family, was orphaned at a young age. His family had long been prosperous, but due to entrusting his affairs to the wrong people, he suffered financial loss and ruin, his resources nearly depleted. When he grew up and came of age, he was young and full of pride, determined to restore his family's former wealth. He sold off all his remaining assets in a desperate gamble, acquiring two thousand gold pieces, and set out as a merchant to Wu, confident that he could soon rival the riches of Cao Cao and Yi.

    At that time, there was a famous courtesan in Wu named Xianlu, who had just turned sixteen and was already renowned for her beauty. The man, still grieving the loss of his wife, heard of her name and, accompanied by a friend and bearing gifts, went to visit her. At first sight, they fell in love, and both gave their hearts to each other. After the banquet ended, he sent his friend away and kept Xianlu for himself. They shared intimate moments in bed, each revealing their innermost feelings, even cutting their arms to seal their oath, vowing to marry one another. From then on, he visited her every day.

    Before a year had passed, however, his funds began to dwindle, and he could no longer satisfy her desires. Suddenly, she turned against him with cold indifference. The man resolved to pawn his clothes and sell his horse to raise money for her expenses. Xianlu, learning of this, said to him: "Do you see me merely as a common courtesan? If, however, you see me as your wife, then you should make plans for us early. Generally speaking, the life of a courtesan requires money to secure attention and favor. Even if one were as rich as a mountain of copper, one could not fill an endless abyss."

    154

    日来侦君囊橐告匮，闻将变质衣物，殊非长策，且过此以往，又将何如？”生沉思久之，答曰：“小生所谓得过且过，过此以往，倘计无所之，拼一死以殉知己！”姬闻之凄然曰：“果尔，则君之计亦左矣！君如实心爱妾，彼此不妨熟商。鸨媪所爱者，货财耳。君在此耗费已复不少，况尔我白首之约，方君盛时，媪曾极口许诺，君试申前说，料亦不忍苛求。得间，第往探之，需价多少？君如不足，妾薄有私蓄，当以相助。”商计已定。越日，生果如言询媪，且谓：“嫁娶之约，姆所亲许，天父地母，实所与闻。小生久客当归，理宜不背前盟，聘资若干，合当如命。敢请？”媪冷笑，扬目答曰：“所说诚然。

    Explanation

    154

    I have noticed that your purse is running low, and I hear you are considering selling your clothes, which is not a good strategy. What will you do after this?” The man pondered for a long time and replied, “I mean to muddle through, but if I have no other plan after this, I am willing to die for a true friend!” The woman heard this and said with a sad expression, “If so, then your plan is also misguided! If you truly love me, we should discuss this thoroughly. The madam loves money. You have already spent a lot here, and besides, our lifelong commitment, when you were at your best, she promised emphatically. Try to bring up your previous proposal, and I think she will not be too harsh. Take the opportunity to ask her, how much does she want? If you are short, I have a small amount of private savings, and I will help you.” They agreed on the plan. The next day, the man indeed asked the madam as suggested and said, “The marriage agreement was personally promised by you, and heaven and earth are witnesses to this truth. I have been away for a long time and am about to return home, and it is reasonable for me not to break our previous promise. How much is the dowry, and I should comply as ordered. Please allow me to ask?” The madam laughed coldly and replied with raised eyes, “What you say is indeed true.

    155

    然自妮子入门，老妇教养不易，今欲脱籍，其身价在他人必得千金，念公子平日伉爽，愿减其半。如有五百金，即惟命是听；不则，莫怪老妇不情也！”生唯唯，退以告姬。姬问生：“能得几何？”曰：“变鬻衣物，可得二百金。”姬曰：“若此，易矣。君速去措办，俾事早偕，迟恐生变。”生急惨澹经营，姬为倾筐倒箧，凑合如数，携以献媪。媪初从五百金非生所能猝办，今竟如数相付，碍难反齿，不得已，纳金署券，乃尽褫去姬之衣裙钗饰，仅存衵，逐令随生速去。时方暮春，天气薄寒，姬再三哀求，始给一旧布絮袄，聊以御冷。生携姬至旅舍，检理行装，所存无几，途长资短，莫知所措。逆旅主人怜两人荏弱，慨赠白金一流，甫得成行。

    Explanation

    155

    Since the girl entered the household, it was not easy for the old woman to raise and educate her. Now she wants to be released from servitude, and her value to others would be a thousand gold pieces. Considering the young gentleman's usual straightforwardness, I am willing to reduce it by half. If you have five hundred gold pieces, I will do as you wish; otherwise, do not blame the old woman for being unreasonable. The young man agreed and then informed the girl. The girl asked the young man, "How much can you get?" He replied, "By selling or pawning clothes and other items, you can get two hundred gold pieces." The girl said, "If so, it is easy. You quickly go and arrange it, so that the matter can be settled quickly. Delaying might cause problems." The young man urgently and frugally arranged matters, and the girl emptied her boxes and chests to gather the exact amount. He brought it to offer to the old woman. The old woman initially thought that five hundred gold pieces was too much for the young man to arrange quickly, but now he has paid the exact amount. It is difficult to refuse now. Reluctantly, she accepted the money and signed the contract. She then stripped the girl of all her clothes and jewelry, leaving only her underwear, and ordered her to leave with the young man quickly. It was late spring, and the weather was slightly cold. The girl repeatedly pleaded, and she finally gave an old cotton quilted coat, just to keep warm. The young man took the girl to an inn, packed their belongings, and they had very little left. The journey was long and their funds were short, and they did not know what to do. The innkeeper felt sorry for the two of them because they were weak and generously gave them a stream of white gold. And they were just able to continue their journey.

    156

    半肩行李，生自负荷之，姬披发韬容，徒步相从，日只行十余里。不及旅店，辄寄人檐下，或宿古刹以为常。经秋始至齐境。资斧不给，会天雨缠绵，泥淖难行，姬足趾皲破，血流濡袜，脱以示生，生叹曰：“小生漂泊穷途，孽所自取，固无尤怨！累卿憔悴若此，心实不忍！”姬曰：“是何言也？妾从君出门时，早知必有今日，但人患无志，不患吃苦。君倾产出贾，为欲恢扩前业也。今旧业未恢，产资荡尽，携妾而归，立锥无地，其能免邻里姗诮乎？”生曰：“奈何？”姬曰：“妾意小作贸易，较跋涉安逸，或冀少得什一，亦可聊以解嘲，还见故乡父老。君以为何如？”生曰：“卿言良是，顾何从得资？”姬曰：“容妾图之。”诘旦，笑脱左履，出珠一颗，大如发菽，付生，入市售得三十金。遂税屋三椽，操业来其，作苦半年，食用粗给。

    Explanation

    156

    With a half-loaded shoulder, the man carried his own luggage, while the woman let her hair down and concealed her appearance, traveling on foot beside him. They traveled only ten to twenty miles a day, unable to reach an inn, so they would stay under someone's eaves or at ancient temples as a regular practice. After autumn, they finally arrived in the territory of Qi, but their funds were insufficient. It happened that it rained continuously, and the mud made it difficult to travel. The woman's toes were cracked and bleeding, with blood seeping through her socks. She took off her socks to show him, and the man sighed, saying, "A poor scholar wandering in a desperate situation, the result of my own misdeeds, I have no one to blame! Seeing you so worn out, I really cannot bear it!" The woman replied, "What are you saying? When I left home with you, I knew this would happen. But people should fear the lack of will, not the suffering. You invested your wealth in trade, to expand your previous business. Now the old business was not revived, and the capital was lost. Bringing you back, we have nowhere to stand, how can we avoid the neighbors' ridicule and mockery?" The man said, "What can we do?" The woman said, "I think we should do some small trade, which is more comfortable than this journey. Perhaps we can make a small profit, which can also help us face our hometown elders. What do you think?" The man said, "Your words are indeed right, but where can we get the funds?" The woman said, "Let me think of a way." The next morning, she smiled and took off her left shoe, taking out a pearl as big as a bean, and gave it to him. He sold it in the market for thirty taels of silver. They then rented a three-room house and started a business together, working hard for half a year, their food and expenses were barely covered.

    157

    姬笑问生：“君业此不厌乎？”生曰：“以今视昨，较仆仆奔驰，凄惶无定，相去不啻天渊矣！何厌为？”姬曰：“固然，但所操太狭，必稍扩充而润色之方可。”生曰：“谈何容易！”姬笑脱右履，又出珠一颗，大如前，付生售之，价亦相等。遂兼货米盐醯酱杂物，日有生色，不半年，渐蓄傭媪。生日操会计，夜拥丽人，自以为人生之乐如是已足。姬忽问生曰。“君将沉浮于此以终身耶？抑尚有他志耶？”曰：“小生得陇不敢望蜀矣。”曰：“敢问贾与仕孰优？”曰：“贾不如仕，又何待言？”曰：“若然，君何不弃贾而仕。

    Explanation

    157

    The lady smiled and asked the young man, "Are you not tired of this profession?" The young man replied, "Compared to the past, it is vastly different from constantly rushing around, being anxious and unsettled. The difference is as great as the distance between heaven and earth! What is there to be tired of?" The lady said, "Indeed, but your business scope is too narrow; it must be slightly expanded and refined to be acceptable." The young man said, "How easy is that!" The lady smiled, took off her right shoe, and took out a pearl as large as the previous one, giving it to the young man to sell. The price was also the same. He then began selling rice, salt, vinegar, sauce, and other miscellaneous items. His daily life became more vibrant, and within half a year, he gradually hired a servant. The young man managed his accounts and enjoyed the company of a beautiful woman, believing that this was the full extent of human happiness. The lady suddenly asked, "Will you spend your entire life floating in this, or do you still have other aspirations?" The young man replied, "I have already gained what I wanted and do not dare to aspire for more." The lady asked, "May I ask which is better, trading or serving in office?" The young man said, "Trading is not as good as serving in office; what need is there to say more?" The lady said, "If so, why don't you abandon trading and serve in office?"

    158

    ”曰：“卿傎也耶？明知小生读书未成，何从求仕？”曰：“妾闻司马文园尚不以货郎为耻，岂古之循良吏皆由科目起家耶？君如有仕志，曷不纳资以求之？”生不觉失笑曰：“卿益复呓语，些须薄资，仅敷糊口，那有多资可纳？”姬正色曰：“君果欲仕，妾自能设法。”曰：“将如之何？”曰：“君意欲何仕之求？”曰：“佐贰，可乎？”姬摇首嗤之曰：“何其卑也！”曰：“丞倅、牧令，可乎？”曰：“未也，盍再等而上之？”曰：“意者其为郡守乎？”曰：“如斯差可，万不得已，亦必须如此，庶可稍展妾志！”生问：“何法之设？”姬笑指旧所著布絮袄谓之曰：“妾筹之已熟，此中所藏，不止一郡守之资，其余可留作资斧，无俟他求也。”以两仪刀折之，中缀明珠不下三千颗。坎第鬻之，共得五万余金。遂罢贾，趣生赴部纳资，铨得粤东太守，指日挈眷之任。

    Explanation

    158

    “Are you out of your mind?” the woman asked. “You know I haven’t finished my studies, how can I seek office?” “I’ve heard that Sima Xiangru, the famous writer, didn’t consider being a peddler shameful,” she replied. “Did all ancient virtuous and upright officials start their careers through the imperial examination system? If you have ambitions for office, why don’t you pay a fee to obtain it?” The young man couldn’t help but laugh and said, “You’re even more delusional. My meager savings are just enough to make ends meet, how can I have extra money to pay a fee?” The woman looked serious and said, “If you really want to seek office, I can help you find a way.” “What should I do?” he asked. “What position do you want to seek?” she asked. “A deputy official, can I do that?” he asked. She shook her head and laughed, “How lowly!” “How about a county magistrate or a local official?” he asked. “Not yet,” she said. “Why not try for a higher position?” “Perhaps a prefect of a prefecture?” he asked. “That would be acceptable,” she said. “If absolutely necessary, it must be done this way, it would allow me to somewhat fulfill my aspirations!” He asked, “What method are you suggesting?” The woman smiled and pointed to the old robe she was wearing and said, “I have thought this through carefully. The contents of this robe are not just enough to buy the position of a prefect. The rest can be kept as funds, without needing to seek elsewhere.” She used a two-yin knife to cut it open and revealed at least three thousand pearls. Selling them in batches, she obtained over fifty thousand taels of gold. She then stopped trading, urged the young man to go to the Ministry to pay the fee, and he was appointed as the prefect of Guangdong, ready to take up his post with his family soon.

    159

    时滨海州郡所在患盗，行旅苦之。姬谓：“居官当首先为民除害。”因嘱生檄所部各邑严行缉捕，又出多资，遍募健儿，散布津隘，以备侦访。连获剧寇百数十人，悉置之法，盗踪顿戢，水慑陆栗，远近颂之。姬固粗晓文义，自生之官，凡催科抚字、听讼察吏，事无大小，皆内决于姬，庶务毕理，生惟优游素餐，坐享其成而已。三年政成，受上考，擢浙东观察使，寻迁浙臬。前后不十年，开藩吴下，以千金报逆旅主人。时鸨媪之夫为怨者所讦，株连系狱，媪伪为卖珠者，夤缘入署，求姬为道地，见姬攒珠簇翠，华妆坐七宝床上，容光四照，较前尤为丰艳，丽婢数辈，环侍承命。媪膝行匍匐而入，伏地不敢仰视，叩称：“老婢死罪死罪，当日不识贵人，过于计较，乞赐恕宥。

    Explanation

    159

    At the time, the coastal prefectures and counties were plagued by bandits, causing great hardship for travelers. Ji said, "When holding office, one should first and foremost eliminate harm for the people." She then instructed her husband to issue a decree to all districts under his jurisdiction to strictly carry out arrests, and she spent a lot of resources to widely recruit strong men, dispersing them at strategic points to be prepared for surveillance and investigation. They captured over a hundred major bandits, all of whom were dealt with according to the law. The bandit activities ceased, and both the water and the land were filled with fear and respect. Both near and far praised her. Ji was indeed well-versed in literature and, since her husband took office, all matters such as tax collection, governance, hearing lawsuits, and overseeing officials, regardless of their size, were decided internally by Ji. All affairs were thoroughly handled, while her husband merely enjoyed leisurely meals, benefiting from the fruits of others' labor. After three years of successful governance, he received the highest evaluation and was promoted to the position of Governor of Zhejiang East. Soon after, he was transferred to the position of Governor of Zhejiang. In less than ten years, he established a fiefdom in Wu and rewarded the innkeeper with a thousand gold pieces. At that time, the innkeeper's husband was imprisoned due to association with a complainant. The innkeeper pretended to be a pearl seller and infiltrated the office through connections, seeking Ji to intercede on his behalf. She saw Ji adorned with pearls and emeralds, wearing a splendid makeup and sitting on a bed made of seven precious materials, her beauty radiating in all directions, even more so than before. Several beautiful maids surrounded her, awaiting orders. The innkeeper crawled in on her knees, lay prostrate on the ground, daring not to look up, and knelt, calling herself an old maid and begging for forgiveness: "I did not recognize a noble person at the time, being overly concerned with small matters. I beg for your mercy."

    160

    ”乃泣白所求。姬鲜腆欠伸，微哂曰：“阿母休矣！濒行时幸叨布袄之惠，不致冻填沟壑，念此一点香火情，容与官人谋之，聊以报德，且藉以补身价五百金，免使阿母齿冷也。”媪汗流淫面，稽颡有声。姬麾令：“去。”不日，即出其夫于狱。后生晋豫抚，向用方殷。莅豫甫半载，姬笑谓生曰：“以君之才，何能独当一面？语云：驽马恋栈，久必蹶蹇。不如避位，免讥覆餗。且人贵知足，大丈夫如此亦足矣，何必画蛇添足耶？”生从其言，遂疏乞罢归。姬封夫人，年尚未四十，望之如二十许人。生二子一女，子读书均成进士，一入部曹，一入词馆，富贵甲于一乡。姬劝生力行善事，培养英才，赒恤穷乏，创设义塾、义仓，仿范文正公条定章程，刊碑以垂永久，乡人至今称颂不衰。

    Explanation

    160

    He then wept and voiced his request. The lady, shyly yawning, smiled slightly and said, "Mother, rest assured! At the time of my departure, I fortunately received the kindness of the cotton coat, which would not let me freeze and die in the ditches. Thinking of this small act of kindness, I will allow myself to discuss it with the official, just to repay the kindness, and also to compensate for the value of 500 gold pieces, to prevent Mother from feeling cold in her heart." The old woman sweated profusely and knelt down with a sound. The lady waved her hand and said, "Go!" In no time, her husband was released from prison. Later, the young man became the governor of Henan, and his use of power was just beginning. After half a year in office, the lady smiled and told him, "With your talent, how can you handle everything alone? It is said: a horse that lingers in the stable will surely stumble after a long time. It would be better to step down, to avoid criticism of failing in one's duties. Moreover, a person values contentment; a great man like this is already enough, why add unnecessary details?" The man followed her advice and submitted a petition to resign. The lady was ennobled as a lady, not yet forty but looking like someone in their twenties. He had two sons and a daughter, all of whom became scholars and passed the imperial examinations; one entered the Ministry of Personnel, and the other entered the Hanlin Academy, making them the wealthiest family in the region. The lady encouraged him to do good deeds, cultivate talented people, provide relief to the poor, establish charitable schools and granaries, and follow the regulations set by Fan Zhongyan, engraving them on stone to last forever, and the villagers still praise him today.

    161

    里乘子曰：高阳生一庸夫耳！变产作孤注，以图恢扩旧业，其计已左；二万金挥霍殆尽，脱非遇姬，亦沟中瘠而已；及贸易少安便无他志，然则生一庸夫耳。乃公然由太守而洊晋封圻，庸夫偏有此庸福，伊谁之力哉！仙露作为，事事令人可爱、可法，初属其求仕，继佐其为政，终促其勇退，且劝力行种种善事，乡人称颂，其见地之高，多非须眉所能及，何况巾帼者，何况巾帼而勾栏者！噫嘻！异矣！

    Explanation

    161

    Li Chengzi said: Gao Yang is just an ordinary man!
