After Yuan’s transcendence, a small portrait of him was left behind. When prayers were made to it, they were always answered.

    201

    钱弗要
    　　
    　　钱福耀，池州府人。其为人也好善。知医市药，为人治疾，不肯责谢；遇穷乞者，辄施药以治之。乡里称其贤，因共赠以别名曰“钱弗要”。家本素丰，坐是中落。城外齐山有洞，窈然而深，为一郡名胜。钱尝于岁暮除日避债洞中，薄暮，又一人贸贸然来，诘之，亦同志者，恻然怜之。问其人止需钱十千，钱叹曰：“我较君所需，不啻数十倍，不得不尔！十千细事，何必至此！”腰橐适存会子钱十千，遂取以授之，曰：“此十千，汝可携归偿宿逋，与家人团聚度岁也。”其人惊喜曰，“君得勿钱弗要先生耶？”曰：“然。

    Explanation

    201

    Qian Fuyao, a man from Chizhou Prefecture, was known for his good character and charity. He knew medicine and sold drugs, treating people's illnesses without demanding payment. When he encountered poor beggars, he would give them medicine to treat their ailments. The villagers praised him for his virtue and gave him the nickname "Mr. Doesn't Want Money." Originally from a wealthy family, his fortunes declined.

    Outside the city, there is a cave on Qishan that is deep and secluded, a famous scenic spot in the region. On New Year's Eve, Qian Fuyao would hide in this cave to avoid creditors. Late in the afternoon, another person stumbled into the cave. When Qian Fuyao asked him, he too was seeking refuge from creditors. Moved by compassion, Qian Fuyao asked how much money the other person needed. The other person replied that he only needed ten thousand coins. Qian Fuyao thought, "My debts are many times greater than yours, and I have no choice but to do this." He said, "Ten thousand coins is a small matter, why go to such lengths?" Just then, he found ten thousand coins in his pouch and gave them to the other person. "Take these ten thousand coins and go home to pay off your old debts and spend the New Year with your family," he said. The other person was surprised and asked, "Are you not Mr. Doesn't Want Money?" Qian Fuyao replied, "Yes, I am."

    202

    ”曰：“闻名未曾识面，何敢遽领厚贶！”钱笑推其背曰：“君请速归，无用多言。”其人拜受，称谢而去。昧爽，钱称娖归家，行至山腰，见两叟坐地谈医，一叟苍髯垂胸，一叟丰颐微髭。钱顿触所好，拱手请教，苍髯者笑曰：“汝亦知医耶？”对曰：“然。”曰：“医有三审，汝知之乎？”曰：“何为三审？”曰：“一审色，二审舌，三审脉。凡为人治疾，先审面色，由色而辨五行，或宜相生，或宜相克；次审舌，辨其枯润；次审脉之浮沉迟数，以分表里虚实，而决其风寒湿热。三审既确，然后斟酌立方，百不失一。汝第识吾言，神而明之，能事毕矣。”钱顿首谢教。

    Explanation

    202

    "他说：‘我只闻其名，未曾谋面，怎敢匆忙接受如此厚礼！’钱笑着推了推他的背说：‘你请快回去吧，不必多言。’那人拜谢后，道谢离去。黎明时分，钱带着礼物回家，走到山腰时，看见两位老人坐在地上谈论医术，一位老人长须垂胸，另一位老人面颊丰满，胡须稀疏。钱突然感到兴趣盎然，拱手请教。长须老人笑着说：‘你也懂医术吗？’钱回答说：‘是的。’老人说：‘医术有三审，你知道吗？’钱问：‘什么是三审？’老人解释道：‘一审面色，二审舌象，三审脉象。为人治病时，先看面色，根据面色辨别五行，或需相生，或需相克；其次看舌象，辨别其干燥或湿润；再看脉象的浮沉迟数，以区分表里虚实，从而判断风寒湿热。三审确定后，然后斟酌处方，百无一失。你只要记住我的话，理解并运用它，就能完成所有任务。’钱顿首感谢教诲。"

    203

    请问两叟姓名，微髭者指苍髯者谓曰：“此三国时华元化先生，予乃唐时孙思邈也。”钱大惊，知为遇仙，急蒲伏叩首，及仰视之，已杳矣。自是医理日进。会太守有母暑月患疾，衣重裘尚自恶寒，群医佥以热剂投之，不效。钱除日给票之人适在署司押，因言钱得仙传，夸张而游扬之。太守大喜，急延钱胗治。钱观病者两颧微赤，舌色黑燥，确系实热内伏，攻去内热，外邪自去，急命饱饮瓜汁。少选，泄去恶滞，然熟睡；寤后，汗出如雨，其疾良瘥。太守喜，酬以金，固辞不受，太夫人性固好施，召钱谓曰：“我疾非汝莫治，酬以金，知汝不受；我私蓄有千金，存汝处，施药济人，藉为我种福可也。

    Explanation

    203

    Qian asked the two elderly men their names. The one with a thin mustache pointed to the one with a gray beard and said, "This is Mr. Hua Yuanhua from the Three Kingdoms period, and I am Sun Simiao from the Tang dynasty." Qian was greatly surprised, realizing he had met immortals. He quickly knelt and bowed, and when he looked up, they were already gone. From then on, Qian's medical knowledge progressed daily. One day, the governor's mother fell ill in the hot summer month, feeling cold even while wearing a heavy fur coat. Many doctors administered hot medicine, but it did not work. Qian, who had been relieved of his duty to collect daily tickets, happened to be in the office. He mentioned that Qian had received divine transmission, and the news spread around. The governor was very pleased and quickly invited Qian to diagnose and treat his mother. Qian observed the patient's slightly red cheeks and black, dry tongue, confirming it was due to internal real heat. He prescribed attacking the internal heat, which would naturally expel the external evil. He quickly ordered the patient to drink abundantly of melon juice. After a short while, the patient expelled the bad stagnation, fell asleep deeply, and after waking up, sweat poured out like rain, greatly improving her condition. The governor was pleased and offered gold as a reward, but Qian firmly declined to accept. The governor's wife, who had a nature that loved to give, called Qian and said, "My illness can only be treated by you. I offered gold as a reward, knowing you would not accept. I have privately saved a thousand gold pieces. Store them with you, distribute medicine to help people, and as a way to plant merit for me."

    204

    ”钱乃顿首受命。亡何，太守以有事晋省，时值中秋，与寮寀谒见中丞某公，公忽白晴上眨，昏然倒地，手足挛搐，多医相视，不敢立方。太守因荐钱医道甚神，方伯商之廉访，遂飞櫂延钱至。钱见某公面白微青，舌白而润，意仲秋金气得令，凉风乘虚而入，引动内风；症属虚寒，治宜扶火泄金，培土制木，遂进参芪术桂等药，而疾果顿愈。中丞大喜，遂商之僚属，延钱在省为官医，并筹资巨万，俾钱市药以广济穷人。钱生计日扩，凡求医者无不应手立效，不责人谢，人必量力以酬之。年九十馀，腰脚强健，为人治疾犹步行，不喜乘舆。三子读书，连入邑庠；孙十馀人，皆幼慧。佥谓好善之报云。

    Explanation

    204

    Mr. Qian bowed deeply and accepted the command. Shortly thereafter, the prefecture governor had official business in the capital of Jin province and, coinciding with the Mid-Autumn Festival, accompanied his officials to pay respects to Governor Zhongcheng. Suddenly, Governor Zhongcheng experienced a fainting spell, collapsing to the ground with convulsions in his limbs. Many doctors gathered to examine him, but dared not prescribe a treatment. The prefecture governor then recommended Mr. Qian, whose medical skills were considered extraordinary. The provincial governor consulted with the inspector general, and they swiftly summoned Mr. Qian. Upon examining Governor Zhongcheng, Mr. Qian observed that he had a pale face with a slight bluish tinge and a white, moist tongue. He deduced that the cool autumn air was taking advantage of the void, triggering an internal imbalance characteristic of a condition of deficiency and coldness. The diagnosis was a case of deficiency and cold, and the treatment should involve supporting fire and releasing metal, nourishing earth and controlling wood. Mr. Qian administered a decoction of ginseng, astragalus, atractylodes, and cinnamon, and indeed, the illness was cured instantly. Governor Zhongcheng was greatly pleased and, consulting with his officials, appointed Mr. Qian as an official physician in the province. They also raised a large sum of money to enable Mr. Qian to purchase medicine to widely help the poor. Mr. Qian's reputation and practice grew daily, and every patient he treated saw immediate and effective results. He did not demand gratitude from his patients, but people repaid him according to their means. At over ninety years old, Mr. Qian remained strong and healthy, walking to treat patients even though he did not enjoy riding in a carriage. His three sons studied diligently and successively entered the local schools; he had more than ten grandchildren, all of whom were young and intelligent. It was generally believed that this was the reward for his good deeds.

    205

    徐霞客
    　　
    　　徐霞客，名宏祖，江阴县人。平生有山水癖，曾作《游记》，厚尺许，真奇观也。相传霞客得异人术，日可行千里。不携傔从，自负行囊，囊有机括，日暮猱升树上，悬囊高枝，启囊展衾枕，蜷卧其中，距地数丈，以远猛兽。尝三至昆仑，山灵厌其贪，夜遣大鹏扇翅扑折树枝，堕霞客崖下而损其胫，霞客度无生理，望乡而泣。昧爽，山灵现寿者相，策杖来问所苦，霞客具告之，且谓：“身填沟壑非所惜，奈家有老母何！”寿者责曰：“昆仑为宇宙第一山，多真仙所居，世人欲一至不得，汝乃三至，天恶其贪，故罹此罚。予怜汝孝思，不忍不一援手。”袖出药一丸授之，曰：“咽之当愈。然君归奉母，勿再远游矣！”霞客拜诺，受药吞之，顿愈。既归，泣拜母前，足胫复损，自是杜门作游记，遂不再出。

    Explanation

    205

    Xu Xiake

    　　Xu Xiake, named Hongzu, was from Jiangyin County. He had a lifelong passion for mountains and waters, and once wrote Travel Notes, about a foot thick, which is truly a marvel. It is said that Xu Xiake acquired a strange skill, allowing him to travel a thousand miles in a day. He did not bring attendants, carrying his own luggage. His bag had a mechanical device that allowed him to climb trees like a monkey at dusk. He would hang his bag on a high branch, unpack it to spread out his bedding, and curl up to sleep, at a distance of several meters from the ground to keep away from wild beasts. Once he visited Mount Kunlun three times. The mountain spirits were tired of his greed and, at night, sent a giant bird to flap its wings, breaking the branches. Xu Xiake fell from the cliff and injured his shins. Thinking he would die, he looked toward his hometown and wept. At dawn, the mountain spirits appeared in the form of a long-lived being, came with a staff to ask about his suffering. Xu Xiake fully informed them, saying, "Filling the ditches is not what I regret, but what will happen to my elderly mother!" The spirit chided him, saying, "Mount Kunlun is the first mountain of the universe, where many true immortals reside. People desire to visit it once but cannot, yet you have come three times. Heaven dislikes your greed, so you have suffered this penalty. I pity your filial piety and cannot help but offer a hand." The spirit took out a pill from his sleeve and gave it to him, saying, "Swallow it and you will recover. But when you return, serve your mother, and do not travel far again!" Xu Xiake bowed and agreed, took the pill and swallowed it, and immediately recovered. After returning, he wept and knelt before his mother, whose shins were injured again. From then on, he closed his door and wrote travel notes, and never went out again.

    206

    里乘子曰：予在江阴，闻其里人言之如此。然观霞客《游记》，出游有时亦复需舟车，不尽徒行也。霞客嫁妾生子，从母姓李，名寄，字介生。
    　　
    　　产怪
    　　
    　　同治七年七月，某官需次皖省。其妇分娩，产一物，微具人形，尖嘴鹰鼻，三目，左右额生二角，手足皆鸟爪，堕地一跃高二三尺许。接生媪大惊，急以被蒙其身，猛力按捺；又令人善视产妇，放帐安寝，不使见之，恐惊骇致疾也。然后呼人以索缚此怪，杀而投诸江中。后亦无他异。此媪识见甚高，处分亦甚得法。袁竹畦参军起所目见者。或谓热天妇人不可露宿，马通亵物俱不宜夜置野处，倘妖气侵之，即不免感生怪物，是或一道也。爰笔识之，使妊妇知所自谨焉。

    Explanation

    206

    Lichengzi said: When I was in Jiangyin, I heard the villagers say it like this. However, when I read Xu Xiake's Travel Notes, I found that when traveling, one sometimes also needs boats and carriages, not all is on foot. Xu Xiake's concubine gave birth to a son, who took his mother's surname, Li, and was named Ji, with the courtesy name Jiasheng.

    Birth of a Monster

    In the seventh month of the seventh year of Tongzhi, a certain official was stationed in Anhui. His wife gave birth to something that slightly resembled a human, with a pointed beak, an eagle's nose, three eyes, two horns on the forehead, and bird-like claws on hands and feet. It fell to the ground and jumped two or three feet high. The midwife was greatly startled, quickly covered it with a blanket, and pressed it down with great force. She then instructed someone to take good care of the mother, allowing her to rest peacefully, so as not to frighten her and cause illness. Then she called for someone to tie up the creature with ropes, killed it, and threw it into the river. There were no other strange occurrences afterward. This midwife had very high insight, and her handling was also very appropriate. What the military officer Yuan Zhuiqi saw with his own eyes. Some say that women should not sleep outdoors in hot weather, and horses and other intimate objects should not be left in the wild at night, for fear that evil spirits might invade and inevitably cause the birth of monsters. This might be one reason. I will record this here, to make pregnant women aware of what to be cautious about.

    207

    祝由科
    　　
    　　相传黄帝有二臣，曰岐伯氏，曰祝由氏，皆善医。岐伯氏治疾，按脉能知人七十二经，投以药，无不效。祝由氏治疾不用药，惟以清水一碗，以手捏剑诀，勅勒书符水面，以饮病者，亦无不效。祝由氏为湖南辰州府人，故今辰州人多擅此术，名曰“祝由科”。为人治疾誓不受钱币之谢，或酬以酒食则可耳。然擅此术者虽多，而真得秘传者甚少。如得真传，实有起死回生之功；否则，第伪托其名以欺人，愚夫愚妇受其欺者，往往坐失巨资，悔之无及矣。或曰得真传者每岁元旦祭祷祝由之神，默占以笤，即预知是年当治几人之疾，随缘巧遇，最为灵验。

    Explanation

    207

    The Art of Zhubu

    It is said that Emperor Huangdi had two ministers, one named Qibo and the other named Zhubu, both skilled in medicine. Qibo treated illnesses by feeling the pulse, which allowed him to understand the 72 meridians of a person, and when he administered medicine, it was always effective. Zhubu, on the other hand, did not use drugs to treat illnesses. Instead, he used a ritual involving a bowl of clear water, where he would write a talisman on the water surface with a command, and then have the patient drink it. This method was also always effective. Zhubu was from the region of Chenzhou in Hunan Province, which is why many people in Chenzhou today are skilled in this art, known as the "Art of Zhubu." Practitioners of this art swear not to accept monetary thanks for their services, though they may accept payment in the form of alcohol and food. However, while many people claim to possess this skill, those who truly receive the secret transmission are very few. If one genuinely masters the art, they can indeed bring the dead back to life; otherwise, some merely pretend to have the skill to deceive people, leading foolish men and women to lose large sums of money, often to the point of regretting it too late. Some say that those who truly master the art perform an annual ritual on the first day of the first month, worshiping and praying to the god of Zhubu, and silently predicting the number of patients they will treat in the coming year using a broom. This method is considered the most effective and accurate.

    208

    吾曩过豫州，其土人言，某年汴城节署旗竿斗损坏，匠人以绳轳辘上升其巅为之整理，竿忽为风折，匠人堕为齑粉，佥谓无再生之理。会有辰州术客过此，笑谓：“无患。”乃命人取板四片，以一板上置黄土，将匠人舁置土上，左右夹以二板，又以黄土遍撒其身，以手捏剑诀，勅勒书符，口中喃喃诵咒，毕，复吸清水噀其面，上盖一板，以麻绳束之。七日后，解去麻绳，启板视之，气热如蒸，匠人竟体大汗，欠伸而起，已霍然苏矣。人问及前事，懵然不知，但谓如梦初觉，殊无所苦。噫嘻！其术如此，真神乎技矣！闻节署前曾泐碑详记其事，惜匆匆过此，未及访阅耳！

    Explanation

    208

    During my past visit to Yuzhou, the local people told me that in a certain year, the flagpole at the office of a high-ranking official in Bianjing was damaged. A craftsman used a rope and pulley system to ascend to the top to repair it. Suddenly, the pole was broken by the wind, and the craftsman fell to the ground, reduced to powder. Everyone believed there was no hope of revival. Just then, a skilled practitioner from Chenzhou passed by and smiled, saying, "There is no worry." He then ordered someone to fetch four boards, placed yellow earth on one board, lifted the craftsman onto the earth, and held him between two boards on either side. He sprinkled yellow earth all over the craftsman's body, formed a sword gesture with his hand, commanded and wrote a talisman, muttered a spell, and finished by inhaling clear water and spraying it on the craftsman's face. He covered the craftsman with one board and tied it with hemp rope. After seven days, he untied the hemp rope, opened the board, and looked inside. The air was hot like steam, and the craftsman was sweating all over his body. He yawned and stretched, stood up, and was already completely revived. When people asked him about what had happened before, he had no memory of it, but said it was as if he had just woken from a dream and felt no discomfort at all. Oh, his skill is truly divine! I heard that in front of the official's office, a stone tablet was once carved to detail this event, but regretfully, I passed by too quickly and did not have time to visit and read it.

    209

    褚祚典
    　　
    　　褚祚典，明嘉靖时关中诸生也。性豪诞，好驰马试剑。尤善超距，尝与诸健儿较艺，一跃能摩真定府铜佛顶，诸健儿咋舌讙赞，谢不及。素嗜赌，一掷千金立罄，负辄与诸健儿伏莽探丸，藉猎获以偿赌资。后以从征倭寇得官，洊擢山东按察使。平时所交绿林锦帆之徒，暮夜犹与往来，遇大腹贾挟重资，必邀褚同猎取之，褚亦自鸣得意，乐与从事。一时童谣有云：“君勿行郊薮，陆有拦路虎。君勿行江湖，水有吞舟鱼。”故凡估客舟车所至，莫不惴惴患盗，各有戒心。抚军闻之，以语褚曰：“道路童谣，君闻之乎？”褚唯唯。

    Explanation

    209

    褚祚典

    　　褚祚典，是明朝嘉靖年间关中的一个学者。他性格豪放不羁，喜欢骑马试剑。尤其擅长跳高，曾经与一群强健的少年比试技艺，一跃便能触及真定府的铜佛顶，那些少年们惊叹不已，连道谢都来不及。他一向嗜好赌博，一掷千金便立刻输光，输光后便与那些强健的少年们潜入荒野偷盗，靠打猎所得来偿还赌债。后来他因参与征讨倭寇而获得官职，多次被提拔为山东按察使。平时他与绿林中的人物和海盗来往密切，夜晚仍然与他们保持联系。遇到携带大量钱财的富商，必定会邀请褚祚典一同去抢劫，褚祚典对此也颇为得意，乐于参与。当时流传着一首童谣：“不要去郊野，陆地上有拦路虎。不要去江湖，水里有吞舟鱼。”因此，凡是商贾们乘坐的船只和车辆所到之处，无不提心吊胆，担心遭遇盗贼，各自心存戒备。巡抚听到这些后，对褚祚典说：“这些路途上的童谣，你听说过吗？”褚祚典只是唯唯诺诺地应答。

    210

    抚军正色曰：“今外寇甫平，萑苻未靖，商贾不通，民何聊生？君职司爽鸠，应为民除害！坐视不理，将何以辞其责也！”褚闻之，无任主臣，急檄所属府州县，严行缉盗，如敢姑息宽纵，立予纠劾不贷。各属奉檄畏劾，叠限比捕，卒无所获。时河南有名捕梁科者，年七十馀，家居柴门，久已罢役。群捕畏比，因醵资具厚币，遣使求助于梁。梁初以年老无能为役，固辞；既款使者以酒，酒酣，谈少年时捕盗事，精悍之色见于眉宇，使者拊掌赞叹，故谀词以激励之，谓：“今盗风虽炽，均非公敌。如勉为一行，使若曹知捕班中尚有老廉颇在，当不敢逞。否则若曹不谓公年老，反笑公胆怯，当日之英雄埽地矣！窃为惜之。”梁不觉耳热技痒，掀髯笑曰：“汝激吾耶？不得已，偕汝一行何如？”使者大喜称善。不日，同至山东，群捕接见甚欢，佥谓：“叠瞷盗踪，实藏臬署。”梁谓：“当侦确虚实，休得孟浪。”乃日伏臬垣左右以伺之。

    Explanation

    210

    The military governor spoke sternly: "The external enemies have just been pacified, but the bandits in the reed marshes have not yet been pacified. Merchants are not passing through, and how can the people survive? Your duty is to manage the area, and you should eliminate the harm to the people! To sit idly by without taking action, how can you escape your responsibility?" Upon hearing this, Chu, disregarding the master-servant relationship, quickly issued an order to all prefectures, counties, and states, strictly enforcing the search for bandits. If anyone dares to show leniency or indulgence, they will be immediately impeached and punished without mercy. The local officials, fearing impeachment, repeatedly set deadlines for capturing bandits, but ultimately captured nothing. At the time, there was a famous hunter named Liang Ke in Henan, over seventy years old, living in a simple cottage, having long since retired from service. The hunters, fearing the deadlines, therefore pooled money and prepared generous gifts, sending envoys to seek help from Liang. Liang initially refused firmly, saying he was too old and unable to serve. After treating the envoys to wine, when the wine was flowing freely, he spoke about his youth when he hunted bandits. His sharp and vigorous appearance was evident in his eyebrows and eyes. The envoy clapped and praised, using flattering words to encourage him, saying, "Now the banditry is rampant, but they are not your enemies. If you would make an effort to go, so that you lot would know there is still an old Lian Po in the ranks of hunters, they would not dare to act. Otherwise, you lot would not consider you old, but would laugh at you for being cowardly, and your heroism of the past would be swept away! I secretly pity you." Liang felt the heat in his ears and itching in his fingers, laughed and said while raising his beard, "Are you provoking me? If it is unavoidable, how about going with you?" The envoy greatly rejoiced and praised. In no time, they arrived together in Shandong, where the hunters met him with great joy. They all agreed, "We have repeatedly observed the bandits' traces, and they actually hide in the governor's office." Liang said, "We should investigate the truth and not act rashly." So he hid every day near the governor's office to watch them.

    211

    一夜，漏二下，果见一人跃垣出，疾如飞鸟而逝。梁故善弹，老犹黑夜弹香火，百步之外，百不失一。漏四下，其人仍如鸟飞还，梁急援勍扣铁丸，从垣下而弹其额，听墙内堕地有声，知已中弹。诘旦，复命历城令，谓大盗斯得。令问：“盗何在？”曰：“在臬署。”令叱曰：“汝呓语耶！焉有臬署而为逋逃薮者！”梁曰：“小人已弹其额，公可白抚军，急谕稽臬署人等，额有伤者即盗，盗复何遁？顾此宜速不宜缓，缓则少纵即逝矣！”令曰：“然。”急谒抚军，谓大盗斯得。抚军问：“盗何在？”曰：“在臬署。”抚军叱曰：“汝呓语耶？焉有臬署而为逋逃之薮者！”令曰：“捕役已弹其额。

    Explanation

    211

    One night, at the second strike of the water clock, a person was seen jumping over the wall, moving swiftly like a flying bird. Liang, who was skilled in archery even in old age, still practiced shooting in the dark with incense and fire, never missing a target within a hundred paces. At the fourth strike of the water clock, the same person returned, moving swiftly like a bird. Liang quickly seized the strong and struck the iron ball, launching it from the wall and hitting the person's forehead. He heard a sound of something falling inside the wall, knowing that the shot had hit the target.

    The next day, Liang reported to the magistrate of Licheng, saying that the great thief had been caught. The magistrate asked, "Where is the thief?" Liang replied, "He is in the office of the censor." The magistrate shouted, "Are you delirious? How can the office of the censor be a refuge for fugitives?" Liang said, "I have already shot his forehead. Please inform the governor, and urgently instruct the officials of the office of the censor. Those with wounds on their forehead are thieves. How can they escape? This matter requires speed, not delay. If delayed, they will escape easily!" The magistrate said, "Yes." He hurried to see the governor, saying that the great thief had been caught. The governor asked, "Where is the thief?" The magistrate replied, "He is in the office of the censor." The governor shouted, "Are you delirious? How can the office of the censor be a refuge for fugitives?" The magistrate said, "The constables have already shot his forehead."

    212

    公可召按察，急谕稽臬署人等，额有伤者即盗，盗复何遁？顾此宜速不宜缓，缓则少纵即逝矣！”抚军曰：“然。”乃遣人召褚。褚辞以有疾，不可以风，请假一月。抚军顿足让之曰：“此何事也！而可待至一月耶！”令鞠躬进曰：“此事关系甚重。如按察有疾，公姑枉驾省视，固不失为谦尊。况僚属一体，必不得已，即寝室亦可晤语，无须远嫌引避也。”抚军然之。比至臬署，褚辞以不敢屈驾，有事俟假满谒见再议；抚军执意省视其疾，褚益固辞。抚军大疑，即自下舆径入其寝室，褚蒙头呻吟，不敢露面。抚军命左右搴被而逼视之，见褚额坟起，缠以布帕，血涔涔外流，确系弹伤无疑也。抚军瞪目诧曰：“奇哉！大盗斯得，我始固料不及此也！”反署，使人风褚速自录供待劾褚料难掩饰，果据实直承为盗不讳。抚军特疏入告，勅即正法自褚伏诛后，盗踪顿戢。

    Explanation

    212

    The governor ordered the summoning of the Censorate and urgently instructed the officials of the Censorate's office: "Anyone with injuries is a thief; how can a thief escape?" He emphasized that the matter required immediate action, as delaying would allow the thief to escape. The governor agreed and sent someone to summon the Censorate. The Censorate, citing illness, refused to meet, stating that he could not withstand the wind and requested a month off. The governor, frustrated, said, "What kind of matter is this! Can it wait for a month?" He then bowed and said, "This matter is of great importance. If the Censorate is ill, the governor may visit to show respect, which is not inappropriate. Moreover, as officials are equals, if absolutely necessary, they may even meet in the bedroom without fear of impropriety." The governor agreed. When he arrived at the Censorate's office, the Censorate again refused, saying he dared not trouble the governor, and suggested waiting until his leave was over to discuss the matter. The governor insisted on visiting, but the Censorate continued to refuse. The governor became suspicious and personally entered the Censorate's bedroom. The Censorate hid his face, pretending to be ill. The governor ordered his attendants to lift the blanket and look closely, and saw that the Censorate had a bullet wound on his forehead, wrapped in a cloth, with blood flowing out. It was clear that he had been shot. The governor, surprised, exclaimed, "Amazing! I have finally caught the thief, though I never expected this!" He returned to his office and ordered the Censorate to confess and be impeached. The Censorate, realizing he could not hide the truth, confessed to being a thief. The governor reported to the emperor, who ordered the thief to be executed. After the Censorate's execution, criminal activities ceased.

    213

    里乘子曰：予曩在临清，有周生者，谈褚事甚详。并言褚工书，楷字摹褚登善，大草仿米元章，皆极入妙，至今山左士夫家藏者尚多。特怪堂堂风宪，甘作盗魁，延颈受诛死而无悔，不知是何居心也。其女名毅华，字果男，乳名讷儿，美而好武。嫁同邑武进士某，亦美男子也。夫妇尝出猎，女骑镂金鞍枣骝马，惯着缁衣素裳，短襟秃袖；腰系五色丝绦，左悬朱弦雕弓，右悬锦箙，内盛鹫翎金仆姑；两足蹑利屣，立镫上纤细如锥。

    Explanation

    213

    Li Chengzi said: I once was in Linqing, and there was a person named Zhou who talked about Chu's affairs in great detail. He also mentioned that Chu was skilled in calligraphy, copying Chu Danxiang's regular script and imitating Mi Yuanzhang's cursive script, both reaching the highest level of skill. Even today, many of Chu's works are still collected by scholars in Shandong. What is particularly strange is that such a high-ranking official willingly became a bandit leader, extending his neck to accept execution without regret, and it is hard to understand what kind of heart he had. His daughter, named Yi Hua, courtesy name Guo Nan, and nicknamed Ne Er, was beautiful and fond of martial arts. She married a martial arts jinshi from the same county, who was also a handsome man. Once, the couple went hunting together. The daughter rode a chestnut bay horse adorned with a gold-plated saddle, used to wear black robes and plain skirts, with short-sleeved and sleeveless clothing; she wore a multi-colored silk belt around her waist, hanging a red-stringed carved bow on the left and a brocade quiver on the right, inside which were eagle-feathered golden arrows; her feet wore sharp shoes, standing on the stirrups as thin as needles.

    214

    头上青帕抹额，发绾盘龙高髻黑如髹漆；髻前插盏大红绒球二颗，迎风颤摇。眉弯新月目莹秋波，玉靥朱唇，不假涂泽。与夫并辔而驰，俨然双璧。后其夫由游击荐擢总戎，每阅武，女必坐屏后观之，有好身手者辄另赏以银币。一时麾下卧彪之俦，技勇非他部所能敌，皆女奖励之功也。或谓女谙彭祖内视法，当随夫之任时年已逾四十，望之犹二十许人。亦善书，尝见骨董客藏所临《十三行经》泥金便面，某太史以朱提一流购得之，狂喜如获异宝云。

    Explanation

    214

    A Woman of Grace and Talent

    Her forehead was adorned with a blue headband, her hair coiled into a high, dragon-like bun as black as lacquer; two large red wool balls were inserted into the front of her hair, swaying gracefully in the wind. Her eyebrows were crescent-shaped, her eyes bright and reflective like autumn waves, her cheeks jade-like and her lips red, all without the need for makeup. Riding side by side with her husband, she appeared as two precious jade discs. Later, her husband was promoted from a lower military rank to a higher command position. During military exercises, she would always sit behind a screen to observe, and those with good skills would receive additional silver rewards. At one time, the elite soldiers under her husband's command, skilled and brave beyond comparison to those of other units, were all due to her encouragement and rewards. It was said that she knew the longevity techniques of Peng Zu, and when she accompanied her husband on his duties, she was already over forty years old, but she looked no more than twenty. She was also skilled in calligraphy, and a collector once owned a copy of her work on a gold-leaf fan, which a historian purchased for a high price and was ecstatic to have acquired as a rare treasure.

    215

    古雏鸾
    　　
    　　江左奚生铁臣，才华丰蔚，容止都美。大名太守与为通家招司记室。尝夜读书，有女搴帷入，姿首娟妍，目波明媚，诚天人也。生意其为妖，愕眙却立，不敢仰视。女掩袖笑曰：“妾狐仙也，古氏雏鸾，以与君有缘，故来相就。非祸君者，可请毋畏。”生固悦其美，以所言委宛动听，遂不复畏，与同寝处，情好甚敦。女畅通文义，兼擅书法，遇生事烦，辄为捉刀，字体秀健，出生之右。每戒生慎勿泄语，久之，人渐有知者。太守起家翰林，亦风雅士，闻之，私以问生。生以通家，质言不敢稍讳，并坚嘱勿以语人。太守戏要之曰：“如不泄语，须君意中人私觌，俾得一亲芳姿，乃如所戒。”生笑曰：“诺。”退以语女，女笑曰：“太守公不怀好意，挟势劫盟，妾必有以小惩之！”一日，生观书，拥女于怀，太守缓步搴帷迅入，女翩若惊鸿，起身遽遁。

    Explanation

    215

    A Woman Named Ancient Phoenix

    In the eastern region of the Yangtze River, there was a man named Xi Sheng Ti Chen, known for his abundant talent and graceful demeanor. The governor of Daming, who was related to him by family ties, invited him to serve as a secretary. One night, while Xi Sheng was reading, a woman pulled the curtain and entered, her appearance delicate and charming, her eyes as bright as waves of light—truly a heavenly being. Xi Sheng initially thought she was an apparition and stood back, startled,不敢仰视。 The woman covered her mouth and laughed, saying, “I am a fox spirit, known as Ancient Phoenix. I have a destined connection with you, so I have come to be with you. I am not here to harm you, so please do not be afraid.” Xi Sheng was captivated by her beauty, and her words were so gentle and persuasive that he no longer feared her. They began to share the same bed and room, and their affection grew deep. The woman was well-versed in literature and rhetoric, and she also excelled in calligraphy. When Xi Sheng was busy, she often wrote for him, and her handwriting was elegant and robust, surpassing his own. She frequently warned him to keep their relationship secret, and over time, people began to learn of it. The governor, who had risen to the position of Hanlin Scholar, was also a refined and cultured man. Upon hearing of the relationship, he privately asked Xi Sheng about it. Xi Sheng, being related to the governor, spoke frankly without any concealment and firmly warned him not to tell others. The governor playfully said, “If you do not reveal it, you must introduce your beloved privately so that I can meet her beauty, and then follow your warning.” Xi Sheng laughed and said, “Yes.” Afterward, he told the woman, and she laughed, saying, “The governor does not have good intentions. He is using his power to force an agreement, and I will surely find a way to punish him lightly!” One day, Xi Sheng was reading, holding the woman in his arms, when the governor slowly pulled the curtain and quickly entered. The woman gracefully rose like a startled swan and quickly fled.

    216

    太守瞥睹其貌，不禁神驰，戏抚生肩笑曰：“君几生修此艳福？佳人不媒而合，夙夜必偕，鄙人一求平视而不可得，能勿羡而生妒乎？”闻暗中嘤嘤小语让之曰：“公以堂堂太守，后房粉白黛绿，争怜斗宠者不知凡几，尚不自慊，而鳃鳃然歆羡于穷措大，能毋愧乎！”太守惭不能答。生以将应京兆试，偶取时艺翻阅，女见之笑曰：“君不知取法乎上，徒此孜孜，纵终日咿唔，口角流沫，何益也！”生惊曰：“卿世外人，亦解此小道耶？”女正色曰：“恶是何言也！时文为词章根本，时文不精，各体俱难畅达。每慨三家村伪儒，时文不通，辄自命以诗古文词名家。

    Explanation

    216

    The governor glanced at her appearance and could not help but be entranced, playfully patting the young man's shoulder and smiling. "How many lifetimes have you cultivated to enjoy such good fortune?" he said. "A beautiful woman has come together with you without the need for a matchmaker, and you will surely be together day and night. I, a lowly person, cannot even get a level look from her. How can I not envy and feel jealous?" A soft, whispered voice from the darkness chided him, "You, as a respectable governor, have countless women in your inner chambers, painted faces and green eyebrows, competing for your favor, yet you still do not feel satisfied. Yet you are so envious of a poor scholar. How can you not feel ashamed?" The governor was ashamed and could not answer.

    The young man, preparing to take the capital examination, happened to pick up and read contemporary essays. The woman saw this and laughed, saying, "You do not know to take the highest standards as your model, just working hard like this. Even if you study all day long, spitting foam from your mouth, what good will it do?" The young man was surprised and said, "You are an outsider to the world, do you understand this trivial skill?" The woman spoke seriously, "What a terrible thing to say! Contemporary essays are the foundation of literary composition. If you do not master contemporary essays, it will be difficult to excel in other forms of writing. I often lament the fake scholars in the villages, who do not understand contemporary essays but still call themselves masters of poetry and ancient prose."

    217

    一时贵耳贱目者不辨真伪，遽以名士相推。彼竟居之不疑，自以为是；不愧学植无根，反大言欺人，肆口丑诋时文为小道，鄙弃不屑染指。而后生小子希慕虚声，乐从其道，相率为伪。文风既坏，学术奚端？是则可忧也。不知时文代圣贤立言，每成一艺，必须镕经铸史，醖酿而出，不可率尔操觚，草草毕事。乃村伧不察，猥以小道目之，不亦慎乎！”生瞿然曰：“诚如卿言，可谓于此道三折肱矣。其源流得失，能道其详否？”女笑日：“略知一二。”生笑请赐教，女曰：“时文自半山作俑，栾城效尤，所作非论非说，半类语录，不过偶尔游戏创为此体。自有明以此取士，谓之‘制艺’，大致仿唐人试帖法，义取骈偶，始名曰‘八股’。

    Explanation

    217

    In an era where people value hearsay over seeing, they cannot distinguish between truth and falsehood, hastily elevating someone to the status of a distinguished scholar. He accepts this status without doubt, believing himself to be right; not ashamed of having no solid foundation in learning, he instead speaks grandly to deceive others, openly insults contemporary prose as a minor pursuit, and despises it, avoiding touching it. Young people then aspire to empty fame, enjoying following his path, and collectively act falsely. The literary style has already deteriorated, and where is the foundation of scholarship? This is indeed a cause for worry. They do not understand that contemporary prose speaks on behalf of sages, and each time a piece is created, it must be refined and shaped through the classics and histories, maturing and emerging, not hastily taken up with the brush, finished carelessly. Villagers who do not understand crudely label it as a minor pursuit, is this not cautious? The student was startled and said, "Indeed, as you say, you can be said to have three times handled this path. Could you explain the origins, development, and gains and losses in detail?" The woman smiled and said, "I know a little bit." The student laughed and asked for instruction, and the woman said, "Contemporary prose was first created by Ban Gu, and Lu Cheng followed suit, their works not being arguments or discussions, half resembling sayings, merely occasionally playing and creating this form. Since the Ming dynasty, this method has been used to select officials, called 'zhiyi' (regulated prose), generally imitating the Tang people's examination methods, taking parallel couplets, and began to be named 'bagu' (eight-part essay)."

    218

    明初之作，传者寥寥，成、宏、正、嘉，规模于焉大备，其朴实说理处，犹不免近于高头讲章；隆、万专尚神韵，多涉浅滑；天、崇则称极盛矣！海阳、太乙、蕴生、大樽四家可称四杰；仲昭文品最卑，文锋最利，可称名场健将。至我朝，熊、刘、韩、马诸公，博大昌明，是真盛世之元音也。

    Explanation

    218

    Early Ming works, few have been transmitted. The Zhengde, Hongzhi, Jiajing, and Wanli eras saw the comprehensive development of literary forms. Their straightforward and rational parts are still close to the style of formal expositions. The Longqing and Wanli periods specialized in elegance and charm, often being superficial and slippery. The Tianqi and Chongzhen periods are considered the peak. The four families of Haiyang, Taiyi, Yunsheng, and Dazun can be called the Four Greats. Zhongzhao's literary quality is the lowest, but his writing is sharp and forceful, making him a strong contender in the literary arena. By our dynasty, Xiong, Liu, Han, and Ma, among others, are broad, grand, and enlightened, truly the essence of a prosperous age.

    219

    此后则惟桐城二方、宜兴诸储各树—帜，能事毕矣，叹观止矣！妾尝谓举业家，于有明宜多读金、陈、黄、陈及项宫詹五家之文，于我朝宜多读二方及储仲子之文，以此八家为之根柢，然后取近科名墨简练揣摩，稍趋风气，及锋而试，尚何患不操胜券哉！君不知取法乎上，但欣赏坊刻脑满肠肥之作，虽弋获科名，亦居下乘；矧得失有命，未必果能幸取也耶！”生闻女言，爽然若失，自是每成一艺，必就女正订，女点窜涂抹，不稍谦让，生极悦服，俨然奉女为师，功亦日进。亡何，试期已及，女偕入闱中，人竟不觉。三场诗文经策，女为斟酌尽善。揭晓，果登魁选。越岁春闱，复请女与俱，女辞曰：“侥幸行险，可一而不可再。秋闱，赖君祖功宗德，未及于祸，然亦君命应得一第，故妾敢放胆效命；今春闱，神明愈多，监察愈严，倘一经逻获，有害于妾，无益于君，是不可以不慎也。”生乃独赴南宫。

    Explanation

    219

    Afterward, only the writings of the two Fangs from Tongcheng and the various Chus from Yixing each established their own styles, and all that could be done was accomplished; it truly reached the pinnacle of perfection! I have often said to scholars preparing for the imperial examinations that during the Ming dynasty, they should read the works of the five writers Jin, Chen, Huang, another Chen, and Xiang Gongjian, and during our dynasty, they should read the works of the two Fangs and Chu Zhongzi. By using these eight writers as the foundation, one should then practice with recent examination papers, slightly adapting to the prevailing trends, and when the opportunity arises, how can one not hold the winning card? You do not know to aim high, but instead appreciate mass-produced books full of nonsense; even if you obtain the degree by luck, it will still be a low achievement;何况得失有命，未必果能幸取也耶！”听到这些话，他感到非常失望，从此每当完成一篇文章，必定请她校正，她毫不谦让地进行修改，他非常满意，俨然把她当作老师，自己的进步也日益显著。不久，考试的时间到了，她陪同他进入考场，竟没有人察觉。三场考试中的诗词、经策，她都帮助他斟酌修改得尽善尽美。结果揭晓，果然他被选为第一名。到了第二年春天的考试，他又请求她一同参加，她拒绝说：“侥幸冒险，可以一次，但不能两次。上次秋天的考试，多亏您的祖先功德，没有遭受祸患，但也只是因为您命中注定应该得中，所以我才敢大胆为您效力；这次春天的考试，神明更加众多，监察更加严格，如果一旦被发现，对我有害，对您无益，这是不可以不谨慎的。”他只好独自前往南宫参加考试。

    220

    既下第归，会太守擢秦中观察，生得乃母手谕，促即南归。爰别太守，邀女偕行，女不肯。生固嫌妻貌寝，叹谓女曰：“内子下体亦差可为葑菲之采，而面目未免可憎，与小生殊不相配！如卿肯偕归，平生愿足矣！”女笑曰：“从来巧女多伴拙夫，而美男亦多娶丑女。人多谓配合颠倒，为之不平；不知天地爱人以德，即此可悟造物妙用。试观秦嘉徐淑、相如文君，伉俪相当，古今有几？然即此四人而论，非女抱沈疴，即男婴渴疾，青春相爱，白首难期；琴瑟常调，弦多中断；转不若梁孟、鲍桓，弃貌取德，反可山河偕老也。

    Explanation

    220

    After failing the imperial examination and returning home, the scholar coincided with the governor being promoted to the position of observer in Qin. He received a letter from his mother, urging him to return south immediately. Upon bidding farewell to the governor, he invited the woman to travel with him, but she refused. The scholar had always disliked his wife's appearance and sighed to the woman, saying, "My wife's lower body is fairly acceptable as a patch of wild vegetables, but her face is somewhat repulsive and completely mismatched with me! If you would accompany me, my lifelong wish would be fulfilled!" The woman smiled and replied, "Clever women often marry clumsy men, and handsome men also often marry ugly women. Many people say that such pairings are upside down and feel it is unfair; but they do not know that heaven and earth love people according to their virtue. From this, one can understand the ingenious use of creation. Consider Qin Jia and Xu Shu, Zuo Xiangru and Zhuo Wenjun—how many well-matched couples are there in ancient and modern times? But considering these four people, either the woman has a serious illness or the man has a chronic disease. They love each other in their youth, but it is hard to expect a long life together; the harmony of the qin and se is often disrupted by broken strings. It is better than the cases of Liang Hong and Meng Guang, Bao Shuya and his wife, who valued virtue over appearance and thus lived together until old age."

    221

    至君嫌夫人貌寝，殊可不必。妾知夫人命薄，不堪为孝廉之配。君归不久，必不免奉倩神伤，旧人既逝，新人即来，君具此才貌，又得一第，何虑无嫱施作匹？妾相君鸾胶重续，定属嘉耦，赠嫁甚丰。君命非仕宦中人，此后可戢景邱园，春晖奉母。日对丽人，坐拥厚资，一袜同心，南面不易。有妾在旁，反使目中添刺。况与君缘分垂尽，何如稍留未了，为他日重会计耶？”生以女言决绝，料不可强，不得已，挥泪而别。归家后，未几，妻果病卒。时同乡某观察，有女甚美，素极钟爱，以妻生，奁资巨万，生藉此经营生产。以狐能前知，遂遵其言，杜门养亲，灰心仕进矣。

    Explanation

    221

    The husband's criticism of his wife's appearance is truly unnecessary. I know that she has a short life and is not suitable to be the wife of a scholar-official. After you return home, you will inevitably experience deep sorrow. Once the old wife has passed away, a new one will come. With your talents and appearance, and having achieved success, why should you worry about not finding a suitable match? I believe that your relationship will be rekindled like the phoenix feather glue, and you will find a good match. The new wife will be given a generous dowry. Your fate is not to be a government official; after this, you can retire to the countryside to care for your mother. You will live a life of luxury and comfort, with a beautiful wife, wealth, and harmony. Having me around would only cause you discomfort. Moreover, our relationship is almost over; it would be better to leave some things unresolved for future reconciliation." The husband decided to end the relationship based on the speaker's words, knowing he could not force the situation, and left with tears.

    After returning home, not long after, his wife indeed fell ill and passed away. At that time, a local magistrate had a very beautiful daughter whom he deeply loved and intended to marry to the husband, providing a dowry of millions. The husband used this opportunity to manage his business. Because the fox could foresee the future, he followed its advice, closed the door, cared for his parents, and gave up his ambition for officialdom.

    222

    里乘子曰：此亦临清周生为予言者。且言狐女工书善画，至今大名人家藏者尚多；他如诗古文词亦略晓其大致，惟于时艺尤极精究，观所持论，亦不无可采。尝有大名府属某县观风，以“道之以政”一章命题，卷呈太守阅评，苦无佳构，女戏拟一篇，太守极为称赏。其警句云：“束缚之以政刑，民仅立于无耻之地；薰陶之以德礼，民乃还其有耻之天。”一时士林为之传诵。予观丛书所载狐知翰墨者甚多，不谓此女竟解此艺，奇矣哉！

    Explanation

    222

    The Master of Li Cheng said: This is also what Zhou, the son of Linqing, told me. He mentioned that a fox girl was skilled in writing and painting, and her works are still widely collected by famous families in Daming; as for poetry, classical prose, and essays, she also had a general understanding of their main points, but especially in the contemporary style of writing, she had studied it very thoroughly. Considering the arguments she presented, they were not without merit. Once, during an imperial tour to observe customs and manners in a county under Daming Prefecture, the topic was set as "govern by political measures." The scrolls were submitted for review by the prefect, but he found none to be particularly well-crafted. The fox girl, in jest, wrote an essay, which the prefect greatly admired. Her striking lines read: "Restrained by political measures and punishments, the people merely stand on the ground of shamelessness; cultivated with moral principles and rites, the people then return to their innate sense of shame." At the time, the scholarly community praised it. I have seen many foxes who know writing and painting in collections of books, but I did not expect this girl to understand this art, how extraordinary it is!

    223

    粤东某甲
    　　
    　　粤东某甲，向在粤海关监督署中司会计，获资巨万。既罢，贪心无厌，复挟资入都，营谋其役。路投逆旅，栖止后楼。薄暮，横枕挑灯，焚吸合甫融膏，忽闻楼梯窸窣作声，见一美少年衣服华烂，举止殊众，步至榻前，向灯而立。甲姑起身为礼，少年略一点首，便横眠其左；试燃膏以献，亦不谦让，约吸数十口，乃起，从容下楼而去，并无一言。甲殊怪其骄蹇无礼，亦姑置之。时新任监督某公，出都赴任，使公子前行，恰与甲同逆旅。公子平日固亦吸合甫融膏，傔从拂榻展被，甫毕，公予以路途劳瘁，一偃仰不觉睡熟，群仆屏息伺应，不敢惊扰。

    Explanation

    223

    A Man from Guangdong

    　　A man from Guangdong, who had previously served as an accountant in the customs supervision office in Guangdong, accumulated a vast fortune. After resigning, he remained greedy and insatiable, and again took his wealth to the capital to seek employment. On the way, he checked into an inn and lodged on the upper floor. Late in the afternoon, he lay on his side with a lamp lit, burning and inhaling opium. Suddenly, he heard the sound of footsteps on the stairs and saw a handsome young man with splendid attire, behaving differently from others, approaching the bed and standing facing the lamp. The man rose to greet him politely, and the young man nodded slightly, then lay down on his left side. The man tried to light more opium to offer, but the young man did not hesitate, inhaled about ten times, then rose and calmly went downstairs, without saying a word. The man found it very strange and presumptuous, but also put it aside. At that time, a newly appointed supervisor, leaving the capital to take up his post, sent his son ahead, and happened to stay at the same inn as the man. The young man usually also smoked opium, and his attendants prepared the bed and spread out the bedding. Just as they finished, the supervisor allowed himself to rest due to the fatigue of the journey, lay down, and fell asleep without realizing. The attendants held their breath and waited in silence, daring not to disturb.

    224

    少选，公子欠伸起曰：“好梦！好梦！”群仆闻之，急燃膏以进，公子摇手止之。命呼逆旅主人至，问：“此有后楼乎？”曰：“然。”“楼上有宿客乎？”曰：“然。”问：“其客年貌若何，衣服若何，然否？”曰：“然。”乃命召某甲至，相视骇愕。公子笑曰：“顷承嘉惠，尚忆之否？”甲漫应曰：“唯唯。”问甲姓名，并入都何事，甲具告之。曰：“如此甚好，可返辕随我赴粤也。”甲叩谢，悄诘傔从，知为公子，喜出望外。初，公子睡熟，魂适出舍，闻膏香，迹至后楼，居然与甲吸膏。醒时为群仆备告前事，群仆转以语甲，忆及前事，实深称异，始恍然悟顷之吸膏者，固公子之魂也。后甲至粤大得宠任，主仆由梦中作合，亦奇缘也。

    Explanation

    224

    A short while later, the young master yawned and stretched, rising with a smile and saying, “What a dream! What a dream!” The attendants, upon hearing this, hurriedly lit the lamp oil and brought it to him, but the young master waved his hand to stop them. He then ordered the innkeeper to be summoned and asked, “Is there a back building here?” “Yes,” came the reply. “Is there a guest staying on the upper floor?” “Yes.” He asked further, “What is the guest’s age and appearance like? What does he wear? Is that so?” “Yes,” the innkeeper answered. The young master then ordered a certain person, named Jia, to be brought before him. When Jia arrived, the two looked at each other in shock. The young master smiled and said, “Recently I received your favor—do you still remember it?” Jia replied indifferently, “Yes, yes.” The young master asked for Jia’s name and the reason he had come to the capital. Jia explained in full. The young master said, “This is very good—return to your carriage and accompany me to Guangdong.” Jia bowed in gratitude and discreetly questioned the attendants, who told him that the one before him was the young master. Overjoyed beyond expectation, Jia was delighted.

    Originally, when the young master had fallen asleep, his soul had just left his body. It smelled the lamp oil and traced its way to the back building, where it unexpectedly drank the oil together with Jia. When he awoke, the attendants informed him of what had happened earlier. The attendants then told Jia, who, upon recalling the previous events, was deeply amazed. Only then did he suddenly realize that the one who had drunk the oil earlier was indeed the young master’s soul. Later, when Jia arrived in Guangdong, he was greatly favored and given an important position. Thus, the master and servant were united through a dream—truly a strange and fateful encounter.

    225

    里乘卷四
    　　
    　　姮儿
    　　
    　　明季，东越尚书某公，年六十乞休归。筑适园于鑑湖之滨，亭台池沼，与平泉绿野比胜。有女素娴，以中秋生，小字姮儿。姿妍性慧，公所钟爱；垂髫自课读书，通晓翰墨；惟选婿綦苛，故及笄犹未字也。同里奚生，本旧家子，成童有声庠序。幼失怙恃，而家甚贫，寄居孀妗家，聊藉笔耕糊口。适园花木极盛，每春花开，公必招客宴饮赋诗为乐，知名之士，靡不毕至。尝赋白牡丹诗，惟生四首称最，中有四联云：“最好文章惟本色，是真富贵不繁华。”“美人原不须修饰，名士由来要率真。”“宜主淡妆脂不涴，太真新浴玉偏温。”“风暖腻融瑶岛雪，月明浓簇玉田烟。

    Explanation

    225

    In the fourth volume of Li Cheng,

    Chang'e

    During the late Ming dynasty, a high-ranking official from the Eastern Yue region, at the age of sixty, requested retirement and returned home. He built the Suo Garden by the shore of Jian Lake, where pavilions, terraces, ponds, and marshes compared favorably with Pingquan and Lüye. He had a daughter named Su Xian, who was born on the Mid-Autumn Festival and was given the nickname Chang'e. She was beautiful and intelligent, deeply loved by the official; from a young age, she taught herself to read and write, becoming proficient in calligraphy. However, her standards for choosing a husband were extremely high, so she remained unmarried even after reaching the age of marriage. In the same village was a man named Xi, originally from an old family, who became famous in school during his adolescence. He lost his parents at a young age, and his family was very poor, so he lived with his widowed aunt, earning a living by writing. The Suo Garden was filled with lush flowers and trees, and every spring when the flowers bloomed, the official would always invite guests to drink and recite poetry for entertainment. All famous scholars came. Once, he composed a poem about white peonies, and only Xi's four poems were considered the best, with four lines that read: "The best writing is true to its nature, true wealth and nobility are not about luxury." "Beauty does not need embellishment, scholars have always valued sincerity." "She should wear light makeup, as if it were not stained, like Tai Zhen after a bath, her skin is particularly warm." "The warm wind melts the snow on the jade island, the bright moon gathers thick clouds over the jade fields."

    226

    ”尤为公所叹赏，顾谓座客曰：“诸公所作固佳，如奚生蕴藉风流，别有寄托，未免推倒一时豪杰矣！”座客唯唯，佥谢不及。宴罢，公以诸诗付姮儿甲乙。姮儿周览一过，亦拔生诗独冠一军。笑谓公曰：“此生的是金华殿中人，安有吐属名隽如此而长贫贱者！”公亦微笑首肯。自是悯生穷困，不时助以膏火，阴有东床之意；以凡事皆决于夫人，不敢遽宣于口，意俟奚生科名小就再议。姮儿雅窥公意，亦殊以为不谬，故每公欲周济生时，必怂恿而赞成之。公幼子年十三，方攻举业，苦无良师，欲延奚生课读，而嫌其年太少。商之姮儿，姮儿笑曰：“昔项橐七岁为圣人师，奚生长于项橐多多矣！况吾弟非圣人比乎，有何不可？”公笑曰：“诺。”遂延奚生课其幼子。初，姮儿第知生才，而未见其貌。家塾在适园外西偏，姮儿绣楼傍适园东角，生课读之暇，恒携公子来园闲眺。

    Explanation

    226

    The guest's work was particularly admired by the host, who turned to the guests and said, "All of your works are indeed excellent, but the work of Guest Xi is graceful and refined, with a deeper meaning, and cannot help but overshadow the current heroes!" The guests nodded in agreement and humbly thanked the host, feeling inadequate. After the banquet, the host gave the poems to姮儿 for ranking. After reviewing them thoroughly,姮儿 also selected the guest's poem as the best. Smiling, she said to the host, "This person is truly a person of the Jinhua Hall; how can someone with such eloquent and refined speech remain poor and lowly forever?" The host also smiled and nodded in agreement. From then on, he pitied the guest's poverty and occasionally helped with expenses, having secret intentions for him as a son-in-law; since all matters were decided by the lady of the house, he dared not speak it openly. He intended to wait until the guest achieved some success before discussing further.姮儿 subtly observed the host's intentions and also thought it was not wrong, so whenever the host wanted to help the guest,姮儿 would encourage and support him. The host's young son, aged thirteen, was studying for the imperial examinations, lacked a good teacher, and wanted to invite the guest to tutor him, but was hesitant because the guest was too young. He consulted姮儿, who smiled and said, "In ancient times, Xiangtuo, at seven, was a teacher to sages, and the guest is much older than Xiangtuo! And my brother is not a sage; what is there to object to?" The host smiled and said, "Agreed." Thus, he invited the guest to tutor his young son. Initially,姮儿 only knew of the guest's talent, but had not seen his appearance. The family school was located west of the Shiyuan, and姮儿's embroidered pavilion was beside the eastern corner of the Shiyuan. During the guest's free time from tutoring, he often brought the young master to the garden for leisurely strolls.

    227

    姮儿自楼窗窥之，见生仪容俊伟，举止不俗，心益喜。生固早耳姬儿才貌双绝，又闻其评诗及怂恿之言，窃幸此身得一知己，我不可以负之；又念一寒至此，岂能妄觊系援；继又叹曰：“此生不娶姮儿，宁终鳏耳！有志者事竟成，彼刘文叔岂非人哉！”同邑大冢宰某公，与公同年登第，权势赫赫，震耀朝野。公鄙其为人，交殊淡漠。其子某甲以荫典得指挥千户，家居，假父势鱼肉乡里，人多侧目。甲以丧偶，托媒求姮儿为继室，公雅不欲，商之夫人，夫人歆其富贵，极口允诺。公争之曰：“甲虽一时富贵，其所行事，恐终难免于祸！”夫人怒曰：“甲与汝无仇，何得信口诅咒！且姮儿日长，似此门第，错过更许谁耶！”公曰：“奚生年少多才，定不久于贫贱。

    Explanation

    227

    Heng'er peered from the window of the tower and saw that the young man had a handsome appearance and a noble bearing, her heart grew even more delighted. The young man had indeed heard of Heng'er's extraordinary beauty and talent, and he also heard her comments on poetry and her encouragement. He secretly felt fortunate to have found a true friend and could not betray her; yet he also thought about how poor he had become, how could he recklessly seek connections? He then sighed and said, "If I don't marry Heng'er, I will remain a bachelor forever! Where there's a will, there's a way. Isn't Liu Wen-shu a man of great virtue?" A high-ranking official from the same county, who was of the same year as the official in passing the imperial examinations, had immense power and influence, shaking the court and the countryside. The official held him in contempt and their relationship was very cold. His son, named Jia, obtained the rank of Thousand Households through imperial favor. At home, he abused his father's authority to oppress the local people, causing many to look at him with disdain. Jia, having lost his wife, asked through a matchmaker to marry Heng'er as his second wife. The official was not inclined to agree and consulted his wife, who was enamored by his wealth and status, and eagerly agreed. The official argued and said, "Although Jia is temporarily wealthy and powerful, his actions may ultimately lead to disaster!" The wife angrily said, "Jia has no grudge against you, why do you speak ill of him so casually! Moreover, Heng'er is growing up, and with such a prestigious family, if we miss this opportunity, who else would she marry!" The official said, "Xitao is young and talented, he will not remain poor for long."

    228

    吾意欲将姮儿妻之，可乎？”夫人唾其面曰：“汝傎也耶！将爱女给乞丐，岂不畏显者笑耶？吾志已决，汝休饶舌！”遂将姮儿许字某甲。以公有欲妻奚生之说，恐留奚生有碍女声名，遂辞奚生去。公无可如何，惟有垂头浩叹而已。奚生既辞公出，仍主孀妗家。知姮儿已字某甲，顿缺所望，镇日喃喃呓语，如失魂魄，眠食俱废。妗固无子，将依甥以终，见生病状，殊切忧虑，不时就问所苦。生日加剧，自恐不起，遂将病源备告妗氏，且谓此生不一见姮儿，死不暝目。妗慰之曰：“儿勿妄想，彼既字某甲，并世簪缨，岂复垂念寒畯！以甥才华，何患不发迹？他日苟得志，又何患无美妇人哉！”生摇首曰：“妗言非也，姮儿我知己，非世俗巾帼可比。彼若知儿病，必蒙垂悯，但苦无人为通消息耳。”姮儿之乳母王媪，与妗比邻，素甚契洽。闻生病，间来省问。妗不得已，以生所语告媪。

    Explanation

    228

    I intend to marry off Heng'er to him, is that acceptable?" The lady spat on his face and said, "Are you crazy? Giving your beloved daughter to a beggar, wouldn't you be afraid of being laughed at by prominent people? My mind is made up, stop talking!" Thus, Heng'er was betrothed to Mr. Jia. Because there was a rumor that the gentleman wanted to marry Xishi, afraid that keeping Xishi around would damage the daughter's reputation, he was dismissed and sent away. The gentleman had no choice but to hang his head and sigh deeply. After Xishi was dismissed, he returned to the home of his aunt, who was a widow. Knowing that Heng'er had already been betrothed to Mr. Jia, he lost all hope. He would mutter to himself all day, as if he had lost his soul, neglecting sleep and food. The aunt, who had no children, intended to rely on her nephew for the rest of her life. Seeing Xishi's illness, she was greatly worried and would often ask about his suffering. Xishi's condition worsened, and he feared he would not recover. He then fully informed the aunt of the cause of his illness and said, "If I cannot see Heng'er one last time, I will not be at peace when I die." The aunt comforted him and said, "Child, don't have wild thoughts. She has already been betrothed to Mr. Jia, and he is a member of the gentry. How could he still think of a poor scholar? With your talents, why worry about not achieving success? In the future, if you achieve your ambitions, why worry about not having beautiful women?" Xishi shook his head and said, "Aunt, you are wrong. Heng'er is my true friend, not like ordinary women. If she knew I was ill, she would surely show compassion. But the problem is that there is no one to convey the message to her." Heng'er's wet nurse, Lady Wang, lived next to the aunt and had always been very close to her. When she heard that Xishi was ill, she occasionally came to visit and inquire. The aunt had no choice but to tell Lady Wang what Xishi had said.

    229

    媪叹曰：“以汝家郎君配我家小娘子，大好事！偏夫人愦愦，贪富贵许某公子，以凤偶鸡，诚可惜！妗不知，我家小娘子亦非寻常人物，此段因缘，甚非所乐。容老妇见时，试以郎君言白之。倘蒙垂悯未可知，然不敢必也。可慰郎君勿自苦，老妇自有以报命。”妗称谢，坚托而别。姮儿知奚生因己辞去，心殊不忍，又知夫人已将己许某甲为继室，稔知甲固纨恶少，自念终身失所托，意忽忽不乐。某甲喜聘姮儿，早涎其美，以中馈需人为词，亲迎之期甚迫，委禽纳采，备极丰腆。夫人大喜，日督趣姮儿检点妆奁。姮儿本系爱女，一言一笑，皆能博堂上欢，近忽神情懒惰，日复一日，渐难搘持。夫人颇深诧异，乃命之曰：“男婚女嫁，人之大伦也。

    Explanation

    229

    The elderly woman sighed and said, "It would be a great good fortune to marry your young man to my daughter! But your mother is so unreasonable, greedy for wealth and status, and has promised you to some other young man. It's like pairing a phoenix with a chicken, truly a pity! Your sister-in-law doesn't know, but my daughter is no ordinary person. This karmic connection is not something to be happy about. Allow me to see her when I meet her, and I will try to explain your words to her. If she shows compassion, it is uncertain, but I dare not be certain. Comfort your young man and do not suffer unnecessarily; I will find a way to fulfill my promise." The sister-in-law expressed her gratitude and firmly entrusted the matter before leaving. Huang'er knew that Xi-sheng had left because of her, and she felt extremely reluctant. She also knew that her mother had already promised her to a certain Mr. Jia as a second wife. She well-informed that Mr. Jia was indeed a spoiled and morally corrupt young man. Thinking that she would lose her partner for life, she became preoccupied and unhappy. Mr. Jia was pleased to marry Huang'er, having long coveted her beauty, and used the need for someone to manage the household as an excuse. The wedding ceremony was very urgent, and the betrothal proposal was accepted with rich and abundant gifts. Her mother was very happy and daily urged Huang'er to prepare her dowry. Huang'er had always been a beloved daughter, and every word and smile of hers could win the joy of the family. Recently, however, she became lethargic in demeanor, day after day, gradually becoming difficult to maintain. Her mother was deeply surprised and then commanded her, saying, "Men marrying and women being married is the great principle of human relations."

    230

    我为汝择配不易，今幸许某公子，此邑中第一等大绅土，其父气焰炙手可热，朝廷向用方殷，指日可望枚卜。不似汝父老不长进，但图逸乐，遽而乞休！即论某公子家道，岂止百万，汝嫁去便督家政，一呼百诺，似此大富贵，何尚郁郁不乐耶？虽难舍我二老，幸在同邑，时可见面，为汝计，当无不乐也。汝日来欢少愁多，我殊不解。岂需何衣物而赧于启齿耶？盍为我言之。”姮儿不答。再三研诘，卒颦眉不发一语。夫人无奈，只得曲意谕慰而去。他日，姮儿晨起较迟，尚未晓妆；侍儿为具早膳，悉却弗用，蓬头对镜，脉脉若有所思。王媪适至，惊曰：“几日未见娘子，何忽清瘦若此！”姮儿叹曰：“我亦不解何忽若此，但觉此心毫无生人之乐。

    Explanation

    230

    I have gone through great effort to find a suitable match for you, and now you are fortunate enough to be engaged to a certain young man, the foremost gentry in the town. His father has immense influence and power, and is currently in high favor with the court. The young man can expect rapid advancement in his career. In contrast, your father has not progressed in life and only seeks pleasure, having recently retired. Once you are married, you will oversee household management and be obeyed by all, enjoying such great wealth and status. Why are you still unhappy despite all this? Although it is difficult to leave my two parents, we are fortunate to live in the same town, so you can still visit us often. What is troubling you? Heng'er remained silent. After repeated questioning, she finally frowned and said nothing. The lady, at a loss, could only offer some consolation before leaving.

    The next day, Heng'er woke up late and had not yet put on makeup. The maidservant prepared breakfast, but Heng'er refused to eat. She sat with disheveled hair, looking into the mirror, appearing lost in thought. Lady Wang just arrived and was surprised, saying, "I have not seen you for several days, why have you suddenly become so thin?" Heng'er sighed, "I do not understand why I have become so thin, but I feel no joy in life."

    231

    古人有言：‘忧能伤人，’我其不能久于人世矣！奈何！”王媪曲为劝慰，因笑谓曰：“可贺娘子喜期已近，某公子是吾邑第一等人家，指日娘子过门，荣华富贵，享用不尽，不知老妇登门尚可望见颜色否也！”姮儿不待言毕，即正色侧身向壁，怒容可掬。王媪自知失言，默坐移时，又问：“近日可否游园？园中有何花开？曾作诗词否？作画否？弹琴否？”姮儿但摇头不语，色稍霁。媪因言：“昨有某秀才，携一古琴，玉轸金徽，据称是甚管夫人旧物；腹并有善画马之赵孟頫手刻多字。央老妇携至贵宅求售，以其索价太昂，又恐损坏难以赔偿，故未将来。”姮儿笑曰：“姆无论如何，早晚能将来一看否？”媪笑曰：“可。”因称娖走近姮儿身旁，低声笑曰：“尚有一可笑事，请宽老妇罪，方敢陈说，愿闻之否？”姮儿笑曰：“有何可笑事？姆试言之，或可破闷，决不汝罪。

    Explanation

    231

    A sage once said, “Grief can harm a person,” and I fear I will not be able to stay in the world for long! What shall I do?” Wang Ao tried to comfort her with gentle words and smiled, saying, “Congratulations, young lady! Your wedding day is approaching. The nobleman is the foremost family in our town. Soon you will enter his household, and enjoy wealth and honor that cannot be fully enjoyed. I wonder if I will still be able to see your face when I come to visit you?” Before Wang Ao finished speaking, Heng'er turned away from her, standing with her back to her, her face filled with anger. Wang Ao realized she had said something wrong and sat silently for a while. Then she asked, “Have you been able to visit the garden recently? What flowers are blooming there? Have you written any poems or songs? Have you painted anything? Have you played the zither?” Heng'er only shook her head without speaking, and her expression softened slightly. Wang Ao then said, “Yesterday, a scholar brought an ancient zither, with jade tuning pegs and gold inlays. It is said to be an old object once owned by Guan Fang, a famous female painter. The body of the zither is inscribed with many characters by Zhao Mengfu, a renowned painter known for his horse paintings. He asked me to bring it to your house to sell it, but the price was too high, and I was afraid it might be damaged and difficult to compensate for, so I did not bring it.” Heng'er smiled and said, “No matter what, can you bring it here to show me sometime?” Wang Ao smiled and said, “Yes.” She then approached Heng'er’s side and whispered, “There is still one amusing thing. Please forgive my rudeness, and I will tell you. Would you like to hear it?” Heng'er smiled and said, “What amusing thing? Please tell me, and perhaps it will lift my spirits. I promise I won’t blame you.”

    232

    ”媪曰：“可笑奚生，的是书痴！不时自说娘子是渠知己，不可负之，此生除却娘子，誓不他娶。前自宅中辞出，渠镇日如失魂魄，眠食俱废，看来难以医治。渠言死不足惜，及生不一见娘子，断不瞑目。旁人多斥其妄，渠泣谓娘子非世俗巾帼可比，若知渠病，必蒙垂悯，但苦无人为通消息。老妇怜其痴而多情，绐其代为转达。天下竟有此种痴情之人，不真令人发笑乎！”姮儿闻之，始则泣涕满面，继则吞声哽咽；及闻赞其非世俗巾帼可比，却喜奚生真不愧知己，平日一片垂悯奚生热心，不觉一时感触，几至放声痛哭矣。媪见姮儿此状，果信奚生之言不谬。少间，姮儿啜泣已，自以罗巾拭泪。媪复进曰：“奚生如此多情，无怪娘子垂悯。老妇明日薄暮送琴来，即暂屈奚生伪为奚奴，污面易衣负琴而至，藉使一见娘子，可乎？”姮儿不语，意似首肯。媪会意，少坐兴辞。

    Explanation

    232

    The elderly woman said, "How laughable is Xishi, truly a bookworm! He often speaks of Lady as his close friend, saying he cannot betray her. This life, except for Lady, he swears not to marry anyone else. Before he left the house, he was all day as if in a daze, neglecting both sleep and food, it seems hard to cure. He says dying is not worth regretting, but until he sees Lady, he will not close his eyes. Others often criticize him as delusional. He tearfully says that Lady is not comparable to ordinary women, if she knew his illness, she would surely show mercy. Unfortunately, there is no one to convey this news. The old woman pities his foolishness and emotion, pretending to relay his message. There are indeed people in the world so devoted, doesn't it really make one laugh?" Hearing this, Chang'er at first had tears streaming down her face, then swallowed her sobs. Upon hearing praise that Lady is not comparable to ordinary women, she was happy that Xishi truly does not disappoint as a close friend, her daily concern for Xishi's enthusiasm, she couldn't help but feel moved, almost bursting into tears. Seeing Chang'er in this state, the old woman truly believed Xishi's words were not wrong. After a while, Chang'er had finished her soft crying, using a silk handkerchief to wipe her tears. The old woman again said, "Xishi is so emotional, no wonder Lady shows mercy. The old woman will bring the lute tomorrow at dusk, temporarily humbling Xishi to pretend to be a servant of X, dirtying his face, changing his clothes, and carrying the lute to see Lady, is that acceptable?" Chang'er did not speak, seemingly agreeing. Understanding her meaning, the old woman stood up and left after a brief stay.

    233

    姮儿曰：“姆须识之勿忘，明日薄暮，务将琴来，切勿失信，劳我盼望。”媪点首者再，曰：“诺。”比归，具告奚生。生霍然兴曰：“我言何如？娘子命我，死且不敢辞，何况奴乎！”日昳，媪令奚生以土污面，衣以须捷，授以琴，负之而趋，俨然奚奴。由适园入，媪先见姮儿，问：“琴曾将来也未？”媪点首，招奚生入。奚生置琴几上，见姮儿淡妆靓服，病容满面，而光采照人，罄折欲拜，姮儿急止之，命坐。怜其为己不惜破衣垢面，不禁双泪承睫，顾素性英爽，寻即收泪，笑谓生曰：“君之痴情，妾已尽知之矣。

    Explanation

    233

    Heng'er said, "Mumu, remember this and do not forget. Tomorrow at dusk, surely bring the qin, do not break your word, and trouble me with my waiting." The old woman nodded twice and said, "Agreed." When she returned, she told everything to Xishi. Xishi stood up immediately and said, "What I said, how is it? The young lady commanded me, even if it means death, I would not dare to refuse, let alone a servant!" At noon, the old woman ordered Xishi to smear dirt on his face, dressed him in a specific type of clothing, gave him the qin, and carried it as he hurried, looking just like Xishi's servant. Entering through the garden, the old woman first saw Heng'er and asked, "Did you bring the qin?" The old woman nodded and invited Xishi to enter. Xishi placed the qin on the table and saw Heng'er with light makeup and elegant dress, a sickly appearance all over her face, but radiating a brilliant light. She wanted to bow deeply, but Heng'er quickly stopped him and ordered him to sit. She pitied him for not hesitating to wear torn clothes and a dirty face for her, and could not help tears falling from her eyelashes. But considering her usual brave and spirited nature, she soon stopped crying and smiled, saying to Xishi, "Your foolish affection, I have fully known it."

    234

    以君之才，宁长贫贱？天下美人，胜于妾者甚多，何患不有嘉耦？妾自知薄命，日来心绪恶劣，慵如中酒，病颇绵惙，其不能久于人世也必矣！君幸努力自爱，好自为之，何必抵死与人争髑髅哉！”生听姮儿言，泪下涔涔，方欲有言，忽侍儿报夫人至。姮儿大惊，急匿生复室中，自扶王媪出户相迎。夫人问：“几上何来一琴？”姮儿谓：“是王媪将来求售者，彼称是管夫人旧物，儿尚未审定。”夫人命将宋锦弢解开，就烛下谛审，见金徽玉轸，断纹甚好；又视其腹镌隶书两行云：“繄龙门兮无枝，妃玉轸兮冰丝。与子期兮静好，偕百年兮友之。”旁行楷书署款云：“皇庆元年中秋，天水子昂为仲姬夫人铭于沤波馆。”夫人赞曰：“铭字刻手俱好，的是魏公旧物无疑。魏公人品，虽不免后人訾议，然究不愧一代才人。

    Explanation

    234

    With your talent, how could you remain poor and lowly? There are many beautiful women in the world who are better than me. Why should you worry about not finding a good match? I know I have a short life. Recently, my mood has been terrible, I feel as lazy as if I were drunk, and I am quite ill. It is certain that I cannot live long in this world. You should take care of yourself and do well. Why should you die fighting with others over a skull? The dragon gate has no branches, the jade strings and ice silk are pure and refined. We promise to be together in peace, to be friends for a hundred years. In the first year of the Huangqing era during the Mid-Autumn Festival, Zishang of Tianshui inscribed this for Lady Zhongji at the Ouboguan. The lady praised, saying, "The inscription and the craftsmanship are both excellent; it is undoubtedly an old item of Wei Gong. Although Wei Gong's character is not without criticism from later generations, he still does not fail to be a talented person of his generation."

    235

    此物可留为妆奁之助，愿吾儿他日能效魏公夫妇，足矣！”顾谓王媪：“索价几何？我处付给。”媪笑曰：“诺。”夫人又与姮儿哓哓絮语，久之始去。漏已初下，宅门前后尽鐍。姮儿问王媪：“奚生在此，将焉置之？”媪曰：“事已知此，娘子不用忧虑，可暂藏婢女房中，老妇再伺隙携出。”姮儿无奈，只得命诸婢同伴己宿，即以婢房暂置奚生。姮儿待侍女素宽，诸婢乐为之用，凡事多不回避。时公冢子已由词馆晋大司成，远官京邸，闻妹已字某甲，心殊不慊。素敦友爱，又以妹系两亲爱女，特遣妻杜氏归，为妹料理嫁事。杜本岐公的裔，明察刚断，胜于男子。到家数日，见姮儿情状，心窃诧异，又闻某甲所为多不法，亦甚腹非翁姑鹵莽错配。

    Explanation

    235

    This item can be kept as an aid for the bridal trousseau. I hope my daughter will one day be able to emulate the Hui Gong couple, and that would be enough!" She then said to Wang Ao, "What is the price? I will pay for it." Wang Ao smiled and said, "Yes." The lady then talked at length with Heng'er, and they finally parted. The clock had already struck the first hour, and the front and back gates of the house were locked. Heng'er asked Wang Ao, "What shall we do with the scholar here?" Wang Ao said, "Since we know about this, Madam need not worry. You can temporarily hide him in the servant girl's room, and I will wait for an opportunity to carry him out." Heng'er had no choice but to order the maids to accompany her and sleep with her, and they temporarily placed the scholar in the servant girl's room. Heng'er treated her maid Su with great leniency, and the maids were happy to serve her, doing everything without any reservations. At that time, the eldest son of the family, who had already been promoted to the position of Grand Master of the Imperial Academy, was far away in the capital. He heard that his sister had been betrothed to someone, and he felt very displeased. She had always been close and loving, and because his sister was also a beloved daughter, he specially sent his wife Du back to arrange his sister's wedding affairs. Du was a descendant of the Qi Gong, clear-sighted and decisive, surpassing men. After arriving home for several days, she saw the state of Heng'er and secretly wondered. She also heard that Mr. Jia's actions were often unlawful, and she greatly disapproved of the elders' hasty and mistaken arrangement.

    236

    偶至姮儿处，适奚生在婢房开半窗外窥，见杜至，遽掩其窗，杜眼明，已瞥见之。默谓姮儿素读书以节义自许，何忽有此暖昧事？殊切惊疑。姮儿素与杜极相得，见杜至，立身含笑，杜执手慰问：“近日眠食如何？”姮儿笑曰：“不过尔尔。”杜见王媪笑曰：“我家小姑子好期在迩，未免难舍两大人膝下，汝来作伴解闷亦大好。”媪笑曰：“唯唯。”杜见房中图书满架，案上一帙，恰是姮儿以乌阑手写蝇头小楷《自选唐人乐府》，内夹近作一首，是《拟李长吉〈宫娃歌〉并次原韵》，云：“捧心一顾粉黛空，先施要宠压六宫。凝脂中酒白玉暖，莺儿教歌蝶拍板。欢娱不足忘朝昏，纡纡新月愁眉痕。烟波一舸谁曾见？好事诬同赋感甄。满溪香水枯春渚，响廊荒草铺路。不如老浣越中沙，白头不到吴中去。

    Explanation

    236

    I happened upon Heng'er's place, and just then, Xi-sheng was peeking out of a half-open window from the servant's room. Upon seeing Du arrive, he quickly closed the window. Du, with his sharp eyes, had already caught a glimpse of him. Silently, he thought to himself, "Heng'er always reads books and considers herself to uphold righteousness and propriety; how could she suddenly engage in such a secretive matter? It truly surprises and puzzles me." Heng'er usually got along very well with Du. When she saw Du arrive, she stood up with a smile. Du held her hand and comforted her, asking, "How have your sleep and meals been lately?" Heng'er smiled and replied, "Nothing special." Du then saw Wang-ao and said with a smile, "My younger sister-in-law is due to arrive soon, and it's hard to part with the two elders. It would be great if you came to keep me company and relieve my boredom." Wang-ao smiled and said, "Yes, yes." Du noticed that the room was filled with books on the shelves, and there was a volume on the table, which happened to be Heng'er's handwritten, small, fine calligraphy of "Self-Selected Tang Dynasty Lyrics," containing a recent work titled "Imitating Li Changji's 'Palace Maid Song' and Following the Original Rhyme Scheme," which reads: "Turning her head with a smile, the makeup and cosmetics are in vain; the first to offer favors seeks favor and surpasses all the concubines. Fatty flesh affected by wine, warm as white jade; the oriole teaches singing, the butterfly beats the drum. Joy is insufficient to forget day and night; the new moon slowly traces the frowning brow. Who has ever seen a boat in the misty waves? Kind-hearted people falsely attribute it to a feeling of gratitude. The stream is full of fragrant water, dried up spring bank; the echoing corridor is overgrown with grass covering the path. Not as good as washing old clothes in Yue, white hair not reaching Wu."

    237

    ”杜氏阅毕，又信手一翻，是张文昌《节妇吟》，见通首丹黄，起四句密圈，上二句旁评云：“既知有夫，似可不赠珠矣；偏赠珠以表其情，可谓痴绝，然不可不谓知己！”下二句旁评云：“既知有夫，似可不接珠矣；乃感其缠绵之意，暂且系之，可见人生不外一情，虽节妇一时亦难恝然拒绝，亦以知己难得也！”中四句单圈，旁评云：“四句凑泊无理！良人既非庸流，尚贪与人絮语，有愧罗敷多矣！”末二句密圈，旁评云：“赖有此耳，马到悬崖，不得不勒，然亦无可奈何时也！”总评云：“此妇已嫁，犹与外人殷殷通词，将置良人于何地？作者且以节字标目，可见古人之恕。尝见世有男才女貌，往往限于门第而不能如愿者，处此境地，尤要确有把持，所谓发乎情，至乎礼义也。司业此诗，大约有为而言，究不可以为训。

    Explanation

    237

    After finishing his reading, Du Shi casually flipped through and found Zhang Wenchang's "Song of the Chaste Wife." He saw that the entire poem was marked throughout with red and yellow annotations. The first four lines were marked with closely spaced circles, and the first two lines had marginal comments:

    "Knowing she has a husband, she should not have given the pearl; yet she gave it to express her feelings, which is utterly foolish, but also shows deep understanding!"

    The next two lines also had marginal comments:

    "Knowing she has a husband, she should not have accepted the pearl; yet she did so out of emotional attachment, showing that even a chaste wife finds it hard to refuse someone she deeply understands."

    The middle four lines were marked with single circles and had marginal comments:

    "These four lines are illogical! Her husband is no common man, yet he still engages in idle chatter, which is shameful compared to Luo Fu!"

    The last two lines were marked with closely spaced circles and had marginal comments:

    "Thankfully, these lines save the poem. It's like a horse reaching a cliff, where one must stop, even if it is helpless!"

    The overall evaluation of the poem stated:

    "This woman, already married, still communicates earnestly with an outsider, leaving her husband in a difficult position. The author uses the term 'chaste' as a title, showing ancient people's leniency. I have often seen talented men and beautiful women who are constrained by social class and cannot achieve their desires. In such a situation, one must have firm self-restraint, as the saying goes, 'to express emotions but to reach the level of propriety and righteousness.' This poem by the examiner was likely written for a specific purpose, but it cannot be taken as a model or lesson."

    238

    ”杜氏读所拟近作并细味评语，见姮儿立论正大，当不至于苟且，因借以风之曰：“适读贤妹大作，为先施翻案极妙，不知果有说乎？”姮儿笑曰：“据《春秋三传》、《国语》，先施本不知所终，以有裹鸱夷沉江之说，后人便附会偕鸱夷泛五湖矣。即《洛神赋》而论，不过陈思脱胎宋玉《神女》、《好色》等赋，偶尔遣兴。留枕之说，荒谬不经，考阿甄与陈思年齿悬殊，况魏文猜忌异常，陈思避嫌不暇，敢赋《感甄》乎？才人信口雌黄，可恨可畏。然二人亦自有瑕可摘，如先施果是范大夫妻，即不当再事吴王；阿甄既为袁妇，既不当再适曹氏。大抵女子须要守礼谨严，稍失防检，即不免后人唐突，是不可以不慎。”杜氏听姬儿所论，殊深欣佩，因之又谓：“贤妹大作命意之旨既闻命矣，敢问所评《节妇吟》，文昌以节许之，名果能称实乎？”姮儿笑曰：“此妇妙在多情而不肯失身，守得身住，便是守得节住。

    238

    Du read her recent works and carefully considered the comments, observing that Heeng's arguments were broad and just, and therefore used it to subtly advise her, saying: "I just read your sister's major work, which overturned the case of Xian Shi in an extremely wonderful way. But does she have a reason for it?" Heeng smiled and said: "According to the 'Three Transmissions of the Spring and Autumn Annals' and the 'Discourses of the States,' Xian Shi originally had no known end, but there is a saying that she was wrapped in a leather bag and sunk in the river. Later people attached themselves to the story of Xian Shi floating on the Five Lakes with a leather bag. Considering the 'Ode to the Goddess of the Luo River,' it is merely a derivative of Cao Zhi's 'Ode to the Goddess of the Luo River' and other works by Song Yu, written occasionally to express one's feelings. The story of leaving a pillow is absurd and unverifiable. Considering the significant age difference between A Zhen and Cao Zhi, and especially the excessive suspicion of Wei Wen, Cao Zhi would not have time to avoid suspicion, how dare he write the 'Ode to A Zhen'? Talented people speak without evidence, which is hateful and frightening. However, both of them also have flaws that can be pointed out. For example, if Xian Shi was indeed the wife of Fan Da, she should not have served King Wu of Wu again; A Zhen, having been the wife of Yuan, should not have remarried to Cao. Generally speaking, women must strictly adhere to etiquette. If they slightly fail to guard themselves, they will inevitably be treated rudely by later people, which cannot be taken lightly." Du listened to Heeng's argument and deeply admired it, and then said: "I have heard the intention behind your sister's major work, but I dare to ask about the evaluation of the 'Song of the Chaste Wife.' Wenchang has praised her for her chastity, does her name truly match her virtue?" Heeng smiled and said: "This woman's charm lies in her being affectionate but not willing to lose her virtue. To keep one's body intact is to keep one's virtue intact."

    239

    ”曰：“然则古人所谓‘内言不出，外言不入’，以此妇律之，毋乃过乎？”曰：“此为泛泛者言之也。若彼此亦既觏止，两相慕悦，外言无翼自能飞入，内言无翼自能飞出。大抵声应气求，直如好友，虽男女异体，亦各忘形；即占同心，即期聚首，情之所钟，真如针芥相投，固结莫解，并非贪人欲之私，贱等淫奔也。即有时情不能禁，偶越范围，必须用力操持，谨守分际，昕夕觌面，俨对大宾；偶一失足，男则狂且，女则荡妇。老子云：‘不见所欲，则其心不乱。’是不可不慎而又慎也。”杜氏听姮儿所言，已窥大意，不禁默默叹惋。乃屏去侍女，悄谓姮儿曰：“贤妹好期已近，非愚嫂妄论，两大人择婿，未免太失检察，奈何！”姮儿闻之，泪下如雨。杜慰之曰：“贤妹不必伤感，如可斡旋，愚嫂必肯效力。”姮儿见杜氏直抉其隐，默自惊异，不觉红晕两颊，益增悲哽。

    Explanation

    239

    She replied, "Then, the ancients' saying 'words inside do not go out, words outside do not come in,' used to govern women, isn't this excessive?" She said, "This is a general guideline. If both parties have already met and come to know each other, and they mutually admire and love each other, external words, without wings, can still fly in, and internal words, without wings, can still fly out. Generally, echoing sounds and seeking breath, it's just like good friends, even though they are different in gender, they also forget their form; if they share the same heart, if they expect to meet, where affection is concentrated, it's truly like a needle and a piece of grass attracting each other, firmly bound and not easily dissolved, not driven by personal desire, degrading promiscuity. Even if at times emotions cannot be restrained, and they occasionally go beyond the boundaries, they must exert effort to control, and strictly adhere to their roles, facing each other morning and evening, as if in the presence of honored guests; if they ever falter, the man becomes a madman, and the woman becomes a promiscuous woman. The Dao De Jing says, 'If one does not see what one desires, then one's mind will not be disturbed.' It is necessary to be cautious and cautious again." Respecting her words, Du had already grasped the main idea, and could not help but silently sigh. She then dismissed the maidservants and quietly said to Chang'er, "Your good time is approaching. It is not that I am making a rash judgment, but the two elders in choosing a husband for you have been overly careless in their inspection. What to do?" Chang'er heard this and tears streamed down her face like rain. Du comforted her, saying, "Dearest sister, you need not grieve. If it can be arranged, I, your foolish elder sister, will certainly help." Chang'er saw that Du was directly uncovering the hidden, and silently amazed, she could not help but blush, and her sadness increased.

    240

    杜曲为劝慰，笑曰：“愚嫂归来，俗冗纷纠，家中房舍多未能到，未审贤妹住屋共几楹也？”姬儿谓：“卧房及婢女所居共八楹。”杜氏故左右周览，信步至婢房前，反手试搴其帷，蓦见奚生，大惊，回首问姮儿：“此何人也？”姮儿以杜前言有因，意已罄悉底蕴，当不媒蘖，乃靦覥直答曰：“此奚生也。”并具告崖末，且谓住此业经三日，无隙可出，如有苟且，神明共殛，惟嫂氏察之。杜氏习闻奚生之才，及详度其仪容举止，的是不凡，默叹姮儿鉴赏有真。又恐因羞致变，乃慰之曰：“贤妹独具特识，如欲缔逑好，久留在此，究非善策，宜速为计。”姮儿含羞答曰：“妹方寸已乱，惟嫂所命。”杜知姮儿之意已决，素稔王媪是姮儿心腹，独召媪至，附耳授计，趣其速归；又正色谓奚生曰：“妾为君事煞费经营，君宜努力进取，为闺中人生色，切勿有负！”奚生感泣再拜，指天信誓。漏初下，杜计王媪已将车至，预遣去适园纪纲人等，悉召姮儿身

    Explanation

    240

    Du Qu comforted her with a smile and said, "My foolish elder sister has returned, and the mundane and complicated matters are intertwined. Many of the rooms in the house have not been visited. I wonder how many rooms your honorable sister lives in?" The young lady replied, "The bedroom and the quarters of the maidservants together total eight rooms." Du Shi deliberately looked around left and right, strolled leisurely to the maidservants' room, reached back with her hand to try to lift the curtain, and suddenly saw Xishi. She was greatly surprised, turned her head, and asked Chang'er, "Who is this person?" Chang'er thought Du Shi's previous words had a reason and she already understood the underlying situation. Therefore, she would not fabricate or exaggerate. She blushed and directly replied, "This is Xishi." She fully informed about the details and said that he has been staying here for three days, there is no opportunity to leave, if there is any impropriety, the gods will punish, only my elder sister can judge. Du Shi was familiar with Xishi's talent and carefully observed his appearance and behavior. Truly extraordinary, she silently praised Chang'er for her genuine appreciation. Fearing that shame might lead to complications, she comforted her by saying, "Your honorable sister has unique insight. If you wish to form a good match, staying here for a long time is not a good strategy. You should make a quick plan." Chang'er replied with embarrassment, "My heart is already in turmoil. Only as my elder sister commands." Du Shi knew Chang'er's mind was made up. She always knew that Wang Ao was Chang'er's confidant. She called Wang Ao alone, whispered a plan into her ear, urged her to return quickly; and spoke sternly to Xishi, "I have spent a lot of effort on your behalf. You should strive to advance, to bring glory to the women in the inner chambers. Do not let me down!" Xishi was moved to tears and bowed twice, swearing by the heavens. As the first drop of water from the water clock fell, Du Shi calculated that Wang Ao's carriage would arrive. Previously sent away the attendants from the garden, she summoned Chang'er completely.

    241

    旁妪婢至己房中，命姮儿结束，略带金珠钗饰，由适园与奚生偕遁。姮儿故有四婢，长名木难，年十四，目听眉语，素解主人意，亦命俱去。又以千金付王媪，留为二人食用之资。杜一一处分已，携姮儿手叮咛赠语。姮儿挥泪裣衽再拜而别，一时竟无一人知者。漏二下，杜命稽察门户，严加鐍键。漏三下，忽报姮儿住房火起，俄顷烈焰炽天，举室惊惶，群争扑灭，而八楹已成灰烬。幸间架不与他屋毗连，尚未延烧别院，惟姮儿未曾拯出，木难尸亦俱燬。公与夫人悲恸欲绝，杜氏再三劝慰乃已。某甲方准备亲迎，忽得此信，大失所望，日惟沉溺勾栏，藉以排解，亡何，而东楼之祸作矣。

    Explanation

    241

    An elderly woman and her maids entered姮儿's room, ordering her to dress up and adorn herself lightly with gold, pearls, and hairpins. Together with 奚生, they left the garden to escape.姮儿 originally had four maids, the eldest named 木难, who was fourteen years old. She could read the master's intentions through her eyes and eyebrows and was also ordered to leave. Additionally, a thousand gold pieces were given to 王媪, to be kept as food expenses for the two of them.杜某 had settled everything, taking姮儿's hand and giving her parting words.姮儿 wiped away tears, bowed deeply twice, and parted, and at that moment, no one else knew of their departure. At two o'clock,杜 ordered the inspection of doors and locks, and they were strictly secured. At three o'clock, suddenly there was a report that姮儿's room was on fire. In a moment, the flames reached the sky, and the whole household was in panic, rushing to extinguish the fire. The eight rooms had already turned to ashes. Fortunately, the structure did not connect to other houses, so it had not yet spread to other courtyards. But姮儿 was not rescued, and 木难's body was also destroyed. The master and his wife were grief-stricken and almost died, and杜氏 repeatedly consoled them until they calmed down.某甲 was preparing for the wedding, but suddenly received this news and was greatly disappointed. Day by day, he immersed himself in brothels, using it as a way to relieve his distress. Not long after, the disaster at East Tower occurred.

    242

    初，某甲藉父势，在乡无恶不作，会直指使者巡方过此，叩马鸣冤者数百人。直指素有包老之称，阅词大怒，据实一一封章入告，并劾其父纳贿鬻爵数条，确有左证。朝廷震怒，即日降旨，削其父子爵，远戍烟瘴充军，沿途不得逗留，所有家产一概籍没入官。某甲在路，恶创溃发，寻斃；冢宰公老年恸子，兼以跋涉劳顿，未几亦殒，一家竟无噍类矣。姮儿既偕奚生出亡，自携木难与王媪，在穷乡买屋一所，竹篱茅舍，荆布自甘，王媪伪称为甥女，见者但诧其美，而不知其为女公子也。奚生仍居妗氏家，偶来与姮儿相见，亲如兄弟，敬如朋友，一言不敢狎亵。以感姮儿知己，惟恐有负，下帷攻苦，连战俱捷，廷试得馆选，授翰林院编修，乞假回籍完姻。是科主试官六人，公冢子已晋少宰预焉。

    Explanation

    242

    In the beginning, a certain individual, relying on his father's influence, committed all kinds of evils in the village. When the high-ranking inspector, who was known for his integrity and justice, toured the region and passed through, hundreds of people stopped his horse to complain. The inspector, who was known as "Mr. Bao" for his uprightness, read the complaints and became furious. He reported the facts in a sealed memorial to the emperor, accusing the individual's father of accepting bribes and selling official positions, with clear evidence.
