The court was greatly angered and immediately issued an edict, stripping both the father and son of their titles, exiling them to a distant, smoky, and malarial region as soldiers, not allowing them to stop along the way, and confiscating all their property. On the journey, the individual's sores burst open, and he soon died. The Grand Tutor, in his old age, grieved for his son and, exhausted from the long journey, soon died as well. Thus, the entire family had no survivors.

    The daughter of the deceased, named Heng'er, fled with her brother Xi Sheng. She carried her jade and accompanied Madam Wang to a remote village, where they bought a hut with a bamboo fence and thatched roof, content with a simple life. Madam Wang pretended to be her aunt, and those who saw her were only amazed by her beauty, unaware that she was a noble lady. Xi Sheng continued to live with his aunt and occasionally visited Heng'er, treating her as if she were his sister and respecting her as a friend, never daring to be overly familiar. Inspired by Heng'er's understanding and support, Xi Sheng studied diligently, achieving success in every exam. He passed the imperial examination and was selected for a post in the imperial library, eventually becoming an editor in the Hanlin Academy. He requested leave to return home to marry. That year, the chief examiners were six in number, and the eldest son of the official had already been promoted to the position of Junior Minister of State.

    243

    以与奚生同里，谒见时倍觉亲洽，闻公旧有东床意，未免枨触同怀之情，悼念己妹薄命惨死；又念某冢宰父子如此结局，妹若在更难为情，反以早死为幸。当奚生旋里，少宰托带家书中，盛夸奚生才品俱优，自庆得人，且属公为之留意执柯。姮儿自奚生计偕北上，日闭门焚香，鼓所购管夫人旧琴，聊以消遣。间或教木难下棋；或遇花开时，对花写画数笔；或作诗填词，以抒怀抱。一日，读老杜《佳人》五古一篇，反复披吟，不胜感慨，因次韵咏怀并忆嫂氏杜少薇夫人，云：“涓涓涧底泉，曲流恋山谷。翩翩枝头鸟，回翔择林木。我生丁不辰，入险胜遭戮。萦缴巧相伺，飞土嗟逐肉。赖得嫂氏贤，明察幽隐烛。翼我出网罗，璞守无瑕玉。岩居等寄巢，戢羽暂栖宿。

    Explanation

    243

    Because I am from the same hometown as Xishi, when I met him, I felt an even closer connection. Hearing that you once had feelings for him, it could not help but touch the same feelings of shared sorrow, mourning my younger sister’s tragic and untimely death. I also thought about the unfortunate fate of a certain Vice-Chancellor and his son, and if my sister were still alive, it would be even more difficult for her. Instead, I consider her early death a blessing. When Xishi returned to our hometown, the Vice-Chancellor entrusted me with a letter to deliver, in which he highly praised Xishi’s talents and character, celebrating having found a worthy person, and asked you to pay attention to arranging the marriage. Heng'er, since Xishi planned to go north, she closed herself in her room and burned incense, playing the old guqin she had purchased from Madam Guan, just to pass the time. Sometimes she taught the difficult art of chess; when flowers bloomed, she would paint a few strokes in front of them; or she would write poems and fill in lyrics to express her feelings. One day, she read Du Fu’s five-character ancient poem "The Beautiful Lady," repeating it over and over, unable to contain her emotions. She then composed a poem in the same rhyme scheme to express her feelings and remember her sister-in-law, Du Shaowei, saying: "A gentle stream at the bottom of a ravine, meandering and lingering in the valley. A graceful bird perched on a branch, flitting and choosing among the trees. I was born in an unlucky time, entering danger and narrowly escaping death. Traps and snares are cleverly set, flying arrows and lamenting the pursuit of flesh. Fortunately, my sister-in-law is virtuous, having a clear understanding of hidden things. She helped me escape the nets, protecting me like a flawless jade. Living in caves is like a temporary nest, tucking away my wings for a temporary rest."

    244

    不寐思两亲，掩袂吞声哭。我心盟白水，肯污浊泥浊？啸歌聊自娱，不隘一橡屋。课婢时种花，笑摘香满掬。平生儿女泪，羞洒潇湘竹。”脱稿复自讽玩，颇自得意。忽王媪与木难从外联袂趋入，大笑称“贺喜”，谓：“顷妗氏传言，奚生已授编修，乞假归娶，不日可到。”姮儿闻之，心窃自贺。及奚生归，先使人报知姮儿；既谒某公，执礼甚恭，袖出少宰家书。公阅之，不胜叹息。送奚生出，归与夫人言及少宰家报，意似怨夫人当日失计，杜氏在旁闻之笑曰：“恭贺两大人，小姑固无恙。今某甲家靡有孑遗，正好鸾胶重续，请勿嗟怨。”公与夫人相视愕眙。杜笑言当日火灾故己所为，乃备述缕。公与夫人大喜，先迎姮儿归，风示奚生遣媒纳聘，涓日合卺如礼。

    Explanation

    244

    Sleepless, I think of my two parents, covering my sleeves and swallowing my sobs. My heart has vowed to the pure White Water—how could it soil itself in muddy filth? I howl and sing to amuse myself, unconfined by a single thatched hut. I teach my servant to plant flowers now and then, laughing as I pluck fragrant blossoms, filling both hands with their scent. The tears of a lifetime, the tears of a child—I am ashamed to let them fall upon the bamboo of the Xiaoxiang.

    After finishing the draft, she read it to herself, finding it amusing, and felt quite pleased with herself. Suddenly, Lady Wang and Mu Nan entered from outside, linking arms and laughing loudly, exclaiming, “Congratulations!” They said, “Not long ago, the mother-in-law’s side spread the news that Xi Sheng has been appointed as a compiler, requested leave to return home and marry, and will arrive soon.” When Heng'er heard this, her heart secretly rejoiced.

    When Xi Sheng returned, he first sent someone to inform Heng'er; after paying respects to a certain official, he observed the rites with great reverence, pulling out a letter from the Minister of the Imperial Household. The official read it and could not help but sigh deeply. After sending Xi Sheng out, he returned home and spoke with his wife about the news from the Minister’s household, seeming to reproach her for her earlier miscalculation. Du, who was present, heard this and laughed, saying, “Congratulations to the two of you, my elders. My younger sister is indeed unharmed. Now, the family of Mr. Ji has no survivors left—this is the perfect time to rekindle the phoenix bond. Please do not lament.”

    The husband and wife looked at each other in astonishment. Du laughed and said that the fire that day had been her own doing, then carefully recounted the details. The husband and wife were overjoyed, and first sent for Heng'er to return home, then ordered Xi Sheng to send a matchmaker and present a marriage proposal, choosing an auspicious day to wed in accordance with the rites.

    245

    里乘子曰：《聊斋》有云：“天生佳丽，将以报名贤；而世俗王公，偏留以赠纨婿。”千古一辄，牢不可破，良可浩叹。奚生密迩佳人，亲敬不敢狎亵，即此一节，不愧名贤；少宰赞其才品俱优，信非溢誉。姮娘材艺无不擅长，读诗两首，窥豹一斑，偶尔发言，亦皆入妙，诚闺阁翘楚之尤。某公择非不知破格求才，惜为阃内所持，几至名花堕溷。幸杜少薇夫人智能应变，卒使淑女君子得遂好逑，只手回天，不露声色，其筹画尽善，即方之古押衙，何多让哉！
    　　
    　　附录：宫娃歌 李贺
    　　
    　　蜡光高悬照纱空，花房夜捣红守宫。象口吹香毾氊暖，七星挂城闻漏板。寒入罘罳殿影昏，彩鸾帘额着霜痕。啼蛄吊月钩栏下，屈膝铜铺锁阿甄。梦入家门上沙渚，天河落处长洲路。愿君光明如太阳，放妾骑鱼撇波去。

    Explanation

    245

    Lǐchéngzǐ said: In Liaozhai Zhiyi, it is written: "Heaven creates beautiful women to name the virtuous; yet the worldly nobles and officials tend to reserve such beauty for their idle sons-in-law." This rule has been followed for millennia and is firmly established, truly worthy of deep sighs. Xí Shēng, who is close to the beautiful woman, respects her and does not act inappropriately. In this regard, he does not disgrace the reputation of a virtuous person; the Minister of Justice praises his talent and character as both excellent, and this is not an exaggeration. Héng Niáng's skills and arts are all highly proficient. Reading two poems gives a glimpse of her talent, like seeing a tiger through a small hole. Occasionally, her words are also profound, truly the most outstanding among women. The official, who is not unaware of the need to break conventions in seeking talent, was held back by his inner circle, nearly leading to a beautiful flower being cast into filth. Fortunately, Madam Dù Shàowēi, with her intelligence and ability to adapt, ultimately allowed the virtuous woman and gentleman to achieve their ideal match, changing the course of fate with one hand, without revealing her actions, her planning was flawless. Even compared to Gu Ya Ya, what more could she yield?

    Appendix: Gōngwá Gē by Li He

    Candlelight hangs high, illuminating the gauzy space; in the flower room, red substances are ground at night. Fragrant carpets warm as if blown by an elephant's mouth; seven stars hang over the city, and the sound of the clock mechanism is heard. Cold seeps into the curtain, dimming the palace's shadow; colorful phoenix curtains bear frost marks. A cricket sings under the curved railing, mourning the moon; a locked gate with a kneeling copper latch confines A Zhēn. In a dream, she enters her home on a sandy bank; the road where the Milky Way falls is the path of the long isle. May you be as bright as the sun, and let me ride a fish to leave the waves behind.

    246

    节妇吟 张籍
    　　
    　　寄东平李司空师道君知妾有夫，赠妾双明珠。感君缠绵意，系在红罗襦。妾家高楼连苑起，良人执戟明光里。知君用心如日月，事夫誓拟同生死。还君明珠双泪垂，何不相逢未嫁时！[《容斋随笔》：“籍在他镇幕府，郸帅李师道又以书币聘之，籍却不纳，作此寄之。徐太史倬谓：按词意婉转，恐非节妇意也，宜以本事为题，则得风人之意。”]
    　　
    　　佳人 杜甫
    　　
    　　绝代有佳人，幽居在空谷。自云良家子，零落归草木。关中昔丧乱，兄弟遭杀戮。官高何足论，不得收骨肉。世情恶衰歇，万事随转烛。夫婿轻薄儿，新人已如玉。合昏尚知时，鸳鸯不独宿。但见新人笑，那闻旧人哭。在山泉水清，出山泉水浊。侍婢卖珠还，牵萝补茅屋。摘花不插发，采柏动盈掬。天寒翠袖薄，日暮倚修竹。

    Explanation

    246

    节妇吟 张籍**

    To the Eastping Li Sike Shidao

    You know I have a husband, yet you give me twin pearls.
    I am moved by your affectionate feelings, which I tie to my red silk robe.
    My home is a high tower connected to a garden, and my husband holds a halberd in the prestigious Mingguang district.
    I know your intentions are as clear as the sun and moon, and I swear to share life and death with my husband.
    Returning your pearls, tears fall from my eyes; why didn't we meet when I was unmarried?
    [From Rongzhai Suiyi: "Ji was in the governor's office of another town, and the general Li Shidao of Danyang sent him books and gifts to invite him, but Ji refused and wrote this poem to send to him. Xu Taishi Zhuo said: According to the meaning of the poem, it seems not to be about a chaste wife, but rather the actual situation. If it were titled after the actual events, it would better convey the meaning of the poet."]

    佳人 杜甫

    A Peerless Beauty

    There is a peerless beauty, living in seclusion in an empty valley.
    She says she is from a respectable family, but now she has fallen like fallen leaves or flowers.
    In the past, the Guanzhong region was in chaos, and her brothers were killed.
    What is the point of a high official position if one cannot even recover the remains of one's family?
    The world's ways hate decline and decay, and all things change like a candle flame.
    Her husband is a frivolous man, and a new wife is already as beautiful as jade.
    The plant that closes its leaves at night still knows the time, and the mandarin ducks do not sleep alone.
    All one sees is the new wife's laughter, and no one hears the old wife's tears.
    Water in the mountains is clear, but water outside the mountains is turbid.
    The servant sold jewelry to repay a debt, and the woman patched her thatched hut with vines.
    She picked flowers but did not wear them in her hair, and she picked up a handful of pine branches.
    On a cold day, her green sleeves are thin, and in the evening, she leans against tall bamboo.

    247

    某太史鬼求代
    　　
    　　京师某太史，情重前鱼，终岁不御妻妾，但狎优伶。尝有友招饮，忽遭优伶所戏侮，为坐客姗笑，羞忿自经，其鬼求代。初，正阳门外某生远游，其妻独居，家小阜。妻兄弟素无赖，时来称贷，妻颇厌之，恒不能遂其所欲。一日，弟乙以有急，又来求姊，会姊往亲戚家，待至薄暮甫归，厕身暗陬，窥姐下车，身后随一美少年，相将入房，大骇，以姊有所私，心殊耻其所为。继思藉此有所挟，计亦得，爰潜身蹑足入，伏窗窥姊坐灯下，面频蹙，若有忧色，少年偎姊身旁，低声耳语，隐不可辨。姊危坐自若，少年或左之或右之，或长揖而跽恳之，丑态百出。无何，更鼓二报，少年似益急迫，跽恳益数。姊意似首肯，起拭泪至案前，挑灯启镜奁，薄加脂粉，转身坐榻上，小声嘤嘤啜泣。

    Explanation

    247

    A Certain Historian's Ghost Seeks Replacement

    In the capital, there was a certain historian who was deeply attached to a past lover, abstaining from his wives and concubines all year long, and only indulging in relationships with actors and musicians. Once, a friend invited him to a drinking party, where he was suddenly mocked and insulted by an actor, and laughed at by the other guests. Ashamed and furious, he hanged himself, and his ghost sought to be replaced. At first, a certain scholar was on a long journey outside the Zhengyang Gate, and his wife was left alone at home, where the household was prosperous. The wife's brothers were usually idle and worthless, often coming to borrow money, which she found quite annoying and could never fully satisfy their demands. One day, her brother Yi came again, claiming an urgent need, and went to ask his sister. Coincidentally, the sister had gone to visit relatives, and he waited until dusk before she returned. He hid himself in a dark corner, watching as his sister got out of the carriage, with a handsome young man following her into the house. He was greatly alarmed, thinking that his sister had an illicit affair, and was particularly ashamed of what they did. Then, he thought of using this as leverage, and the plan also succeeded. He secretly entered, tiptoeing, and crouched by the window, peering at his sister sitting by the lamp. Her face was often furrowed, showing signs of worry. The young man leaned close to her sister, whispering softly in her ear, but it was unclear what they were saying. The sister sat upright, calm and composed, while the young man moved left or right, or bowed deeply and knelt to plead with her, displaying many ugly and improper behaviors. After a while, the second drum beat of the night watch sounded, and the young man seemed even more urgent, kneeling and pleading even more frequently. It seemed that the sister agreed, and she stood up, wiped her tears, and went to the desk. She lit the lamp and opened the makeup box, lightly applied makeup, turned around, and sat on the bed, sobbing softly.

    248

    少年频为拭面而殷情之，便见姊起身解带挂梁上，少年不禁狂喜，或拊掌，或踊足，或伏地雀啄，笑容可掬。乙莫喻其故，既见姊上榻向外跪，少年笑援梁上带授之，姊引带纳项下，意将投缳。乙骇甚，始悟少年非人，系缢鬼之求代者。乃大声疾呼：“有鬼！”时甫二更，市上行人尚众，闻声毕至，佐乙破扉入房。乙急解其缳，放姊卧榻上，意甚痴，默不一语，灌以姜汤，顿苏。而市人至者益夥，屋狭，鬼皇遽不得出，侧身引避，形嵌壁上，宛然写照。有识者谛视之，诧曰：“是某太史也。”佥称怪事。太史家闻之，争来濯洗，竟不能去。急延僧讽诵经忏，日以法水祓除，匝月方灭其迹。后某生归，诘妻前事，则曰：“自君之出，意忽忽如有所失。他日自某家归，觉耳畔有人，极称生愁不如死乐，不觉心动，入其彀中。实其时身亦不能自主也。”某生夫妇从此德乙，有无遂常相通云。

    Explanation

    248

    The young man frequently wiped his face and showed deep affection, and soon the narrator saw his sister get up, untie her sash, and hang it on the beam. The young man could not help but be wildly delighted, clapping his hands, jumping with his feet, and crouching on the ground and pecking like a sparrow, with a smile that could be held in one's hands. The narrator did not understand the reason, but when he saw his sister climb onto the bed and kneel facing outward, the young man laughed and handed her the sash from the beam, which she then placed around her neck, intending to hang herself. The narrator was greatly startled and suddenly realized that the young man was not human but a hanged ghost seeking a replacement. He then shouted loudly, "There's a ghost!" It was just after the second watch, and there were still many people on the streets who heard the cry and rushed to help the narrator break down the door and enter the room. The narrator quickly untied the sash, laid his sister on the bed, and was deeply moved, saying nothing, and poured ginger soup, which caused her to suddenly come to. The crowd that came grew larger, and the room was narrow, so the ghost could not escape and had to turn sideways and hide, its form embedded in the wall, looking exactly like a portrait. Those who knew the truth carefully examined it and exclaimed in surprise, "It's a certain historian!" All called it a strange event. The historian's family heard about it and rushed to wash away the ghost, but they could not get rid of it. They quickly invited monks to recite sutras and repentance texts, and daily used sacred water to purify the place, and after a month, the trace finally disappeared. Later, the narrator returned home and asked his wife about the previous events, and she said, "Since you left, I felt distracted as if something were missing. One day, returning from a certain house, I heard someone whispering by my ear, praising the idea that life's troubles are less pleasant than death's peace, and I was so moved that I fell into its trap. In fact, at that time, I was also unable to control myself." From then on, the narrator and his wife were grateful to the man, and their relationship became close.

    249

    里乘子曰：往予游京师，友人招饮，座客无不各召歌童侑酒，否则即不合时宜，举座皆为不欢。而歌童闻召即至者，主人意气洋洋，如膺九锡；脱有再三候之不至者，座客辄冷语相诮，主人颜色惨沮，坐立不安，观其盼望情切，直不减于秀才望榜。恶习相沿，牢不可破，真为咄咄怪事！至若太史为优伶死，尤为绝无仅有，可补《情史》之缺。予尝谓：烈士殉名，荡子殉情。情之所钟，正在我辈。古今忠孝节义，皆不外一情宇，但恐人误用其情耳。以堂堂太史，屏妻妾而割骨肉之爱，昵优伶而乘阴阳之和，下流忘反，已属有玷清班；而一遭戏侮，即不惜以身殉之，亦何性命鸿毛乃尔耶！乃生既包羞，死犹无赖，以求代故，几波及无辜妇女，卒至壁上留形，万目攒视，互相传述，以为笑谈；幸菩萨有灵，法水洗垢，不然光天化日之下，千古遗此奇迹，岂不于贰负之臣外，又添一话柄也哉！

    Explanation

    249

    The scholar from the village said: In the past, I traveled to the capital city, and a friend invited me to a banquet. All the guests summoned singing boys to entertain with wine, otherwise it would be out of fashion, and the entire gathering would be unhappy. Those singing boys who came immediately upon being summoned, the host was full of pride, as if receiving the nine bestowals; if they were repeatedly summoned but did not come, the guests would coldly ridicule him, the host's face turned pale and he sat or stood uneasily, to see how eagerly he looked forward to it, it was no less than a scholar waiting for the imperial examination results. This bad habit has been passed down and is firmly entrenched, it is truly a strange and alarming thing! As for the historian who died for an actor, it is extremely rare and can fill a gap in the History of Emotions. I have often said: a hero sacrifices his life for his reputation, a libertine sacrifices his life for love. Where passion is concentrated, it is among us. Loyalty, filial piety, righteousness, and integrity in ancient and modern times are all encompassed by the word 'emotion,' but one fears that people misuse their emotions. A distinguished historian, who shunned his wife and concubines and cut off his love for his family, became close to actors and indulged in the harmony of yin and yang, forgot his high status and became involved with lowly people, which tarnished his reputation; and when he was mocked, he was not afraid to sacrifice himself, how could he value his life so lightly! He lived in shame and died in disgrace, in order to replace him, almost innocent women were implicated, finally, his image was left on the wall, and everyone stared at it, they told each other about it and made it a laughing matter; fortunately, the Bodhisattva was merciful and cleansed the stain, otherwise, in broad daylight, this miracle would be left for thousands of years, wouldn't this add another talking point to the list of disgraced officials.

    250

    柯寿鞠
    　　
    　　柯寿鞠，字丹慧，广陵乐工女也。其大母八十诞辰，梦女冠持赠丹菊一枝为寿，翌辰女生，遂以名之。髫龀失怙恃，叔无赖，鬻入勾栏中。六七岁，闻人诵诗，窃爱之，见文士即求指授，一听了了。十岁初度，口占一绝云：“戏控青鸾下碧空，十年坐梦堕西风。此生不作韩枢密，愿抱秋心老蕊宫。”一时传诵，佥谓是儿命薄心高，恐非佳兆。及长，美而侠，富儿大贾，争以缠头媚之，辄时分济寒畯。年二十，自以千金脱籍，私谓狎客某甲曰：“儿齿渐增矣，浮沉风尘中终无了局。

    Explanation

    250

    柯寿鞠，字丹慧，是广陵一位乐工的女儿。她的祖母在八十大寿时，梦见一位女冠手持一支红菊作为寿礼，第二天她出生了，于是便以“丹慧”为名。她在幼年时失去了父母，叔叔品行不端，将她卖入了勾栏。六七岁时，她听到有人吟诗，心中暗生喜爱，见到文人便求他们指点，一学便会。十岁生日时，她即兴作了一首绝句：“戏控青鸾下碧空，十年坐梦堕西风。此生不作韩枢密，愿抱秋心老蕊宫。”这首诗一时传诵开来，人们都说这个孩子命运薄，志向高，恐怕不是吉兆。长大后，她美丽而侠义，富家子弟和大商人争相送她礼物以讨好她，她也常常分给贫寒的文人。二十岁时，她用千金买下了自己的自由，私下对一位亲密的朋友说：“我的年纪渐渐大了，在这风尘中终无了局。”

    251

    频年私积不下十万金，颇可自给，愿乘色未衰，择一才貌俱优、可同白首者，托一终身。君阅人多矣，烦留心物色，倘当意，不吝谢也。”甲笑曰：“诺，容徐图之。”有山阴陶公子者，少年俊美，薄游广陵，艳女之名，兼利其资，赂甲求为说合。时女已独居谢客，甲特往述公子向慕意，并盛夸其门第才貌。女命导公子至，相而后可。既至，果一见目成，两心相许。公子言妻病瘵频年，死在旦夕，虽暂屈簉室，一俟中馈虚人，即当正位。甲居中怂恿，女喜，遂订割臂之盟。定情后，两情缱绻，誓同生死。居无何，公子告女，将如京师纳资求官。问：“何官之求？”曰：“悴丞可耳。

    Explanation

    251

    Over the years, she had accumulated a private fortune of no less than ten thousand gold pieces, which was quite sufficient for self-sufficiency. She wished to take advantage of her youthful beauty and choose a man who was both talented and handsome, someone she could spend her life with. You have seen many people, so please keep an eye out for a suitable match. If you find someone who meets her expectations, do not hesitate to reward me. A smiled and said, "I understand. I will look into it gradually." There was a young man from Shanyin named Tao, who was handsome and charming. He traveled lightly to Guangling, seeking the reputation of a beautiful woman and also for her wealth. He bribed A to mediate the marriage. At the time, the woman was living alone and refusing visitors. A went especially to convey the young man's admiration and highly praised his family background and appearance. The woman instructed that the young man be brought to her, and she would decide after seeing him. When he arrived, they indeed fell in love at first sight, and their hearts were mutually agreed. The young man explained that his wife had been suffering from consumption for many years and was dying soon. Even if temporarily as a secondary wife, once the main wife position was vacant, he would become the main husband. A acted as an intermediary and urged them, and the woman was pleased, so they made a blood pact. After they became engaged, their feelings were deeply entwined, and they swore to share life and death. Not long after, the young man told her that he intended to go to the capital to pay a fee to obtain an official position. He asked, "What kind of official position do you want?" She replied, "A minor official position will do."

    252

    ”问：“何不求守牧？”曰：“固所愿也，奈资不足何！”问：“所绌几何？”曰：“五千金足矣。”女笑曰：“此亦甚易办，妾当足成之，奈何甘就冷宦！”公子大悦。筮日，女为治任祖饯，出五千金付公子，趣速经营，“早去早归，免妾久盼！”公子唯唯，订期珍重而别。逾期，公子不至。女问某甲，但饰语支吾；及坚诘不已，甲乃实告公子固携金遁归乡里，“入京求官”皆属诳语；且其妻悍妒，亦不敢纳妾媵。女知为公子所赚，殊不恚愤，笑谓甲曰：“妾初见若言大气浮，固虑少年轻薄，不可终恃，今果然也。

    Explanation

    252

    "Asked, 'Why not seek to become a governor?' she replied, 'It is indeed what I wish for, but what can be done about the lack of funds?' Asked, 'How much is lacking?' she said, 'Five thousand gold coins would be enough.' She laughed and said, 'This is also quite easy to arrange. I will make up the difference. Why would you willingly accept a cold official position?' The young man was greatly pleased. They chose a date by divination, and she prepared the journey and held a farewell banquet, giving him five thousand gold coins. She urged him to hurry and manage his affairs, saying, 'Go early and return early, so as not to make me wait too long!' The young man agreed, and they agreed on a date and parted ways with care. After the agreed date, the young man did not arrive. She asked Mr. A, who only used evasive and ambiguous language; when she persisted in questioning him, Mr. A finally truthfully informed her that the young man had taken the money and fled back to his hometown. 'Going to the capital to seek an official position' was all lies. Moreover, his wife is fierce and jealous, and he dares not take concubines. She knew she had been cheated by the young man, but she was not angry or resentful. She laughed and said to Mr. A, 'At first, I thought you were a man of great spirit and presence, but I was worried that you might be a young man who is frivolous and unreliable. Now it turns out to be true.'

    253

    ”因详问公子里居第宅，自买太平巨舫，携媪婢五六人径如山阴，僦屋而居，与公子望衡对宇，戒众勿泄。瞷公子母寿辰，贺客盈门，女华妆命舆往。公子方肃宾在堂，骤见女至，大惊失色；众客不知谁何，睹女容光焕发，讶为天人，凛然不敢正视。女乃向众客裣衽，致词曰：“妾广陵乐工女柯寿鞠也。诸公非公子族党，亦必贵戚，妾有微忱，愿为诸公陈之，可乎？”佥曰：“愿闻。”女遂备述公子赚己始末，已，乃指公子而数之曰：“始妾以若贵家子，必知自爱，故遽以终身相托。不虞轻薄儿居心龌龊，但涎妾卖笑金，巧设骗局，自以为得计。

    Explanation

    253

    Carefully inquiring about the young man's residence, he bought a large, peaceful boat for himself, carrying with him five or six old women and maids, and directly traveled to Shanyin. He rented a house and lived there, facing the young nobleman's house across the courtyard, and warned everyone not to reveal his presence. Observing the young nobleman's mother's birthday, he saw her house filled with guests congratulating her. The woman dressed in finery and ordered a carriage to go. The young nobleman was solemnly receiving guests in the hall when he suddenly saw the woman arrive and was greatly shocked, turning pale; the guests did not know who she was, but seeing her radiant beauty, they were surprised to think she was a heavenly being, and were too awed to look directly at her. The woman then bowed to the guests and said, "I am Ko Shouju, the daughter of a musician from Guangling. Gentlemen, if you are not part of the young nobleman's clan, you are certainly noble relatives. I have a small request, and I wish to present it to you gentlemen, may I?" All said, "We wish to hear." The woman then detailed the young nobleman's deceit of her from beginning to end. Afterward, she pointed at the young nobleman and accused him, saying, "At first I thought you, a noble son, would surely know self-love, so I hastily entrusted my life to you. I did not expect the frivolous boy's intentions to be so filthy, only coveting my money from selling smiles. He cleverly set up a scam and thought he had succeeded."

    254

    不知妾卖笑金固用之不竭，特笑若太器小，无福以消受之耳！”公子闻之，汗流满面，惶愧俯首，默无一词。众客为之缓颊，并好言抚慰，愿共为调停，令公子谢过，仍践前盟。女谢曰：“诸公休矣！此等龌龊儿，妾誓不与相见！今所以不惮劳苦千里而来者，诚以若今日可负妾，异日负君负亲负妻负友亦何不可？故特将若为人暴告诸公，俾各慎与交游，勿受其诈耳！”众以女语言爽决，知不可挽，因谓：“公子所携归五千金，当如数返璧。”女笑曰：“此尤细事。若重利轻义，妾则不然。今既为若所赚，直如当日缠头少博此戋戋耳。

    Explanation

    254

    The money from my laughter may indeed be inexhaustible, but perhaps your heart is too small, and you lack the fortune to enjoy it!" Upon hearing this, the young master broke out in a cold sweat, feeling ashamed and guilty, and bowed his head silently, unable to speak. The guests tried to smooth things over, offering kind words of comfort, and were willing to mediate, hoping to make the young master apologize and fulfill his previous promise. The woman replied, "Gentlemen, please stop! Such a dirty boy, I swear I will never see him again! Now, the reason why I am not afraid of the hardship and have come here from afar is that if you can betray me today, what can't you betray tomorrow—your lord, your parents, your wife, and your friends? Therefore, I特意将你的人品恶劣告诉各位, so that each of you may be cautious in your dealings and not fall prey to his deceit!" The guests, recognizing the woman's firm and resolute words, knew they could not change her mind, and thus said, "The young master has taken five thousand gold with him, and we should return it to you in full." The woman smiled and said, "This is a trivial matter. If you value profit over righteousness, I am not like that. Now that I have been cheated by you, it is as if I have only won a small amount of money from my previous earnings."

    255

    况妾平日賙济穷困，浪掷何止倍蓰。若既爱之，亦第蹴尔与之，以大快其欲可也。妾去矣。”遂别众，从容上舆，登舟而去。公子面如死灰，众相对叹息，但姗诮公子薄幸而已。女旋广陵，幡然变计，曰：“一误不可再误，今必得一中年名士之在官者而事之！且非续娶不可！”会淮安府教授周广文，五十丧偶，遣媒求为继室；女素耳周固名士，欣然许之。嫁后琴瑟甚敦。越岁生一子，周益嬖之。前室固有二子，尝与女言冷官多子，虑垂老无以资俯育。女曰：“奈何？”周曰：“老夫固善鸱夷术，向苦无资，闻卿多私蓄，若假我权子母，不患不得什百息也。

    Explanation

    255

    The woman said, "Ever since I began to provide relief to the poor and needy, I have squandered resources many times over. If you truly love me, you might as well give me something in a disdainful manner to greatly satisfy your desires. I am leaving now." She then bid farewell to everyone, calmly mounted the carriage, and boarded the boat to depart. The young man's face turned ashen, and the crowd sighed in unison, only to mock his unfaithfulness. The woman returned to Yangzhou and suddenly changed her mind, saying, "One mistake cannot be repeated; now I must find a middle-aged scholar in a government position to marry! And it must be a second marriage!" It so happened that Zhou Guangwen, the professor of Huaian, was fifty years old and had recently lost his wife. He sent a matchmaker to propose marriage to her as a second wife. The woman had heard of Zhou's reputation as a scholar and agreed happily. After the marriage, their relationship was very harmonious. A year later, they had a son, and Zhou grew even more fond of her. Zhou had two sons from his previous marriage and once told the woman that a low-ranking official often has many children, but he was worried about supporting and raising them when he grew old. The woman asked, "What can be done?" Zhou replied, "I am skilled in the method of lending money at interest, but I have lacked capital. I have heard that you have substantial savings. If you lend me money at interest, I will not worry about not achieving a tenfold or hundredfold return."

    256

    ”女曰：“业夫妻矣，曷不早言？妾物即君物，但挥霍耳，何假为！”遂倾箱罄出所蓄十万金付之。周得金，罢官业鹾，不三年得子金三十万。即罢所业，肆筵设席，延女上座，自奉卮以献曰：“赖卿母金，得少弋获，子孙不忧冻馁，皆卿之赐。虽然，卿出身平康，无不知者，仆纵疏狂，亦不合俨然聘为继配；即仆自愿之，其如天下后世口实何？”女曰：“妾从君生子已扶床矣，何忽出此言？岂畴昔申旦之誓非君意耶？”周曰：“良有之。向以闻卿所蓄甚富，姑妄言之，籍可运筹生色，一洗寒酸。

    Explanation

    256

    The woman said, "We are already married, why didn’t you say it earlier? What belongs to me now belongs to you, it’s just a matter of spending it, why bother?" She then emptied her entire box and handed over ten thousand gold. Zhou received the gold, retired from office, and took up the salt trade. Within three years, he gained thirty thousand gold. He then stopped his business, set up a banquet, invited her to sit in the seat of honor, and raised his own cup to offer, saying, "Thanks to your mother’s wealth, I gained a small harvest, our descendants will not suffer from cold and hunger, all of this is your gift. However, you come from a place of comfort, everyone knows about it, I may be careless, but it’s not right to formally marry you as a second wife; even if I voluntarily do it, what will people in the future say?" The woman replied, "I have already given birth to a child who can walk, why suddenly say this? Isn’t the oath we made last night not your intention?" Zhou said, "Indeed. I spoke it carelessly earlier, thinking you had a lot of wealth, and I could use it to plan and create, to wash away poverty."

    257

    今幸如愿，卿之母金当仍归赵，并酬以什一之息，我有旨蓄，亦以御冬。老夫髦矣，卿近中年，独处鳏居，两足存活，自今以往，请永与卿诀矣。”女曰：“诀则诀矣！妾所生雏将焉置之？”曰：“卿如难割爱，将雏俱去可耳。”女曰：“诺。”即日携子挟金，仍旋广陵。乃鸠工庀材，大治第宅，购良田沃产，择老成纪纲司之。每岁出纳，躬自会计，日益饶富。不惜厚俸，聘延名师，以课其子。子十四岁，周殁，女赍重赙，携子斩齐临吊。周之二子拒之，不许入门，恸哭而返。或谓女十岁时所为诗终成谶语，所谓心高命薄者非耶！自以郁郁不乐，四十岁后改号瘦菊老人，然风骨珊珊，虽当中年，望之犹如二十许人。

    Explanation

    257

    Now that the wish has been fulfilled, your mother's dowry shall be returned to the Zhao family, and I will repay you with a tenth of the interest. I have stored provisions for the winter as well. I am an old man now, and you are nearing middle age, living alone and unmarried. From now on, please allow me to bid you farewell forever." The woman replied, "Farewell then! But what shall I do with the child I have borne?" He said, "If it is difficult for you to part with the child, you may take the child along." The woman said, "Agreed." On the same day, she took her son and the dowry and returned to Guangling. She then gathered workers and materials to renovate her grand house, purchased fertile land and productive property, and selected experienced and reliable individuals to manage her affairs. Each year, she personally oversaw the income and expenses, becoming increasingly wealthy. She generously provided salaries to attract renowned teachers to educate her son. When her son was fourteen, Zhou died, and the woman brought heavy mourning gifts and accompanied the child in formal mourning attire to pay respects. Zhou's two sons refused to let them enter and did not allow them to come inside, causing the woman to return home in tears. Some said that the woman's poem written at ten years old had foretold her fate, as if to say, "Ambitious but short-lived." Feeling unhappy, she changed her name to "Old Man of Lean Chrysanthemum" after forty, but despite this, she maintained a graceful demeanor and appeared youthful even in middle age.

    258

    里乘子曰：女谓“一误不可再误”，若陶之言大气浮，少年轻薄，不可托以终身，犹在女之意中；至周之为人，俨然名士，齿已中年，名称继配，女之幡然变计，亦殊斟酌尽善矣！况琴瑟在御，子已扶床，“我物即君物，听君挥霍”，岂尚有意外变欤？不谓一洗寒酸，顿思决绝，始知畴昔申旦之誓，不过藉为渔利之媒；一旦如愿，还金遽促携雏俱去，似周之叵测无情，诚非女所及料也。而乃哓哓致辨，强以天下后世口实为词，然则陶闻女言，始终愧不一语，似天良尚未丧尽；周则巧言颜厚，口给御人，真可谓老而无耻矣！世之所谓名士者，周如是耶？

    Explanation

    258

    Li Chengzi said: The woman said, "Once you make a mistake, you cannot make another one." If Tao spoke of his superficiality and frivolity, and his youthfulness and lack of seriousness, making him unsuitable as a lifelong partner, this was still within the woman's thoughts. As for Zhou, who appeared to be a respected scholar, being in his middle years and known as a secondary wife, the woman's sudden change of heart was indeed a well-considered and wise decision! Moreover, with the harmonious marriage symbolized by the music of the qin and se, and her child learning to walk, "what belongs to me belongs to you, let you dispose of things as you see fit," how could there still be unexpected changes? She did not expect to cleanse herself of poverty and shabbiness, suddenly deciding to sever ties, and only then did she realize that the solemn oath made at dawn was merely a means to exploit Zhou for personal gain; once her wish was fulfilled, she quickly returned the money and took her children away, revealing Zhou's unpredictable and heartless nature, which truly was beyond the woman's expectations. Yet, she continued to defend herself, using the argument of public opinion, but Tao, upon hearing her words, remained silent throughout, as if his sense of right and wrong had not completely disappeared; Zhou, however, was deceitful and shameless, speaking flatteringly and manipulating people, truly deserving the title of an old and shameless man! Is this what the world calls a scholar?

    259

    伊莘农相国言
    　　
    　　伊莘农相国言，人生枯菀升沉，或由福而祸，或由祸而福，皆有定数，殊难逆料。不见予年五十，犹于滇省节署堂皇西偏，枯坐胡床，仰屋默数木椽方砖时耶！客请其说，曰：余初铨除云南通判，因公罣，吏议去官。穷滞不得回旗，欲谒抚军求谕寅采，凑赆资斧。司阍者以絓误废员，斥不与通。恳告再三，始颔之，令少待。但见大小吏分队晋谒白事，司阍者次第传命：“司道也”，入；“司道也”，出。“府厅也”，入；“府厅也”，出。“州县也”，入；“州县也”，出。“佐贰也”，入，“佐贰也”，出。“武弁也”，入；“武弁也”，出。意以为当及己也，时日濒晡，忽闻司阍者大声言曰：“抚军今日接见属吏，一一处分公事，为时久，甚矣惫，闲人毋得干嬲。

    Explanation

    259

    The Statement of the Viceroy Yi Shenong

    The Viceroy Yi Shenong once said: Life is full of ups and downs, with fortune often turning to misfortune, and misfortune sometimes giving way to fortune. All things are governed by a fixed destiny, yet it is extremely difficult to foresee. Have you not seen me, at the age of fifty, still sitting idly on a trapezoidal mat in the western wing of the provincial governor's residence in Yunnan, staring at the ceiling and silently counting the wooden beams and square bricks? When a guest asked for an explanation, I said: I was first appointed as a magistrate of Yunnan, but due to an official transgression, I was dismissed by the officials’ judgment. Stranded in poverty, I could not return to my homeland, and I wished to pay respects to the provincial commander in order to seek his favor and patronage, so I collected funds for the journey. The gatekeeper, citing my involvement in an error and my status as a dismissed official, refused to let me pass. After I pleaded earnestly several times, he finally nodded and told me to wait a while. But I saw the officials, both high and low, entering in groups to report their affairs, and the gatekeeper relayed commands in order: “A provincial office official,” enter; “a provincial office official,” exit. “A prefectural official,” enter; “a prefectural official,” exit. “A county official,” enter; “a county official,” exit. “A deputy official,” enter; “a deputy official,” exit. “A military officer,” enter; “a military officer,” exit. I thought it would be my turn, but as the day neared dusk, I suddenly heard the gatekeeper loudly say: “The governor is receiving his subordinates today, handling each matter one by one. It has taken a long time, and he is very tired. Please do not bother him with trivial matters.”

    260

    尔且退，期以诘朝相见。”予次且徒步归。凡往返三日，皆如之。惟日于节署堂皇西偏，支胡床，屏息枯坐，一无所事。始仰屋默数堂皇自西讫东木椽若干，继默数椽上承尘方砖若干，目谛心识，顺算逆覆，周而复始，藉攻沉闷。既抚军但语郡守为道地，仅共敛白金百两为赆，而抚军固终未之得见也。滇省去京师万里，途长资短，可奈何！计惟暂置妻孥，孑身入都向亲友称贷，再事区画。不谓都中亲友见予免官归，相率避道，无一存问。故事旗员因公去官，例许请觐。有旧胥谓予曰：“君困若此，盍援例请觐？倘沐旷典未可知。”如言，蒐腰缠仅存所赆金三十两，罄付作孤注，得具文上请。时朝廷方厪念滇中苗疆事宜，以予从滇来，特召见垂问苗情，予谨据实条陈。奏对称旨，上意嘉悦，敕以原官，仍回滇视事。

    Explanation

    260

    The governor said, "Wait for a moment, arrange to meet the next morning." I reluctantly walked back home on foot. In total, it took three days for the round trip, all following the same pattern. Only during those days, I would sit on a folding chair in the hall, sit silently and motionlessly, with nothing to do. I started by counting the wooden beams in the hall from west to east, then continued by counting the square bricks on the ceiling. I looked intently and mentally noted each count, repeating the process forward and backward, over and over again, using this method to alleviate my boredom. The governor only instructed the prefect to prepare the way, and they collected only one hundred taels of silver as a gift, but the governor ultimately did not get to see me. The distance from Yunnan to the capital is ten thousand miles, with a long journey and limited resources, what can be done? The plan was to temporarily leave my family and go alone to the capital to borrow money from friends, then make further plans. However, the friends in the capital, upon seeing me dismissed from office, all avoided me, and none inquired after me. According to the old rules, flag officers who leave their posts for official reasons are allowed to request an audience. An old clerk said to me, "In your current situation, why not follow the example and request an audience? Perhaps you might be granted a special favor." Following his advice, I gathered the remaining thirty taels of the gift money, spent all the remaining money in one go, and submitted a formal request. At that time, the court was concerned about matters in the Miao region of Yunnan, and because I came from Yunnan, I was specially summoned to ask about the situation in the Miao region. I carefully presented my observations. My response was in line with the emperor's wishes, and I was ordered to return to my original post in Yunnan.

    261

    亲友闻予复官，渐有来庆贺者；及陛辞遄发，旋奉命超擢郡守，亲友来者愈众。不惟庆贺，有推荐纪纲者矣，有馈饷食物者矣，且有不向称贷而殷殷嘉惠程币、惟恐拒而不受者矣。予迫于朝命，不敢濡滞，甫出都门，便奉诏简授监司，并谕兼程驰驿赴任。既抵滇省，妻孥相见，彼此慰藉，恍疑梦中。即日，遵典礼参谒抚军，前司阍者见余至，亟趋前罄折起居，言笑和悦，不似前气象愁惨。比将命入，抚军即传命曰：“请。”相见之下，吉词奖庆，备极谦宠。见余着监司冠服，讶曰：“君尚不知耶？昨已奉诏，特命君陈臬滇中，君尚不知而犹着此耶？”命左右速为具按察冠服，就于节署更易。两年之间，由滇臬荐转布政，坐迁巡抚。

    Explanation

    261

    Relatives and friends heard that I was reinstated, and gradually there were people coming to congratulate me; upon bidding farewell to the emperor and departing quickly, I was soon after ordered to be promoted to the position of prefect. The number of relatives and friends who came increased even more. It was not just for congratulations; some recommended me for positions of responsibility, others offered food and supplies, and there were even those who earnestly bestowed favors with money or gifts, fearing that I might refuse them. Pressured by the imperial decree, I dared not delay, and as soon as I left the capital city, I was ordered to assume the role of a high-ranking official overseeing provincial affairs and was instructed to travel swiftly by post to take up my new position. Upon arriving in Yunnan, I saw my wife and children, and we comforted each other, as if in a dream. On the same day, I followed the proper rituals to pay homage to the provincial governor. The gatekeeper, seeing me arrive, hurried forward to greet me with great respect, speaking and smiling in a friendly manner, unlike his previous gloomy demeanor. When he was about to announce my arrival, the provincial governor immediately called out, “Please come in.” Upon meeting, he expressed his best wishes and congratulations, showing the utmost humility and favor. Seeing me dressed in the attire of a high-ranking official overseeing provincial affairs, he was surprised and said, “Do you not know yet? Yesterday, I received an imperial decree especially ordering you to serve as the provincial governor in Yunnan. You do not know this and yet you are still wearing this attire?” He then ordered the attendants to quickly prepare the proper ceremonial attire for me to change into at the provincial governor’s office. Within two years, I was recommended for a promotion to the position of provincial governor due to my merit and was subsequently appointed as the provincial governor.

    262

    受命之日，恭诣节署堂皇，焚香设案，望阙九拜谢恩，接纳印绶毕，忽仰见堂皇西偏屋椽方砖，历历在目，因忆昔支胡床枯坐其下，三日往返，欲求一望抚军颜色而不可得，其时犬马齿已迫曰艾，固不料当日求见不得之抚军，甫两易寒暑，竟俨然及身，起而代之也。予方木立神溯，冥迫默忾，忽予阍人来报：“凡滇省大小属吏，咸临宇下待命谒贺。”予次第接见，犹是“司道也”，入，“司道也”，出；“府厅也”，入，“府厅也”，出；‘州县也”，入，“州县也”，出；“佐贰也”，入，“佐贰也”，出；“武弁也”，入，“武弁也”，出。彼一时也，此一时也，抚今追昔，惶槐惶愧。予接见各吏既毕，乃进司阍者而戒之曰：“尔曹识之，自今以往，但有来谒者必将命，尔曹务接以和悦，切勿以愁惨之气象相加，慎毋令堂皇西偏，再有人枯坐胡床，求见不得，徒劳其仰屋默数木椽若干、方砖若干也！相国名伊里布，沈阳人。罢相后，尝为人言之。

    Explanation

    262

    On the day I was appointed, I respectfully arrived at the governor's office, burned incense, set up a table, and bowed nine times toward the imperial palace to express my gratitude. After receiving the seal and sash, I suddenly looked up and saw the beams and tiles of the western side of the hall clearly. This reminded me of the past when I had sat on a trundle bed and waited there for three days, going back and forth, wanting to see the governor's face but unable to. At that time, my age was already approaching old age, and I did not expect that the governor I had once sought to see but could not, would now take over the position in just two years. I stood motionless, lost in thought, with a sense of awe and sorrow. Suddenly, my gatekeeper reported, "All officials from Yunnan Province, both high and low, have gathered below to await your command and pay their respects." I received them in order: "The provincial officials," they entered, "the provincial officials," they left; "the prefectural and county officials," they entered, "the prefectural and county officials," they left; "the state and county officials," they entered, "the state and county officials," they left; "the deputy officials," they entered, "the deputy officials," they left; "the military officers," they entered, "the military officers," they left. It was one time then, and another time now. Reflecting on the past and present, I felt a deep sense of humility and shame. After receiving all the officials, I summoned my gatekeeper and instructed him, "You must remember this: from now on, anyone who comes to visit must be summoned. Make sure to greet them warmly, and do not show a gloomy demeanor. Be careful not to let anyone else sit on a trundle bed in the western side of the hall, unable to see me, and wasting their time counting the beams and tiles." The former prime minister's name was Iribu, from Shenyang. After he resigned, he once mentioned this to others.

    263

    里乘子曰：往予读《国策》、《汉书》，睹苏季子、朱翁子已事，未尝不感慨太息。今闻相国所云，然是鼎有足矣！人生世上，势利富贵，盖可忽乎！
    　　
    　　圆光二则
    　　
    　　梅生，四川人，久客皖江，思乡綦切。尝馆六安州幕中，会署有术士，愿为作法。先令尽量酣饮而卧，戒众勿惊，自坐生榻前，骈二指自画左掌心，口中喃喃诵咒，呼十二岁识字童子谛视之。少选，童子曰：“掌中放光，圆明如镜矣。”又曰：“镜中现馆舍，梅生卧榻上矣。”又曰：“梅生兴矣，出门矣，方渡于水之涯矣，旋陟于山之巅矣，升峻岭矣，履坦途矣，抵屋一所，登门矣，升堂矣，入室矣。怪哉！怪哉！室中一少妇，凭几握管作书，梅生竟笑倚其傍，且拊其鬟而玩其字矣。”术士曰：“是矣，汝第谛视所书云何？”童子一一口诵，术士另纸笔之，盖其妇方作寄夫书也。

    Explanation

    263

    Li Chengzi said: In the past, I read Diplomatic Strategies and Records of the Grand Historian, observing the deeds of Sun Qinzi and Zhu Wengzi, and never failed to feel moved and sigh. Now hearing what the chancellor has said, this tripod is indeed sufficient! In life on earth, wealth, power, and status, can they be neglected?

    Two Cases of Circular Light

    Mei Sheng, a man from Sichuan, had been a long-term resident of Wanjiang, missing his hometown intensely. Once, he stayed in the office of Liu'an Prefecture, where there happened to be a magician who was willing to perform a ritual. First, he ordered him to drink to his heart's content and then lie down, warning everyone not to be alarmed. He sat in front of Mei Sheng's bed, joined two fingers together and drew on the palm of his left hand, muttering spells in his mouth, and called a twelve-year-old literate child to look closely at it. A short while later, the child said, "Light is emitting from the palm, round and bright like a mirror." Then he said, "The mirror shows the office, and Mei Sheng is lying on the bed." He continued, "Mei Sheng rises, goes out, crosses the water, climbs to the top of the mountain, ascends a steep hill, walks on a flat path, reaches a house, enters the door, enters the hall, and enters the room. Amazing! Amazing! In the room, a young woman is leaning on a table and writing with a brush, and Mei Sheng smiles and leans against her side, caressing her hair while admiring her writing." The magician said, "Yes, indeed, just look closely at what is written." The child recited it word for word, and the magician wrote it down on another piece of paper. It seems that her wife was writing a letter to her husband.

    264

    须臾，书毕，妇缄叠完好。童子以语术士，术士曰：“先生不可久留矣。”复骈指画其掌，仍令童子视之。则曰：“梅生出室矣，出门矣，由坦途而峻岭矣，又陟山而渡水矣，犹是入馆舍而上榻矣。”童子之言甫毕，梅生遽从榻上欠伸起，竟体大汗如雨，拭目叹曰：“奇哉幻梦乎！”术士叩其梦中所历，与童子所言相符，因笑而谓之曰：“此真境，固非幻梦，君如不信，俟夫人家报至，自知。”未几，生妻家书至，验之，果与生梦中所见，并童子口诵而术士笔之者无少异。后生归，与妻话及此事，妻讶曰：“方妾作书时，恍惚鬓边有物作祟，固不虞是老奴作此狡狯伎俩也。”相与粲然一笑。

    Explanation

    264

    After a short while, the writing was finished, and the wife carefully sealed and folded the paper. The boy told the magician, who said, "Sir, you cannot stay here for long." The magician then drew on his palm and asked the boy to look at it. He said, "Mr. Mei has left the room, gone out the door, traveled along a flat road and up a steep mountain, climbed a mountain and crossed a river, and finally entered a guesthouse and lay down on a bed." As soon as the boy had finished speaking, Mr. Mei suddenly stretched and stood up from the bed, sweating profusely. Wiping his eyes, he exclaimed, "How strange and dreamlike it was!" The magician asked about what he had experienced in his dream, which matched what the boy had said. Smiling, he said to him, "This is the real world, not a dream. If you do not believe me, wait for your wife's message to arrive, and you will know." Not long after, a letter from Mr. Mei's wife arrived, and upon checking it, it was indeed exactly as Mr. Mei had seen in his dream, as well as what the boy had recited and the magician had written. After Mr. Mei returned home, he talked to his wife about this matter. His wife was surprised and said, "When I was writing the letter, I felt something strange at my temple, but I didn't expect the old servant to be so cunning." They both laughed heartily.

    265

    里乘子曰：宋鲁应《龙括异志》载：“三山曾陟，尝馆于陈氏，七载音信不通。夏月，青衿俱歇，独处一室。有道人自吴山来，谓曰：‘子思乡之切，何不少归？’陟曰：‘水陆三千里，几时得到？’道人剪纸为马，令合眼上马，以水噀之，其疾如风，祝曰：‘汝归不可久留！’须臾到家，门户如旧，妻令入浴易新衣，陟曰：‘我便去。’妻曰，‘才归便去，何不念父母妻子乎？’陟便上马而行，所骑马足惊折，寤，乃身在书馆中，随身衣服皆新制者。道人亦不见，惟留一药篮，中有一诗云：‘一骑如龙送客归，银鬃绿耳步相随。佳人未许轻分别，不是仙翁那得知？’”此事与梅生差相类，然以身骑纸马而归，较梦中不更为神异乎！

    Explanation

    265

    Lichengzi said: According to Song Lu Ying's "Long Kuo Yi Zhi," it is recorded: "Zeng Zhi from Sanchuan once stayed at Chen's house for seven years without any news. In the summer, all scholars had rested, and he was alone in a room. A Taoist priest came from Wushan and said to him, 'Your longing for your hometown is so intense, why not return a little?' Zhi replied, 'Three thousand li by water and land, when will I get there?' The priest cut paper to make a horse, told him to close his eyes and get on the horse, and sprayed water on it. The horse was as fast as the wind. The priest blessed, 'You must not stay long!' In a moment, he arrived home, and the doors and windows were as before. His wife told him to take a bath and change into new clothes. Zhi said, 'I will go.' His wife said, 'You just came back and are leaving again, why don't you think of your parents and family?' Zhi got on the horse and started to ride, but the horse's legs were startled and broke. He awoke to find himself in the study, and his clothes were all newly made. The priest was also not seen, only left a medicine basket, in which there was a poem that said: 'One ride like a dragon sends the guest back, silver mane and green ears walk side by side. The beautiful person does not allow us to part lightly, who but the immortal knows?' This event is somewhat similar to that of Meisheng, but riding a paper horse and returning is even more miraculous than a dream, is it not?"

    266

    杭州沈公子，世居吴山之西，食旧德，拥厚资，丰衣美馔，粥粥群雌，顾非心之所好，惟馀桃断袖，嗜而溺之。少美仪容，生性徇通，喜涉猎图史。尝独居斋中，左右给役皆二八娈童，有鲍婉奴、蒋霞媖者色尤姣好，宠信倍隆。家藏祖遗夜光珠一颗，面面圆匀，大如雀卵，诚为希世之珍，最所宝贵；缀于角巾，黑夜光照一室，不肯轻以示人。会残暑初退，天气晚凉，夜脱巾庋斋中案头，晨兴，求之已亡。

    Explanation

    266

    The Young Nobleman of Hangzhou

    The young nobleman of Hangzhou, Shen, lived in the western part of Wushan Mountain, inheriting wealth and social standing. He enjoyed a life of luxury, surrounded by many women, yet he found no joy in their company. Instead, he was deeply addicted to and indulged in male homosexuality, particularly fond of young boys. From a young age, he had a beautiful appearance and was naturally adaptable and perceptive, with a broad interest in reading and learning. He often lived alone in his study, where his attendants were all young, handsome boys, two of whom, Bao Wannu and Jiang Xiaoying, were especially beautiful and received his great favor and trust. In his home, he kept a rare and valuable pearl, passed down from his ancestors, which was perfectly round and smooth, about the size of a sparrow's egg. It was truly a treasure of the world, highly prized by him. He attached it to his headband, and at night, its light illuminated the entire room, yet he was reluctant to show it to others. When the intense summer heat had just subsided and the weather turned cool in the evening, one night he removed his headband and placed it on the desk in his study. In the morning, upon waking, he searched for it but it was gone.

    267

    斋故在花园中，不许外人阑入，意必两童之所窃攘，讯之，坚不肯承；鞭之，各负重伤。与两童约，速为觅获，倘合浦不还，则蚁命难活。绍兴俞仲华茂才万春，素谙圆光之技。某甲见两童鞭扑之惨，特造俞告其事，谓：“君术妙圆澄，曷不一试？”俞不肯，甲嬲之曰：“我非为公子失珠，所愿是非立见，庶免波及无辜。果是两童则已，否则，亦可为辨其诬，俾性命得全，功胜合尖浮图也。”俞固好善，恻然心动，乃诺之。甲喜，趋告公子。礼延俞至，命备黄纸十二张，骈指作剑诀，书勅勒其上，口默诵咒，焚投地上，选十二岁以内童子十馀人，环视之。

    Explanation

    267

    The study was located in the garden, and outsiders were not allowed to enter. It was believed that the two boys had stolen the pearl. When questioned, they stubbornly refused to admit it; when beaten, each sustained severe injuries. He made a deal with the two boys, promising to quickly find the pearl. If the pearl is not returned, then the ant's life will be in danger. Yu Zhonghua, a scholar from Shaoxing named Wan Chun, was well-versed in the technique of divination. Someone saw the two boys being severely beaten and specially went to see Yu, telling him about the incident, saying, "Your technique is very precise and clear, why not try it once?" Yu refused, but the person persisted, saying, "I am not here for the loss of the pearl, but I wish to see the truth immediately, to avoid innocent people being implicated. If it is indeed the two boys, then that is it. Otherwise, it can also clear their false accusation, allowing them to live, which would be a great deed." Yu, who was good and kind, was moved by compassion and agreed. The person was pleased and hurriedly informed the young master. He invited Yu respectfully and ordered the preparation of twelve yellow sheets of paper. Yu clasped his fingers together like a sword and wrote the command on it, silently reciting a spell and burning it on the ground. He selected more than ten children under twelve years old and had them circle around it.

    268

    俞戒之曰：“尔曹第有所见，据实以报，勿得妄言！事毕，各有厚赏不吝！”少间，群儿报称地现镜光，圆如釜口，镜中现花园，园中池亭、花木，竹石、鱼鸟、阑干、罘罳，帘幕，及斋中几榻、图书、鼎彝之属，一一毕具。则见公子着罗衫、首戴缀珠角巾至斋中，两童从其后。公子脱衫，冒椸上，着短衫，据案观书。两童或燃烛，或瀹茗，或进瓜果菱藕，或拳背按摩、挥扇驱蚊，展衾拂簟。公子欠伸起自私，手除角巾置案头，解衣脱袜上榻卧，两童安置虎子，压幕剔灯，联背拽门而去。俞问：“两童子去后，角巾尚在案头乎？”曰：“然。”俞曰：“是非正在此刻矣，尔曹须用心观之。

    Explanation

    268

    Yu warned them, saying, "You all just report what you see, according to the facts, and do not fabricate! When the matter is done, each will receive a generous reward without hesitation!" After a while, the children reported that the ground showed mirror-like light, round like the mouth of a pot. In the mirror, a garden appeared, with ponds and pavilions, flowers and trees, bamboo and stones, fish and birds, railings, screens, curtains, and in the study, tables and chairs, books, and bronze vessels, all present. Then one saw the young master wearing a silk robe and a hat adorned with beads, entering the study, with two boys following behind him. The young master took off his robe and hung it on a clothes rack, then wore a short-sleeved shirt, sat at the desk, and read books. One of the boys lit a candle, another boiled tea, another brought in melons, fruits, lotus root, and water chestnuts, one massaged his back, one waved a fan to drive away mosquitoes, and one spread out the bedding and fanned the mat. The young master yawned and stretched, getting up on his own, removed his hat with his hands, and put it on the desk, took off his shirt and socks, got into bed, and lay down. The two boys arranged the urinal, put down the curtains, put out the lamp, linked their backs, pulled the door closed, and left. Yu asked, "After the two boys leave, is the hat still on the desk?" "Yes," they replied. Yu said, "The truth is right now, you all must observe carefully."

    269

    ”俄，群儿又报，称：“园中荷池花叶纷披中，忽立一白髯老者，四顾而嗤，何也？”俞曰：“是必有异，须再用心观之。”忽闻群儿惊曰：“噫！是何怪也？老者上岸，则人首而蛇身也。由岸而升阶，伏窗棂而内窥矣。径由窗棂入，至榻前揭幕笑向公子矣。回身至案前，顾角巾而笑，以口衔珠，仍从窗棂出，下阶蜿蜒入池，遁不见矣。”俞大笑曰：“得之矣！”斯时帘外帘内观者百数十人，闻群儿所言，佥咄咄称怪，且同声赞俞曰：“先生真神术哉！微先生，则屈煞两童矣！”公子疑信参半，命以车涸池水，果见角巾在焉，巾则犹是也，而珠已化为乌有矣。盖池旧有蛇妖，以珠为宝物而袭取去也。噫！失物顾可妄诬人哉！

    Explanation

    269

    Soon after, the children reported again, saying: "In the midst of the lotus pond in the garden, among the lush leaves and flowers, suddenly stood an old man with a white beard. He looked around and sneered, what is this?" Mr. Yu said: "There must be something strange; we must observe more carefully." Suddenly, the children exclaimed in surprise: "Ah! What a strange thing is this? The old man went ashore, with a human head and a snake's body. He climbed the stairs from the shore, crouched on the window lattice and peered inside. He entered directly through the window lattice, went to the bed, lifted the curtain, and laughed at the young lord. He turned around to the table, looked at the headband and laughed, held the pearl in his mouth, and still went out through the window lattice, descended the stairs, and slithered into the pond, vanishing without a trace." Mr. Yu laughed and said: "I have it now!" At that time, there were over a hundred people inside and outside the curtains, who, upon hearing the children's report, all exclaimed in amazement and praised Mr. Yu in unison: "Sir, you truly have magical skills! Without you, the two boys would have been frustrated!" The young lord was doubtful and believing half, and ordered to drain the pond water. Indeed, the headband was found there, still the same, but the pearl had vanished. It is said that there was a snake spirit in the pond, which took the pearl as a treasure and took it away. Ah! Can one falsely accuse someone for a lost object?

    270

    里乘子曰：《晋宋艺术传》载：“石勒将擒刘曜，佛图澄令一童子絜斋七日，取麻油合胭脂，躬自研于掌中，举手示童子，粲然有辉。童子惊曰：‘军马甚众，见一人长大白皙，以朱丝缚其肘。’澄曰：‘此即曜也。’”钱竹汀先生大昕谓即近日圆光之术，盖本于此。
    　　
    　　楚北王某
    　　
    　　楚北王某，性豪而黠。家固不封，常与人缓急相通，辄贷此偿彼，不爽所约。西邻富翁某，素贪且吝，极有心计，王欲贷其资贸易，恐不见诺，而思有以赚之。会岁将暮，虑人索逋，以妇翁家颇小康，特诣称贷百金，约岁周归还，并加什一之利，妇翁知王不失信，如数予之。王以百金兑钱，不待索逋者登门，一律先期偿讫。尚馀钱数十千，市布帛为妻孥制衣裳，焕然一新。

    Explanation

    270

    里乘子曰：《晋宋艺术传》载：“石勒将擒刘曜，佛图澄令一童子絜斋七日，取麻油合胭脂，躬自研于掌中，举手示童子，粲然有辉。童子惊曰：‘军马甚众，见一人长大白皙，以朱丝缚其肘。’澄曰：‘此即曜也。’”钱竹汀先生大昕谓即近日圆光之术，盖本于此。

    楚北王某

    楚北王某，性豪而黠。家固不封，常与人缓急相通，辄贷此偿彼，不爽所约。西邻富翁某，素贪且吝，极有心计，王欲贷其资贸易，恐不见诺，而思有以赚之。会岁将暮，虑人索逋，以妇翁家颇小康，特诣称贷百金，约岁周归还，并加什一之利，妇翁知王不失信，如数予之。王以百金兑钱，不待索逋者登门，一律先期偿讫。尚馀钱数十千，市布帛为妻孥制衣裳，焕然一新。

    271

    富翁心果生疑，谓王每岁暮索逋者踵相接，今不待剥啄，一律先期偿讫，且为合家细弱制衣裳焕然一新，何其绰绰有馀裕也！得勿掘发窖藏耶？既届除夕，听王宅爆竹声震耳不绝，潜往侦察，见王门已闭，心益疑之。谓除夕迎神，类皆重门洞开，今反闭之，何诡秘乃尔也！王宅故湫隘，门内即是堂皇，窃侧目从门隙内窥，但见堂上灯烛辉煌，家人嘻嘻，笑语甚欢，几上累累堆积朱提，大小不下数百铤，默计何止盈万，不胜骇羡，益信掘发窖藏无疑。因念王本窭人子，一旦幸邀天贶，暴获巨资，足征时运亨泰，倘与合伙贸易，利当倍蓰，即什伯千万不难也。献岁，特诹吉日，折柬独召王饮，肆筵设席，极其丰腆。

    Explanation

    271

    The wealthy man's heart indeed harbored doubts. He thought that Wang, who usually collected debts one after another at the end of the year, now paid them all ahead of time without waiting for knocks on the door. Moreover, he had prepared new clothes for the entire family, including women and children, making everything look completely renewed. How abundantly wealthy he seemed to be! Could it be that Wang had dug up hidden treasures? When the New Year's Eve arrived, the wealthy man heard the continuous sound of firecrackers at Wang's house, which was deafening. He secretly went to investigate and found that Wang's door was closed. His doubts grew even stronger. He thought that on New Year's Eve, when welcoming deities, doors were usually wide open, but now they were closed. How mysterious was this! Considering that Wang was originally a poor man, who suddenly received a great fortune by chance, it was evident that his luck was good. If he could partner with him in trade, the profit would surely be doubled, even reaching ten thousand or more. At the beginning of the year, he specially chose an auspicious day, sent a letter to invite Wang privately, and prepared a grand feast, which was extremely abundant.

    272

    酒酣，笑王曰：“贺君得巨资，能以奇零贷我否？”王蹙然曰：“小子守命安贫，君所知也，何得巨资？”富翁笑附王耳小语曰：“君瞒他人可，不必瞒我，我知之稔矣。尚何虞诈为也？”王力辨其无，富翁曰：“君不愿贷我，我却愿贷君。今请以五千金付君，同为贸易，君愿之乎？”王摇首固辞曰：“小子赤贫，受君重资，倘为折阅，其何以报？不敢从命！”富翁曰：“如获利，彼此均之；如有折阅，誓不责偿，何如？”说之再，王始首肯。富翁乃慨以五千金相付。王赍至江西，见菜油满市，爰倾囊购归。是秋楚北菜油翔贵，获利数倍。

    Explanation

    272

    After drinking to the point of intoxication, the wealthy man smiled and said to Wang, "Congratulations on obtaining a large sum of money. Can you lend me a small amount of it?" Wang frowned and said, "I have always been content with my fate and lived a simple life, as you know. How could I have a large sum of money?" The wealthy man laughed and whispered in Wang's ear, "You can deceive others, but there's no need to deceive me. I have known about it for a long time. Why should I worry about deceit?" Wang strongly argued that he didn't have it. The wealthy man said, "You don't want to lend me money, but I want to lend you. Now, please accept this five thousand gold pieces and let's engage in trade together. Would you like to do that?" Wang shook his head and firmly declined, saying, "I am extremely poor. If I were to lose or damage the money, how could I repay you? I dare not accept your offer!" The wealthy man said, "If we make a profit, we will share it equally. If we incur a loss, I swear not to hold you responsible. What do you think?" After persuading him repeatedly, Wang finally agreed. The wealthy man then generously gave him five thousand gold pieces. Wang carried the money to Jiangxi, where he saw that the market was full of vegetable oil. He emptied his bag to buy some and brought it back. That autumn, vegetable oil in the north of Chu province became very expensive, and he made several times the profit.

    273

    富翁大喜，仍以母子金尽付王，听其筹运。往无不利，不十年，累资百万，彼此均之。至今两家子孙犹称素封焉。富翁尝自鸣得意，为王备述畴昔除夕侦察一事，谓：“君至黠，乃竟为我所赚。”王笑曰：“微君言，亦将明告。畴昔几上累累堆积阿堵物，固是锡箔粘成，计君倘来窥，姑以相衒，窃冀一垂涎，即藉以饵之，果赖得君资，致有今日。诚如君言，非君赚我，实我赚君矣。不然家无儋石储，纵说如苏张，君亦安肯慨以重资相付乎？”富翁闻之，始恍然如梦初觉。乃前席笑拍王肩戏诋之曰：“我自谓颇有心计，不图竟为君所赚，促狭儿，何其谲也！”两人相视粲然一笑，遂订为莫逆交云。

    Explanation

    273

    The rich man was delighted and still gave all the money to Wang, allowing him to manage it. Everything he did was successful, and within ten years, he had accumulated a million in wealth, which was equally divided between them. To this day, both families' descendants still call themselves wealthy. The rich man once boasted of his success, recounting a past New Year's Eve reconnaissance for Wang, saying, "You are very clever, but you were actually tricked by me." Wang laughed and said, "Without your words, I would have told you myself. The past accumulation of many things on the table was actually made of tin foil. I thought you might come to look, so I tried to impress you, secretly hoping you would be interested, and use it to lure you. Indeed, I relied on your money to reach this point. As you said, it wasn't you who tricked me, but I tricked you. Otherwise, my family had no storage of even a small amount, even if you spoke like Su Qin or Zhang Yi, would you really be willing to give me a large sum of money?" The rich man heard this and finally woke up as if from a dream. He then leaned forward, laughed, and playfully scolded Wang, saying, "I thought I was quite cunning, but I didn't expect to be tricked by you, tricky little fellow, how clever you are!" The two of them smiled at each other happily and thus became sworn brothers.

    274

    爱儿
    　　
    　　舒城田舍翁某，年四十，生一女，名爱儿，以中年所出，甚珍爱之。爰字于同里之农家子，谓相距密迩，便于往返。亡何，翁妻卒，女才十龄，即育于嫂氏，以憨稚贪于嬉戏，嫂甚厌恶之，往往相对恶谑，并以语恐之曰：“若已十龄，不为嫛婗，尚自蜀束好弄，闻若婿与若齿相若，其势已甚伟，将来齿日增，更不知何若！日后若嫁去，吾甚为若危之，看若犹能嬉戏否！”嫂平居与女相对，辄道及此，以谑语出之，或有时又以庄语出之，甚至故作颦蹙状，若以为是真为女厪虑也者。爱儿闻之既熟，甚以为惧。不数年，女已及笄，往嫁有日，嫂犹时以为盲。爱儿默自计曰：“诚如嫂言，吾命休矣！奈何！”又自幸距家不远，脱有危，姑遁归再作计较。未几，桃夭期届，冰人在门，采舆将发。

    Explanation

    274

    A Beloved Daughter

    In the countryside of Shucheng, there lived a farmer in his forties who had a daughter named Ai'er. Because she was born later in life, he cherished her dearly. He arranged for her to be betrothed to a farmer's son from the same village, thinking that they were close by and it would be convenient for visits. Soon after, the farmer's wife passed away, and Ai'er was only ten years old at the time. She was then raised by her elder sister-in-law. Ai'er, being innocent and fond of play, was greatly disliked by her elder sister-in-law, who often mocked her and even threatened her, saying, "You are already ten years old and no longer a baby. You still love to play and are mischievous. I hear that your future husband is around your age, and he is already very strong. As you grow older, who knows what will happen! When you get married in the future, I am very worried for you. Will you still be able to play?" The elder sister-in-law frequently spoke to Ai'er about this, sometimes in jest and sometimes seriously, even making a frowning face as if she were truly concerned for Ai'er's well-being. Ai'er, having heard these words many times, became very afraid. A few years later, when Ai'er had reached the age of marriage, the wedding was to take place soon. The elder sister-in-law still occasionally warned her. Ai'er silently thought to herself, "If what my sister-in-law says is true, my fate is sealed! What can I do?" She also felt fortunate that she was not far from home and, if there was any danger, she could escape back to her family and make plans again. Soon after, the time of the peach blossom festival arrived, the matchmaker came to the door, and the wedding carriage was about to depart.

    275

    嫂固不喜爱儿，今当吉期，故以不祥之语咒之，便擥女手佯为悲泣而送之，曰：“阿姑须珍重自卫，但愿人言不实，则我与若相见犹有日；假使其言不谬，若此一去，吾将见若出，而不能再见若入也。呜呼伤哉！呜呼哀哉！”爱儿闻之，甚感嫂氏之多情，倍益恇怯。是夕合卺后，众宾既散，新郎虽农家子，年裁弱冠，亦甚温存腼腆，至夜将阑，乃低声促女曰：“寒夜难耐，与卿睡休。”爱儿正怀疑惧，忽闻此言，如九天之发霹雳，不觉震惊，汗流浃背，低首面壁，默不敢声。少选，新郎又前搴女袂，再四敦迫。爱儿计不能免，不得已解衣入帏。新硎初试，其利可知，爱儿仅志嫂言，深自防卫，才一着体，已自难御，益信嫂言有征，低死支拒，不使遽尽其器；而新郎欲焰正炽，势难中止，女不得已，绐之曰：“尔我夫妇，为日正长，奴今适有小恙，一俟全愈，惟君所欲，断不敢再事推却以逆君意。”新郎闻而怜之，遂为罢战。女喜获免，窃庆再生。

    Explanation

    275

    The Elder Sister, who originally did not favor the son, now, on the auspicious day of the wedding, used ominous words to curse him, taking the girl's hand and pretending to cry as she sent her off, saying, "Aunt must take great care of herself. If people's words are not true, then I and you will still have days to meet; if their words are not mistaken, once you leave, I will see you go out but not see you come back in. Alas, how sorrowful! Alas, how pitiful!" The girl, upon hearing this, was deeply moved by the aunt's affection and felt even more fearful. That night, after the wedding ceremony, when the guests had all left, the groom, though a farmer's son and just twenty years old, was also very gentle and shy. As the night approached its end, he softly urged the girl, "The cold night is hard to endure; let's sleep together." The girl, who was already suspicious and fearful, suddenly heard these words and felt as if struck by a thunderclap from the ninth heaven, not realizing her shock, she sweated profusely, bowed her head and faced the wall, silently not daring to speak. After a short while, the groom approached and pulled the girl's sleeve, repeatedly urging her. The girl, unable to avoid it, had no choice but to undress and enter the bed. The new blade was being tested, its sharpness evident; the girl, remembering the aunt's words, deeply defended herself, but as soon as she touched him, she found it difficult to resist, further believing the aunt's words were true, she lay down and firmly resisted, not allowing him to fully use his instrument; but the groom's desire was at its peak, making it difficult to stop. The girl, with no choice, deceived him, saying, "Our married life will be long. I just have a slight illness; once I recover, I will do whatever you want, and I will not refuse again to go against your wishes." The groom, upon hearing this, felt pity and stopped. The girl was happy to be spared and secretly rejoiced at her rebirth.

    276

    伺新郎睡熟，托以溲溺，潜开后门，将窜归谋之嫂氏，转达于翁，愿长侍于膝下，没齿不嫁，以全性命。天明，农家子醒，意女溲溺，呼之不应，急着衣起觇之，阒其无人，惊呼，家人皆兴。知开后门窜走，急遣人往翁家问之，云：“昨方吉期，何得遽归！”彼此惊讶，难测其由。惟嫂氏心知有异，默笑不言。是夜大雪盈尺，共遵其雪迹寻之。道旁故有一眢井，群议暮夜独行，雪光迷炫，保毋失足堕落，乃缒一人下井窥视，果有一尸，大骇，意必是女。拽起觇之，非女也，乃僧也，囟顶劈裂，血痕犹新。众人相觑，益深骇愕。知难隐匿，遂牵连而诉诸官，穷极研讯，卒无联兆。历久轇轕，不能剖决。越五年，翁有族子至豫经纪，路过一市，忽见爱儿在此当垆贳酒，怪为面似，迫审良然。默识其地，归以报，翁即自驰往迹之。女方在门首梳发，见翁至，大惊。翁前持抱泣曰：“儿何至此，累吾实甚。”女亦泣。既诰至此之由，女具告之。

    Explanation

    276

    Waiting for the groom to fall asleep, she feigned the need to urinate, secretly opened the back door, and intended to flee to consult her sister-in-law, who would then relay the message to her father-in-law, expressing her wish to serve at his feet for life, never to marry, in order to preserve her life. At dawn, the farmer's son awoke, thinking she had gone to urinate, and called for her, but she did not respond. He quickly dressed and went to check, finding the room empty. He shouted in alarm, and the whole family awoke. Realizing she had opened the back door and fled, he sent people immediately to his father-in-law’s house to inquire. They said, “Yesterday was the wedding day—how could she return so soon?” Both sides were surprised, unable to discern the cause. Only the sister-in-law knew something was amiss, silently smiling but saying nothing.

    That night, a foot of snow fell, and they all followed the snow trail in search of her. By the roadside, there was an old abandoned well. They discussed the dangers of walking alone at night in the snowlight, fearing she might have lost her footing and fallen in. So they lowered one person down the well to look inside. Indeed, there was a corpse, which greatly shocked them—they thought it must be the girl. They pulled the body up to examine it, but it was not the girl; it was a monk, his skull split open, with fresh bloodstains. The crowd looked at one another, growing even more shocked and bewildered. Realizing it would be difficult to hide the truth, they implicated others and reported the matter to the authorities. The officials thoroughly investigated, but in the end found no evidence of connection. The case remained unresolved for a long time, entangled and unclarified.

    Five years later, the father-in-law’s cousin traveled to Yu for business. Passing through a market, he suddenly saw his beloved daughter there selling wine at a counter. He was startled by her resemblance and closely examined her, confirming it was indeed her. Remembering the location, he returned to report it, and the father-in-law immediately rode there to follow up. The girl was combing her hair at the door when she saw her father-in-law arrive, and was greatly startled. The father-in-law ran forward, embraced her, and wept, saying, “How could you end up here? You have greatly burdened me.” The girl also wept. When asked about the reason for her being there, she fully explained the whole story.

    277

    盖随某乙来此，贳酒营生，颇称小有，翁佯为大喜。俄顷乙至，女使拜父，居然称翁婿焉，情甚亲昵。问：“讼事结束未？”绐以早结，农家予已别娶多年，今抱子矣。乙乃放心。翁便讽女宜偕乙归里，女谋于乙，乙以为无事，遂治装偕女归。翁既到家，即密诣县上状，遣隶拘乙至，讯得巅末，其案乃结。先是，爱儿夜窜时，雪迷失路，堕眢井中，呼救。某寺僧晨出募斋，闻知女子，大喜，正将缒绳下拽；某乙故里中无赖，夜博方毕，过此见之，遂与僧同拽起。悦女之色，欲挟以私奔，虑僧败露，乘其不意，取扁杖当头力劈，僧痛楚仆地，乃拖入井中，然后以言胁女，偕遁至河南，竟成夫妇。官乃断以乙抵僧罪，爱儿仍归原夫；以嫂氏谑语起衅，令批其颊以示薄惩，人皆称快。厥后，嫂氏两颊因挞成创，终身脓腐，臭不可迩，邻里鄙其为人，都置不齿。爱儿既仍归农家子，夫妇重聚，皆知为嫂氏所骗，伉俪倍笃，由此衔嫂入骨，毕世不与通庆吊。

    Explanation

    277

    A certain man named Yi came to this place, where he ran a wine business and became fairly prosperous. The old man pretended to be very happy. Shortly after, Yi arrived, and the daughter paid respects to her father, unexpectedly calling him both father-in-law and son-in-law. They were very close. The old man asked, "Has the legal case been resolved?" Yi deceived him, saying it had ended early, and that the farmer had already remarried for many years and now had a child. Yi then felt at ease. The old man then hinted to the daughter that she should go back with Yi to their hometown. The daughter consulted with Yi, who thought there was nothing wrong, so they prepared and went back together.

    Once the old man arrived home, he immediately secretly went to the county to file a report, sending officials to arrest Yi. After interrogating, they got to the bottom of the matter, and the case was finally resolved. Previously, the beloved child had run away at night, lost in the snow, and fell into a dry well, calling for help. A monk from a certain temple went out in the morning to collect alms, heard about the woman, and was very happy, about to lower a rope to pull her up. A ruffian from Yi's hometown, who had finished gambling at night, passed by and saw her, then pulled her up together with the monk. Attracted to the woman's appearance, he wanted to abduct her and run away, fearing the monk would reveal it, took advantage of his lack of suspicion, took a flat stick and struck him hard on the head, causing the monk to fall to the ground in pain, then dragged him into the well. He then threatened the woman with words, fled together to Henan, and eventually became husband and wife.

    The official ruled that Yi should be punished for the monk's crime, and the beloved child returned to her original husband. Because of the teasing words of the sister-in-law, the official ordered her to be slapped on the cheek as a light punishment, and everyone felt relieved. Later, the sister-in-law's cheeks were bruised and formed sores, remaining infected and rotten for life, smelling so bad that it was unbearable to be near. Neighbors looked down on her character and disregarded her completely. The beloved child returned to her original husband, and the couple reunited. Everyone knew they were tricked by the sister-in-law, and their marital bond became even stronger. Therefore, they deeply hated the sister-in-law and never communicated with her again, not even for celebrations or condolences.

    278

    里乘子曰：妇人群居，谑浪笑傲，殊足滋事，况以之施于发才覆额之小女郎，更难保其无意外之患。爱儿之狱，实由嫂氏恶谑阶厉，犹幸轇轕五年不能剖决，赖族子无心路遇，报翁赚归，杀人者抵，疑狱顿雪，可称快事。迨至旧弦重续，琴瑟永调，追忆畴昔恶谑之言，至是始悟其妄；衔嫂入骨，永绝庆吊，固其宜也。观嫂氏批颊成创，孽报显然。似此恶作剧，生虽已惨受官刑，窃恐死犹不免下拔舌地狱也。闺阁闻之，可不互相警戒欤！
    　　
    　　变驿马
    　　
    　　倪次郊运同，宰陕西兴平县时，有吏来白，养济院某叟病革，顾谓同侣，手足俱为纳履。或谓：“汝足业已着履，手何须此？”叟曰：“否否，缘平日受豢养恩，一无报效，今将变驿马图报于来世，非履何以驰驱耶？”爰手足俱为纳以瓷碗，寻毙。观此，凡受主人豢养恩者，当及时酬报，庶免偿补于来世耳！

    Explanation

    278

    The sage of the village said: When women gather together, they joke and laugh boisterously, which is sufficient to cause trouble. How much more so when such behavior is directed at young girls with hair barely covering their foreheads, making it even harder to ensure there are no unexpected dangers. The case involving the beloved child was actually due to the wicked teasing of the sister-in-law. Fortunately, the case was entangled for five years without resolution, but thanks to an unthinking encounter with a cousin, the grandfather was tricked back home. The killer was punished, and the suspicious case was suddenly cleared, which can be called a pleasant event. Until the old strings were renewed, and the qin and se were always in harmony, reflecting on past wicked teasing, only now did they realize it was false. Holding a grudge against the sister-in-law to the bone, forever severing all greetings and condolences, it is indeed appropriate. Observing the sister-in-law's cheek being slapped, the retribution was clearly evident. Such wicked pranks, though already suffering a cruel punishment, secretly fearing that in death one might still not escape the hell of being pulled from the tongue. Women should not fail to warn each other.

    Change of Horses

    When Ni Cixiao, the magistrate of Xingping County in Shaanxi, was in office, an official came to report that an old man in the Relief Hospital was gravely ill. He looked at his companions and said, "Both my hands and feet are covered with shoes." Someone asked, "Your feet are already wearing shoes, why do you need hands for this?" The old man replied, "No, no. Because I have received the kindness of being raised and cared for, but I have not repaid it in any way. Now, I will transform into a horse in the afterlife to repay this favor, so without shoes, how can I run and drive?" Thus, he covered both his hands and feet with porcelain bowls. Soon after, he died. From this, we see that anyone who has received the kindness of being raised and cared for by a master should repay it promptly, so as to avoid having to make up for it in the afterlife.

    279

    厨媪
    　　
    　　吾皖藩司书吏，多金陵人。饮食起居，享过豪贵，其侪日以酒食相征逐。道光丙申夏，藩吏某甲家，招集侪侣，醵金为撇兰之会。既敛钱付厨媪，使市酒脯，至肆，雷雨大作，电光辄烛媪衣袂，媪骇甚，急趋回家，雷声随之，隆隆然响震四壁。佥谓媪有隐慝，使速自暴白，求神赦宥。媪自念生平殊无甚作恶，沉思久之，乃赦颜谓众曰：“顷身入月，仓卒间取破《宪书》缠诸秽处，得毋是耶？”众闻而诃曰：“是矣！”急命取出，以净水濯而焚之；佥跽地代媪求神恕其无知，雷乃止。后视筐中所敛钱，皆为雷火镕成铜丸，称钱之多寡以判丸之大小；内惟二百绳穿无恙，验之，盖西席某先生所出一分也。

    Explanation

    279

    A Cook Servant

    The clerks in the provincial administration of Anhui were mostly from Nanjing. Their diet and daily life were as luxurious as those of the wealthy and noble, and they often gathered for feasts and banquets. In the summer of 1826, during the reign of Emperor Daoguang, a certain official in the provincial administration, Mr. Jia, invited his colleagues to collect money for a gathering to pluck orchids. After collecting the money and giving it to the cook to buy food and drink, they went to the market. Suddenly, a thunderstorm broke out, and lightning illuminated the cook's sleeves. The cook was extremely frightened and hurriedly returned home. The thunder followed her, shaking the walls with its loud rumble. Everyone suspected the cook of some hidden misdeed and urged her to quickly confess, asking the gods to forgive her. The cook thought she had not committed any serious wrongdoings in her life and, after much reflection, spoke to the crowd with a relieved expression. She said, "Recently, I entered the period of menstruation and, in a hurry, wrapped an old almanac in a dirty place. Could this be the reason?" The crowd heard and scolded her, saying, "Yes!" They quickly ordered her to take it out, wash it with clean water, and burn it. Everyone knelt to ask the gods to forgive the cook for her ignorance, and the thunder stopped. Later, they looked at the money collected in the basket and found that all of it had been melted into copper balls by the lightning. The size of the copper balls was determined by the amount of money. Only 200 strings of coins remained intact. Upon checking, it was found that these were the contributions of a certain teacher, Mr. Jia.

    280

    里乘子曰：厨媪所为，悖谬已极，雷所以不遽击之者，恕其懵懂无知耳。敛钱为铜丸，惟西席某先生一分二百文绳穿无恙，其他阿堵物来历可想！即此小事，仓卒间尚有甄别，所以为神！
    　　
    　　玄坛
    　　
    　　世俗商贾所祀黑虎玄坛，称赵大元帅，其实非也。神姓陈氏，初当捕役。相传捕役至穿窬家，类皆奉如贵官。陈一日至穿窬某甲家，时已二鼓，甲虑无以款客，家惟蓄一牝鸡，因与妻谋，诘旦将伸一脔之敬，时抱卵方鷧。陈夜闻母鸡作人言，训其子曰：“吾命明日合休，素受主人豢养恩，鼎镬所不敢辞；惟念尔曹尚在哺乳，未免不能忘怀！此后尔曹须自慎重，无事在家，勿妄出门，恐鹰抓狸攫，致罹非命，切记切记！勿似我在日有所恃也！”诸雏一一诺之，声甚凄楚。陈闻而悯之。

    Explanation

    280

    The Wise Man of the Village said: The actions of the kitchen woman were utterly absurd, and the reason why the thunder did not immediately strike her was out of mercy for her ignorance and foolishness. The money was collected and melted into copper balls, with only one hundred and twenty coins strung together without any issue; the origin of other valuables can be imagined! Even in this small matter, there was still discernment in a hurry, which is why he is a god!

    Xuantan

    Ordinary merchants worship the Black Tiger Xuantan, calling him Zhao Daxuanyuan, but this is not actually the case. The deity's surname is Chen, and he was originally a police officer. It is said that when police officers visited thieves' houses, they were all treated like high-ranking officials. One day, Chen visited the house of a thief named Jia, and it was already two drums (2 AM). Jia was worried about not having enough to entertain guests, and his home only had a hen. So he planned with his wife to offer a small portion of respect the next day, while the hen was incubating eggs. Chen heard the hen speak human words at night and instructed her chicks: "My fate is to die tomorrow; I have always received the master's kindness, and I would not dare to avoid the cauldron and pot. I only think of you all still being nursed, and I cannot help but be concerned! From now on, you must be careful, stay at home when there is nothing to do, do not go out recklessly, fear being caught by eagles or cats, to suffer an untimely death. Remember this! Do not rely on anything like I did when I was alive!" The chicks all nodded in agreement, and their voices were very sorrowful. Chen heard this and felt pity for them.

    281

    明发将行，甲再三坚留不可，并戒毋得杀鸡，千万叮咛而别。陈由此顿大悔悟，闻某寺长老高行，往求皈依。长老察其意诚，因与陈约：必忍饿七日，勺水不咽，并将各瓮水担满，方许摩顶。陈谨受教。寺中僧众数百人，各瓮水不下百石，陈既饿七日，将荷扁丈出而担水，忽见寺门外来一黑虎，眈眈相视，陈叱曰：“我一切皆空，岂畏咥者！俟担水毕，施汝一斋可也。”虎果掉头去。陈担水毕，出门将供虎食，但见黑虎复来，帖耳摇尾，驯若犬马，不似前其欲逐逐。陈异之，因祝曰：“岂天赐我坐骑乎？”虎颔之。陈乃横杖跨虎而去。世所塑像手持金鞭，即扁杖也。
    　　
    　　里乘子曰：此陈祥浦为陈西堂言者，西堂为予述之，以其近似，故笔之。嗟乎！一念之善，顿悟大道；当日不忍一脔，遂致万世血食遍天下。可见圣贤仙佛，皆同此好生之心也。

    Explanation

    281

    Brightly he set out to travel, but Jia repeatedly insisted but could not persuade him, and warned him not to kill the chicken, repeatedly admonishing and parting ways. Chen suddenly greatly repented and awakened, hearing that there was an elder with high virtue in a certain temple, he went to seek refuge. The elder observed his sincere intention and made an agreement with Chen: he must endure hunger for seven days, not even swallow a drop of water, and fill all the jars with water before he would be allowed to touch the top of his head. Chen respectfully accepted the instruction. There were hundreds of monks in the temple, and each jar contained no less than a hundred shi of water. After Chen had fasted for seven days, he shouldered his staff and went out to carry the water. Suddenly, he saw a black tiger approaching the temple gate, staring intently at him. Chen shouted, "All is emptiness, how can I fear those who would devour me! Wait until the water is carried, and I will give you a meal." The tiger indeed turned away. When Chen finished carrying the water, he went out to prepare the tiger's food, but saw the black tiger return, calm and wagging its tail, tame like a dog or horse, not like before, eager to chase. Chen found it strange and said, "Is it heaven granting me a steed?" The tiger nodded. Chen then crossed his staff and rode the tiger away. The statues in the world hold golden whips, which are the same as the staff.

    The commentator said: This story was told by Chen Xiangpu to Chen Xitang, who recounted it to me because it was similar. Ah! A single good thought can lead to sudden enlightenment; that day, unwilling to eat a morsel, led to the widespread offering of sacrifices throughout the world. It is evident that sages, saints, immortals, and Buddhas all share this heart for preserving life.

    282

    邑人某甲
    　　
    　　邑人某甲，岁除，无以为计，家仅存一旧竹榻，妻使负往西村富翁家，冀易升斗粟。富翁爱其光如髹漆，言定以斗米千钱易之。会邻人来，翁指以相示，且鸣得意。邻人冷笑曰：“君尝自矜算无遗策，然以有用之资易此不时之物，其计无乃太左，尚何得意之有！”富翁素固啬吝，果悔而反齿。甲无奈，负竹榻归。与妻言之，且咒且泣，相对计穷，中夜俱投缳死。翌日元旦，邻人祀祖甫毕，忽见甲夫妇披发喋血入门，戟手指而詈曰：“我以物易钱米，干汝甚事！富翁已有成说，为汝一言所偾，致我夫妻计穷，戕于非命，汝心安乎？今已诉于冥府，亦不令汝家泰然度岁也！”言讫，直前相攫，邻人骇绝倒地。妻子趋扶床上，但见色如死灰，以手挠心自责曰：“吾过矣！吾过矣！”寻卒。无何，妻亦病卒。其子尚未成童，家产尽为匪人所赚，后竟不能成家矣。

    Explanation

    282

    A Resident of the Town
