    During the eve of the lunar New Year, a resident of the town, named Mr. A, had no means to cope with his dire situation. His family had only an old bamboo bed left. His wife sent him to the rich man's house in the west village, hoping to exchange it for a small amount of grain. The rich man admired the rich, polished appearance of the bamboo bed and agreed to exchange it for a measure of rice and a thousand coins. Just as a neighbor arrived, the rich man pointed the bamboo bed out to show, and boasted of his satisfaction. The neighbor coldly said, "You often boasted of your infallible plans, but to exchange valuable assets for something useless, your plan is surely too wrong, how can you still feel satisfied?" The rich man, who was usually stingy, indeed regretted and reneged. Mr. A, having no choice, carried the bamboo bed back home. He told his wife about it, cursing and weeping, and they were both at a loss, eventually hanging themselves in the middle of the night. The next day, on New Year's Day, the neighbor had just finished ancestor worship when he suddenly saw Mr. A and his wife, with disheveled hair and bloody, entering the door, pointing with his finger and cursing, "I exchanged goods for money and grain, what business is it of yours! The rich man had already made a deal, was undone by your words, leading us to a dead end, and we died an untimely death, are you at peace? Now I have reported to the underworld, and I will not let your family enjoy the New Year peacefully!" After saying this, he grabbed the neighbor directly, who was shocked and fell to the ground. His wife and children hurried to support him on the bed, but saw his face ashen, scratching his heart and blaming himself, saying, "I was wrong! I was wrong!" Soon after, he died. Not long after, his wife also died of illness. His son was still a child, and all the family property was taken by thieves, and he could never establish a family.

    283

    里乘子曰：富翁素性吝啬，高兴其偶然也。邻人如解事，迎机赞成固妙；不则缄口不置一词可也。乃冷笑相诮，语直刺心，彼吝啬人闻之，焉得不反齿？嗟乎！某甲当计无所之之时，甫得生机，倏尔失望，斯时邻人试扪心自问：富翁因我反齿，于人固有损矣，于己复何益乎？卒致夫妻泣咒，相对俱尽，能不抱恨泉壤也哉！先师孔子有云：“君子成人之美。”文昌帝君《阴骘文》有云：“行时时之方便，积种种之阴功。”观邻人一言偾事，亦何不成人美，不行方便乃尔耶？其获惨报也固宜！

    Explanation

    283

    Master Li Cheng says: The rich man is inherently miserly, and he is happy about things happening by chance. If the neighbor understands the situation, it is good to seize the opportunity to support or encourage him; otherwise, keeping silent and not uttering a word is also acceptable. Instead, the neighbor mocks with a cold laugh, speaking words that directly pierce the heart. How could the miser not turn against it upon hearing it? Alas! When a person is at a loss and suddenly gains hope, only to be disappointed, at that moment, the neighbor should question himself: The rich man turns against me because of my words, which indeed harms others, but what benefit does it bring to myself? In the end, the couple weeps and curses, facing each other until they are both gone, how could they not carry regret into the grave? The Master Confucius said: "A gentleman helps others to achieve their good deeds." The God of Literature and Culture in the "Yinzhi Wen" said: "Perform acts of kindness at all times and accumulate various merits." Observing the neighbor's words that ruin the matter, how could they not help others to achieve their good deeds and perform acts of kindness? It is indeed fitting that they receive severe retribution!

    284

    秦氏妇
    　　
    　　姑苏有秦与蔡二姓，自祖以来合计在楚贸易，后生业日隆，资盈百万。乡人蒋某羡而且嫉。会秦有悍妇，武断烈于须眉，蒋因以甘言之，唆与蔡氏割业分资。妇信之，遂搆衅兴讼。不十年，两姓家业，凋败殆尽。悍妇忽膺奇疾，矢从口出，医不能治；未几，蒋患舌创，中断如割，狂叫一声而逝。是可为听信谗言及阴险搆衅者戒。此葛菊人所言，两姓皆与菊人有瓜葛也。

    Explanation

    284

    A Woman of the Qin Family

    In Suzhou, there were two families, the Qin and the Cai, who had been engaged in trade in the state of Chu since the founding of their families. Over time, their businesses grew increasingly prosperous, and their wealth accumulated to a million. A local man named Jiang envied and was jealous of them. Coincidentally, the Qin family had a domineering and fierce wife, who was more fierce than men. Jiang, taking advantage of this, used sweet words to manipulate her, persuading her to cut ties with the Cai family and divide their wealth. The wife believed him and thus caused disputes and lawsuits. Within ten years, the fortunes of both families were almost completely ruined. The domineering wife suddenly fell ill with a strange disease, with arrows shooting out of her mouth, and the doctors could not treat her. Soon after, Jiang himself developed a tongue ulcer, which felt as if it were being cut in half, and he screamed wildly once before dying. This serves as a warning to those who listen to slander and engage in secretly and maliciously causing disputes. This story was told by Ge Juren, and both families were connected to him by distant relationships.

    285

    里乘子曰：两人奇疾，足见冥罚之当。
    　　
    　　某媪
    　　
    　　姑苏洞庭山寡媪某氏，每遇婺妇，必设法怂恿，说其改醮，己则从中渔利，藉以糊口。他日，自苏州城中执柯毕，趁舟旋家。舟至中流，忽雷雨大作，云雾模糊，人不能自见其掌。须臾，雨霁雷止，遍稽舟中不见某媪，群甚讶之。既舟将泊岸，闻芦苇中有人呼救声，群审其音，疑是某媪，迹之果然。但见泥涂遍体，狼藉不成人形。盖天怒其平日败人名节，姑薄谴以示警也。媪后卒改为善，不敢复为冯妇矣。此亦葛菊人言。

    Explanation

    285

    里乘子说：两人得了奇怪的病，足以看出天谴的恰当。

    某寡妇
    姑苏洞庭山的寡妇某氏，每当遇到已婚妇女，必定设法怂恿她们改嫁，自己则从中渔利，以此维持生计。有一天，她从苏州城中完成了一桩婚事后，乘船回家。船行至江心，忽然雷雨大作，云雾弥漫，人们连自己的手掌都看不见。不久，雨停雷止，大家在船上四处寻找，却不见某氏寡妇的踪影，众人十分惊讶。船将靠岸时，听到芦苇丛中有人呼救，众人听声音，怀疑是某氏寡妇，追踪过去果然如此。只见她全身泥涂，狼狈不堪，不成人形。原来天怒她平日败坏他人名节，所以轻罚她以示警告。寡妇后来终于改过自新，不敢再像冯妇那样行事了。这也是葛菊人所说。

    286

    里乘子曰：此媪卒改为善，较胜冯妇。向使薄谴而不知自警，雷岂能终恕之哉！天道许人自新，省过者皆当以媪为法。
    　　
    　　吴明府
    　　
    　　山右吴明府咸之，初以进士出为县令，颇著政声。丁艰服阙，选直隶新城县令。到任匝月，薄暮，见一人毡笠布衫，面目薰黑，徘徊堂皇，叱问：“谁何？”其人怒目答曰：“我。踪迹到处，今始得之，不汝恕矣！”直前相扑。吴大骇，疾呼役隶，叱命缚之，役隶问：“所缚何人？”吴怒让之曰：“立堂皇者，尔曹岂不见耶！”群谓不见，吴知是鬼物，大恐，疾趋入内。其人亦尾其后，手挈一棒，长尺有半，立吴左右。

    Explanation

    286

    Lǐ Chéngzǐ said: This old woman finally changed for the better, surpassing Feng Fu. Had she been lightly punished and not self-warned, how could Thunder (a deity) have finally forgiven her! Heaven's way allows people to reform themselves; those who reflect on their mistakes should all take this old woman as a model.

    Mr. Wu, the magistrate of Shanxi,
    Initially appointed as a county magistrate after passing the imperial examinations, he gained a good reputation for his governance. After completing mourning for a deceased parent, he was selected to serve as the magistrate of Xincheng County in Zhili. After one month in office, late in the afternoon, he saw a person wearing a felt cap and a cotton robe, with a sooty black face, lingering in the hall. He shouted and asked, "Who are you?" The person answered with a furious look, "I. Traced everywhere, now finally found me, will not forgive you!" The person charged forward to wrestle. Mr. Wu was greatly frightened and shouted urgently for the servants, ordering them to bind him. The servants asked, "Who is the person being bound?" Mr. Wu angrily scolded them, saying, "The person standing in the hall, haven't you seen him?" They all said they didn't see. Mr. Wu knew it was a ghost and was greatly frightened, quickly retreating inside. The person followed him, holding a stick, one and a half feet long, standing on either side of Mr. Wu.

    287

    自是，凡饮食时，即从棒横截吴颔下，滴水粒粟，不能下咽。且曰：“汝昔以此物戕我命，我今亦以此物还报之，不亦可乎！”吴忧惧成疾，署中各友书房迁避几遍，皆不能免。有时瞰其人不在侧，急索饮食，甫入唇，其人即至，骂曰：“汝尚欲饮食求生耶！”以棒横截如初，寻以饿死。或曰：“吴初任时，曾因赃以棒痛搒一人，并绝其粒，瘐死狱中，一时冤之。故其鬼来索偿也。”然耶？否耶？有民社之责者，览之亦可自警。吾友葛少莪司马，在吴幕中，目所亲睹，同游滦中为予言。
    　　
    　　里乘子曰：或谓吴初在都中，贷乡人某万金，得官后，携其人至任，约偿其债。既以数甚巨，吝欲负约，遂谋构其人于法，而潜令狱卒绝其粒，竟致饿死。果尔，天良丧尽，鬼即以其道还治其身，可谓冤冤相报，丝毫不爽。

    Explanation

    287

    From then on, whenever Wu was eating, someone would use a stick to block his throat, preventing even a drop of water or a grain of rice from going down. They would also say, "You once used this to take my life; I will now use this to repay you. Isn't that just?" Wu became ill from worry and fear. His friends in the office moved their study rooms several times to avoid him, but none could escape. Sometimes, when the person was not nearby, Wu would quickly seek food, but as soon as it entered his mouth, the person would arrive and scold, "Do you still want to eat and live?" They would then block his throat again as before, and eventually, he starved to death. Some said, "When Wu first took office, he once beat someone severely with a stick for a bribe and deprived them of food, leading to their death in prison from neglect. People felt sorry for him at the time, so his ghost came to seek retribution." Is this true? Or is it not? Those in charge of local administration, when reading this, can also take it as a warning. My friend, General Ge Shao'e, who was in Wu's entourage, saw this with his own eyes and told me while traveling together in Puan.

    Li Chengzi said: Some say that when Wu was in the capital, he borrowed ten thousand gold from a villager. After getting a government position, he took the person to his post and agreed to repay the debt. Since the amount was very large, he was reluctant to keep the agreement. He then plotted to have the person charged with a crime and secretly ordered the prison guards to deprive them of food, ultimately leading to their starvation and death. If that is true, he had lost all sense of morality, and the ghost then repaid him in kind. This can be called a cycle of vengeance, without any deviation.

    288

    陈司马
    　　
    　　绍兴陈鑑，伉爽卞急，向为直隶制军司奏牍，品学为当道所倚重。族人有庆陛者，以同知需次，鑑为游扬补官。后，鑑亦纳资除大名同知，寻以卓异受上考。适庆陛任满，例得迁擢，时正定守出缺，心颇觊觎，恐鑑夺其席，遂造蜚语，嗾言官劾之，鑑以议停迁。后访知为庆陛之谋，以恩施而得仇报，恚愤而卒。事既传播，自大府以及寅属，佥不直庆陛，遂以才具庸劣，勒令休致回籍。甫抵家门，即谓左右云：“陈司马同我一路来，尔曹速汛扫厅事，小心款待。”左右问：“陈司马何在？”曰，“已升阶矣，尔等岂不见耶？”家人见其神情恍惚，情知有异，急扶至寝室。但闻喃喃呓语，谓：“陈司马要我对质，我去矣。”寻卒。无何，龙虎山天师牒至，正定府城隍，已补绍兴陈鑑。
    　　
    　　里乘子曰：可见阴曹黜陟，与阳世不殊。鑑既真除冥官，知庆陛亦必有应得之罪也。然则忮刻者观此，不亦可废然自反哉！此亦陈西堂言。

    Explanation

    288

    Chen the Military Governor

    Chen Jian of Shaoxing was bold, straightforward, and quick-tempered. He once served as a scribe for the military governor of Zhili and was highly regarded for his character and learning by those in power. Among his relatives was a man named Qingbi, who was waiting for an official position as an assistant governor. Chen Jian recommended him for the post. Later, Chen Jian himself paid money to secure the position of assistant governor of Daming. Soon after, he was evaluated as outstanding and received a high rating. At the time, Qingbi's term was up and he was eligible for promotion, and the position of governor of Zhengding was vacant. Qingbi coveted the position and feared that Chen Jian would take it, so he fabricated rumors and incited officials to impeach Chen Jian. Chen Jian was thus prevented from being promoted. Later, it was discovered that Qingbi had orchestrated the slander. Chen Jian, feeling wronged and angry, died shortly after. The incident spread, and from the highest officials to those in lower positions, all condemned Qingbi. He was eventually forced to retire and return to his hometown due to his mediocre abilities. Upon arriving home, he immediately told his attendants, "Chen the Military Governor is coming with me. You all should quickly clean the hall and be careful to treat him well." The attendants asked, "Where is Chen the Military Governor?" He replied, "He has already ascended the steps. Haven't you seen him?" Seeing his confused state, the family realized something was amiss and quickly helped him to his bedroom. They heard him muttering, "Chen the Military Governor wants to confront me. I am leaving." Soon after, he died. Shortly thereafter, an edict arrived from the Taoist priest of Longhu Mountain, stating that the city god of Zhengding had been appointed as the new Chen Jian from Shaoxing.

    The author of the local chronicle said: It is evident that the promotion and demotion in the afterlife are no different from those in the mortal world. Since Chen Jian was truly appointed as an official in the afterlife, it is clear that Qingbi must have deserved his punishment. Therefore, those who are jealous and harsh should reflect on this and reconsider their actions. This is also what Chen Xitang said.

    289

    林远村方伯
    　　
    　　吾皖林远村方伯之望，赋性端方，束躬严谨。自言开藩甘肃时，督师攻剿回匪，驿馆后有楼，颇觉幽静，爰命傔从解装其上。夜漏三下，甫就寝，忽闻楼梯窸窣有声，一女子搴帷遽入，走傍榻前，含睇微笑，妖艳异常，粉香扑鼻。方伯知为妖物，将取床头宝剑击之。女子睨之笑曰：“公何为者，而忍恶作剧耶！”方伯虑其为祟，急掣剑在手，女子却步摇手曰：“咄咄，公勿恶作剧，我去！我去！”言讫，然不见。后闻人言，楼固有狐。然方伯居此月馀，亦竟不敢再至矣。
    　　
    　　里乘子曰：老子谓：“不见所欲，则其心不乱。”吾谓：见所欲，则其心尤不可乱。左氏谓：“妖由人兴。”彼妖物之来，而能如方伯之心不少乱，虽妖物亦无如何矣。谚有之曰：“色不迷人人自迷。”凡见色而心辄乱者，无不为所迷矣，况妖也耶！噫嘻！可不慎哉！可不慎哉！

    Explanation

    289

    Magistrate Lin Yuan Cun

    Our Anhui's Magistrate Lin Yuan Cun, by nature upright and fair, was strict in his self-discipline. He recounted that when he was opening a province in Gansu and supervising the army to attack and suppress Hui rebels, there was a tower at the rear of the official inn, which was quite secluded. He ordered his attendants to unpack their belongings there. At the third watch of the night, just as he was about to sleep, he suddenly heard the sound of footsteps on the stairs. A woman tore aside the curtain and entered hastily, walking beside the bed, smiling with a glance, and her beauty was extraordinary, with the scent of powder filling the air. Knowing she was an evil spirit, Magistrate Lin was about to take the sword from the bed to strike her. The woman glanced at him and laughed, saying, "What are you doing, and why are you so willing to play tricks?" Worried that she might bring misfortune, Magistrate Lin quickly drew his sword, and the woman stepped back and shook her hands, saying, "Hmph, you don't want to play tricks, I'm leaving! I'm leaving!" After she spoke, she vanished without a trace. Later, he heard people say that the tower was indeed haunted by a fox. Despite this, Magistrate Lin stayed there for over a month and never dared to return again.

    The commentator said: Laozi said, "If one does not see what one desires, then the heart will not be disturbed." I say: If one sees what one desires, then the heart is even more difficult to keep calm. The Zuo Zhuan says, "Evil spirits arise from human actions." When evil spirits come, if one's heart remains undisturbed, even evil spirits have no way to affect one. There is a saying, "Beauty does not seduce others, but it seduces oneself." Anyone who sees beauty and their heart becomes chaotic will inevitably be bewitched, let alone by an evil spirit! Ah! How cautious one must be!

    290

    浙江学使署狐
    　　
    　　太湖李南叔学士国杞视学浙江，招予襄校试卷。署有楼三楹，李诗屏大令居其东，予居其西。楼上旧有狐仙，往往闻履声槖槖，相处既久，亦不以为怪。楼之右有定香亭，四面环水，绕岸遍栽修竹，暑月，同人纳凉其中，说鬼谈天，嗢噱甚乐。一日清晨，闻楼上嘤嘤聚哭，其声甚繁，不解其故。忽汛埽夫来言，有物大于猫，被犬噬毙亭畔。同人趋视，知为狐。予恐其类迁怒修怨，因共焚香致祝，谓：“犬无知，有伤其类，于人无与。汝曹如欲雪愤，但请择所噬之犬而自诛之可也。”越日，果有一犬，身受多伤而殪。先是，西蜀卓海帆相国督学时，豢西犬三十馀头以防穿窬。同人夜话亭中，狐每坐檐牙窃听，是夜人散，狐亦踽踽将归，不虞为犬所戕云。

    Explanation

    290

    A Fox at the Zhejiang Provincial Examination Office

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    291

    里乘子曰：曩在定香亭暑夜纳凉，同人以予年才弱冠，辄举丛书所载鬼狐事相戏。予所居官舍后楼西楹，天热，窗棂尽除，遍悬虾须帘。倏届新秋，暴雨初霁，窗外梧桐数株，月影娟娟。同人散后，予归就寝，倚枕反侧不能成寐，忽隐隐闻环珮声，见帘外一女子，靓妆高髻，徘徊延伫，既以掌拍槛者三。心知为仙，急摄神敛虑，默默致祝，谓：“同人戏语，究属无稽，鲰生虽愚，讵敢唐突！仙如有灵，伏惟亮察。”祝毕，恍睹衣袂翩跹，飘然而去。至今思之，犹为悚惧。

    Explanation

    291

    Li Chengzi said: In the past, during a hot summer night at Dingxiang Pavilion, my fellow scholars, seeing that I was still young, would often tell me stories of ghosts and fox spirits from the compendiums as a form of entertainment. The room I stayed in was on the west side of the back building of my official residence. It was very hot, so all the window grilles were removed and replaced with fine, transparent curtains. Suddenly, it was the beginning of autumn, and after a heavy rain had just cleared up, there were several Paulownia trees outside the window, casting a gentle, graceful moonlight. After my fellow scholars had dispersed, I returned to my room to sleep, lying on my pillow and turning restlessly, unable to fall asleep. Suddenly, I heard the faint sound of jade pendants, and saw a woman outside the curtain, elegantly dressed with a high hairdo, standing and looking around, and then clapping her hands three times against the railing. I knew she must be a spirit, and quickly focused my mind and suppressed my thoughts, silently offering a prayer: "My fellow scholars' stories are just playful, and ultimately baseless. Though I am foolish, how dare I be presumptuous! If you are a spirit, I hope you will understand." After the prayer, I vaguely saw her robes fluttering, and she disappeared gracefully. Even now, thinking about it, I still feel fearful.

    292

    里乘卷五
    　　
    　　摺差
    　　
    　　四川武弁某，赍摺入都，限日行三百里，惫甚。时冬月望后，白昼苦短。薄暮，路逢古庙，天色已曛，思入庙暂憩，俟月出再行。下马入庙，见衰草败叶，填塞没径，并无僧道；佛殿二重，前殿正中供佛香火断绝；两偏厢房，西已倾圮，东厢房中停枯柩，双扉半掩；殿后小院，桤树数株，皆粗十围，爰系马树间，仍到前殿。瞷佛座下洞然中空，可容一人，四围嵌木板，颇可避风，乃解佩刀置佛龛中，展被座下。甫经偃仰，闻马蹄声自远渐近，倏至庙前，姑就板窍窥之。月裁出山，形状隐约可辨，前一老者乘马，修髯垂胸，年约五十许，气象壮伟；后一少年徒步相从，貌亦魁梧。老者下马，以鞭授少年，相将入庙，系马西楹。

    Explanation

    292

    Volume Five of Li Cheng
    The Difference in Dispatches

    A certain military official from Sichuan carried a memorandum to the capital, with a daily quota of traveling 300 li, and was extremely exhausted. It was after the full moon in the winter month, and the daylight was short. As dusk approached, he encountered an ancient temple on the road, and the sky had already grown dim. Thinking to rest briefly inside the temple until the moon rose and then continue his journey, he dismounted and entered. He saw withered grass and fallen leaves filling the path to the point of blocking it, and there were no monks or Taoists present. The temple had two-tiered halls; in the main hall, the central altar was devoid of incense and offerings. The two side rooms were in poor condition—the western wing had collapsed, while in the eastern wing, a dried-up coffin was placed inside, its double doors half open. Behind the hall was a small courtyard with several qī trees, all with trunks as thick as ten wei (units of circumference). He tied his horse between the trees and returned to the front hall. Peering beneath the Buddha’s seat, he noticed a hollow space large enough to hold one person, with wooden planks embedded around it, offering some protection from the wind. He then removed his sword and placed it in the Buddha’s shrine, spreading his blanket beneath the seat. No sooner had he lain down than he heard the sound of horse hooves coming from afar, gradually growing nearer, until they suddenly arrived at the temple’s entrance. He cautiously peered through the cracks in the wooden planks. The moon had just begun to rise over the mountain, its shape faintly discernible. In front was an elderly man riding a horse, with a long beard hanging down to his chest, appearing to be about fifty years old, with a dignified and imposing presence. Behind him walked a young man, also looking strong and robust. The old man dismounted, handed his whip to the young man, and together they entered the temple, tying the horse to the western pillar.

    293

    老者命铺褥殿阶，居中端坐，少年屏息侍立，意殊惶悚。忽闻老者叱曰：“我自弱冠浪迹江湖，虽藉猎获为生，然必取之有道，丝毫不敢狂悖，妄肆淫杀，有干天遣。至今三十馀年，幸逃法网，清夜扪心，未尝不职此之故。不图汝甫入吾门，便乱吾法。前日之役，若非我独殿后，汝曹二十馀人，能一得生者乎？”少年鞠躬对曰：“唯唯，佥谓幸赖吾师虎威，各叨幸免。”老者又冷笑曰：“后闻其家有少妇，守节抚孤，汝入其室，既污其母，又杀其雏，汝心安乎！似此恶毒残忍，令人发指，皇天有知，决不恕汝！一时侥幸漏网，终必不免于大辟。有日擒获，实为吾党之累！汝试思之，我尚能贷汝乎？”少年闻之，仓皇伏地叩首，曰：“弟子罪该万死，求师息怒，愿受责罚。

    Explanation

    293

    The elder commanded the young man to lay a mat on the steps of the hall, and the elder sat in the center, while the young man stood respectfully, looking very frightened. Suddenly, the elder shouted, saying, "Since I was a young man, I have wandered the rivers and lakes, making a living by hunting. However, I always took what I needed in a righteous way, never acting recklessly or committing excessive killing, which would offend the will of heaven. To this day, after more than thirty years, I have luckily escaped legal punishment. In the quiet of the night, when I reflect on myself, I have never done anything else but follow this principle. I did not expect that you, having just joined my group, would disrupt my rules. In the past battle, if it were not for me standing at the rear, none of you twenty or so could have survived!" The young man bowed respectfully and replied, "Yes, yes, everyone says we were lucky to escape thanks to the tiger-like might of our teacher." The elder then coldly laughed and said, "Later, I heard that there was a young widow in the family, who maintained her chastity and cared for an orphan. You entered her room, defiling her mother and killing her child. Is your conscience at peace? Such cruel and heartless acts make one's hair stand on end. The heavens will know, and they will definitely not forgive you! Escaping temporarily by chance, you will eventually face the death penalty. If you are captured one day, you will truly be a burden to our group! Try to think about it, can I still pardon you?" The young man, upon hearing this, frantically bowed to the ground and said, "As a disciple, I deserve to die a thousand deaths. I plead with the teacher to calm down and am willing to accept punishment."

    294

    ”老者唾其面曰：“囨！汝尚欲求生乎？圣人之道，不外一恕字，汝家亦有妇孺，罹此惨辱，汝心甘乎？毋多言！”拔所佩刀授之曰：“速伏刃以谢寡妇，且慰孺子之魂可也！”少年果受刀，再拜，自刭而死。老者起立，顾尸长叹，拾刀拭血，弢而佩之，又自取褥覆马背，牵出门外，乘之鞭驰而去。某闻之历历，初颇愕悸，以老者所言居然盗不离道，不禁点首称叹。月色将中，不敢再寝，将起赶路，突闻东厢噭然有声，厉如老枭，某大惊，姑再窥之。俄，东厢房门启，一物自内出，长丈许，遍体白毛蒙茸，走至院中，东向望月，目光睒睗，烱若挚电。

    Explanation

    294

    An elderly man spat on his face and said, "Fie! Do you still wish to live? The way of the sages is nothing more than the principle of forgiveness. Your family also has women and children who have suffered such humiliation. Are you willing to accept it? Do not speak more!" He drew the sword he wore and handed it to him, saying, "Quickly cut yourself to apologize to the widow and to comfort the spirit of the child!" The young man indeed took the knife, bowed twice, and cut his own throat, dying. The old man stood up, looked at the body, and sighed deeply. He picked up the knife, wiped the blood, sheathed it, and wore it again. Then he took a mat and covered the horse's back, led it out the door, and rode it away swiftly. I heard it clearly, and at first, I was very surprised and frightened. According to what the old man said, it turns out that thieves do not stray from the right path, and I could not help but nod and praise. The moon was high in the sky, and I dared not sleep again. I was about to get up and continue the journey when suddenly, I heard a loud sound from the east room, as harsh as an old owl. I was greatly surprised and temporarily looked again. Soon, the door of the east room opened, and something came out from inside. It was about a meter long, covered with white, thick fur. It walked to the courtyard, looked east at the moon, its eyes flashing, bright like a lightning bolt.

    295

    俯视地上死尸，拊掌狂笑，试蹴以足，复佝身抱尸起，将首级摘掷地下，褫去衣服，始则就腔饮血，继则咀胾嚼骨，齿牙轧轧，如猫食鼠，久之始尽。又将首级拾起，撩发作浑脱舞，左盘右旋，乐不可支。隐隐遥听村鸡鸣，其物张皇四顾，掷去首级，复对月掀唇狂笑，拊掌者再，徐徐入东厢房去。某毛发森竖，骇汗浃衣，悄起蹑足，将所赍摺束被中，缚负背上，往后院牵马出门，跨鞍疾驰。约行五里许，忽忆所佩刀脱置佛龛，仓卒未及携带。庙中死人尸为怪物所食，仅存首级衣服，日后必为疑谳，刀上故錾己姓名，恐因刀牵连，受累难免；欲返身携取，又恐东厢怪物复出，难与抵敌，踌躇再四，意鸡已鸣，怪物当不足为患，决意策转马头。至庙下马，轻步趋赴佛龛，取刀疾出，果闻东厢房棺盖格格作响，骇甚，飞身上马，绝尘疾驰五十里，东方大明，心始帖然。

    Explanation

    295

    Looking down at the corpse on the ground, he clapped his hands and laughed wildly, then tried to kick it with his foot. He bent over to pick up the corpse, removed the head and threw it to the ground, stripped off the clothes, and began by drinking blood from the body, then chewing on the meat and bones. His teeth ground and gnashed, like a cat eating a mouse, and after a long time, he finished. He then picked up the head again, fluffed out his hair, and danced wildly, turning left and right, unable to contain his joy. Faintly hearing the roosters crowing in the village, the creature looked around in panic, threw away the head, and laughed wildly at the moon, clapping his hands twice. He slowly entered the eastern wing of the house. The person's hair stood on end, drenched in cold sweat, and he quietly got up and tiptoed. He wrapped the documents in cloth and tied them to his back, went to the backyard, led the horse out, and rode away at full speed. After traveling about five miles, he suddenly remembered that the dagger was left behind in the Buddha altar, in haste, not having time to take it. The dead body in the temple was eaten by the monster, leaving only the head and clothes, which would surely become a subject of suspicion and investigation. The dagger had his name engraved on it, fearing that he would be implicated and suffer. Wanting to return to get it, but fearing that the monster in the eastern wing would come out again, making it hard to fight, he hesitated repeatedly, thinking that the roosters had already crowed, and the monster would no longer be a threat. Deciding to turn the horse around, he reached the temple, dismounted, and quickly retrieved the dagger. Hearing the coffin lid creaking in the eastern wing, he was terrified, leaped onto the horse, and galloped away at full speed for fifty miles. As the east grew bright, his heart finally settled.

    296

    里乘子曰：老者殆盗而近于道者欤？所言不外天理人情，此可谓盗不盗，非常盗也已！少年所谓死有馀辜，尸葬怪腹，亦天罚所及也。惟此弁欲图少憩，意外搅扰，不少交睫，所窥二事，是真闻所未闻、见所未见。返身取刀，不为无识，然出险入险，胆略实属不弱。我今闻之，毛发犹为之森竖也。
    　　
    　　欧公子
    　　
    　　粤东慈云庵内供观音大士，香火极盛。比邱尼繁有徒，绾髻缠足，衣饰与人家闺阁无异。托名清净法场，其实色界情天，较勾栏为尤甚。少年美男子无意独身误入，必使身登佛国，生还无望也。有欧公子者，甫成童，貌媲曲逆。春日踏青，偶过庵外，见倚门两尼，一年可十七八，一裁及笄，色皆娇艳，而少者尤幽媚，目盼笑倩，风致天成。童子何知，不觉停趾注目，爱恋綦切。两尼见公子眈眈，长者笑附少者，不知作何耳语，便向招手，公子大喜，趋就之。长者遽挽其袂，向内偕行，少者缓步相从。

    Explanation

    296

    Li Chengzi said: Is the elderly person perhaps a thief but close to the Way? What he said does not go beyond natural law and human sentiment. This can be called a thief who is not a thief, not an ordinary thief! The young man said that death is a just punishment, burying the corpse in a strange belly, it is also a punishment from heaven. Only this hat-wearer wants to take a short rest, unexpected disturbances, not allowing even a moment's sleep. The two things observed are truly things never heard of, never seen before. Turning around to take a knife, not without understanding, yet going from danger into danger, courage and strategy are indeed not weak. I hear it now, and my hair still stands on end.

    Eu Gongzi

    In the Ciyun Temple in Guangdong, the Bodhisattva Guanyin is worshipped, and the incense and fire are extremely prosperous. The number of nuns is numerous, they tie their hair in buns and bind their feet, their clothing and decorations are no different from those of women in the inner chambers of ordinary households. Pretending to be a pure and clean field of Dharma, in reality, it is a realm of desire and emotion, even more so than the brothel. A young and handsome man accidentally enters without intending to, he must ascend to the Buddha's realm, with no hope of returning to life. There was a young man named Eu Gongzi, just becoming a child, his appearance rivals that of Qu Yi. In spring, going on a walk to enjoy the scenery, he accidentally passed by the temple's exterior. He saw two nuns leaning on the door, one was about seventeen or eighteen, the other had just reached the age of笄 (coming of age for women). Both were beautiful and charming, but the younger one was especially subtle and alluring. Her eyes glanced with a charming smile, her grace was naturally formed. What does a child know? Unconsciously, he stopped and gazed, deeply loving and yearning. The two nuns saw the young man staring intently, the elder one smiled and leaned towards the younger one, not knowing what they whispered to each other, then they waved to him. The young man was greatly pleased and hurried to join them. The elder one quickly pulled his sleeve, walked inside together, the younger one followed slowly.

    297

    既至内室，床奁陈设，珍丽异常，炉焚龙涎、百和等香，薰脑袭髓。拉公子并坐榻上，锦衾绣褥，俱极软腻，命小鬟进茶，手捧献公子，展问姓名门第。自言妙梅，法号净影，此间三十六花禅，己行十九，君在此小住，当共相见。公子问：“少者何名？何遽不见？”净影笑曰：“渠名妙莲，法号净香。齿最稚，向羞见生客，熟则可常相聚。君但宽心住此，此间饮食起居，无不可惟君所欲，恐历久厮熟，君当乐死不愿生也。”正絮语间，忽闻屧声杂沓，环佩丁当，遥从门外笑语喧呼曰：“闻十九婢招有嘉客，何秘而独乐，不使人知耶？”净影谓公子曰：“三姊领群姊妹至矣。”俄已入房，生起问讯。

    Explanation

    297

    Upon entering the inner chamber, the bed and dressing table were arranged with extremely precious and beautiful items. The incense of dragon's saliva and a hundred fragrances was burned, and the fragrance permeated the brain and marrow. She invited the young man to sit on the couch with her, and the brocade quilt and embroidered cushion were extremely soft and smooth. She ordered a young servant to bring tea and personally presented it to the young man, asking for his name and family background. She introduced herself as "Wonderful Plum" and her Buddhist name as "Pure Shadow," and mentioned that there were thirty-six flower Buddhas here, and she had met nineteen of them. She said, "You will stay here for a while, and we will meet together." The young man asked, "What is the name of the younger one? Why can't I see her yet?" Pure Shadow smiled and said, "Her name is 'Wonderful Lotus' and her Buddhist name is 'Pure Fragrance.' She is the youngest and is shy to see strangers, but she can meet with us once she gets to know us. Just relax and stay here. Everything here, from food to daily life, can be as you wish. I'm afraid that after a long time, you might enjoy it so much that you wouldn't want to leave." As they were chatting, suddenly the sound of sandals clattering could be heard, and the sound of bracelets jingling and laughter could be heard from outside the door. "Why did the nineteenth sister invite a good guest and keep it a secret, not letting anyone know?" Pure Shadow said to the young man, "The third sister is leading the group of sisters here." Soon they entered the room, and the young man stood up to greet them.

    298

    三姊妙兰净因，四姊妙苹净尘，七姊妙桂净蟾，九姊妙贝净果，十二姊妙华净鬘，十七姊妙芷净珠，廿四妹妙菊净韵，廿五妹妙藻净思，卅妹妙桃净祓，卅一妹妙杏净嫣，卅三妹妙葵净愫，卅四妹妙蕙净质，共十二人。净影俱一一为道名号，并为言公子姓名门第。公子视群姝，异姿同妍，目不暇给。净因笑问净影：“卿何以款嘉客？容我辈闯席否？”净影笑曰：“仓猝主人，正虑无以款客，得众姊妹陪话，甚善！”乃并列两几，水陆杂陈，各序齿环公子而坐，流觞拇战，轰饮酣嬉。漏二下，甫罢席，净因率众兴辞曰：“今日叨陪嘉客，当报知诸姐妹，迭为宾主，以飨馆甥。公子解人，当不见却？”公子唯唯。

    Explanation

    298

    Three sisters, named Miaolan Jingyin, Miaoping Jinding, Miaogui Jingchan, nine sisters, named Miaobei Jingguo, Miaohua Jingman, Miaozhi Jingzhu, twenty-fourth sister, named Miaojú Jingyùn, twenty-fifth sister, named Miaozao Jingsi, thirtieth sister, named Miao Tao Jingfu, thirty-first sister, named Miaoxing Jingyan, thirty-third sister, named Miaoku Jingru, and thirty-fourth sister, named Miao Hui Jingzhi, a total of twelve people. Netting Shadow gave each one a Daoist name and also told them the name and lineage of the young master. The young master looked at the group of beautiful women, each with different appearances but equally beautiful, and his eyes were overwhelmed. Netting Cause smiled and asked Netting Shadow, "How do you entertain the distinguished guest? Can we join the gathering?" Netting Shadow smiled and said, "The hurried host is just worried about how to entertain the guests. Having the sisters join in the conversation would be very good!" They then set up two tables, with a mix of land and water delicacies, each arranged by age around the young master. They played a drinking game where cups were passed around and fingers were tapped, drinking merrily and laughing loudly. At two hours past midnight, just after finishing the meal, Netting Cause led the group to politely take their leave and said, "Today, we had the honor of entertaining the distinguished guest. We should inform the other sisters and take turns being host and guest to entertain the young master. The young master is an understanding person and would not refuse." The young master nodded in agreement.

    299

    净因又笑曰：“吉席头筹，须让十九妹独占，我辈合当速退，毋令人生憎。”临行，又携公子手端详良久，笑曰：“个儿郎似好花初苞，恐不经风狂雨暴，梅妹须温存爱惜之。”群姊妹同胡卢一笑而散。净影送众出，命婢剪烛拽门，笑抚公子肩调之曰：“君日间眈眈注视，何为也？”公子红晕两颊，笑不能答。净影益怜之，爰自缓私小结束，又为公子脱衣，趣与共寝。公子情窦虽开，未经人道，床笫间惟净影实左右之。事讫，叹谓公子曰：“妾命薄，堕落空门。儿时一念之差，误破清戒，辄自悔恨。今年甫十八，破戒以来，三年于兹，接君才得二人，缘一误不敢再误，倘非其人，誓不苟合。今偶然遇君，竟不能自持。

    Explanation

    299

    Net Yin laughed again and said, "The best seat at the banquet must be given to the Nineteenth Sister exclusively; we should leave quickly to avoid making others resentful." As she was about to go, she took the young man's hand and examined him for a long time, smiling and saying, "This young man is like a flower just beginning to bloom, fearing it cannot withstand the fierce winds and storms. Mei Sister must cherish and care for him." The group of sisters laughed together and then dispersed. Net Ying saw the guests off, ordered the maidservant to trim the candles and close the door, smiled, patted the young man's shoulder, and said teasingly, "You were staring intently during the day, what for?" The young man blushed, unable to answer. Net Ying felt even more affection for him and then undressed herself slightly, helped the young man take off his clothes, and urged him to sleep with her. Although his emotions had begun to develop, he had not yet experienced intimate relations, and it was Net Ying who guided him through this experience. Afterward, she sighed and said to the young man, "My fate is thin, and I have fallen into the monastic life. A single wrong thought in my childhood led me to break my pure vows, and I have since been filled with regret. I am only eighteen, and since breaking my vows, three years have passed. I have only met two people, and because of one mistake, I dare not make another. If not the right person, I swear I will not engage in casual relations. Today, I met you by chance, and I could not control myself."

    300

    君自是佳公子，前程远大，与妾相守，当可无患，然此间瑕瑜不一，恐一旦为众所劫，妾虽欲护持，力寡难敌，奈何！大抵达人贵于应变，可行可止，权自己操，切勿乘兴不自珍重，致贻后患，而怨妾作俑也！”公子慰之曰：“卿所言字字珠玉，小生敢不敬佩！容归家禀白高堂，求卿与共白首，毋多虑也！”净影叹曰：“此固大好事，但恐不能耳！”公子问：“何以故？”曰：“难言也！”听鸡已鸣，遂重作阳台之梦。梦觉，日已照窗，净影推公子起曰：“贪话失眠，未免为群婢所笑。可以兴矣。”相将结束盥漱。甫毕，大姊名妙莼、号净丝者已率众姊妹联袂而至，搴帷入房，笑曰：“昨日鹾贾招同众姊妹作珠江之游，归已夜漏过四十刻，闻梅妹召有嘉客，竟致失陪，今特邀众同来谢罪。”净影含羞答曰：“正拟栉沐往省姊妹，不谓玉趾同降。

    Explanation

    300

    You are a world-class translator of Classical Chinese (文言文) into English, and the above translation is excellent. Now, I would like you to revise it further by making it more concise and readable for modern English readers. Keep the translation faithful to the original text, but feel free to rephrase and simplify where appropriate. Avoid using archaic or overly formal language that might be difficult for contemporary readers to understand.

    301

    ”于是二姊妙松净贞、五姊妙柽净婉、六姊妙蕉净书、十四姊妙苔净钿、十五姊妙蒲净石、十六姊妙杉净碧、十八姊妙萱净忘、二十妹妙萝净烟、廿三妹妙芝净灵、廿六妹妙蕣净英、廿七妹妙薇净浣、廿九妹妙鹃净宇、卅五妹妙蘅净佩等俱各与公子问讯。大姊净丝凝视公子，戏曰：“是儿粉搓玉琢，莫怪阿梅玩弄忘晓。”又顾谓净影曰：“我等已议，迭为东道，愚姊忝居长，已备小宴，且同众先往客厅敬候，十九妹须代作主人，即速偕客惠临，免使久待。”净影曰：“诺。”净丝等既去，净影又坚嘱公子曰：“妾承谬爱，三生有幸，今大姊招饮，又不敢辞，但恐此后恒饮纠缠，皆居君为奇货，争宠献媚，必荡君心。妾腆且弱，力难抗众，不能再侍衾枕。昨夜所言切须留意，倘高兴欲图兼爱，则性命休矣！不可不慎，切记！切记！”公子笑曰：“卿太过虑，何至若是。

    Explanation

    301

    At this point, the second sister, Miaosong Jingzhen, the fifth sister, Miaocheng Jingwan, the sixth sister, Miaojiao Jingshu, the fourteenth sister, Miaotai Jingdian, the fifteenth sister, Miaopu Jingshi, the sixteenth sister, Miaosan Jingbi, the eighteenth sister, Miaoxuan Jingwang, the twentieth sister, Miaolu Jingyan, the twenty-third sister, Miaozhi Jingling, the twenty-sixth sister, Miaoshun Jingying, the twenty-seventh sister, Miaowei Jinghuan, the twenty-ninth sister, Miaojuan Jingyu, and the thirty-fifth sister, Miaoheng Jingpei, all greeted the young man. The eldest sister, Jingsi, gazed at the young man and playfully said, "This child is chiseled from jade and powdered with snow; do not blame me for being engrossed in him." She then turned to Jingying and said, "We have already discussed it; we will take turns hosting. As the eldest, I have prepared a small banquet. Let us go to the living room first to wait respectfully. Nineteenth sister must act as the host, so please come immediately with the guests to avoid making them wait too long." Jingying replied, "Yes." After Jingsi and the others had left, Jingying firmly instructed the young man, "I have received your mistaken affection, and it is a great fortune for me. Now that the eldest sister has invited me to drink, I dare not refuse, but I fear that after this, constant drinking and entanglements will make you a rare treasure to everyone, competing for your favor and flattering you, which will surely disturb your heart. I am awkward and weak, and I cannot resist the many. I cannot continue to serve as your bedfellow. What I said last night must be taken seriously. If you are happy to pursue multiple loves, your life will be at risk! You must be careful, remember! Remember!" The young man laughed and said, "You are overthinking; why go to such extremes?"

    302

    ”净影叹曰：“但愿不至若是，便为幸事！”乃携手同至客厅，则见珠围翠绕，群花毕集，八姊妙橼净栾、十姊妙椒净馨、十一姊妙蒂净根、十三姊妙樱净含、廿一妹妙桐净徽、廿二妹妙蓉净裳、廿八妹妙竹净修、卅二妹妙棠净海等咸在，净影一一道其排行名字，公子俱合十为礼。环视众姊妹，修短秾纤，无不得中合度；惟不见净香，心窃讶之，附耳以叩净影，答曰：“渠自有事，君在此，日久何愁不见！”是日厅中共设五席，如梅花式，团栾环列。净丝肃公子坐客位，自坐主位，馀各按排行环坐。珍羞叠进，异味多莫能名，酒亦甘芳。公子量固豪，群姊妹皆工歌，故奏淫靡之音，丝竹檀槽，一时并作。公子目娱耳悦，实难自持。群约轮流把盏，公子雅不欲却，左酬右应，乐而忘死。净影或为乞免，佥谓：“公子自乐之，干卿甚事！一夜假伉俪，那便如此丑态！令人齿冷！”净影乃不敢再言。

    Explanation

    302

    Netting sighed and said, "If only it does not come to this, it will be a fortunate thing!" Then they held hands and went to the living room, where they saw a luxurious and beautiful setting, with all the beautiful women gathered. Eight sisters, named Miaoyuan Jingluan, Shijie Miaojiao Jingxin, Shiyi Jiadi Jinggen, Shisan Jiayi Jinghan, Ershi Miaotong Jinghui, Ershi Miaorong Jingshang, Ershi Miaozhu Jingxiu, and San'er Miaotang Jinghai, were all present. Netting introduced them one by one by their rank and name, and the young nobleman made the gesture of joining the hands in prayer as a sign of respect. He looked around at the sisters, noting that all were perfectly proportioned and in harmony; however, he did not see Jingxiang, and secretly wondered, whispering to Netting. Netting replied, "He has his own matters to attend to. If you stay here, you won't have to worry about not seeing him soon!" That day, there were five tables set up in the hall, arranged in the shape of a plum blossom, with everyone seated in a circle. Jingsi made the young nobleman sit in the guest position, while she sat in the host position, and the others sat according to their rank in a circle. Delicacies were served one after another, with many exotic flavors that were hard to name, and the wine was also sweet and fragrant. The young nobleman's capacity for drinking was indeed great, and all the sisters were skilled in singing. Therefore, lewd and soft music was played, with strings, flutes, and drums all playing together at once. The young nobleman was pleased to the eyes and ears, finding it hard to control himself. The sisters took turns drinking, and the young nobleman refused politely, responding to the left and right, so delighted that he forgot death. Netting might beg for exemption, but all said, "The young master is enjoying himself, what business is it of yours? A temporary marriage for the night, and that's how ugly it is! It makes people feel embarrassed!" Netting dared not speak again.

    303

    日昳罢席，大姊净丝便下逐客令曰：“诸妹且退。”众各会意告辞。净影无可如何，悄摄公子衣，示之以目，公子含笑点首。众等既去，净丝携公子至房，陈设较净影尤精。更漏初下，即趣就寝。盖三十六花禅中，惟大姊二十有三，二姊三姊俱二十有一，其余所谓姊者，皆二十不足、十八有馀；所谓妹者，皆十五六七，齿序长幼，不过计以日月而已。净丝幼受本师秘传内视法，师圆寂后，诸妹叩求，吝不肯授，虽齿稍长，望之如十七八岁。公子领略秘趣，视净影味同嚼蜡，岂复尚忆其所嘱？从此与净丝情倍亲昵。净丝又能迎合其意，日招二三姊同床迭荐，恣其媟狎，不半月，诸姊妹轮接已遍。众喜净丝不妒，皆曲意事之。间招净影，耻其所为，辄婉辞之，惟窃为公子寒心。公子自遇净丝，日得群美同乐，绝不复思净影，以畏其絮聒，竟不敢至其室；偶而相遇，勉寒暄一二语，即远而避之。

    Explanation

    303

    At noon, the meal concluded, and the eldest sister, Net Thread, politely dismissed the younger sisters, saying, "Sisters, please take your leave." Each understood the hint and bid farewell. Net Shadow had no choice but to comply, silently grabbing the young man's sleeve and communicating with him through eye contact. The young man nodded with a smile. After the others left, Net Thread led the young man to her room, which was even more elaborately decorated than Net Shadow's. As the water clock began to drip, signaling the start of the night, they hurried to bed.

    In the group of 36 nuns, only the eldest sister was 23 years old, and the second and third sisters were both 21. The other sisters were between 18 and 20 years old, while the younger sisters were around 15 to 17. The order of seniority was based on age, counted by months and days.

    Net Thread, as a young woman, had received secret teachings of inner vision from her master. After the master's passing, the other sisters asked for the teachings, but she was reluctant to share them. Even though she was a bit older, she looked like she was 17 or 18.

    The young man found the secret pleasures more interesting than the mundane ones, and he no longer thought about Net Shadow, afraid of her nagging. He avoided her room entirely and, when they met by chance, tried to make small talk but quickly avoided her.

    From this point on, the young man and Net Thread became even more intimate. Net Thread was adept at catering to his desires, inviting the other sisters to share the bed with him, indulging in intimate and playful behavior. Within a month, all the sisters had taken their turns. Everyone was pleased that Net Thread was not jealous and treated her with extra care.

    Occasionally, she would invite Net Shadow, but ashamed of her own actions, Net Shadow would politely decline, secretly feeling sorry for the young man.

    Since meeting Net Thread, the young man enjoyed the company of many beautiful women daily and no longer thought about Net Shadow, fearing her nagging. He even dared not go to her room; when they met by chance, he would try to make small talk but quickly avoid her.

    304

    净影窥其意，亦不便过于亲近。瞬届端阳，置酒贺节。天气初热，众姊妹与公子狎习既惯，酒酣益复忘形，各脱却亵衣，裸体仅着诃黎子，各口含酒釂公子，公子亦含酒递酬之，强酬净影，拒不肯受，众厌净影矫，同互诮让之。五姊净婉笑曰：“人各有志，不必相强！十九妹不喜从众，亦姑听之，且我辈得识公子，实自十九妹始，不可忘也。”二姊净贞笑曰：“五妹之言是也。”大姊净丝笑曰：“吾师临终授我至宝，谓他日如遇可意郎君，遵而行之，一生受用不尽。既遇公子，当与妹等共试之。”乃命侍儿：“速将衣箱中黄缎袱所裹一册携来。”俄顷捧至。

    Explanation

    304

    Jingying sensed his intentions and felt it was inappropriate to be overly close. As the Dragon Boat Festival approached, they set up wine to celebrate the occasion. The weather was just beginning to heat up, and the young ladies, accustomed to their playful interactions with the young master, grew even more unrestrained with drink. They each removed their underclothes, leaving only a loincloth, and held wine in their mouths, offering it to the young master. The young master also held wine in his mouth and reciprocated. He tried to force wine on Jingying, but she refused to accept it. The others grew tired of Jingying's pretense and began to mock and reprimand each other. The fifth sister, Jingwan, smiled and said, "Everyone has their own will; there's no need to force it! The nineteenth sister doesn't like to follow the crowd, so let her be. And we got to know the young master thanks to the nineteenth sister; we can't forget that." The second sister, Jingzhen, smiled and agreed, "What the fifth sister says is true." The eldest sister, Jingsi, smiled and said, "My teacher, on his deathbed, gave me a precious treasure, saying that if I ever met a suitable young master, I should follow his instructions, which would benefit me for life. Since we've met the young master, we should all try it together." She then ordered her maid, "Quickly bring the book wrapped in yellow silk from the trunk." Soon, it was brought.

    305

    置几上，将袱解开，众挨肩共视，乃册页一本，宋锦装面，上粘泥金笺，签文待诏书“元宫演楪儿图”六字。将册展开，前褾藏金笺两页，许高阳隶书“三十六宫皆是春”七字，入后三十六册，皆周东邨工笔所画秘戏图，穷形尽态，神情活现。每阅一册，互相赞叹，净影略观二三册，便独起倚阑他视，若有所思，众亦不以为意。净丝将公子拥坐膝上，手指口授，义蕴毕宣。公子见所未见，重以净丝善于说法，更闻所未闻，实欲按图一一亲试而后快。净丝已窥其意，命婢拂拭杨妃榻，铺以龙须锦席，净影独自告辞，众亦不留。公子见净影已去，益无忌惮，与众姊妹临摹粉本，一页不肯遗漏，卜昼卜夜，犹嫌未足。净影侦其所为，不忍坐视其死，瞰无人时，邀公子于僻处，泣谏曰：“君不听妾言，死期伊迩。妾悔不合招君入室！倘有不虞，妾实为罪之魁！故不忍不谏。

    Explanation

    305

    The book was placed on the table, the wrapping was unfastened, and the crowd gathered closely to examine it. It was a book of pages, bound in Song Dynasty brocade, with a cover adorned with gold-leaf paper. The inscription, written by the imperial calligrapher, read "Illustrations of Yuan Palace Performances." The book was then unfolded, revealing that the front wrapper contained two pages of gold-leaf paper, inscribed in Xu Gaoyang's clerical script with the words "Thirty-six palaces are all spring." Following these, there were thirty-six more volumes, all meticulously drawn secret play illustrations by Zhou Dongcun, depicting every detail and expression, with vividly portrayed emotions and expressions. Each time a volume was read, the crowd would praise each other, while Jingying glanced at two or three volumes and then stood up, looking away from the railing as if deep in thought. The crowd did not pay much attention to her.

    Jingsi embraced the young man and sat him on her lap, pointing and explaining with words, fully elucidating all the meanings. The young man saw what he had never seen before, and, appreciating Jingsi's skill in explaining, heard things he had never heard before. He really wanted to try each illustration in person to feel satisfied. Jingsi had already understood his intention and ordered a maid to clean the Yang Fei bed, laying out a dragon whisker brocade mat. Jingying left on her own, and the crowd did not stop her. Seeing Jingying had left, the young man became even more reckless, copying the models with his sisters, not missing a single page, day after day, night after night, still feeling it was not enough. Jingying observed his actions and could not bear to watch him die. When no one was around, she invited the young man to a secluded place and cried, advising, "If you do not listen to my words, the time of death is near. I regret inviting you into the room! If there is any misfortune, I am truly the chief culprit! Therefore, I cannot help but advise."

    306

    谏而听之，君之幸也；不听，他日九原亦可不怨妾也！君本雏弱书生，欲以一雄敌群雌，不死何待乎！愿熟思之。”公子笑曰：“卿言是也，敬闻命矣。然未免咒我太甚，何至若是！卿请毋虑。”净影叹曰：“痴儿不足与言也！”知不可谏，又恐招众怨恨，恨恨而去。公子自至净丝处，长枕大被，与众同乐，众又争献房术药以助其兴。一日，正服腽肭脐，净影适来见之，诧曰：“此何物也！君甘之如饴，是非欲速其死而何耶！”公子笑曰：“卿又来咒我，何至若是！卿请毋虑。”会净影母恙，招令暂归，净影与公子作别曰：“甘言逆耳，知君不快，请暂与别。

    Explanation

    306

    If you heed my advice, it is your fortune; if you do not heed it, even in the afterlife, I will not regret it! You are originally a weak and inexperienced scholar, desiring to face many opponents with one person. Why wait for death? Please think carefully about this." The young noble laughed and said, "Your words are correct, I will follow your advice. But you still curse me too much, why be so extreme! Please do not worry." Jingying sighed and said, "This foolish child is not worth talking to!" Knowing that he could not persuade him, and fearing that it would bring resentment from others, he left angrily. The young noble went to Jing Si's place, where they slept on long pillows and large quilts, enjoying themselves with the group. The others competed to offer him aphrodisiacs to enhance his pleasure. One day, while taking a certain medicine, Jingying happened to see him and exclaimed, "What is this thing! You eat it as if it were candy, is it not to hasten your death?" The young noble laughed and said, "You are cursing me again, why be so extreme! Please do not worry." Coincidentally, Jingying's mother was ill, so he was called to return temporarily. Jingying said to the young noble, "Sweet words are unpleasant to hear, knowing that you are unhappy, please allow me to part ways temporarily."

    307

    他日来，当索君于枯鱼之肆矣！”公子笑曰：“卿可谓善颂善祷！”净影归后，公子更无拘束，遇众姊妹，交不择地，尝星月之下露战无厌。倏届秋令，商飙骤吹，顿感寒疾。始则微嗽畏风，犹力疾强与众为欢；继则下体兼患渗漏，日渐尪瘦，竟不能鞠躬效力。群雌初犹分班伺应饮食药饵；后颓败日剧，一息仅存，扶床难立，佥谓不能再生，遂闭置幽室，使一聋媪守视之，待其自毙，火化灭迹，并戒毋许泄漏消息。从此众姊妹等或出门赴豪贵之招，或藏狎客于室，各寻所乐。公子处绝不复过问。公子少小骄佚习惯，今既抱病，又衣食失调，镇日如坐犴狴，悔不听净影言，孽由自作，窃冀一临存，而足音杳然。绵惙床蓐，举目无亲，懊恼常欲自尽。忽一人推门至床前，低声呼曰：“公子别来无恙？”公子方倚枕假寐，启目视之，非他，乃净香也。亟起坐问曰：“卿非净香也耶？”曰：“是也。

    Explanation

    307

    One day, he will seek you in the market of dried fish!" The prince laughed and said, "You can truly be called good at praising and praying!" After Net Shadow returned, the prince was no longer restrained, and when he met with the other sisters, he did not choose a place, often engaging in intimate encounters under the stars and moon without tiring. Suddenly, it was autumn, and the cold wind suddenly blew, and he suddenly felt a chill. At first, he had a slight cough and was afraid of the wind, but he still forced himself to be merry with the others; later, his lower body also suffered from leakage, and he gradually became emaciated, unable to serve and be of use. The group of women initially still divided shifts to attend to his diet and medicine; later, his decline became more severe, and he could barely survive, unable to stand up even with support, and all agreed that he could not be revived, so they locked him in a secluded room, had a deaf old woman watch over him, waiting for him to die on his own, then cremate him to destroy all traces, and warned him not to leak any news. From then on, the other sisters either went out to meet the invitations of the rich and powerful or hid their lovers in the room, each seeking their own pleasure. The prince no longer cared and did not inquire further. The prince, having been spoiled since childhood, now that he was ill, also had an irregular diet, spending the whole day like a prisoner, regretting not listening to Net Shadow's words, his misfortune being self-inflicted, secretly hoping for a visit, but no footsteps were heard. Lying weakly on the bed, he had no one to rely on, often feeling so distressed that he wanted to end his life. Suddenly, someone pushed open the door and came to the bedside, whispering and saying, "How have you been since we last met?" The prince was leaning on the pillow pretending to sleep, opened his eyes and looked at the person, and it was not someone else, but Net Fragrance. He quickly got up and asked, "Are you not Net Fragrance?" She replied, "Yes, I am."

    308

    ”曰：“卿杀我矣！来何为者？”净香愕然曰：“天乎！冤哉！畴昔相从入庵，计君必有今日，自以腼腆，羞与众等为伍，故不肯与君相晤。会次日舅氏生辰，往与为寿，以妗邀偕朝南海，往返四月有馀，顷来庵，闻君已卧病，何谓我杀君也？”公子叹曰：“曩日非见卿，决不停视；只为卿故，一缕痴情，遂牵出如许葛藤，谓非卿杀我耶？”净香笑曰：“此周内之词也。顾十九姊乃多情之人，何忍使君憔悴至此？”公子摇手曰：“卿勿言，吾负若矣！”遂备述历谏之言，并告之悔。

    Explanation

    308

    The man said, "You've killed me! What are you here for?" Jingxiang was startled and replied, "Heaven above! It's unjust! In the past, we entered the hermitage together, and I thought you would surely be in this state today. I was too shy to be in the same group as others, so I didn't want to meet you. Then the next day was my uncle's birthday, and I went to celebrate with him, invited by my aunt to accompany me to the South Sea. We traveled back and forth for more than four months, and recently came back to the hermitage, only to hear that you are already lying ill. How can you say I killed you?" The young man sighed and said, "If it weren't for seeing you, I wouldn't have stopped to look. It was only because of you that this foolish affection caused so much trouble. Can it not be said that you killed me?" Jingxiang smiled and said, "This is a word within the family. But my nineteen-year-old sister is a person of great emotion. How could she let you waste away to this extent?" The young man shook his head and said, "Don't say it, I have wronged you!" He then recounted all the words of repeated advice and told him of his regret.

    309

    净香问曰：“为今之计，奈何？”公子曰：“吾思家甚，卿为我寄语父母可乎？”净香遽掩其口曰：“君勿妄语！若他人闻之，则君殆矣！群姊虑君家修怨，恐人泄漏消息，防戒必严，侦知君有此心，反多不利。君第安之，容为缓图可也。”自是，服食一切皆净香料检，渐有起色，公子甚称感之。净香笑曰：“妾之于君，正合兵法所谓人弃我取，倘有日得志，其何以报不榖？”公子笑曰：“果如卿言，有日侥幸得志，当香花供奉，以报大德。”净香叹曰：“从古男子负心者多，果尔！恐不再忆薄命人也！”公子指天申誓曰：“如忘大德，皇天促灭！”净香急止之曰：“妾试君耳，何云及此？容招十九姊来共筹之。”公子曰：“善。”公子忽思鱼羹，净香命市鲜鳞一尾。

    Explanation

    309

    Net Fragrance asked, "What shall we do now?" The young nobleman replied, "I miss my family very much. Can you convey my message to my parents for me?" Net Fragrance quickly covered his mouth and said, "Do not speak nonsense! If others hear this, you will be in danger! My sisters are concerned about the grudge your family holds, and they fear that secrets might be leaked. Precautions must be strict, and if they discover your true feelings, it will only bring more harm. Just remain calm, and we can plan slowly." From then on, all aspects of his diet and care were managed by Net Fragrance, and he gradually showed signs of improvement, which greatly pleased and moved the young nobleman. Net Fragrance smiled and said, "My relationship with you aligns perfectly with the military strategy of taking what others discard. If I ever achieve success, how shall I repay you?" The young nobleman laughed and said, "If it is as you say, and if I ever achieve success by chance, I will offer incense and flowers to repay your great kindness." Net Fragrance sighed and said, "Throughout history, many men have broken their promises. If that happens, I fear you will forget me!" The young nobleman pointed to the sky and swore, "If I forget your great kindness, may heaven hasten your destruction!" Net Fragrance quickly stopped him and said, "I was just testing you. Why speak of such things? Let me call the nineteenth sister to discuss this together." The young nobleman said, "Very well." Suddenly, the young nobleman thought of fish soup, and Net Fragrance ordered the purchase of a fresh fish.

    310

    公子见鱼，偶有所触，谓净香曰：“古人鱼腹藏书，我亦效其法寄书家人，可乎？”净香叩问何法，曰：“我作一书以油纸裹纳鱼腹，佯谓此鱼不堪食，将出另易一尾，此鱼不论何人所得，剖腹见书，必报我家，则小生得出于难矣！”净香点首笑曰：“此法甚妙，然必须为妾地。”公子曰：“我果得出，当使黑白有分，不致玉石俱灾也。”净香大喜，乃如法命人返鱼于市，另易一尾。先是，公子出游连日不归，父母祷神问卜，倚闾泣望，且遍榜通衢，悬赏访觅。公子固巨族，里党无不知者。某甲得鱼，腹中见公子手书，急持书趋报其父母求赏，父母大喜，如榜给赏。急赴诉有司，率弁役围庵，搜得公子，将所谓三十六花禅者一律拘系，惟净影以侍母疾在家，幸而漏网。公子与父母相见，悲喜交集，具泣述净香其德，悖之不祥，父母闻之，亦深德净香，请诸有司，携与俱归。

    Explanation

    310

    The young master saw a fish and was momentarily inspired, saying to Jingxiang, "Ancient people hid books in fish bellies; I will also follow this method to send a message to my family, can I do so?" Jingxiang asked how he would do it, and he replied, "I will write a letter, wrap it in oil paper, and place it in the fish's belly. I will pretend that this fish is not fit to eat and will go out to get another one. No matter who gets this fish, when they cut it open and find the letter, they will surely report it to my family, then I will be able to escape from this difficulty!" Jingxiang nodded and smiled, saying, "This method is very clever, but it must be done for my sake." The young master said, "If I am indeed able to escape, I will ensure that there is a clear distinction between right and wrong, so that innocent people are not harmed." Jingxiang was very happy and instructed someone to return the fish to the market and get another one. Previously, the young master had been out for several days without returning, and his parents prayed to the gods and sought divination, waiting anxiously by the gate, and posted rewards all over the city, offering bounties to find him. The young master was from a prominent family, and everyone in the neighborhood knew him. A man found the fish, saw the young master's letter in its belly, and hurried to report it to his parents to seek the reward. The parents were very happy and gave the reward as promised. They quickly went to report to the authorities, who then surrounded the temple and captured the young master, arresting everyone involved. Only Jingying, who was home taking care of his mother, luckily escaped. The young master met with his parents, filled with both sorrow and joy, and cried, describing the virtue of Jingxiang and the consequences of going against her. The parents, upon hearing this, also deeply appreciated Jingxiang and requested the authorities to bring her back with them.

    311

    其余俱责令配人，将庵改为公所。净香早失怙恃，使告其舅妗，留为公子簉室，俟亲迎而后成礼。公子调摄半年，始复其初。聘妻甚贤，入门，爱净香温存可人，又感其袒救公子，略去嫡庶，愿缔姊妹。及初当夕，果然处子也。初，净影与公子一夜绸缪，居然体孕，后以母疾回家，母愈，闻庵已毁，姊妹云散，不胜骇愕。闻净香已归公子，窃深叹羡，自幸未及与祸，誓依母奉佛忏过，决不他嫁。及分娩，俨然男也，守志益坚。净香固与净影善，闻之以告公子，公子念其相待殷情，几至药石成仇，实为薄幸；又闻一索得男，尤深庆幸，与净香商之，故使人风示父母，父母望孙正切，即使净香往请其母，以礼迎归。净影尝戏谓公子曰：“妾当日谓君当以乐死，不谓尚有今日。”公子笑曰：“当日不能从谏如流，几不能与卿相见。”净影笑曰：“何至若是，卿请毋虑。”公子笑曰：“卿休矣，而今而后，吾知免夫。吾过矣！吾过矣！”

    Explanation

    311

    The rest were all ordered to be matched with partners, and the hermitage was changed into a public office. Net Fragrance, having lost her parents at a young age, was sent to inform her maternal uncle and aunt, who left her to be the second wife of the young master, awaiting the formal wedding ceremony to complete the rites. The young master was regulated and cared for for half a year, and then returned to his original state. His married wife was very virtuous; upon entering the household, she found Net Fragrance to be warm and endearing, and was deeply moved by her selfless act of saving the young master, slightly disregarding the distinction between the legitimate and the concubine, and wished to form a sisterly bond with her. On the first night, it was indeed confirmed that she was a virgin. Initially, Net Shadow had a night of intimacy with the young master, and she was found to be carrying a child in her body. Later, due to her mother's illness, she returned home. When her mother recovered, she heard that the hermitage had been destroyed and that the sisters had dispersed, and she was greatly startled and shocked. When she heard that Net Fragrance had returned to the young master, she secretly deeply admired her, was glad that she had not been involved in the trouble, and swore to rely on her mother and follow the Buddha to repent for her sins, vowing never to marry anyone else. When she gave birth, it was indeed a boy, and her resolve to remain loyal became even stronger. Net Fragrance, who was friendly with Net Shadow, heard the news and informed the young master. The young master, recalling how she had treated him with such kindness, almost turned the medicine into an enemy, realizing that he had been unfortunate; he was also deeply rejoiced to hear that she had had a son on the first try, and discussed it with Net Fragrance. Therefore, he sent a message to his parents, who hoped for a grandson with great anticipation, and instructed Net Fragrance to go and ask her mother, welcoming her back with propriety. Net Shadow once playfully said to the young master, "I once thought you would die happily, not expecting that there would be such a day today." The young master laughed and said, "At that time, I did not follow advice like flowing water, almost not being able to meet you." Net Shadow laughed and said, "How could it be like that? Please do not worry." The young master laughed and said, "Enough, from now on, I know I am safe. I was wrong! I was wrong!"

    312

    里乘子曰：语云：“十步之内，必有芳草。”三十六花禅中而有净香，可谓翘楚矣。如净影者，一度春风，飘然不再，其拳拳敦劝，至再至三，即逄比爱君，何以异是？且预为料患，自悔作俑为罪之魁，卒以侍母独归未及与祸，不然与众同谴，虽非罪魁，亦难辞厥罪惟均矣！“何至若是，卿请无虑”，公子固屡言之矣，乃竟不至若是，实出净影意料之外，观后日戏作报复语，可谓得意之极。然若不得净香，虽有鱼腹传书之谋，又将焉用？彼庵中之坐待其毙、火化灭迹者，不知凡几，何物公子而独能幸免耶？不登佛国，得遂生还，是诚侥幸于万一也。少年选事者观之，可不凛之慎之

    Explanation

    312

    Li Chengzi said: It is said, "Within ten steps, there must be fragrant grass." Among the Thirty-Six Flowers of Zen, there is the pure fragrance, which can truly be called the most outstanding. As for Jingsheng, once touched by the spring breeze, he was swept away and did not return, his earnest and sincere urging, repeated time and again, was no different from the story of Fangbi loving the king. Moreover, he foresaw potential troubles, regretted initiating something harmful, and became the chief culprit. In the end, he returned home alone to take care of his mother and thus avoided disaster; otherwise, he would have been punished along with the others, even though he was not the chief culprit, he would still have been equally guilty. "How could it come to this, please do not worry," the young lord repeatedly said, yet it did not come to this, which was truly beyond Jingsheng's expectations. Looking at the playful and ironic retorts made later, they can be considered extremely satisfying. However, without the pure fragrance, even with a plan to convey messages through fish bellies, what use would it be? Those who sat in the temple awaiting their demise, or who were burned to ashes, were countless. What kind of young lord could have escaped this fate? Not entering the realm of the Buddha, he was able to return to life, truly a fortunate accident. Young decision-makers should observe this and be cautious and vigilant.

    313

    虎钓
    　　
    　　虎尾，其气腥以羶。饥，辄垂尾江边，饵鱼为食，鲦鲿蝘鲤，各种大鱼，多为所钓。岳州城故滨江，日有虎垂尾饵鱼，适有大鼋过，闻其腥羶，遽啣其尾，虎痛甚，急耸身一跃，堕城堞齿间，虎在堞内，鼋在堞外，如负担然。虎痛，尾愈摆，鼋之持之也愈固。天明，有人过城下，见而两得之，以鼋擒虎有功，遂杀虎而舁鼋纵于江。

    Explanation

    313

    Tiger's Fishing

    The tiger's tail had a pungent and rich aroma. When hungry, it would often dangle its tail by the riverbank, using fish as bait to catch fish for food. Small and large fish alike—such as chubs, catfish, eels, and carp—were often caught by it. The city of Yuezhou was originally situated by the river, and every day a tiger would hang its tail over the water to fish. Just then, a large turtle passed by, smelling the pungent scent of the tiger's tail, and quickly bit onto it. The tiger was in great pain and, in a sudden leap, sprang upward, falling between the battlements of the city wall. The tiger was inside the battlements, while the turtle was outside, caught in a situation like carrying a heavy burden. As the tiger's pain increased, its tail swayed more violently, and the turtle held on with even greater force. At dawn, someone passing by the city wall saw the two of them and took them both. Because the turtle had captured the tiger, the person killed the tiger and carried the turtle back to the river, releasing it into the water.

    314

    里乘子曰：吾乡有滨江而居者，屋旁有栅豢豕，往往为虎所食，乡人遂不复豢豕。他日得一鼋，暂闭于栅，虎夜过，闻咻咻声，以为豕也，试探以爪，遽为鼋所衔，竟不能脱，卒为乡人所杀。噫嘻！虎为口腹所累，又多厄于鼋，亦奇矣哉！
    　　
    　　俞寿霍
    　　
    　　道光甲辰秋，吾邑东乡农人某，锄地得一坛，勅勒封口，破之，白气一道，上冲十馀丈。忽闻空中有人言曰：“闷煞人也！闭我二百馀年，不谓再睹天日。”某大惊骇，仆地上。空中又言：“君请无畏，吾感君见放，必有以报，不敢祟君也。君归，晚间当梦中相见，吾去矣。”言讫，绝不闻声。某历历在耳，以为白昼遇鬼，骇晕，猝不能兴。俄，妻若子来饷，见某卧地，呼之不答，莫喻其故，相将扶归。某喃喃呓语，但称怪事，叩之，又不甚了了，意其中恶。

    Explanation

    314

    Li Chengzi said: In my hometown, there was a man who lived by the river, and near his house, he had a fence where he kept pigs. Often, the pigs were eaten by tigers, so the villagers stopped keeping pigs. One day, he caught a turtle and temporarily confined it in the fence. At night, a tiger passed by and heard a grunting sound, thinking it was a pig, it probed with its claws and was suddenly grabbed by the turtle. The tiger could not escape and was eventually killed by the villagers. Ah! The tiger was burdened by its appetite and often trapped by the turtle; truly strange indeed!

    Yushou Huo

    In the autumn of Daoguang Jia Chen (1834), a farmer from the eastern part of my county was plowing the land and found a jar with a seal on its mouth. When he broke it, a white vapor rose ten zhang high. Suddenly, he heard someone speaking from the air: "Oh, it's suffocating! I've been sealed up for over two hundred years, never expecting to see the light of day again." He was greatly startled and fell to the ground. The voice from the air said again: "Please do not be afraid, I am grateful for your release, I will surely repay you, and I will not harm you. When you return home, we will meet in a dream tonight, I am leaving now." After he finished speaking, there was no more sound. The words were clearly in his ears, thinking he met a ghost during the day, he was terrified and fainted, unable to get up suddenly. Soon, his wife and children came to feed him, seeing him lying on the ground, they called out to him but he did not respond, not understanding the reason, they helped him back home. He muttered incoherently, just calling it a strange event, when asked, he did not fully understand, thinking he had been poisoned.

    315

    俗以清水一碗，置三箸其中，左手扶箸，右手掬水浇箸上，口默呼鬼神名，箸直立碗水中，即知为某神鬼所凭；取茶米一撮撒碗中禳之，病立愈，名曰“出汤”，效验最速。某妻如法禳之，果少安思睡，戒人勿扰。夜果梦一古衣冠少年，揖而谓曰：“我俞寿霍也。感君破坛得出，特来相谢。”某审其由，曰：“予与君同里。前明思宗殉国之次年乙酉七月，昼寝，见一皂隶来，出铁索系予颈，拘至一处，俨如官署。堂上坐一官，气象尊严，怒容可掬。旁一吏唱名，令跽案下。上坐者叱曰：‘汝家累世受国豢养，奈何勾贼屠毒生灵？罪应炮烙！’即见鬼役舁铜柱一具，高约八尺许，置堂西隅，柱中燃炭，烈焰飞腾，上下俱赤。上坐厉声喝令将予炮烙，左右两鬼役，青面赤须，状貌狰狞，应声曰：‘诺。

    Explanation

    315

    A common practice involves using a bowl of clear water and three chopsticks. One holds the chopsticks with the left hand and pours water over them with the right hand while silently invoking the names of spirits. If the chopsticks stand upright in the water, it indicates that a particular spirit or ghost is possessing the sick person. To ward off the spirit, one sprinkles a handful of tea and rice into the bowl. The disease is cured immediately, and this practice is called "pouring out the soup" (出汤), known for its quick effectiveness. The patient's wife followed this ritual, and the patient felt better and fell asleep, with instructions to others not to disturb him. That night, the patient dreamed of a young man dressed in ancient attire who bowed and said, "I am Yu Shouhao. I thank you for breaking the jar and freeing me. I am from the same village as you. During the year following the death of Emperor Chongzhen of the Ming Dynasty, specifically the year 1645 (乙酉年), I was sleeping during the day when a bailiff appeared, tied an iron chain around my neck, and took me to a place resembling an official office. On the dais sat an official with a dignified and angry expression. A clerk called out my name and ordered me to kneel before the desk. The official shouted, 'Your family has served the state for generations, yet you betrayed it by aiding the enemy and causing harm to innocent people. Your crime deserves punishment by the iron pillar!' I then saw ghostly servants carrying a copper pillar about eight feet high, placed in the west corner of the hall, with a fire burning inside, causing intense heat. The official ordered the young man to be punished by the iron pillar. The ghostly servants, with blue faces, red beards, and terrifying appearances, responded affirmatively.

    316

    ’一撩予发，一褫予衣，予觳媟觫股栗，心胆俱碎，急大声哀呼曰：‘小人并未勾贼，受此酷刑，心实不甘！’上坐怒目叱曰：‘汝犹呼冤也耶！’即将一册掷付予阅。册上大书‘极凶鬼犯一名，余寿鹤，南直隶铜陵县人。顺治二年夏，左师次九江，该犯勾其部曲扰劫沿江一带居民，除淫掳不计外，共杀老幼男女六万八千四百三十五人，罪应炮烙六千八百四十次。’予阅之急辨曰：‘小人乃桐城俞寿霍，生平并不识左帅部曲，尚求察宥。’上坐颜色顿霁，曰：‘汝非铜陵余寿鹤耶？’曰：‘然。’上坐命予起，并命吏稽予阳寿，尚有三十七年，上坐叱责皂隶误拘，命笞三百，仍令送予还阳。

    Explanation

    316

    A person's hair was suddenly pulled, and their clothes were stripped off. The person shivered, trembled, and shook with fear, their heart and courage shattered. In a loud, desperate cry, they exclaimed, "I have not recruited bandits, and yet I am enduring this cruel punishment. I truly cannot accept this!" The person sitting above glared angrily and shouted, "Are you still calling for justice?" They then threw a book towards the person to read. The book clearly stated, "One extremely wicked ghost offender, Yu Shouhuo from Tongcheng, a native of Tongling County in the southern direct jurisdiction. In the summer of the second year of the Shunzhi era, General Zuo stationed his troops at Jiujiang. This offender recruited his troops to disturb and rob the residents along the river, excluding rape and abduction, killing a total of 68,435 men, women, and children. The punishment should be cauldron roasting 6,840 times." The person read it and quickly argued, "I am Yu Shouhuo from Tongcheng. I have never known the troops of General Zuo. I still ask for your mercy." The person sitting above's expression suddenly brightened and said, "Are you not Yu Shouhuo from Tongling?" The person replied, "Yes." The person sitting above ordered the person to stand up and also ordered the official to check their remaining years of life, which still had 37 years left. The person sitting above scolded the clerk for the mistake and ordered 300 strokes of the cane, still ordering to send the person back to the living world.

    317

    甫逾一日，天热，尸已腐臭，皂隶惧曰：‘殆矣！’予执其袂问曰：‘汝将焉置予也？’皂隶踌躇久之，曰：‘我思得一策，较生人为乐，君愿之否？’予问：‘如何？’曰：“南山有狐，拜月炼丹，大道垂成，吾窃夺丹付汝吞之，可作鬼仙，并可从心所欲，不较生人更乐耶？’予见尸已溃，无可如何，只合首肯。皂隶夜携予至南山，果见一大赤狐跪而拜月，口含一珠，忽吐忽吸，大如弹丸，宝光耀目。

    Explanation

    317

    Just over one day had passed, and the heat had caused the body to rot and stink. The servant, alarmed, said, "It's bad!" I took his sleeve and asked, "Where will you put me?" The servant hesitated for a long time and said, "I have thought of a plan, more enjoyable than being alive. Would you like it?" I asked, "How?" He said, "There is a fox on the southern mountain that worships the moon and creates elixirs, on the verge of achieving the great Dao. I will secretly steal the elixir and give it to you to swallow, so you can become a ghost immortal and do as you please, isn't that more enjoyable than being alive?" I saw that the body had already decayed, and there was no choice, so I only nodded in agreement. At night, the servant took me to the southern mountain, and indeed, I saw a large red fox kneeling and worshipping the moon, holding a pearl in its mouth, suddenly spitting it out and sucking it in, as big as a bullet, radiant with precious light.

    318

    皂隶潜出其后，伺其吐时，遽攘以授予吞之。狐失丹大哭，起与皂隶斗，皂隶笑曰：‘始吾畏子，今失丹则畜也，吾不畏子矣。’狐无如何，白眼怒视予曰：‘丹被子吞，此殆天命，得登仙箓，须无忘我！然又须迟我千年乃得成道！’一怒跳跃而去。予吞丹，顷刻即成人体，并可隐形。皂隶嘱曰：‘贺君吞丹，如一意修道，十二年即成地仙，又十二年可晋天仙。最忌犯色戒，而孀妇、处女、比邱尼三种人尤宜忌之，犯者必干天谴，且露形骇世，亦所不利，均宜严戒。君其勉之。

    Explanation

    318

    The low-ranking official sneaked up behind me, waited for me to spit it out, and suddenly snatched it away to give it to me, who then swallowed it. The fox, having lost the pill, cried bitterly and rose to fight the official. The official laughed and said, "At first I feared you, but now that you've lost the pill, you're just an animal, and I no longer fear you." The fox, having no recourse, looked at me with a sideways glance and said angrily, "The pill was swallowed by you; this is likely fate. To ascend to the list of immortals, you must not forget me! But you must also delay me by a thousand years before I can achieve enlightenment!" In a rage, it jumped away. I swallowed the pill, and instantly became human form, and could even become invisible. The official advised me, "Congratulations on swallowing the pill. If you focus on cultivating the Dao, after twelve years you will become a terrestrial immortal, and after another twelve years you can ascend to celestial immortality. The most important thing is to avoid violating the precept of sexual conduct, especially widows, virgins, and nuns. Those who violate it will surely incur divine retribution, and will also expose themselves to the world in a shocking manner, which is also disadvantageous. All should be strictly warned. You should strive diligently."

    319

    ’予谢受教。自后，予乃探名山，济巨川，陆不车，水不船；下达九幽之地，上升九重之天，凡十洲三岛，宇内所称名胜者，无不悉恣予之游观。不饥不寒，随遇而安，凭虚御风，居然散仙。惟欲心未断，见美色不能忘情。初犹谨守皂隶教，其所戒三种人概不敢犯，意勾栏中人无伤名节，因择妓之尤者而狎之。色戒既破，心日放而难收，后见良家好妇人，亦渐垂涎而染指焉。予服狐丹，与寻常鬼怪不同，不惟能具人体，并可幻美男子，陈平、潘安，随意变化，以故良家子多半乐从，予意益得。偶过浙东温州府，会城外爨弄，男女观者如堵，中一及笄女郎，色独殊众。鞠部适演祅庙火，女郎睹其媟亵之状，不禁神痴。

    Explanation

    319

    I thank you for your instruction. Afterward, I explored famous mountains and crossed great rivers, traveling on land without a vehicle and on water without a boat. I descended to the deepest underworld and ascended to the highest heavens, visiting all the legendary islands and continents, and indulging in my travels and observations of all the famous scenic spots in the world. I was neither hungry nor cold, content wherever I was, riding the wind and mist, truly a wandering immortal. However, my desire was not fully extinguished, and I could not forget feelings when seeing beautiful women. Initially, I strictly followed the teachings of the lowly servant, avoiding the three forbidden types of people. I believed that those in the brothels did not harm one's reputation and virtue, so I chose the most exceptional courtesans to be intimate with. After the prohibition against lust was broken, my heart gradually became unrestrained and difficult to control. Later, seeing good women from respectable families, I also gradually became tempted and touched them. I took a fox pill, which made me different from ordinary ghosts and spirits, not only capable of possessing a human body but also transforming into a handsome man, mimicking the beauty and charm of historical figures like Chen Ping and Pan An. As a result, most women from respectable families were happy to follow me, and my intentions were increasingly satisfied. Once passing through Wenzhou, Zhejiang, I happened to see a performance outside the city, where men and women were watching as if a wall. Among them was a young lady of marriageable age whose beauty was unique among the crowd. The performance troupe was just acting out a fire at a temple of evil, and the young lady, seeing the indecent and obscene scenes, could not help but become entranced.

    320

    予窃笑曰：‘此儿情窦开矣！’乃幻为美少年，试挑以目，女郎不以为忤，亦遥以目送情。予喜曰：‘得之矣。’女郎为富室某翁爱女，薄暮，予复隐形，尾之而归。夜分人静，女郎下帏掩户，对灯若有所思。予料其可犯，复幻美男子，推门搴帷径入。女郎惊问：‘君何人也？夤夜那得来此？’予揖之曰：‘日间幸睹玉容，爱慕不忍舍，特逾墙相从，求卿见怜。’女郎沉吟良久，便低声问予邦族，诡词以对。女郎意甚欣然，嘱勿高声。予遽扉要与共寝，曲尽绸缪，情均伉俪。年馀，渐有窃议者，女郎告予来去切须慎密，防人觉察。予慰之曰：‘小生幼受异人秘传，可以隐形，虽天神地祇亦不能奈我何，卿请放心。’女郎不信，予姑隐形使觇之，乃大喜。

    Explanation

    320

    I secretly laughed and said, "This girl's heart is beginning to open!" Then, I transformed into a handsome young man and flirted with her with a look. The girl did not take it as an offense and sent feelings with a distant look. I was delighted and said, "I have it now." The girl was the beloved daughter of a wealthy man. At dusk, I became invisible again and followed her home. At midnight, when everyone was quiet, the girl lowered the curtain and closed the door, sitting by the lamp as if thinking. I thought she could be approached and transformed into a handsome man again. I pushed the door open, lifted the curtain, and walked in directly. The girl asked in surprise, "Who are you? How could you come here in the middle of the night?" I bowed to her and said, "I was fortunate enough to see your beautiful face during the day, and my love and admiration made me unable to leave. I especially climbed over the wall to follow you, asking you to show mercy." The girl thought for a long time and then asked about my family background. I answered with a fabricated story. The girl was very pleased and told me not to speak loudly. I quickly opened the door and wanted to sleep together, fully expressing affection and care, our feelings as equal as those of a married couple. After over a year, there began to be those who secretly discussed us. The girl told me to be careful and discreet in coming and going, preventing others from noticing. I reassured her, "I was young and received secret teachings from a mysterious person. I can become invisible, and even gods and spirits cannot do anything to me. Please rest assured." The girl did not believe me, so I temporarily became invisible to observe. She then greatly delighted.

    321

    其家察予踪迹不得，或有猜为妖者，因使阿母来伴女寝，伺母睡熟，两人交如故。一夜母醒，闻而大呼，家人执梃破扉入，索予不得，益信为妖。富翁遍祷东岳、城隍各神，又延僧道祓除，皆无效。富翁怒，使人往龙虎山诉于天师，寻遣法官来治。登坛礼蘸，伎俩亦与僧道等。予顾而姗笑之，戏谓女郎曰：‘技止此耳。’越三日，甫交子初，法官具表上申，召请上界星官神将，予犹不以为意。忽见法官将袖一拂，掌中霹雳有声，天门骤开，诸神齐降。予大惊，急欲窜避，而六合俱设罗网，无处可遁。法官以戒方拍案，喝曰：‘妖将何往？诸神速为予擒之！’诸神果遵法旨将予擒置坛中以付，法官勅勒书符封口，笑谓富翁曰：‘幸不辱命，大功告成矣。

    Explanation

    321

    The family could not trace my footsteps, and some suspected me of being a demon. So they sent the grandmother to accompany the girl to sleep, waiting for her to fall asleep, when the two resumed their usual intimacy. One night, the grandmother woke up, heard something, and cried out loudly. The family members, holding sticks, broke down the door and searched for me but could not find me, thus believing more firmly that I was a demon. The wealthy man prayed to all the gods, including the Eastern Peak and the City God, and also invited monks and Taoists to perform exorcisms, but none of these efforts were effective. The wealthy man became angry and sent someone to the Dragon Tiger Mountain to report to the Celestial Master. Soon after, a judge was dispatched to handle the situation. The judge ascended the altar to perform rituals, and his skills were on par with those of the monks and Taoists. I looked at him and laughed derisively, jokingly telling the girl, "That's all they can do." Three days later, just as midnight approached, the judge prepared a report to submit to higher authorities and summoned celestial officials and gods. I still did not take it seriously. Suddenly, I saw the judge wave his sleeve, and there was a clap of thunder in his hand, the gates of heaven suddenly opened, and all the gods descended together. I was greatly startled and wanted to flee, but all six directions were set with nets, leaving no place to escape. The judge struck the table with a talisman and shouted, "Where will the demon go? The gods, quickly capture it for me!" The gods indeed followed his orders and captured me, placing me in the altar. The judge sealed it with a talisman and a spell, smiling at the wealthy man and saying, "I have not failed my mission, the great task is accomplished."

    322

    ’予初进坛，懊闷欲死，犹隐隐闻女郎泣而啼曰：‘何物妖道？杀吾郎矣！’予闻之，肝肠寸断。富翁怒甚，将就坛而烹焉，法官止之曰：‘不可，是无死罪，遣人埋诸故土，幽囚二百年而后释焉可也。’”因问某：“今为何世？”某具告之，屈指恰二百年。又谓某曰：“曩者，女郎曾赠金条脱一只、白金十铤，予无所用，藏某山马鞍石后，计当无恙，坎地三尺必得之。感君见放，聊以报德。”某问：“今将何往？”曰：“予深悔前愆，今将韬迹深山，省过修心，期证大道，庶不负皂隶所教耳。君第识予言，请从此别。”拱手径去。某顿惊寤，病良瘥。追忆梦语，疑信参半。询诸故老，旧果有俞寿霍其人者。某山固有大石，形肖马鞍，山距某家仅里许。翌辰，试坎其地三尺许，果如言得女郎所赠物。

    Explanation

    322

    When I first stepped onto the altar, I was overcome with despair, nearly dying of grief. I still faintly heard the young lady weeping and crying, saying, “What demon or sorcerer are you? You have killed my beloved!” When I heard this, my heart was torn into pieces. The wealthy man was furious and was about to have me boiled at the altar, but the judge stopped him, saying, “You cannot do that. He has no capital crime. It is sufficient to have people bury him in his native soil, imprison him in darkness for two hundred years, and then release him.” He then asked me, “What year is this?” I told him in full, and when I counted, it had been exactly two hundred years. He said to me, “In the past, the young lady once gave me a single gold ring and ten ingots of silver. I had no use for them, so I hid them behind the horse-saddle stone of a certain mountain. I believe they should still be safe. If you dig three feet into the ground, you will surely find them. I am grateful that you have set me free, and I offer this as a token of my gratitude.” I asked, “Where will you go now?” He replied, “I deeply regret my former sins. Now I intend to conceal my tracks in the deep mountains, examine my faults and cultivate my mind, and strive to achieve the Great Way, so as not to辜负 the teachings of the lowly servant. Please remember my words, and take your leave from here.” With a bow, he turned and departed. I suddenly awoke in alarm, and my illness was completely cured. Recalling the words of the dream, I was half doubtful and half believing. I inquired among the old villagers, and indeed there had once been a man named Yu Shouhuo. There is indeed a large stone in that mountain, shaped like a horse saddle, and the mountain is only about a mile from my home. The next morning, I tried digging three feet into the ground, and as he had said, I found the gift given by the young lady.

    323

    里乘子曰：勾贼殃民，大干天怒，炮烙无已，冥法极当。稗史载明末张、李二贼至今在冥司犹日上火床，观此，益信其言不谬。俞氏子既服狐丹，天仙可望，惜隳色戒，久滞幽囚；然一经再睹天日，便知悔悟，深山韬迹，省过修心，倘坚持弗懈，大道之成，可为操券。
    　　
    　　萧状元
    　　
    　　茶陵萧殿撰，未达时，尝于除夜梦登天门，入一官第，案置一册，历书天下士子姓名，首册第一行，上书“萧锦忠”三字，下书某科状元；后有字折角不可窥测，旁一吏揭以示之，乃“死于火”三字，大惊而寤。萧初名某，以梦兆，改曰锦忠，果于某科以第一人及第。素嗜饮酒，饮少辄醉。会冬日有亲故招饮，夜阑留宿，酷冷，炽炭于室，萧闭门拥炉独坐，倦甚，垂头睡熟，足踏炭上，醉酣竟不自觉，果焚死。

    Explanation

    323

    The Sage of the Village said: "Rebels who harm the people greatly offend heaven, and their punishment is endless. The laws of the underworld are just. Minor historical records recount that Zhang and Li, two rebels from the late Ming dynasty, are still punished daily on the iron bed in the underworld. Seeing this, one can believe that his words are not false. Yushisan, after taking the fox pill, could hope for immortality, but he regretfully broke his vow of chastity and was long imprisoned in seclusion. However, once he saw the light of day again, he knew to repent, hiding in the deep mountains, reflecting on his faults and cultivating his mind. If he persists without slackening, he can achieve the great way, and it can be said with certainty."
