He summoned the younger brother, held his hand, and wept as he said, "My illness is so severe that I may not recover. From now on, we will be separated like hands and feet! The inheritance left by our father will not lead to starvation, but what will we do about the lack of offspring? Your wife is virtuous, as you know, and she is fortunately pregnant. If it is a boy, help your wife raise him well, and when he grows up, be sure to teach him to read; if it is a girl, treat her well too, and choose a good son-in-law for her, so that she does not lose her place. You will have many children in the future, and you should take over my responsibilities and lineage, helping your wife uphold the chastity of the Bochu. My eyes are closed. If you value the bond of blood, remember my final instructions and do not forget them." The younger brother comforted him, saying, "Brother, please take care of yourself and rest. It will not come to that. If by misfortune it does, I dare not disobey your command?" The elder brother nodded twice and passed away shortly thereafter.

    406

    仲为治丧殊草草，嫂虽不慊，犹以奢不如从俭，曲谅其无他也。无何，嫂分娩，视呱呱者俨然男也，心大快慰。仲亦欣然庆兄有后。儿状甚英伟，但苦善啼，乳之不哺，百方呵抚之不止。延医谛视指纹，佥谓无病，而哭则未尝一刻缀也。嫂甚忧之。甫三日，竟以不乳殇。嫂抱尸恸泣，几不欲生，戚党再三劝慰乃啜泣。召仲谓曰：“亡人寄托之言历历在耳，嫂所以忍死须臾者，恋恋此一块肉耳。今已矣，复何望哉！汝如重骨肉情，必厚其衣衾棺槨，待此藐诸孤以成人之礼。

    Explanation

    406

    During the funeral arrangements, Zhong handled everything very frugally. Although his sister-in-law was not entirely satisfied, she still believed that simplicity was better than extravagance and excused him without suspicion. Soon after, the sister-in-law gave birth to a baby boy, and seeing the newborn clearly a boy, she was greatly relieved. Zhong also joyfully congratulated his brother for having an heir. The child looked very strong and handsome, but he suffered from frequent crying and refused to nurse. Despite trying every method to soothe and comfort him, the efforts were in vain. They consulted a doctor who examined the child's fingerprints, and all agreed that there was no illness, but the child continued to cry without stopping for a moment. The sister-in-law was very worried about the child. After just three days, the child died from refusing to nurse. The sister-in-law cried over the child's body and almost wanted to die, but was consoled by family and friends and finally wept softly. She then called Zhong and said to him, "The words of trust left by the deceased are still fresh in my ears. I have endured for a moment, clinging to this piece of flesh. Now it is all over, what else can I hope for! If you value the ties of kinship, you must provide him with good clothing, bedding, and a decent coffin, and treat this young orphan with the rites of adulthood."

    407

    俟殡殓毕，吾亦将从汝兄地下，所有资产壹以付汝。汝如重违我意，我夫妇九泉亦必默佑也！汝意云何？”仲不得已允之。爰市美材殓儿，权厝兄墓之侧。嫂复延僧唪诵经咒，所费不资，仲心怏怏而不敢言。七日事毕，嫂遂绝食，粒米勺水不入于口，终日嚎啕恸哭，血泪俱枯，邻里闻之，无不寒心。时方冬月，朔风怒号，黑云四合，气象愁惨，忽霹雳一声，天顿晴霁。人言伯与儿棺均为雷击开，有二人跽墓侧，不知何故。嫂闻亟挥泪力疾趋视，见伯与儿俱生，而跽者则仲与邻村某妪也。

    Explanation

    407

    After the burial and funeral rites were completed, I too will follow your brother into the afterlife, and I will give all my assets to you. If you strongly go against my wishes, my husband and I in the afterlife will certainly silently protect you! What is your opinion?" Zhong reluctantly agreed. Then he bought fine materials to bury the child, temporarily interring him beside his brother's tomb. The sister-in-law again invited monks to recite sutras and mantras, and the expenses were excessive. Zhong was unhappy but did not dare to speak. After seven days, the affairs were finished, and the sister-in-law then stopped eating. Neither grains of rice nor a spoonful of water entered her mouth. She wailed and wept bitterly all day, her tears of blood both dried up. The neighbors, hearing of it, all felt heartbroken. At that time, just in the winter month, the northern wind howled fiercely, and dark clouds gathered around. The atmosphere was gloomy and sorrowful. Suddenly, there was a thunderbolt sound, and the sky suddenly cleared up. People said that the coffins of the elder brother and the child were both struck open by lightning, and there were two people kneeling beside the tomb, but they did not know why. Upon hearing, the sister-in-law quickly shed tears and with great effort rushed to look. She saw that the elder brother and the child were both alive, but those who were kneeling were Zhong and a certain old woman from the neighboring village.

    408

    急令人舁伯，自抱儿从与俱归，进食进乳，居然各庆再生矣。先是，仲以伯既死，虑生儿不能并其产，以某妪素为人接生，重贿之曰：“女也则已；男也则为计戕之。愿以五十金为寿。”妪初不肯，请倍之，妪利其贿，乃诺之。为嫂接生时，悄以花针纳儿脐中，而他人不知也。至是仲与妪被击，各手持元宝一枚，脐针则拔插妪额，其半入骨，血缕缕犹未绝也。噫！谁谓彼苍者愦愦哉！众议以伯与儿棺殓仲、妪，即同葬其穴。后伯夫妇寿至八十，生四子。怜仲无子，以一嗣焉。儿后举孝廉。

    Explanation

    408

    Immediately order someone to carry the elder brother, and carry the child himself and return together. They fed him and nursed him, and indeed, they all celebrated as if reborn. Before this, the second brother, because the elder brother had died, was concerned that the living child could not inherit his property. He bribed a certain old woman, who had always been a midwife, saying, "If it is a girl, then it is enough; if it is a boy, then plan to kill it. I wish to give fifty gold pieces as a gift." The old woman initially refused, but when asked for double the amount, she was lured by the bribe and agreed to it. When the sister-in-law was giving birth, she secretly inserted a needle into the child's navel, but others did not know about it. At this point, the second brother and the old woman were struck, each holding one ingot of silver. The needle in the navel was pulled out and stuck in the old woman's forehead, half of it going into the bone, and the blood was still flowing. Ah! Who would say that the heavens are so foolish! The crowd decided to bury the elder brother and the child in the same coffin with the second brother and the old woman, and bury them together in the same grave. Later, the elder brother and his wife lived to be eighty, having four sons. Pitying the second brother for having no children, they gave him one as a successor. The child later passed the examination for filial piety and integrity.

    409

    里乘子曰：或谓雷击仲、妪诚快，可惜太晚；倘击于二人贿商时，不较直捷乎？予曰：否否。伯之再生，实嫂之烈感之也。倘击于贿商时，不过儿幸免无害，而嫂烈不彰，伯何由再生也耶！夫嫂贤虽伯所素知，而天必试其果否。迨儿死取决意绝食从伯地下，嚎啕干哭，人不忍闻，岂天听在上而忍闻之乎？至是，特于冬藏时为嫂破格奋其一击之威，不惟当时人心称快，即千载闻之，亦无不同声称快。不然儿生可矣，谓伯与俱生，非嫂之烈有以感之，吾不信也。彼苍赏罚，历历不爽如是，可不畏哉！

    Explanation

    409

    里乘子说：有人说雷击中了仲和老妇人，确实令人痛快，可惜来得太晚；如果他们在贿赂商人时就被雷击，不是更直接吗？我说：不是这样。伯的重生，实际上是嫂的强烈情感所感召的。如果他们在贿赂商人时被雷击，不过孩子侥幸免于伤害，而嫂的烈性未能彰显，伯又如何能重生呢！嫂的贤德虽然伯一直知道，但天必定要测试他们是否真正贤德。等到孩子死后，嫂决定绝食追随伯到地下，她嚎啕大哭，人们不忍心听，难道天在上而能忍心听吗？于是，在冬藏时，特别为嫂破例发威一击，不仅当时人们称快，即使千载之后听到，也没有不同意见称快。否则，孩子活着可以，但说伯和孩子一起活着，如果不是嫂的烈性感动了天，我是不信的。天的赏罚，历历分明，如此分明，怎能不敬畏呢！

    410

    大通佘翁
    　　
    　　铜陵大通镇，前滨大江，商估云集。镇佘姓，巨族也。有佘翁设旗亭于江浒，过客就而啜茗，日繁有徒。一日，客散后，翁于座上拾一布槖，内贮白金三百两，知客所遗，姑藏待之。少顷，一客遑遽而来，东西寻觅。翁问客：“胡为者，得勿有所失乎？”客曰：“然，顷遗一布槖，内有白金若干，翁见之否？”翁以其言既符，笑曰：“君言不谬。幸是老夫拾得，理合奉还。”遂携槖出，并请验封识件数而后归之。客曰：“翁真长者，愿分半相酬。”翁坚辞不受。客感激拜谢，称叹而去。

    Explanation

    410

    Grand Master Shé of Dàtōng

    Dàtōng Town in Tongling City, Anhui Province, is located on the banks of the Yangtze River, where merchants and traders gather like clouds. The Shé family is a large and influential clan. There was an elderly man named Shé who set up a flag pavilion by the riverbank, where travelers would come to rest and drink tea, and it became a popular spot daily.

    One day, after the guests had left, the old man picked up a cloth bag on his seat, which contained 300 taels of silver. Knowing it was left by a guest, he temporarily stored it and waited for the owner. A short while later, a guest came anxiously, searching everywhere. The old man asked the guest, "What are you doing? Have you not lost something?" The guest replied, "Yes, I just lost a cloth bag containing 300 taels of silver. Did you see it?" The old man found the guest's words to be true and smiled, saying, "You are not mistaken. Fortunately, I found it. It is proper to return it." He then took the bag out and asked the guest to verify the seal and count the items before returning it. The guest, deeply moved, bowed in thanks and said, "The old man is truly a noble person. I wish to share half as a reward." The old man firmly declined, and the guest expressed deep gratitude and left, praising the old man's integrity.

    411

    将渡江，适遇大风，见中流一舟，簸荡欹侧，势甚危急，客恻然心动，以失金幸返，不如舍此傥来物作一善事。乃手持白金大声呼曰：“有能救来舟之急者，当重犒之。”岸檥渔舟甚多，贪利，争破浪往，将来舟众人扶登渔舟，来舟载轻，得以无恙。既达岸，客出金分犒舟人，同声称谢。内一少年擥客袂曰：“小人危急，脱不遇君子，断无生理。请至舍间，当使合家拜德，稍伸谢忱。”客再三辞之，不得已至其家。少年亟呼父出见客，其父非他，即佘翁也。翁年六旬仅此一子，向非还金，则已葬鱼腹矣。还金而救子厄，天道亦巧矣哉！

    Explanation

    411

    As he was about to cross the river, he encountered a sudden strong wind and saw a boat in midstream, rocking violently and tilting precariously, in a very dangerous situation. Moved with compassion, the guest reflected that hoping to return safely by keeping the gold was less virtuous than giving up this accidental possession and doing a good deed. He then took the silver in his hand and called out loudly, "Whoever can rescue the boat in danger, I will reward them handsomely." Many fishing boats were moored along the shore; greedy for the reward, they rushed forward through the waves. The people from the distressed boat were helped onto the fishing boat, and since the boat was now lighter, it was able to reach the shore safely. Once they had reached the shore, the guest took out the silver and distributed it among the boatmen, who all thanked him together. One of the youths seized the guest’s sleeve and said, "In my moment of danger, had I not met a gentleman, I would certainly have perished. Please come to my home, and I will have my whole family bow in gratitude, to slightly express our heartfelt thanks." The guest refused many times, but in the end had to go to his home. The youth hurriedly called his father to come out and meet the guest; his father was none other than Mr. She. The old man, now sixty years of age and having only this one son, would have already been buried in the belly of a fish had the gold not been returned. Returning the gold and thus saving his son from disaster—how skillfully the Way of Heaven works!

    412

    里乘子曰：好善之心，人所同有。施者初原不望报，而受者究断不可不报。古今负恩之人，岂有能常昌者哉！顾人之报施本于情，天之报施准于理。人情不漓，天理自然不爽。太上曰：“善恶之报，如影随形。”言其速也。彼佘翁还金救子，其祸福转移，只争瞬息，天之报之，可谓巧且速矣。观此，而好善之心有不油然而生者乎？
    　　
    　　雷击三则
    　　
    　　九华山庸通和尚言，池阳有塾师，设帐古寺，授徒凡十有三人。忽雷雨暴作，霹雳一声，将其徒俱击死，环跽寺外。远近来观，见冠者半，童子半，面各置钱三千八百四十六文，麻绳贯之，莫喻其故。旁一人言：“此得勿某生卖妇钱耶？若此，雷击宜矣！”众问何故，曰：“距寺密迩有某生，贫病交集，家唯一妻，伉俪甚敦。以无所为计，不得已将妻鬻去。夫妇相持痛哭而别。生得钱五十千文，铺垫草荐下，钥门出。

    Explanation

    412

    里乘子曰：好善之心，人所同有。施者初原不望报，而受者究断不可不报。古今负恩之人，岂有能常昌者哉！顾人之报施本于情，天之报施准于理。人情不漓，天理自然不爽。太上曰：“善恶之报，如影随形。”言其速也。彼佘翁还金救子，其祸福转移，只争瞬息，天之报之，可谓巧且速矣。观此，而好善之心有不油然而生者乎？

    The Master of the Path said: The heart inclined to goodness is something all people share. The giver originally does not expect repayment, but the receiver ultimately must not fail to repay. Throughout history, those who have been ungrateful—how could they ever prosper? Human sentiments are not yet diluted, and heavenly principles naturally do not err. The Tao Te Ching states: "The retribution for good and evil follows like a shadow." This speaks of how swift it is. Consider the case of the old man who returned the gold to save his son—the shift between fortune and misfortune happens in an instant. Heaven's retribution can be both clever and swift. Seeing this, can one not feel a spontaneous urge to cultivate a benevolent heart?

    雷击三则

    Three Cases of Lightning Strikes

    Nine华山的庸通和尚说，在池阳有一位私塾老师，在一座古寺中设帐授徒，共有十三名学生。突然间雷雨大作，一声霹雳，将他的学生们全部击死，他们围坐在寺外。远近的人都来看，发现一半是成年男子，一半是儿童，每个人脸上都放着三千八百四十六文钱，用麻绳穿在一起，没有人能理解其中的原因。旁边有人说：“这难道不是某人卖妻的钱吗？如果是这样，被雷击中也是应该的！”众人问原因，那人说：“离寺庙不远处有个叫某人的男子，他贫穷多病，家里只有一个妻子，夫妻感情深厚。因为没有其他办法，不得已将妻子卖掉。夫妇俩紧紧相拥痛哭而别。他得到了五十千文钱，将钱放在草荐下，锁上门离开了。

    413

    及归，索钱不得，既恨失资，又恸失妇，遂雉经死。妇闻之失声大恸，哭曰：‘是奴负吾夫也夫！是奴负吾夫也夫！’乃援剪刀刺喉以殉。然则此钱得勿即某生者耶？”众统为计算之，恰符其数。盖其徒阚某生出，窃攫而瓜分之也。众议以钱殡殓某生。且商之后夫，迎妇棺归，与生合葬。墓在池郡东南三十里，山上恒生连理树，人以为夫妇义烈所感，因称为“义烈墓”。吾皖定远县北炉桥，巨镇也。有客冬月赁车过桥上，闻下有呱呱声，异而觇之，则席裹男婴存焉。

    Explanation

    413

    Upon returning home, he could not find the money. Already regretting the loss of funds, he mourned the loss of his wife and then committed suicide by hanging. The wife heard this and cried out in great grief, saying, "This slave has wronged my husband!" She then took a pair of scissors and stabbed her throat to die with him. Could these coins not have belonged to someone named Mou? The crowd calculated it, and it exactly matched the amount. It seems that one of his disciples, named Kan Mou, stole and divided them. The crowd agreed to use the money to bury Mou. They also arranged for the second husband to bring the wife's coffin back and bury her alongside Mou. The tomb is located thirty miles southeast of Chi County. A tree with intertwined branches always grows on the mountain. People believe it is due to the loyalty and righteousness of the couple, and it is therefore called the "Tomb of Loyalty and Righteousness." North Furnace Bridge in Dingyuan County, Anhui Province, is a large town. A guest rented a carriage to cross the bridge in winter. He heard a crying sound below and looked down curiously, and found a baby boy wrapped in a mat.

    414

    客恻然谓车子曰：“我在客中，无可位置。汝土人，倘知有乳母能收养此儿，愿出白金五十两为哺儿之资。”车子喜曰：“小人妻新生女，正苦无子。将此儿与女共哺，他日长成，即为螟蛉，不亦可乎？”客大称善。乃取羊裘裹儿，并出元宝一枚付车子，令抱儿送交其妻。车子至河上，掷儿水中，以银与裘付妻，徒手含笑而返。客问如何，曰：“比小人归，妻正哺女，得儿大喜，且颂大德不置也。”越明年三月，雷击车子死，背书“贪财伤命”四字。

    Explanation

    414

    The guest, moved with pity, said to the cart driver, "I have no place to stay. You, a native, if you know of a wet nurse who can take care of this child, I am willing to pay fifty taels of silver as a fee for nursing the child." The cart driver was pleased and said, "My wife has just given birth to a daughter, and she has no child. If I give this child to my wife and daughter to nurse together, he will be our adopted son, will he not?" The guest greatly praised this idea. He then took the child and wrapped him in a sheepskin, giving the cart driver a silver ingot and saying, "Take the child and the sheepskin to your wife." The cart driver went to the river, threw the child into the water, and gave the silver and sheepskin to his wife, returning home empty-handed and laughing. The guest asked what happened, and the cart driver replied, "By the time I returned, my wife was nursing the child and was very happy, praising your great kindness without end." The next year in March, lightning struck the cart driver dead, and the words "greed for wealth harms life" were written on his back.

    415

    人问其妻，始知前事。某年春夏之交，定远苦早，早禾无收。六月中旬，甫得甘霖，农人心稍慰。佥谓种荞可以救荒，但苦无种。某甲藏陈荞甚多，虑颗瘪色黯，不能出售，以甑蒸之，顿觉硕大光润，大喜，遂榜其门曰：“出卖荞种。”于是争往购求。荞价翔贵，获利千万。后所种荞竟无一出者，众无所归，怨叹为天意使然。然由此饥饿以死者指不胜屈矣。无何，雷击某甲死，釜底书“蒸荞误人，粉身莫赎”八字。人始知甲险恶而不能逃天诛也。此上二则，皆定远陈逸仙茂才维翰言之。
    　　
    　　里乘子曰：天诛所以补国法之所不及，而较国法更为快意。何也？国法本乎律例，天诛本乎情理；律例但守乎经，情理可行乎权。彼车子溺婴，某甲蒸荞，及十三人以五十千钱而伤二命，倘执律例，或未必尽置之死；而揆诸情理，似此恶作剧，岂尚可一人而贷之以生乎？嗟乎！明明在上，凡蚩蚩之氓，可不时存警畏哉！

    Explanation

    415

    A man asked his wife, and only then did he learn the details of the previous events. In a certain year, during the transition between spring and summer, Dingyuan suffered from a severe drought, and the early rice crop failed. In mid-June, just as the much-needed rain began to fall, the farmers' hearts were somewhat comforted. It was generally believed that planting buckwheat could save from famine, but there was a shortage of seeds. Mr. A had hidden a large quantity of old buckwheat, worrying that the seeds were shriveled and dark, making them unsellable. He steamed the buckwheat with a steamer, and it suddenly appeared larger and lustrous. Delighted, he posted a notice on his door saying, "Selling buckwheat seeds." People rushed to buy them. The price of buckwheat soared, and Mr. A made a profit of millions. However, the buckwheat they planted did not produce a single sprout, leaving the people with nowhere to turn. They complained and lamented that it was the will of heaven. Nevertheless, many died of hunger, and the number of deaths was uncountable. Soon after, Mr. A was struck by lightning and died. On the bottom of a pot, the words "Steaming buckwheat misled people, even if ground into powder, it cannot be redeemed" were written. People realized that Mr. A was both cunning and evil, and he could not escape divine retribution. These two stories were told by Chen Yixian, a talented and virtuous person from Dingyuan, whose courtesy name was Weihan.

    The commentator, Li Chengzi, said: Divine retribution serves to make up for the inadequacies of state law and is even more satisfying than state law. Why is this so? State law is based on legal statutes, while divine retribution is based on human sentiments and reason. Legal statutes merely adhere to the classics, while human sentiments and reason allow for pragmatism. For instance, Mr. Zhi drowned an infant, Mr. A steamed buckwheat, and thirteen people with fifty thousand coins injured two lives. If one strictly adheres to legal statutes, they might not all be sentenced to death. But if one considers human sentiments and reason, such mischievous acts, could one still spare the lives of these individuals? Alas! The bright and clear one above, all the foolish and ignorant masses, should always keep a sense of fear and caution!

    416

    猎人某
    　　
    　　池阳猎人某，素性暴逆。母年五十许，奴畜之。常以烹鹿蹄失饪，挞几死。妻贤，每以规劝，并遭箠楚。一日，网得生兔，命母饲养，绳弛逸去，母惧，伏匿积薪中。某求兔不得，拔剑向妻问母所在，妻不以告。某怒曰：“老畜得毋匿积薪中？待搜得，当杀却！”母闻而大惧，亟出狂奔。某见往追，妻哭，力揽其襟，某奋不得脱，益怒，杀妻，并断其手。复追母，母过关圣庙，大声哭且呼曰：“关爷救命！关爷救命！”某刚至庙门，圣帝座前泥塑周将军蓦驰出，以木刀腰斩某倒地上。远近闻而来观，见神像一足立户外，瞪目望尸，须眉奕奕，若有馀怒，刀上血犹淋漓滴沥也。众乃舁去某尸，拭净血迹，醵资延道士礼蘸三日；率其母刑牲祷祭，妥神归庙。自是不孝者闻之，皆悚然改辄，不敢再忤其亲矣。

    Explanation

    416

    A Hunter of Chiyang

    A certain hunter from Chiyang was by nature violent and rebellious. His mother, who was about fifty years old, was treated by him like a servant or beast. He often cooked deer hooves improperly and beat her nearly to death. His wife was virtuous and often tried to advise him, but she too was beaten with whips.

    One day, he caught a live rabbit and ordered his mother to raise it. The rabbit escaped when the rope loosened, and the mother, frightened, hid among a pile of firewood. When the hunter could not find the rabbit, he drew his sword and confronted his wife, demanding to know where his mother was. When she refused to tell him, he grew angry and said, “Could it be that the old beast is hiding among the firewood? If I find her, I will kill her!” Hearing this, the mother was greatly terrified and ran out in a frenzy. The hunter saw her fleeing and went after her. His wife cried and tried with all her strength to hold him back by grasping his robe, but he struggled and could not break free. Even more enraged, he killed his wife and also cut off her hand.

    He then pursued his mother, who passed by the Temple of Guan Yu and cried out, “Lord Guan, save me! Lord Guan, save me!” Just as the hunter reached the temple gate, the clay statue of General Zhou, standing before the Divine Emperor’s throne, suddenly galloped out and struck the hunter down with a wooden blade, cutting him in two at the waist and sending him crashing to the ground.

    People from far and near came to see what had happened and saw the deity’s statue standing on one foot outside the temple, staring intently at the body, its beard and eyebrows vivid and lifelike, as if still filled with anger. Blood still dripped from the blade.

    The people then carried away the hunter’s body, wiped away the bloodstains, pooled funds to invite Taoist priests, and performed a three-day ritual of purification. They also led the hunter’s mother in animal sacrifice and prayer, to appease the deity and return it to the temple.

    From that time on, those who were disrespectful to their parents, upon hearing this tale, all became fearful and changed their ways, and dared not again defy their parents.

    417

    里乘子曰：世传周将军灵异不少，陈延祚《三国志》竟不载其名；惟元人《关圣庙碑》有“乘赤兔兮从周仓”之语，自有所本，必非杜撰。或谓昔吴亭关圣麾下一偏将，持大刀，揽鲁肃袂历声责之，即是将军，惜史氏失载耳。木刀杀人，奇甚！如猎人之暴逆，脱不遇将军，则母休矣。天下有理之所无、而事之所有者，况乱臣贼子，人人得而诛之，岂有神武如将军，血食一方，而忍漠视不顾者乎？吾愿世之为人子者，闻将军之风，皆当加警矣。

    Explanation

    417

    The author of the Li Cheng Zi says: It is said in the world that General Zhou has many mysterious and supernatural occurrences. Chen Yanzuo's Records of the Three Kingdoms does not even mention his name. Only a Yuan dynasty stele at the Guan Gong Temple mentions "Riding the Red Hare and followed by Zhou Cang." There must be a basis for this, it cannot be fabricated. Some say that a subordinate general under Guan Gong in Wu, holding a large blade and sternly reprimanding Lu Su, was this very general. Killing with a wooden blade, very strange. Like the hunter's violent and rebellious act, if not for the encounter with the general, her mother would have been killed. There are things in the world that are impossible by reason but possible by fact. Traitors and rebels, everyone can kill them. As mighty as the general, feasting on blood in one region, would anyone dare to ignore or neglect him? I wish for the world's children, to hear the general's spirit, all should be on alert.

    418

    行脚僧
    　　
    　　池阳有老衲，独居古刹。丐募积有微资，以钱易朱提十馀铤，铤各五两许，吝惜备至，衣常不周，食常不饱，晏如也。一日，晨起啜粥，会九华某僧行脚至刹，饥甚，出钵乞食，再三哀求，衲啜不顾。某僧饥忿莫遏，拔戒刀洞老衲胸殪之。饱啖，搜攫其资而遁。越日，土人见老衲杀死刹中，以其无主，瘗而火其刹，其事以寝。某僧既杀老衲，缠资腰中，以无人知，意甚得。乃腰缠日重，久之，几如佩千钧，苦难行走。不得已，至深山中，以刀坎地薶其半，而重如故，尽埋乃已。

    Explanation

    418

    A Wandering Monk

    In Chiyang, there was an old monk who lived alone in an ancient temple. Through begging and alms, he had accumulated a small amount of wealth, which he exchanged for over ten bars of gold, each weighing about five taels. He was extremely frugal, often not well-dressed and often not well-fed, yet he remained calm and unperturbed. One day, after drinking porridge at dawn, he encountered a wandering monk from Jiuhua Mountain who had arrived at the temple. The wandering monk was very hungry, so he took out his begging bowl to ask for food, pleading repeatedly, but the old monk ignored him. The wandering monk, unable to contain his hunger and anger, drew his precept knife and stabbed the old monk in the chest, killing him. Satisfied, he ate heartily, searched for and took the old monk's wealth, and then fled. The next day, the locals found the old monk killed in the temple. Since there was no owner, they buried him and burned the temple, and the matter was thus settled. After killing the old monk, the wandering monk tied the wealth to his waist. Because no one knew, he felt very satisfied. However, the weight around his waist grew heavier over time, almost like carrying a thousand catties, making it difficult for him to walk. Unable to bear it, he went to a deep mountain and dug a hole to bury half of the wealth, but it was still as heavy. Eventually, he buried it all and then stopped.

    419

    又每日暮，即见老衲在左右，且闻口宣佛号曰：“阿弥陀佛。”喃喃不休。心恶之，知其冤孽难解，计朝菩萨或可忏悔，乃遍至五台、峨眉、南海各灵山，顶礼求忏，卒无验。无可如何，仍归九华，甫跻上天门，惨然色变，大声呼曰：“老衲又来索命矣！我去，我去！”遂历将前事朗告诸人，言毕，自挝其胸曰：“此事何能瞒人？我死晚矣！”耸身一跃，崖下适有枯枝，恰坠其上，贯胸而死。斯时崖上崖下观者数千人，无不咋舌骇叹，佥合十作偈曰：“孽由自作，信不可活。凡我众生，鉴此行脚。”又下一转语曰：“吝粥一勺，甘丧躯壳。凡我众生，鉴彼老衲。”

    Explanation

    419

    Every evening, I would see an old monk nearby and hear him recite the Buddha's name aloud: "Amitabha Buddha." He muttered incessantly. I disliked this in my heart, knowing that his karmic debts were hard to resolve. I thought that perhaps seeking the Bodhisattva could bring repentance, so I traveled to the sacred mountains of Wutai, Emei, and Nanshan, prostrating myself in prayer and seeking atonement, but ultimately found no results. Helpless, I returned to Jiuhua Mountain. As soon as I reached the gate to heaven, my face turned pale with fear, and I shouted loudly, "The old monk is coming to claim my life again! I am going, I am going!" Then I recounted all the previous events to the people present, and after finishing, I struck my own chest and said, "How could this be hidden from people? I will die later!" With a sudden leap, I fell onto a dry branch below the cliff, which pierced through my chest, causing my death. At that moment, thousands of people on the cliff above and below gasped in horror and sighed. They all joined their hands and composed a verse: "Karma is self-created, belief cannot save. All sentient beings, learn from this wanderer." They added another line: "To be stingy with a single spoon of porridge, willingly lose one's body. All sentient beings, learn from that old monk."

    420

    里乘子曰：财之为祟大矣哉！老衲积资，行脚攫资，究竟两人丝毫未曾用得，转坐此结怨成冤，各丧厥命，斯岂非财之为祟也哉！崖上崖下人两偈，的是见道之语。吾亦从而为之说曰：合十两偈，当头棒喝。凡我众生，从善毋恶。
    　　
    　　当涂令
    　　
    　　某明府以进士宰当涂，性庸暗。县滨大江，冲烦难治，凶岁倍形拮据。某履任，恰值大水，田畂漂没殆尽，官庖突烟垂绝，而饥民日塞署前，嗷嗷求哺。某书空嗟叹，谓幕友曰：“我贫甚矣。自谋不暇，何能顾及若曹？”幕友笑曰：“公何太迂？大水民困若此，谁不知者？据实上状请赈，大吏必许，可得四万金，何忧贫？”某大喜称善。亟具牍申请大府，果发帑金四万。某半侵肥己槖，以半分犒合署幕友暨纪纲人等，而饥民沾惠未及十分之一。死者枕籍于道，惨不能状。

    Explanation

    420

    Li Chengzi said: How great is the calamity caused by wealth! An old monk accumulated wealth through traveling and seizing it, but in the end, neither of the two had ever used a single coin. Instead, they beget enmity and grievances, leading to their own deaths. Is this not the calamity of wealth? The two verses spoken by the people on the mountain and in the valley are indeed words of enlightenment. I also join my hands and recite two verses: A sudden, direct shock to the mind. All sentient beings, follow good and avoid evil.

    The County Magistrate of Dangtu

    A certain county magistrate, who passed the imperial examination, was appointed as the magistrate of Dangtu. He was of mediocre and dim-witted character. The county borders a great river and is difficult to govern due to its bustling nature. In times of famine, the difficulties were even more pronounced. When he took office, a major flood occurred, and almost all the fields were washed away. The fire in the official kitchen was about to go out, and starving people gathered outside the government office, crying out for food. The magistrate sighed and lamented, saying to his advisors, "I am very poor. I cannot even take care of myself, how can I think of you?" The advisor smiled and said, "How can you be so foolish? Who does not know that the people are suffering from the flood? Report the truth and request relief. The higher officials will surely approve, and you can get 40,000 gold coins. Why worry about poverty?" The magistrate was very pleased and praised the suggestion. He quickly prepared a document and applied to the higher authorities, and果然, the government funds of 40,000 gold coins were disbursed. The magistrate embezzled half of the funds for his own gain and distributed the other half to his advisors and staff. However, the starving people received only a fraction of the benefit. Dead bodies were piled up on the road, and the scene was too horrifying to describe.

    421

    某视之莫如也。亡何，某父子与幕友俱染疫死，所吞蚀赈金，耗费无存。某之子妇与二女并幕友之妻子俱留寓当涂，竟不能扶榇回籍。典质既罄，无以为生。始则藉针黹聊以糊口，继为奸媪所诱，觉刺绣又不如倚市门矣。道光丙午，德化万鳌峰夫子官斯土，招予幕府。邑绅蔡翁招饮，以予少年喜谈风月，谓某之雏女甫破瓜，艳而善歌，将召侑觞。予亟止之曰：“君休矣。此女流荡不偶，固乃翁居官作孽使然。然物伤其类，小子虽不肖，实不忍与见也。”蔡点首曰：“君言是也。”乃止。

    Explanation

    421

    A certain man considered himself unmatched in his field. Soon after, his father and son, along with his associates, all died from an epidemic, having embezzled relief funds which were completely spent. His widow and two daughters, along with the wives and children of his associates, remained in Dangtu, unable to return the coffins to their hometowns. With all their collateral exhausted, they had no means of livelihood. At first, they relied on needlework to barely make ends meet, but later, they were lured by a wicked old woman, realizing that embroidery was no longer as good as standing by the market gate. In the year Daoguang Bingwu (1836), the respected scholar Wan Aofeng of Dehua was in charge of the region, inviting me to his office. A local gentry named Cai invited me to a banquet, mentioning that I, being young and fond of discussing poetry and beauty, should attend. He mentioned that the young daughter of someone had just turned sixteen, was beautiful and skilled at singing, and planned to invite her to entertain at the banquet. I immediately stopped him, saying, "You should stop. This woman has been wandering and has had bad luck, certainly because her father-in-law's actions as an official were wicked. However, I feel sympathy for those in similar situations. Though I am unworthy, I truly cannot bear to see her." Cai nodded and said, "Your words are correct." And so, he stopped.

    422

    金钱李二
    　　
    　　吾皖某公，巡抚黔南，其公子自任所归应乡试，挟资甚丰。舟过岳州，忽岸上一客，襆被遥呼趁舟，榜人不答。公子命割席地载之，榜人谓江湖险阻，多不测，不可不慎。公子笑曰：“我舟甚大，正苦岑寂，得一人相伴闲话亦大好。况客只身无长物，当不至有意外变，载之何害？汝勿多疑。”榜人以公子说之谆谆，乃重违其意，檥岸招客登舟。视客年可四十许，紫面阔颡，髯丛如蝟。

    Explanation

    422

    A Certain Official from Anhui and His Son

    A certain official from Anhui served as an inspector in Qian Nan. His son, returning from his post to take the provincial examination, carried a large amount of money and wealth with him. As they passed through Yuezhou, a guest far away on the shore called out to board the boat, but the boatman did not respond. The son of the official commanded the boatman to make room for the guest, and the boatman warned that the river was dangerous and unpredictable, advising caution. The son of the official smiled and said, "Our boat is very large, and we are quite lonely. Having someone to keep us company and chat would be wonderful. Besides, the guest is traveling alone with nothing but himself, so there should be no unexpected trouble. What harm could it do to take him aboard? Please do not worry too much." The boatman, persuaded by the son's persistent words, reluctantly agreed and docked the boat to invite the guest on board. The guest was about forty years old, with a purple complexion, broad forehead, and a thick beard like a hedgehog.

    423

    拱手加额，谢公子高义，并叩邦族，公子以告，客敛容致敬曰：“固是贵公子。小人不知，失礼勿怪。”自言姓李、行二，江西人，“他日舟过敝境，当少伸地主之敬，幸勿见却。”公子颔之。见李语言豪爽，试与谈史事，颇熟，意非庸流。每饭必招与俱，李亦不辞，且豪于饮，醉后论古今成败得失，尤娓娓可听。公子大乐，相见恨晚。一日薄暮，舟泊村市，李携白金一笏登岸。公子笑问：“何为？”李笑曰：“日扰公子，殊切愧恧，将薄市酒脯，聊酬万一。”公子摇手止之，李不顾，掉头径去。

    Explanation

    423

    With a hand salute and a forehead bow, the young master expressed his gratitude for the noble conduct of the young master, and acknowledged the young master's family and social status. The young master informed someone about the encounter, and the guest, showing respect, said, "Indeed, you are a noble young master. I was unaware of your status, please forgive my rudeness." The guest introduced himself as Li, the second son from Jiangxi, and said, "If we meet again, I will show you the hospitality of a host, please don't refuse." The young master nodded in agreement. The young master found Li's speech to be bold and straightforward, and when they talked about history, Li was very knowledgeable, indicating he was not an ordinary person. The young master would invite Li to eat with him, and Li never refused, and he enjoyed drinking. After getting drunk, they would discuss the successes and failures of ancient and modern times, which was particularly engaging to listen to. The young master was very pleased and wished they had met earlier. One evening, when the boat stopped at a village market, Li took a piece of silver and went ashore. The young master smiled and asked, "What for?" Li laughed and said, "I have been bothering you, I feel very ashamed, I will buy some wine and meat to slightly repay you." The young master waved his hand to stop him, but Li ignored him and walked away directly.

    424

    公子窃命仆展视襆被，内一匕首，长尺有半，莹然如雪，寒光逼人；又腊干人手一具，馀无他物。公子惊异，急命依旧束好，意李非侠客即盗魁，心窃自危，而又不敢形于词色，计惟以礼款之，以德结之，或可相感。少选，见李命人担酒一大瓮，并一彘十鸡上船。公子笑谓：“何太过费。”李曰：“小人幸得附舟，此中大有香火因缘。不腆之敬，聊酬公子，兼觞从者，并犒三老。”乃浼庖人代为烹饪。言次，以目视襆被，似已知公子窃发。殽酒既陈，李飞三大觥釂公子，公子亦飞大觥酬之。

    Explanation

    424

    The young master secretly ordered the servant to unfold the wrapped bundle, which contained a dagger, one and a half feet long, shining like snow with a cold, piercing light. There was also a dried human hand, and nothing else. The young master was astonished and quickly ordered it to be wrapped up again. He thought Li was either a knight-errant or a bandit leader, and he secretly felt uneasy, yet dared not show it in his words or expressions. He decided to treat him with courtesy and kindness, hoping it might win his favor. Soon, he saw Li ordering someone to bring a large vat of wine and ten chickens and a pig onto the boat. The young master smiled and said, "Why so extravagant?" Li replied, "I am fortunate to have joined your boat, and there is much good karma here. This modest offering is just to repay you, and to drink with your attendants and honor the respected elders." He then asked the cook to prepare the food. While speaking, he glanced at the wrapped bundle, as if he already knew what the young master had done. When the food and wine were set out, Li drank three large cups to the young master, and the young master also drank a large cup in return.

    425

    酒酣耳热，肝胆毕露，李掀髯笑谓公子曰：“公知小人何许人也？”公子笑拊其背曰：“君为何许人仆所不知，然义气见于眉宇，亦一世豪杰也。”李拊掌狂笑曰：“公真知我也。既遇知己，即不敢不沥诚相告。我非他，乃江湖盗魁金钱李二是也。公囊中黄金若干，白金若干，信否？”公子料不能隐，直应曰：“唯唯，君何以知之？”曰：“曩舟过洞庭，我于岳阳楼望气即已知之。初颇欲不利于公，以遇我厚，厚遇而惨报之，义所不屑也。自今以往，但请毋畏。然公子手无缚鸡力，挟多资远涉江湖，亦危也矣。

    Explanation

    425

    Drunk to the point of warmth in the ears, with his true feelings laid bare, Li lifted his beard and laughed, saying to the young man, "Do you know who I am?" The young man smiled and patted Li's back, saying, "I do not know who you are, but your sense of righteousness is evident in your demeanor, and you are indeed a hero of this era." Li clapped his hands and laughed wildly, saying, "You truly know me. Since we have met, I dare not withhold my sincerity. I am no one else but the pirate leader Money Li the Second of the rivers and lakes. There is a certain amount of gold and silver in your bag, is there not?" The young man, realizing he could not hide it, replied directly, "Yes, yes. How did you know?" Li said, "When we passed Dongting Lake on the boat, I saw the aura from the Yueyang Tower and knew it already. At first, I had intended to harm you, but since you treated me generously, I would not repay such kindness with cruelty, which goes against my principles. From now on, please do not fear me. However, the young man, with no strength to bind a chicken, traveling far with a large sum of money in the rivers and lakes, is also in great danger."

    426

    ”公子闻而失色，如坐芒刺，强为言笑，觥筹交错，痛饮尽欢。既过道士洑，李命榜人系缆芦洲，谓公子曰：“沿途辱公雅爱，业至敝境，即须拜别。蜗庐咫尺，敢请暂税大驾，为林壑生色。”公子辞曰：“理合造庐，一瞻胜境，缘试期已近，早归为幸。”李笑曰：“顷裁夏首，屈指距试期尚远。不敢多留，但作平原十日之饮可耳。”公子再四确辞。时天色已黑，牙月初升，李长啸一声，忽芦苇中无数舴艋，蜂拥而至。公子大惊，李喝曰：“止，止。

    Explanation

    426

    The young lord heard and turned pale, feeling as if he were sitting on a bed of needles. Despite his unease, he forced a smile and engaged in the lively drinking scene, where cups and tokens intermingled, and they drank deeply and enjoyed themselves. After passing through the Taoist Ferry, Li ordered the boatman to tie the vessel to the reed bank and said to the young lord, "I have been honored by your gracious affection along the way, and now that we have reached my humble domain, I must bid you farewell. My modest dwelling is within arm's reach; dare I ask for a brief stay of your esteemed presence to bring color to the mountains and ravines?" The young lord replied, "It is proper to visit the humble abode and see the beautiful scenery, but because the examination period is approaching, it would be fortunate to return early." Li smiled and said, "It is now early summer, and counting on fingers, the examination period is still far off. I dare not keep you long, but a ten-day drinking feast like in the plain is acceptable." The young lord repeatedly and firmly refused. By then, the sky had already turned dark, and the first sliver of the moon had risen. Li let out a long cry, and suddenly, from the reeds, countless small boats came swarming in. The young lord was greatly surprised, and Li shouted, "Stop, stop!"

    427

    勿惊贵人！”乃笑谓公子曰：“特召儿曹延请贵人，别无他意，请勿惊畏。”乃自扶公子登小舟刺入芦苇。谓公子：“尊舟泊此，尽可放心，已命儿曹加意逻守，保无他虑。”二三里许，抵一山谷，已有筍将相待，舍舟而陆，道路蜿蜒，或险或夷。约行四十馀里，前隐隐有鸡犬声，树木阴翳中灯火如星。李曰：“至矣。请公子下舆。”见庄舍一所，涂垩华美，气象甚壮。门外健儿百数十人，左右侍立。李肃公子升堂，铺毡张幔，华烛灿列。时漏已四下，膳毕，请公子就东厢客舍栖宿，供设亦殊不俗。

    Explanation

    427

    "Do not be alarmed, esteemed person!" He smiled and said to the young lord, "I have specially called my attendants to invite a distinguished guest; there is no other intention, please do not be alarmed or afraid." Then he personally escorted the young lord onto a small boat and guided it into the reeds. He told the young lord, "Your boat is anchored here; you can rest assured, as I have already ordered my attendants to guard with extra care, ensuring no other worries." After traveling about two or three miles, they arrived at a valley, where attendants were already waiting. They left the boat and went ashore, following a winding road that was sometimes steep and sometimes level. After traveling about forty miles, they heard faint sounds of chickens and dogs in the distance, and among the dark trees, lights shone like stars. Li said, "We have arrived. Please, sir, disembark." They saw a manor house painted and decorated beautifully, with a very grand atmosphere. Outside, there were more than a hundred strong men standing on both sides. Li escorted the young lord up to the hall, where carpets were laid out and curtains were hung, with luxurious candles arranged brightly. It was already past midnight, and after the meal, Li invited the young lord to rest in the eastern guest quarters, where the furnishings were also quite elegant.

    428

    翌日，张乐设席，水陆杂陈，梨园甚精，奢侈过于王侯。如是者六日，公子颇觉厌倦，李已窥其意，便命停乐。家故有园亭，花木、山石、池沼毕具，接构曲而有致。李尝邀公子游览，颇为赞赏。李乞命名，公子笑曰：“君乃虬髯之流，即名‘虬园’，何如？”李大乐，即请为作擘窠书榜之于楣。日偕公子宴饮虬园，出家姬十馀人，清歌侑觞，态度装饰亦颇不恶。内一雏娃，头绾偏髻，发蓬松覆额，风致韶妙，如奇花之初胎；歌喉清脆，善弹琵琶，目波时溜睨公子，公子亦甚惓惓。

    Explanation

    428

    The next day, music was played and a feast was set up, with a variety of dishes, both aquatic and terrestrial, served. The performances by the imperial music academy were highly skilled, and the luxury surpassed that of kings and dukes. This continued for six days, after which the young man began to feel quite tired. Li, noticing his weariness, ordered the music to stop. The house had a garden with all kinds of flowers, trees, rocks, and ponds, beautifully arranged in a winding and tasteful manner. Li often invited the young man to tour the garden, which he greatly admired. Li asked for a name for the garden, and the young man smiled and said, "Since you are of the same type as the hero with a curly beard, how about calling it 'Qiu Garden'?" Li was very pleased and asked him to write the name in large, bold script for the signboard. Every day, they would gather in the Qiu Garden for feasts, with more than ten of Li's maids serving, singing and entertaining with wine. Their demeanor and appearance were quite good. Among them was a young girl with a half-up hairstyle, her hair loose and covering her forehead, who was graceful and charming, like a bud of a rare flower. Her voice was clear and sweet, and she was skilled at playing the pipa. Her eyes would occasionally glance at the young man, who was also very fond of her.

    429

    李已会意，笑曰：“此儿名夜月，年十三矣。性颇不拙，山妻素所眷爱，尝留意为之择主。公子如不嫌蜀束，俟其成人，当以相赠。”公子唯唯称谢。李有二子，俱从师读书。公子偶至书斋，见其师年可五十许，须发苍然，清癯如鹤。叩之，与李同宗，以多病不乐应试，藉课读养静。颇工导引之术，吐词亦甚博雅。李之二子，长十六，次十二，头角崭然，揖客居然彬彬有礼。室中图书环列，经史悉备。视案头所读本乃孙吴、穰苴《司马法》各书。公子问师：“何不令攻举业？”师笑曰：“若辈之志不在毛锥。

    Explanation

    429

    Li understood, smiled, and said, "This child is named Night Moon and is thirteen years old. He is rather not clumsy, and my wife has always cherished him. She has often been careful in choosing a suitable match for him. If you do not mind that he is from Shu, wait until he grows up, and I will give him to you." The young man nodded and expressed his gratitude. Li has two sons, both studying under a teacher. One day, the young man happened to visit the study room and saw that the teacher was about fifty years old, with gray hair and beard, thin and gaunt like a crane. He asked the teacher, who said he was of the same clan as Li, but due to frequent illness, he did not wish to take exams. Instead, he teaches to maintain a calm and peaceful life. He is skilled in guiding and leading techniques and his speech is also very learned and refined. Li's two sons, the elder being sixteen and the younger twelve, have prominent features, indicating potential or talent. They bow to guests and are indeed polite and courteous. The room is filled with books and scrolls, and all classics and histories are available. The books they read are those of Sun Wu, Sima Rangju, and the Sima Fa (Military Methods). The young man asked the teacher, "Why not make them study for the imperial exams?" The teacher smiled and said, "Their aspirations are not in writing."

    430

    姑令粗解韬钤，万一他日有事，未尝不可执干戈以卫社稷也。”公子嘉叹不置。唯以李维絷甚殷，欲归不得，殊切主臣。乃谓李曰：“君言平原之饮，今既半月，业已过之，愿早就道，免误试期。受惠不浅，况一江上下，路所必经，他日过此，当趋访以续末了之缘可也。”李笑曰：“公子归心甚急，不敢久留，明日即送启行。但君子一言，重于九鼎，他日过此，务求枉顾。夜月既荷不弃，暂为寄育，俟重来携与俱归。

    Explanation

    430

    The young lord said, "Even a temporary, basic understanding of military strategy could be useful in the future, and if there were ever an emergency, I would not hesitate to wield weapons to defend the state." The young lord praised and admired this without end. However, his desire to return home was very earnest, but he was unable to do so, creating a pressing need to fulfill his duty as a subject. He then said to Li, "You mentioned the drink at the plain; it has been half a month since then, and the time has already passed. I wish to leave early to avoid missing the important test. I have received great benefits from you, and since the river is a necessary route, I will visit again in the future to continue the unfinished relationship." Li smiled and said, "The young lord's desire to return home is very urgent, and I dare not keep him long. I will send him off tomorrow. But a gentleman's word is heavier than nine tripods; I hope you will grace me with your presence when you pass by again. Having already received your acceptance, I will temporarily entrust and nurture you, and wait until the next visit to bring and return together."

    431

    切勿负约！”时夜月正侍饮在坐，李拔其金钗一股授公子，亦请公子解所佩玉授夜月，令各藏之，聊以表信。明日日晡，设祖帐于虬园。使夜月清歌送行。夜月唱《会真记》“长亭饯别”一阕，深情绵邈，泪随声进。公子相对脉脉，于邑无色。李强与拇战，使为欢笑。漏初下，始罢席。公子兴辞，李袖出小旗一角授公子，令善藏之：“江湖倘有不虞，出示便可免患。”复以千金为赆。公子受旗而返其赆，李作色曰：“公以小人之物为盗泉耶？盗泉去贪泉几何？如恐为盗泉所污，则公囊中所有，亦未必果皆廉泉也！”公子以李言激烈，只合拜受。

    Explanation

    431

    "Do not break the promise!" At that time, Lady Night Moon was serving drinks and sitting among the guests. Li pulled out one of her gold hairpins and gave it to the young man, and she also asked the young man to take off the jade pendant he was wearing and give it to Lady Night Moon. They were told to keep them well, to serve as tokens of trust. The next day at mid-afternoon, they set up a farewell banquet at the Qiu Garden. They had Lady Night Moon sing a clear song to see him off. Lady Night Moon sang the Hui Zhen Ji ('The True Meeting') tune 'Farewell at the Long Pavilion.' Her deep and lingering emotions were so profound that tears followed the sound. The young man looked at her silently, with no color on his face. Li forced a game of thumb wrestling, to make them laugh. As the night watch began, they finally ended the banquet. The young man stood up to leave, and Li took out a small flag from his sleeve and gave it to the young man, telling him to keep it well: "If there is any danger on the road, showing it can prevent trouble." He also gave him a thousand gold pieces as a gift. The young man accepted the flag but returned the gift. Li changed his expression and said, "Are you treating my gift as if it were from a spring of thieves? How far is the spring of thieves from the spring of greed? If you fear being tainted by the spring of thieves, then everything in your bag may not all be from a spring of integrity!" The young man, finding Li's words too harsh, only fit to bow and accept.

    432

    李坚订后约，谓泊舟处时命儿曹侦伺，过客至此，问贱名无不知者。嘱毕，仍命肩舆送公于归舟。道路坦夷，不似旧径。昧爽始达舟次。舟人见公子，皆大欢喜，佥谓公子去后，李遣人逻守维谨。公子大喜，重犒来寄育，俟重来携与俱归。切勿负约！”时夜月正侍饮在坐，李拔其金钗一股授公子，亦请公子解所珮玉授夜月，令各藏之，聊以表信。明日日晡，设祖帐于虬园。使夜月清歌送行。夜月唱《会真记》“长亭饯别”一阕，深情绵邈，泪随声迸。公子相对脉脉，于邑无色。

    Explanation

    432

    Li Jian made a promise to meet again, saying that when the boat was moored, he would instruct his children to keep watch. Any traveler who arrived there would know his humble name. After giving these instructions, he still ordered a sedan chair to escort the young master to his returning boat. The road was smooth and level, unlike the old path. They arrived at the boat at dawn. The boatmen, seeing the young master, were all greatly pleased and said that after the young master left, Mr. Li sent people to guard carefully. The young master was very happy and promised to heavily reward those who came to care for him, waiting for his return to bring them back together. He emphasized not to break the promise! At that time, Night Moon, serving drinks, was present. Li Jian took one hairpin from Night Moon and gave it to the young master, also asking the young master to take off the jade pendant he was wearing and give it to Night Moon. They each kept them, just to show their trust. The next day at noon, a farewell banquet was set up in the Qiu Garden. He had Night Moon sing a clear song to see him off. Night Moon sang "The Long Pavilion Farewell" from The True Meeting, with deep and lingering feelings, and tears flowed with the sound. The young master looked at her silently, with no color in his face.

    433

    李强与拇战，使为欢笑。漏初下，始罢席。公子兴辞，李袖出小旗一角授公子，令善藏之：“江湖倘有不虞，出示便可免患。”复以千金为赆。公子受旗而返其赆，李作色曰：“公以小人之物为盗泉耶？盗泉去贪泉几何？如恐为盗泉所污，则公囊中所有，亦未必果皆廉泉也！”公子以李言激烈，只合拜受。李坚订后约，谓：“泊舟处时命儿曹侦伺，过客至此，问贱名无不知者。”嘱毕，仍命肩舆送公子归舟。道路坦夷，不似旧径。昧爽始达舟次。舟人见公子，皆大欢喜，佥谓公子去后，李遣人逻守维谨。

    Explanation

    433

    Li Qiang engaged in a game involving thumbs, bringing laughter. As the water clock began to mark the onset of night, the gathering came to an end. The young lord rose to bid farewell, and Li Qiang discreetly handed him a small flag, instructing him to keep it well: "If there is any unexpected trouble in the world, showing this flag can prevent disaster." He then gave a thousand gold pieces as a parting gift. The young lord accepted the flag but returned the gift. Li Qiang became stern and said, "Are you treating something of a lowly person as if it were tainted by the corrupting waters of the 'Thief's Spring'? How far is the 'Thief's Spring' from the 'Greedy Spring'? If you fear being corrupted by the 'Thief's Spring,' then perhaps the contents of your bag are not all pure as the cleansing waters of the 'Honest Spring'!" The young lord found Li Qiang's words strong and decided to accept the gift with a bow. Li Qiang firmly agreed to a future meeting, saying, "When the boat is moored, I will have my servants watch over it. When travelers arrive here, ask for my humble name, and no one will not know it." After giving these instructions, he still had the young lord escorted back to the boat by a sedan chair. The road was smooth and level, unlike the old path. They finally reached the boat at dawn. The boatmen saw the young lord and were all very happy, saying that after the young lord left, Li Qiang had sent people to guard the area very carefully.

    434

    公子大喜，重犒来众，并寄语致谢李。急命解缆，以幸脱虎口，不可常恃。自后涉江，时有戒心。下第后，由陆赴黔。明年春，方夜读书，忽闻檐际有声，如鸟飞堕，举首见李二昂然而入。公子惊问：“何来？”李曰：“顷有事交阯，必得抚军信矢，乃可免关津之阻，惟公子图之。”公子笑诺。明夜，果窃以付李。李叹曰：“公子真磊落丈夫也！此去匝月，缴还不误。”甫届一月，晨起，果见案头信矢一枝，李附函鸣谢，谓后会有期，必报大德。公子惊诧，以李行踪不测，遂秘不敢告抚军。

    Explanation

    434

    The nobleman was greatly pleased and generously rewarded those who had come with the news, sending a message of thanks to Li. He quickly ordered the ropes to be untied, having escaped from a dangerous situation, and knowing that one could not always rely on such good fortune. Afterward, as he crossed the river, he remained cautious. Following his failure in the imperial examinations, he traveled by land to Guizhou. The following spring, while reading at night, he suddenly heard a sound near the eaves, like that of a bird falling. He looked up and saw Li, who entered boldly. Startled, the nobleman asked, "What brings you here?" Li replied, "Recently, there was an urgent matter in Jiaozhi. I must obtain a token from the governor to avoid the difficulties at customs. Only you can help me with this." The nobleman smiled and agreed. The next night, he secretly gave the token to Li. Li sighed and said, "You truly are a man of noble character! I will return it within a month, without delay." Just after a month, in the morning, the nobleman indeed found a token on his desk. Li sent a letter of thanks, saying they would meet again in the future and promising to repay his great kindness. The nobleman was astonished, as Li's movements were unpredictable, and thus he kept the matter secret, not telling the governor.

    435

    然心常惴惴，殊切隐忧。越三载，又挈重资旋里，恐过道士洑为李党所识，遂避道由蕲水陆行。一日，暮过峻岭，忽林中吹唇作声，健儿百数十人持械骤至，仆辈骇奔，如鸟兽散。健儿将公子与行装掳至一村，献捷渠魁。俄一男子袒衣秉烛出，见公子，惊曰：“是非某公子耶？”公子睨之，固是李二，急呼曰：“故人救我！故人救我！”李笑携公子手问：“何由至此？”公子托言：“以君言江湖险阻，不敢再事舟楫。”李笑曰：“有我小旗，即泛舟何害？此我别业，若不在此，则公休矣。

    Explanation

    435

    He often felt anxious and deeply concerned. After three years, he again carried a large sum of money back to his hometown, fearing that passing through the place called "Dao Shi Fu" would lead to recognition by the Li faction. To avoid this, he took an alternative route via Qizhui and traveled by land. One day, as dusk fell and he was crossing a steep mountain, suddenly, from the forest, a whistling sound was heard, and more than a hundred armed men rushed towards him. His servants panicked and fled, scattering like birds and beasts. The armed men led the young master and his traveling gear to a village, presenting their capture to the leader. Soon, a man appeared, bare-chested and holding a candle, and upon seeing the young master, exclaimed in surprise, "Is this not your young master?" The young master looked at him and recognized him as Li Er, and cried out urgently, "Old friend, save me! Old friend, save me!" Li smiled and took the young master's hand, asking, "How did you end up here?" The young master lied, saying, "Because you spoke of the dangers of the rivers and lakes, I dared not travel by boat again." Li laughed and said, "With my small flag, what harm could there be in traveling by boat? This is my other residence; if you are not here, then you are finished."

    436

    ”公子再三称谢。李亦谢信矢之惠，谓交阯之役，获利不少。彼此殷殷叙阔。公子问：“夜月无恙否？”李笑曰：“我以公为忘之矣。此儿的是痴情，日把所赠珮玉，玩不释手。金钱叠卜，望眼欲穿。公在此少住，不日即可接至。”越日，夜月果至。李笑谓公子曰：“此禁脔也，完璧奉赠。”公子笑谢。视夜月云髻初盘，玉容半腆，较前尤艳。亸袖隅坐，颦眉不语，似怨公子负约。李谓陆行究不如舟行之逸，以公子仆辈散失，命健儿护侍，溯江而上。

    Explanation

    436

    The young master repeatedly expressed his thanks. Li also thanked for the kindness shown by the letter, saying that the campaign in Jiaozhi had been quite profitable. They spoke earnestly about their long separation. The young master asked, "Is Night Moon well?" Li laughed and said, "I thought you had forgotten her. This girl is truly devoted, playing with the jade pendant given to her every day, not letting go. She repeatedly consults the money for divination, eagerly awaiting. If you stay here for a few days, she will be brought to you soon." The next day, Night Moon indeed arrived. Li smiled and said to the young master, "This is a precious treasure, and I will present her to you in her entirety." The young master laughed and thanked him. He saw that Night Moon had newly styled her cloud-like hair, her beautiful face slightly flushed, and she was even more beautiful than before. She sat at the corner with her sleeves hanging down, frowning and not speaking, as if resenting the young master for breaking his promise. Li said that traveling by land was ultimately less convenient than traveling by boat, because the young master's attendants had been lost. He ordered strong men to guard and accompany, traveling upstream along the river.

    437

    自送公子与夜月登舟，并还所掳之物，拳拳挥手而别。公子问知夜月固良家子，李夫妇皆善视之。自许公子后，倍加爱重，并未涉一戏言，公子不胜称叹。夜月问公子何以负约，谓：“君如再不来，儿已判祝发，托命空王矣。”言毕，以帕揾泪，哽不成声。公子揖而谢曰：“书生胆怯多疑，几负李君高义。劳卿盼望，罪不容辞。然往者不究，来者可追，此后报卿之日方长也，愿卿怜而恕之。”夜月笑曰：“妾所言非敢抱怨，亦非要宠。缘数载痴情，不可不略使君知耳。”公子拥坐怀中，甘语而慰藉之。夜间侍寝，果然处子也。

    Explanation

    437

    After the protagonist had sent the gentleman and night moon onto the boat and returned the items that were taken, they parted with heartfelt gestures. The gentleman learned that night moon was indeed a woman of respectable lineage, and the couple Li treated her well. Since she had pledged her affection to the gentleman, she was loved and respected even more, and she had not uttered a single jest. The gentleman could not help but praise her. Night moon asked the gentleman why he had broken his promise, saying, "If you do not come again, I have decided to become a nun and entrust my life to the Buddha." After saying this, she wiped her tears with a handkerchief and choked with emotion, unable to speak. The gentleman bowed and thanked her, saying, "A scholar is timid and suspicious, and I almost betrayed the high righteousness of Master Li. I have bothered you with waiting, and I am guilty but unable to avoid responsibility. However, we should not dwell on the past, and the future can still be corrected. The day to repay you is long, and I hope you will pity and forgive me." Night moon smiled and said, "What I say is not meant to complain, nor do I seek favor. It is just to let you know a bit about my years of devoted love." The gentleman embraced her in his lap, comforting her with sweet words. That night, when they were in bed together, she was indeed a virgin.

    438

    里乘子曰：李二之于某公子，赆金赠美，执礼极恭，窥其用意，大抵不过欲劫假信矢耳。交阯之役不知何事，公子竟窃符相授，可谓孟浪。以李行踪不测，秘不告亲；而所赠小旗又胆怯不敢一试，甚至畏而避道，直忘夙约，脱非蕲水意外之遇，亦徒令痴情人望穿两眼而已。此等公子，若明若暗，若贤若愚，若有情若无情，首鼠羝羊，不能名状，令人失笑。李二其盗而近于侠者欤？居然粗熟史事，又能延师课子。观其师侃侃数言，亦非寻常村学究可比。惜二子究竟渺不可知，殊为歉然。惟李谓公子高义，公子亦谓李高义，此种高义似是而非，傥即邹峄氏所谓“非义之义”也耶？

    Explanation

    438

    The Narrator said: Li Er, in his dealings with a certain young nobleman, offered gifts of money and beauty, observing etiquette with utmost respect. Upon examining his intentions, it was evident that he was merely trying to deceive with false promises. The matter of the campaign in Jiaozhi is unknown, but the young nobleman ultimately stole the seal and gave it to someone, which can be called reckless. Given Li Er's unpredictable movements, he kept it secret from his family; and the small flag he gave was too timid to even be tried once, to the point of fearing and avoiding the path, completely forgetting the previous agreement. If it were not for the unexpected encounter in Qizhui, it would have only left the devoted person looking until their eyes popped out. Such young noblemen are ambiguous, sometimes wise and sometimes foolish, sometimes affectionate and sometimes indifferent, hesitating like a ram, beyond description, making one laugh. Is Li Er a thief who is close to being a hero? He is surprisingly well-versed in historical matters and is also able to hire a teacher to educate his children. Seeing his teacher speak confidently and fluently, he is not comparable to an ordinary village scholar. It is very regrettable that the two children are ultimately unknown and untraceable. Only Li Er claims that the young nobleman has high virtue, and the young nobleman also claims that Li Er has high virtue. This kind of high virtue seems to be neither true nor false, perhaps it is what Zou Yi referred to as "a virtue that is not genuine."

    439

    九山洞
    　　
    　　九山在吾皖定远县东北，一名韭山。其下有洞，土人采石钟乳，恒至其中。春夏秋内多毒蛇，必俟严冬，蜇伏殆尽，乃敢结伴裏而入。洞口极狭，秉烛蛇行十里许，腰始直；又二十里，至风簸纽，豁然开朗，别有天地。石路一线，阔不满尺，左峭壁矗竦，无藤萝可扪，右临深壑，俯不见底。至此辄相戒，箝口兢兢，次且蹑足，行约三里许，渐坦夷，始可放胆纵步。顾多歧途，凡所由之路，非以灰志其迹，则迷不能辨。小山无数，石齿齿，童无寸草。钟乳到处皆是，无事寻觅。再行十馀里，有河一道，阔可十丈，其水清可鉴影；探之冷彻指骨，其深莫测。

    Explanation

    439

    Jiu Mountain Cave

    Jiu Mountain is located in the northeastern part of Dingyuan County, Anhui Province, and is also known as Jiu Mountain. Below the mountain is a cave, where the locals mine stone stalactites and frequently visit. During spring, summer, and autumn, there are many poisonous snakes inside, so they must wait until the severe winter when the snakes are almost completely hibernated before they dare to go in together. The cave entrance is extremely narrow, and they carry a candle and crawl like a snake for about ten miles, only then does the waist straighten. Another twenty miles, they reach a place called Wind Shaking Knot, which suddenly becomes bright and open, revealing a different world. The stone path is a single line, not more than a foot wide. The left side is a sheer cliff with no vines to hold onto, and the right side is next to a deep ravine, the bottom of which cannot be seen. They warn each other to keep silent and be cautious, stepping carefully. They walk for about three miles, and it gradually becomes flat, only then can they walk boldly. However, there are many diverging paths, and all the paths they take are not marked with ash, otherwise they get lost and cannot distinguish. There are countless small hills, and the stones are jagged, with not an inch of grass. Stalactites are everywhere, and there is no need to search. They walk another ten or so miles, and there is a river that is ten zhang wide. The water is clear enough to see reflections, and when touched, it is cold to the bone. Its depth is immeasurable.

    440

    河滨泊一石船，狭仅容刀，而不能任载，试以手按之，立沉水底，须臾仍浮故处。相传水通淮河，昔有好事者放鸭其中，颈系木片，书己姓名识之；既淮滨人网得鸭，见木片，来访此人，询之果然。岸旁有石枰，子嵌其上，可推运而不可拈取，足供象戏。对岸衰草遍地，浩如翰海。远望，隐隐一山，横亘天外，山腰露城垣一角，城上时仿佛有人来往，惜无处可以问津耳。地温暖，无雨雪，隆冬袒臂犹汗，夜藉地露宿，亦殊不冷。尤奇者，至其境即不饥、不渴、不私，人往往乐而忘返。故每冬至前后入洞，偻指交春启蛰，始缓缓归矣。洞近禅觉寺，冬月丐者多巢其中，寺僧恶之，凿巨石堵洞口，其道遂绝。
    　　
    　　里乘子曰：尘世有此福地，倘可栖眷其中，此生真不志温饱矣。况隔河仙山楼阁隐隐在望，脱使石船可渡，许我问津，则彼岸诞登，又何须远涉弱水而访蓬莱也哉！

    Explanation

    440

    By the riverbank, there is a stone boat, narrow enough to fit only a knife, and unable to bear a load. When one tries to press it with their hand, it immediately sinks to the bottom, but in a moment, it still floats at its original place. It is said that the water connects to the Huai River. In the past, a well-intentioned person released ducks into it, with wooden slips tied to their necks, writing their own names to identify them. When people by the Huai River caught the ducks, they saw the wooden slips and came to visit this person. They inquired and indeed found it true. By the shore, there is a stone chessboard with pieces embedded on it, which can be pushed and moved but not picked up, sufficient for playing chess. On the opposite bank, the ground is covered with withered grass, vast like the ocean. Looking far, a mountain is faintly visible, stretched across the sky, with a corner of the city wall visible at its waist. On the city wall, people are sometimes faintly seen moving, but alas, there is no way to inquire about it. The place is warm, with no rain or snow. Even in severe winter, one can expose their arms and still sweat, sleeping on the ground at night, which is not particularly cold. Especially strange is that upon arriving in this place, one does not feel hungry, thirsty, or possessive, and people often enjoy it so much that they forget to return. Therefore, every year around the Winter Solstice, people enter the cave, and after counting the days until the Spring Awakening of Insects, they slowly return. The cave is near the Chanjue Temple, where in winter, beggars often make their homes. The temple monks disliked them and chiseled a large stone to block the cave entrance, thus cutting off the path.

    Lichengzi said: There is such a blessed place in the mortal world. If one could live there with one's family, this life would truly not seek warmth and food. Especially since across the river, the fairy mountains and towers are faintly visible. If the stone boat could cross, allowing me to inquire about it, then I would be able to reach the other side. Why would I need to travel far across the weak water to visit the Penglai Islands?

    441

    少年客
    　　
    　　庐州李某，由宁波附海艘赴沪。共载者五十馀人，内一客年甚少，美秀而文，与李联床，彼此接谈，意气颇洽。李叩其姓名，但漫应之。中途，客附李耳低语曰：“君知舟子何如人也？”李曰：“不知。”客曰：“君不惯涉江湖，不知道途之险。我日来细察，舟子殊非良善，其柁工、篙师等人，面亦多露杀气。若曹居心甚叵测，唯我能辨之。计一路惟某洲最险，倘经其处不停泊，当无患。君须识之。”李惊问曰：“倘在此停泊，奈何？”客笑曰：“君毋多言，幸有我在，彼何能为？”李疑信参半，姑默识以觇之。既至某洲，日甫晡，舟子果命其党下碇系缆。诸客佥谓天早，尚可趁程，何遽停泊？舟子不答，诸客大哗，少年以目止之。洲孤悬海中，四望无际，更无别舟停泊。

    Explanation

    441

    A Young Guest

    In Lu'an, there was a man named Li, who traveled from Ningbo to Shanghai aboard a sea vessel. There were more than fifty passengers on board, among whom was a young guest who was exceptionally handsome and well-educated. He shared a bed with Li, and they conversed amicably. Li asked him for his name, but he vaguely replied. Midway through the journey, the young guest whispered into Li's ear, "Do you know what kind of person the boatman is?" Li said, "I don't know." The young guest replied, "You are not accustomed to traveling on rivers and lakes, so you don't know the dangers of the journey. I have carefully observed, and the boatman is far from being good and kind. His helmsman and poleman, among others, often show signs of murderous intent. Your intentions are very suspicious, but I can discern them. Along the way, only a certain island is the most dangerous. If we pass by without stopping, there will be no trouble. You must remember this." Li was surprised and asked, "What if we stop here? What then?" The young guest smiled and said, "Don't worry, I am here, and they won't be able to do anything." Li was half-doubting and half-believing, and decided to keep it in mind and see what happens. When they reached the island, the sun was just setting, and the boatman indeed ordered his crew to drop anchor and tie up the boat. The other passengers all said that they could still make progress and asked why they were stopping so suddenly. The boatman did not answer, and the passengers made a lot of noise. The young guest stopped them with a look. The island was suspended alone in the sea, and looking in all directions, there was no end. There were no other boats stopping there.

    442

    少选饭熟，舟人但自饱啖，并不食客。众饥索食，亦竟置若罔闻。无何，夕阳西沈，诸客饥焰中烧，颇不能耐。舟子忽率其党各执刀械进前，厉声谓诸客曰：“此地险要，向为群盗出没之区，汝等所挟资可速献出，我当为善藏之。不则恐有不虞，悔无及也！”客多巨贾，挟资颇重，闻言互相惊愕。知其来意不善，乃哀告曰：“我等既同一舟，患难自当与共。各挟薄资固在舟中，纵有不虞，亦全仗主人防卫，如可免患，不惜重酬。又何必献出，劳为善藏也。”舟子闻言，怒目叱曰：“尔辈死在目前，犹哓哓饶舌，想作看财虏耶？”言毕，回顾其党曰：“此时不速了结，复有何待？”其党闻之，争持刀械而入，诸客相视觫觳，计无所之。李依少年傍，亦窃自危。第见少年从容起立，喝谓舟子曰：“汝休孟浪！汝抑知步家

    Explanation

    442

    A short while later, the meal was ready, but the boatmen only ate their fill and did not feed the guests. The guests, hungry, asked for food, but the boatmen completely ignored their pleas. Soon, the sun set in the west, and the guests' hunger burned like a flame, becoming quite unbearable. Suddenly, the boatman led his companions, each armed with weapons, forward and shouted at the guests, "This area is dangerous, and it has been a place where bandits frequently appear. You all should quickly hand over the money you carry. I will hide it well for you. Otherwise, you might face unexpected trouble, and it will be too late to regret!" The guests were mostly wealthy merchants carrying a lot of money. Upon hearing this, they were shocked and alarmed. Knowing the boatmen's intentions were not good, they pleaded, "We are all in the same boat. Hardships should be shared together. We each carry a small amount of money, which is still in the boat. Even if there is trouble, we rely entirely on you for protection. If we can avoid any danger, we will not hesitate to offer a high reward. Why must you hand it over, and trouble you to hide it well?" The boatman, upon hearing this, glared angrily and shouted, "You are about to die, yet you still chatter on, thinking you can be greedy for money?" After saying this, he turned to his companions and said, "If we don't resolve this quickly, what are we waiting for?" Upon hearing this, his companions rushed in with their weapons. The guests looked at each other in fear, with no plan of action. Li, standing beside the young man, also felt uneasy. He saw the young man calmly stand up and shout at the boatman, "Stop being reckless! Do you even know about the 'Bù' family?"

    443

    兄弟乎？”舟子卒然敛容答曰：“唯唯，小人不敢。”少年叱曰：“汝率党入内，意将何为？”乃以肱一挥，即堕其最犷悍者五人于海。舟子等大恐，急弃刀械，环跽乞命。少年口中不知作何语，舟子等益恐，崩角叩舷，声如撒菽。自称无知冒犯，罪该万死，愿出资另为买舟，载众至沪，以求赎罪。少年叱曰：“汝既知罪，姑暂宽贷。仍乘汝舟，亦不必另买。如再萌恶念，决不轻恕！”舟子等稽颡，唯唯而退，急命具酒食以款诸客。一路小心将事，伺应惟谨。至沪后，同人佥德少年，谋有以报，少年悉笑而却之。李私叩寓所，容图踵谢，少年笑曰：“我居无定所，君盍告我所寓，暇当相访耳。”李具告之。后三日，少年果来作别，问将何往，亦不答，究不测其为何许人也。

    Explanation

    443

    "Brother?" The boatman suddenly assumed a solemn expression and replied, "Yes, yes, I dare not." The young man shouted, "What are you and your group planning to do?" He then swung his arm, immediately throwing five of the most fierce and violent ones into the sea. The boatmen were greatly frightened, quickly abandoning their weapons, and knelt in a circle, pleading for mercy. The young man spoke words that were unclear, causing the boatmen to become even more terrified, their heads hitting the ship's side with a sound like scattering beans. They admitted to being ignorant and having committed a grave offense, deserving death, and were willing to pay money to buy another boat to take everyone to Shanghai to atone for their sins. The young man shouted, "Since you know your crime, I will temporarily forgive you. Continue using your boat, there is no need to buy another. If you harbor evil thoughts again, I will not lightly forgive you!" The boatmen bowed formally, submissively retreated, and quickly ordered the preparation of food and drink to entertain the guests. They carefully managed affairs, serving with utmost diligence. After arriving in Shanghai, everyone praised the young man, planning to repay him, but the young man laughed and refused. Li privately visited his residence, intending to follow up and thank him, and the young man laughed and said, "I have no fixed residence, why don't you tell me where I can find me, I will visit you when I have free time." Li informed him. Three days later, the young man came to say goodbye, asked where he was going, but did not answer, and it was ultimately unknown who he was.

    444

    剑侠
    　　
    　　某太守由部曹出守吾皖宁国府，途中遇一少年，美皙，弱如处女，口操秦音。问其邦族，自言齐产，韦氏，弦佩，固应京兆试，下第南归者。试与谈论今古，博闻强识，无所不晓。太守大悦，款洽甚殷，因聘为司掌书记。韦至署中，镇日下帷焚香静坐，不甚出户。同幕诸友偶过访其室，蔼然可亲，却无訑訑拒人之意，人以是乐暱就之。觇其室中，寒暑只一布帐、一布衾、一藤席，一皮匣为枕，案上唯设笔砚，此外别无长物。月必出游数日，佥问：“何往？”谓：“少嗜山水。今客游至此，如皖北之潜霍，江右之匡庐，浙江之西湖、严濑、四明、天台，池州之九华，新安之黄山、白岳，皆近在咫尺，不得不次第遍揽其胜。

    Explanation

    444

    A Swordswoman

    Once, a prefect who had been transferred from his post in the central government to govern the Ningguo prefecture in Anhui province, encountered a young man during his journey. The young man was fair-skinned, frail like a maiden, and spoke with a Qin accent. When asked about his clan, he said he was from Qi, a member of the Wei family, skilled in music, and had failed the imperial examination in the capital region and returned south. The prefect tried to discuss ancient and modern matters with him, and the young man had a broad knowledge and strong memory, knowing everything. The prefect was very pleased and treated him with great warmth and hospitality, then hired him as a secretary. Wei arrived at the office and spent all day lowering the curtain, burning incense, and sitting quietly, rarely going out. Fellow officials occasionally visited his room, and he was warm and approachable, without any arrogant or rejecting attitude, making people enjoy being close to him. When they secretly observed his room, they found that he had only a cloth canopy, a cotton quilt, a bamboo mat, and a leather box as a pillow. On the desk, only a writing brush and inkstone were set, and there were no other possessions. Every month, he would go out on trips for several days. When asked where he went, he said, "I have a fondness for mountains and waters. Now that I am a guest here, places like the Qian and Huo mountains in northern Anhui, Mount Lu in Jiangxi, the West Lake, Yan River, Si Ming, and Tian Tai in Zhejiang, Jiuhua Mountain in Chizhou, and Huangshan and Baiyue in Xuanan are all close at hand, and I cannot help but visit them one by one and enjoy their beauty."

    445

    ”佥谓：“君所言名胜之地，近则百里，远则千里，欲遍揽其胜，谈何容易？”韦笑曰：“仆少遇异人授秘术，日可行千里，何难之有？”佥窃异之。一日游归，神色皇遽。人叩其故，谓：“昨游黄山，登天都峰绝顶，遇一野僧，欲与角艺，以空拳难敌，急驰而遁。渠蹑迹穷追，不日行且至矣。”佥问：“如何？”曰：“黄山唯天都峰最高，峰腰细削，如壶庐之有颈，人迹罕到。近时闻甘风池曾陟其巅。见上有席棚，一少妇掩泣其中。甘问知为山下村妇，被妖僧飞劫至此。问僧何往，曰：‘朝出募斋，暮则来归。幽妾于此，待死而已。’甘慰令无恐，乃急负妇人驰送其家。复自登峰，瞷僧暮归，乘其不备，反肱力麾之，僧遽挽甘臂，相将俱坠。幸甘压僧上，出死力搤其吭而殪之。

    Explanation

    445

    Everyone said, "The famous places you mentioned are within a hundred miles, or perhaps as far as a thousand. How could one possibly visit all of them?" Wei smiled and replied, "When I was young, I met a strange person who taught me a secret technique, allowing me to travel a thousand miles in a day. What difficulty is there?" Everyone found this remarkable. One day, after returning from a trip, Wei looked frightened and disheveled. Someone asked him why, and he said, "Yesterday, I visited Mount Huang and climbed to the summit of Tiandu Peak. There, I encountered a wild monk who wanted to challenge me in a contest of skills. With empty fists, I could not match him, so I fled hastily. He followed the trail and pursued relentlessly, and he will arrive soon." They asked, "What happened?" Wei explained, "Mount Huang is the highest of all peaks. The waist of Tiandu Peak is narrowly cut, like a jar with a neck, and it is rarely visited by people. Recently, I heard that Gan Fengchi once climbed to its summit. There, he saw a pavilion above, where a young woman was weeping inside. Gan asked and found out she was a village woman from below the mountain, who had been abducted by a wicked monk to this place. When Gan asked the monk where he went, the monk replied, 'I leave in the morning to collect alms and return in the evening. She is imprisoned here, waiting to die.' Gan comforted her and told her not to be afraid, then quickly carried the woman to her home. He then ascended the peak again, watching the monk return in the evening. Taking advantage of his inattention, Gan struck him with all his might. The monk suddenly grabbed Gan's arm, and they both fell. Fortunately, Gan was on top of the monk, and with all his strength, he choked the monk's throat and killed him."

    446

    僧技固出甘右，脱压甘上，危矣。盖僧恶贯满盈，天故假甘手以诛之也。仆素耳其事，而窃慕甘之为人，故欲登峰一访其遗迹。不谓甫至其巅，又有席棚在焉。比瞷棚中，一僧枯坐炼气，见予至，侧目微睨，意予非常人，口出青气一道，将先发以制。予知为剑术，急驰遁以避之，倘非善行，要领殊矣！计此僧不日且至，逼人已甚，予不得不一挫其锋。诸君如有胆略，可作壁上观也。”越日，僧果至，声言请韦角艺。韦笑谓诸人曰：“野衲不自度德量力，予不能再恕之矣！”遂订约：夜漏二下，相会于敬亭山之阳。有好事者偕往觇之，于时新月上弦，蟾影西斜，疏星交映。

    Explanation

    446

    The monk's skill indeed surpassed Gan's right, and was about to surpass Gan entirely, which was dangerous. The monk's evil deeds had reached their peak, and heaven intentionally used Gan's hands to punish him. I have always heard of this matter and secretly admired Gan's character, so I wanted to climb the peak to visit his legacy. I did not expect to just arrive at the peak and find another pavilion there. Upon looking into the pavilion, I saw a monk sitting motionlessly practicing qi cultivation. When he saw me, he glanced sideways with a slight look, thinking I was not an ordinary person, and exhaled a green qi, intending to attack first. I knew it was swordsmanship, so I quickly fled to avoid it. If not for good running, my neck and waist would be severed! I calculated that this monk would arrive soon, and he is very threatening. I cannot help but defeat him once. If any of you have courage, you can watch from the sidelines. The next day, the monk indeed arrived and announced he wanted to challenge Wei Jiao. Wei smiled and said to everyone, "This humble monk did not measure his virtue and strength, and I cannot forgive him again!" They then made an agreement: to meet at two drum strikes at night on the sunny side of Jingting Mountain. There were those who were interested and went along to watch. At that time, a new moon in its crescent phase was slanting westward, and sparse stars were shining together.

    447

    既至其处，见韦与僧左右相向而立，僧口吐青气一道，韦口吐白气一道，互相激射，盘旋天际，夭矫闪烁，如电交掣。少选，青气渐弱，白气益锐，砉然一声，响若裂缯，将青气划为两断，堕地顿灭。白气直射僧顶，僧惶惧投地膜拜，稽颡乞命。韦笑曰：“吾戏汝耳，胆何弱也？汝如捐去故技，从事正道，吾贷汝矣。”乃吸白气于口，以手麾令僧去。僧稽颡再拜，以袖障面而窜。观者咋舌失色，叩问何术。韦曰：“皆剑术也。彼所炼青气为雌锋，是谓邪道；吾所炼白气为雄锋，是谓正道。雌不能胜雄，实邪不能胜正。彼挟此术，横行江湖，已称无敌，惟予足以克之。今既折断，已成废物，为人除害不鲜也。向游黄山，剑藏皮匣，未曾携得，不然，天都峰顶早已互决雌雄，毕乃事矣。”观者旋署，述诸太守，怵然称异。诘旦诣韦，将请试其术，至其室，已阒然无踪，人物俱杳矣。

    Explanation

    447

    Upon arriving at the place, Wei and the monk stood facing each other. The monk exhaled a green gas, and Wei exhaled a white gas. The two gases interacted, swirling in the sky, twisting and flickering like electricity crossing. Soon, the green gas weakened, while the white gas grew stronger. A sharp sound, like tearing silk, split the green gas into two parts, causing it to fall to the ground and vanish. The white gas shot directly at the monk's head. Frightened, the monk fell to the ground, prostrated himself, and begged for mercy. Wei laughed and said, "I was just playing with you. How weak your courage is! If you abandon your old skills and engage in the righteous path, I will forgive you." He then inhaled the white gas and, with a gesture, commanded the monk to leave. The monk bowed twice, covered his face with his sleeve, and fled. The spectators were speechless and pale, asking what kind of skill it was. Wei replied, "It is all swordsmanship. The green gas he refined is the feminine edge, called the evil path; the white gas I refined is the masculine edge, called the righteous path. The feminine cannot defeat the masculine, just as evil cannot defeat righteousness. He used this skill to wreak havoc in the rivers and lakes, already claiming to be invincible, but only I am capable of defeating him. Now that it has been broken, it has become useless, not infrequently removing harm for people. Previously, I toured Mount Huang, where my sword was stored in a leather case. I did not bring it along, otherwise, long ago, we would have already determined the winner on the summit of Tiandu Peak, and completed the matter." The spectators soon gathered and reported to the governor, who was shocked and praised it. The next day, the governor visited Wei, intending to request a demonstration of his skill. Upon arriving at his room, it was empty, and he had vanished, with no trace of people or things.

    448

    父子同日合卺
    　　
    　　曩游蜀中，闻土人言，乡有某生者，幼聘舅氏女一妹为妻，以中表亲，素不避面。生成童，从塾师读。他日归，过舅氏之门，见女独自在家推磨，生入，问舅妗俱他出，戏曰：“妹役良苦，我为效劳好否？”女曰：“甚善。”时女已及笄，两人情窦俱开，调笑甚乐，以无人，遂私焉。生素畏舅，既讫事，自念女脱有孕，舅知之奈何？别女而出，徘徊中道，遂逃亡不知所之。越日，师使人探诸其家，家固以为在塾，彼此诘究，互相诧，到处使人踪迹之，卒无朕兆。而女身果体妊，久之，腹渐膨脝。母察有异，诘之，计不能隐，遂吐其实，乃使人告生父母。其父母仅此一子，以出亡，方切隐忧，闻女有孕，大喜。商诸冰人，以礼迎归，待生归家，再为成礼。初，生出亡，乞食至汉口。质库主人某翁，见生貌不类乞人，留使学贾。既喜其勤谨，委司会计，大为宠任。生频年蓄积，不下万余金。爰与人合夥开张布店，特归省视。

    Explanation

    448

    A Father and Son Marrying on the Same Day

    In the past, I traveled to Sichuan and heard the locals say that in a certain village, there was a young man named Mr. X who, when he was young, was betrothed to his maternal uncle's daughter as his wife. Because they were cousins, they never avoided each other. The son grew up and studied under a private tutor. One day, he returned home and passed by his maternal uncle's house, seeing the daughter alone at home grinding grain. He entered and asked if his maternal uncle and aunt had gone out. He jokingly said, "Your labor is very hard; would it be good if I helped you?" The daughter replied, "Very good." At that time, the daughter had already reached the age of marriage, and both of them had opened their hearts, and they were very happy in playful banter. Because there was no one around, they secretly did it. The son always feared his maternal uncle, after finishing, he thought to himself, if the daughter were to become pregnant, what would the uncle do? He left the daughter and walked around the middle of the road, then escaped and no one knew where he went. The next day, the teacher sent someone to investigate at his home, the family still thought he was at school, they questioned each other, and everyone was surprised, they searched everywhere, but there was no trace. But the daughter indeed became pregnant, after a long time, her belly gradually swelled. The mother noticed something unusual, questioned her, and she couldn't hide it, so she told the truth. Then she sent someone to inform the son's parents. His parents only had this one son, because he had run away, they were deeply worried, but when they heard the daughter was pregnant, they were very happy. They consulted the matchmaker, welcomed her back with propriety, and waited for the son to return home to complete the wedding. Originally, when the son ran away, he begged for food and went to Hankou. The owner of a pawnshop, a certain old man, saw that the son looked different from a beggar, so he kept him and made him learn business. He was pleased with the son's diligence and carefulness, entrusted him with accounting, and greatly favored him. The son accumulated wealth for many years, not less than ten thousand gold pieces. He then partnered with someone to open a cloth shop and specially returned home to visit.

    449

    既至乡里，见道周鼓吹伧伫，车马喧耀，询之旁人，谓某氏子亲迎，是固有母无父者，今娶妻矣。生闻惊喜。既念生平只一索，那便有子？试详探之，果然。先是，一妹迎归分娩，果幸得男。比长，读书甚慧。十三岁应童子试，学使赏其文，拔冠一军，名噪庠序。同里某富翁有爱女，遂以字之。今适于归。生到家，见宾客满堂，姑与为礼。佥问客从何来，生诡言至自楚北，为某生作寄书邮者。

    Explanation

    449

    Having arrived in his hometown, he saw a grand procession with music and a noisy display of carriages and horses. He asked the people around, and they said that the son of a certain family was going to fetch his bride. It was indeed someone who had a mother but no father, and now he had married. The narrator heard and was surprised and delighted. After thinking that he had only one child, how could he have a son? He carefully investigated and it turned out to be true. Previously, his sister-in-law had returned to give birth, and indeed, she had a boy. As he grew up, he was very intelligent in his studies. At the age of thirteen, he took the examination for young scholars, and the provincial examiner praised his essay, making him the top student, and his name became famous in the school. A wealthy man in the same village had a beloved daughter, and so he was betrothed to her. Now she has returned home. The narrator arrived home, saw the hall full of guests, and greeted his mother-in-law with courtesy. Everyone asked where the guests came from, and the narrator lied, saying he had come from the north of Chu to deliver a letter for someone.

    450

    其子闻有父书，喜出叩见，问：“父书何在？”生笑抚其背曰：“儿不知耶？我即汝父是也。”问父母，已先后去世，不胜凄然。其子惊喜犹疑，生窥其意，谓曰：“儿如不信，可呼汝母出见，自能知之。”其子不得已，入请母出。生遽前揖之曰：“卿幸别来无恙。推磨推磨，不如我与汝磨。”其母闻之，喜谓其子曰：“果儿父也。”盖生所云，乃当日推磨时相谑之词，非他人所与知也。宾客闻之，交口称贺，佥请具香烛酒醴，即于是日父子姑妇同拜祭天地、祖先，行庙见礼而合卺焉。

    Explanation

    450

    The son heard there was a letter from his father and, overjoyed, went to pay a visit, asking, "Where is Father's letter?" The father smiled and patted his back, saying, "Do you not know? I am your father." The son asked about his parents, who had passed away one after the other, and was deeply saddened. The son was both surprised and doubtful, and the father, seeing this, said, "If you do not believe me, you may call your mother out to see, and she will know." The son, having no choice, went inside to ask his mother to come out. The father immediately stepped forward and bowed to her, saying, "I am glad you have been well since we last met. Pushing the millstone, pushing the millstone, is not as good as me and you grinding together." The mother, upon hearing this, happily told her son, "Indeed, he is your father." What the father had said was a playful phrase they used when they ground together, a private joke known only to them. The guests, upon hearing this, all praised and congratulated them, and they all requested the preparation of incense, candles, wine, and liquor.
