If you are willing to marry him, to assist in managing the household, one action would achieve two good outcomes. What do you think?" Shi Gu blushed and remained silent. The aunt questioned her again, and after a long pause, she replied, "If the elder has thoroughly examined and found it to be true, I will obey your command without fail." The aunt was pleased and said, "I have examined it thoroughly, so why should I doubt further?" The next day, she told Xingsheng, ordering him to quickly arrange the betrothal. Xingsheng replied, "This is a major event in his life, and our family is originally poor and simple; how dare we act rashly!" The aunt laughed and said, "You stop being sly! My mind is made up. From now on, all the rituals will still be kept simple, and I will not be overly strict with you." Under pressure from the aunt's command, Xingsheng quickly sent a matchmaker to arrange the marriage, and he went to fetch her and brought her home. When Shi Gu arrived at Xingsheng's home, she saw the room empty and desolate, with nothing else of value. She asked if he truly had donated charity funds, but Xingsheng only smiled and did not speak. Shi Gu sighed, "Poverty and wealth are matters of fate; I do not resent it. If a man does not read books, he will not make much progress. If you truly study diligently, I will work hard with my hands, and we will not suffer from cold or hunger. Do you wish for this?" Xingsheng smiled and said, "Reading books is within my duties as a scholar, so why should I not do it? But I feel very sorry to burden you with hard work. Do not worry, I have my own way of making a living."

    492

    ”柿姑以生言殊涉恍惚，恚曰：“若是，君果发有义财？秘他人何亦秘妻耶？”生笑曰：“至时自当相告。”他日生出，柿姑见后有小院，蓬蒿满径，试遍锄之。果于墙角见瓮口，再发之，白镪累累然。大喜，仍覆以土。越日，生有急，将质物，柿姑笑曰：“君休矣。明明有财不用而质物，宁非傎耶？”生闻而惊。既而曰：“汝何知我有财耶？”柿姑笑曰：“汝秘不肯告我，而谓我终不知耶？”乃笑携锄，搴生裾至墙角掘之，瓮口毕露，白镪充牣，指让生曰：“此何物也？而谓我不知耶？”生大喜，乃诡答曰：“既卿知之，亦复何秘？然置此终非长策，不如运而藏之为是。”柿姑笑曰：“然。”两人竟夜运取，共计五千金有奇。柿姑谓：“得财但秘而不用，是守财奴也，复何益哉！”乃渐出金置备产业。不数年，良田夏屋，居然素封矣。

    Explanation

    492

    Shi Gu thought Mr. Sheng's words were unusually vague and uncertain, and angrily said, "If so, did you really make a fortune? Why keep secrets from others but not from your wife?" Mr. Sheng smiled and replied, "I will tell you when the time comes." One day, Mr. Sheng left, and Shi Gu saw a small courtyard behind, with weeds and thorns covering the path. She tried to clear it all. Indeed, at the corner of the wall, she found a jar mouth, opened it again, and saw silver coins piled up. She was very happy and covered it back with soil. The next day, Mr. Sheng was in a hurry and wanted to pawn something. Shi Gu laughed and said, "Stop now. You clearly have wealth but are using it to pawn things. Isn't this foolish?" Mr. Sheng was surprised to hear this and then said, "How do you know I have wealth?" Shi Gu laughed and said, "You kept it a secret and didn't tell me, but you thought I wouldn't find out?" She then laughed and took a hoe, pulled Mr. Sheng's robe, and went to the corner of the wall to dig. The jar mouth was fully exposed, and silver coins were abundant. She pointed and said to Mr. Sheng, "What is this? And you thought I wouldn't know?" Mr. Sheng was very happy and then said in a roundabout way, "Since you know, why keep it a secret? But leaving it here is not a good plan. It's better to move it and hide it." Shi Gu laughed and said, "Yes." They worked all night to move it, and a total of more than five thousand gold coins were moved. Shi Gu said, "To obtain wealth and keep it secret without using it is being a miser. What good does it do?" They gradually spent the money to acquire property. In a few years, they had fertile fields and summer houses, and suddenly became wealthy landowners.

    493

    初，某公子为柿姑所侮，心时衔恨；既闻嫁生，且暴发义财，益羡且妒。爰贿剧盗，指生为窝藏，以图雪愤。时生果遵妻教读书，已入邑庠。柿姑以秋闱伊迩，督攻举业甚严。忽苍头喘汗来言，有县役持签到门，谓主人窝藏剧盗，立提候讯。生闻面色沮丧。柿姑让之曰：“君岂真窝藏者耶？何懦怯之甚也！”生曰：“奈何？”柿姑曰：“妾思此必仇人所嗾，断非畏避可以了事。君第挺身赴公堂，侃侃辨论，堂上官未必果不分皂白。万一愦愦，谁良谁莠，乡里自有公论。妾当鸣众绅衿，同上公词辨冤。如一畏避，则假反成真，转为仇人所笑也。”生闻，胆气顿壮，立愿赴邑辨白。柿姑乃自见来役，命之曰：“我家官人在家读书，素安分守法。今为仇人所嗾，横被盗诬，既蒙邑宰提讯，理合前去辨白。今将官人交汝，好为照拂，大钱十千，聊备不腆。

    Explanation

    493

    At first, a certain young gentleman had been insulted by Shi Gu, and harbored resentment in his heart; later, upon learning that she had married the scholar and had suddenly become wealthy through righteous means, he felt both admiration and jealousy. Therefore, he bribed a notorious bandit to accuse the scholar of harboring a criminal, in order to avenge his grievance. At that time, the scholar indeed followed his wife’s teachings and studied diligently, having already entered the local academy. Shi Gu, aware that the autumn examination was approaching soon, was strict in supervising his studies. Suddenly, a servant came gasping and sweating to announce that a county official with a summons had arrived at the door, stating that the master was accused of harboring a notorious bandit and was to be immediately taken for questioning. Upon hearing this, the scholar’s face fell with dejection. Shi Gu reprimanded him, saying, “Are you truly the one who harbored the criminal? How cowardly you are!” The scholar replied, “What can I do?” Shi Gu said, “I believe this must be the doing of an enemy. It is certainly not something that can be resolved by fear and avoidance. You should simply stand up and go to the public hall, speak eloquently and persuasively in your defense. The official on the bench may not necessarily be blind to the facts. If he is confused and cannot distinguish between good and bad, the village will have its own public opinion. I shall summon the local gentry and scholars to file a joint petition to clear your name. If you show even the slightest fear, the false accusation will become true, and you will be laughed at by your enemies.” Upon hearing this, the scholar’s courage suddenly grew strong, and he immediately vowed to go to the county to clear his name. Shi Gu then personally went to meet the official who had arrived, and instructed him, saying, “My husband has been studying at home, always law-abiding and well-behaved. Now he has been falsely accused by an enemy, and having been summoned by the county magistrate for questioning, it is only proper for him to go and clear his name. I now entrust my husband to you, please take good care of him. Here are ten thousand large coins, just as a modest offering.”

    494

    俟无事回家，当另犒赏。如妄恣鱼肉，九阍虽遥，勿谓我女流不能披发上叩也。”初县役所望甚奢，今见柿姑容光照人，且复语言刚决，料非善弱，只得唯唯听命。生既至县，暂寄囹圄，邑宰并不讯问。柿姑不时使人探视，待至十日，更不能耐。乃折柬遍招绅衿，肆筵设席。席罢，柿姑于屏后白所求，众人彼此相视，默无一语。柿姑知众无能为助，不禁浩然长叹，含泪归房。乃自走笔具词为夫辨冤。翌辰，遣仆赍呈邑宰。中有联云：“将错就错，参乎几误杀人；以讹传讹，回也岂真霸产。”宰见词叹赏，立提生面讯。既问：“汝同乡有蒋九成上舍，曾识其人否？”生叩首对曰：“蒋上舍即生外舅。”宰改容起立曰：“上舍固吾师也。然则汝妻即上舍之女，无怪立言得体也。”遂以柿姑所上之词付生阅之。即日出生于狱。邑宰某公，固长白人，父官都统。

    Explanation

    494

    Awaiting the return home when there is nothing else to do, he shall be rewarded separately. If one acts recklessly and cruelly, though the nine gates of heaven are far away, do not think that as a woman I cannot cut off my hair and ascend to make a plea. Initially, the county official had great expectations, but now seeing Miss Persimmon's radiant appearance and firm and decisive words, he knew she was not a weak or good-natured person and could only nod in obedience. The man arrived at the county and was temporarily imprisoned, but the county magistrate did not interrogate him. Miss Persimmon frequently sent someone to visit him, and after ten days, she could no longer bear it. She then sent invitations to all the gentry and scholars and spread out the tables to set up a grand feast. After the banquet, Miss Persimmon whispered her request behind the screen, and the people looked at each other silently without a word. Knowing that the people could not help, she could not help but sigh deeply and returned to her room with tears. She then wrote a petition to clear her husband's name. The next day, she sent a servant to present it to the county magistrate. The petition included couplets that said: "To follow a mistake with another mistake, Zengzi, how many people have you mistakenly killed? To spread rumors or misinformation, Ruizi, could you truly be a bandit?" The magistrate saw the petition and praised it, immediately summoning the man for questioning. After asking, "Do you have a fellow townsman named Jiang Jiucheng, who is a scholar? Have you met him before?" The man bowed and replied, "Jiang Jiucheng is my father-in-law." The magistrate changed his expression and stood up, saying, "Jiang Jiucheng is indeed my teacher. Then your wife is the daughter of the scholar, no wonder her words are so appropriate." He then gave the man the petition to read. That day, the man was released from prison. The county magistrate, Mr. So-and-so, is originally from Changbai, and his father held the position of Duting (都统).

    495

    柿姑之父少以诸生纳粟太学，应试京兆，都统延课其子，即某公也，今由笔政授邑宰矣。柿姑襁褓失父，九岁失母，儿时仿佛闻母言“汝父曾在京授徒”，而不知即公也。生归述公语，柿姑大喜，属生衣冠往谢。公于师谊最为惓惓，越日枉驾答拜，并请见柿姑，问师身后事甚详。闻柿姑终鲜兄弟，不胜叹息，并遗以锦段笔砚等物。临行，柿姑谢且请曰：“寒家赖公袒荫，幸免覆盆。顾平日与盗无仇，是必有人指使。尚求研究昭晰，幸甚！”公点首笑诺。归署，果提盗研讯，初不肯吐，搒之，乃供称为某公子所嗾。公怒，飞签提究。某大惧，诣生谢过，并求关白，愿以千金为寿。生辞曰：“窝藏剧盗，罪名匪轻，诬人者应自坐之，复有何说！”某益惧，渐增至三千金。生入以问柿姑，柿姑笑曰：“破悭囊助君膏火，计亦得，质告公当无不诺。且冤宜解不宜结，怨者以德遇之，善莫大焉。

    Explanation

    495

    The father of Miss Shi, when young, was a student of Confucian classics who donated grain to the Imperial University and took an exam in the capital region. A military official invited him to teach his son, who is now a certain official, currently serving as a magistrate due to his position in literature. Miss Shi lost her father at a very young age and her mother when she was nine. As a child, she vaguely heard her mother say, "Your father once taught in the capital," but she did not know that he was the same official. The student returned home and told Miss Shi about the official's words, and she was greatly pleased, instructing the student to dress formally and go to thank him. The official was particularly devoted to the teacher-student relationship, and the next day, he came himself to return the visit, and asked to meet Miss Shi, inquiring in great detail about the teacher's affairs after death. He learned that Miss Shi had no brothers or sisters and could not help but sigh, and he also gave her fine silk, writing implements, and other items. As he was leaving, Miss Shi expressed her gratitude and requested, "Our humble family relies on your protection and support, and we have fortunately avoided being wronged. But there was no enmity with the bandit in the past, so there must be someone who prompted him. I still request a thorough investigation, which would be greatly appreciated!" The official nodded and smiled in agreement. Returning to his office, he indeed sent a fast message to investigate the bandit. Initially, the bandit refused to confess, but after being beaten, he confessed that he was prompted by a certain young master. The official became angry and sent a fast message to investigate further. The young master was greatly afraid, visited the student to apologize, and requested to inform, wishing to give a thousand gold pieces as a gift. The student replied, "Sheltering a notorious bandit is a serious crime, and those who falsely accuse should take responsibility, what more is there to say!" The young master became even more afraid and gradually increased the offer to three thousand gold pieces. The student went in to ask Miss Shi, and she smiled and said, "Spending money to help with your studies is also a good plan. Reporting to the official, he will certainly agree. And grievances should be resolved, not accumulated. To meet grievances with virtue, nothing is better than this."

    496

    顾小人多反复，必先令彼输金，而后为道地，乃万全也。”生曰：“善。”出以告某，大喜称谢。即日，果辇金以献。生诣公，备告所以。公喜诺曰：“我方为君筹膏火资，而苦无法，若此甚善。”某幸免究，深感生德，前隙尽捐。自是，公常召生至署论文，夫人亦时与柿姑来往。公尝问柿姑：“贤妹，前词谓回也霸产，典出何书？”柿姑掩口笑曰：“妹子俭腹荒疏，以‘参乎杀人’无对，因忆《聊斋志异》载有原壤控颜回一事，姑戏拈凑塞责。原属一时急就，既拟用拾尘故事，将‘霸产’改‘盗食’二字，以业已缮好，懒再涂鸦，果为大方家所笑。”公赞曰：“《志异》虽小说，将来必传。足见贤妹无书不窥。霸产二字较盗食为新鲜，不改何害！”柿姑感婶教养，迎其母子同居。后生成进士，官部曹；椒哥领乡荐，官中翰；公由邑宰荐擢楚抚。彼此以通家之谊，休戚常相关焉。

    Explanation

    496

    But untrustworthy people often change their minds, so one must first make them pay money, and then make the arrangements, only then will it be completely safe." The student said, "Good." He went out and told a certain person, who was greatly pleased and thanked him. The next day, as expected, the person indeed transported the money to present it. The student went to see the official and fully informed him of the reason. The official happily agreed, saying, "I was just struggling to find a way to raise funds for you, and this method is very good." The person was fortunate to be spared further investigation and deeply appreciated the student's kindness, forgiving all past grievances. From then on, the official often invited the student to his office to discuss literature, and his wife also sometimes visited with Sister Persimmon. The official once asked Sister Persimmon, "Sister, your previous statement mentioned that Yan Hui was wealthy, which book is this from?" Sister Persimmon covered her mouth and laughed, saying, "My sister is narrow-minded and has forgotten much. I couldn't respond to 'Zengzi killed someone,' so I recalled that Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio mentions an incident where Yuanrang accused Yan Hui. I playfully patched together a story to fulfill the requirement. It was originally written hastily in a moment. I had intended to use an old story, but I changed 'wealth' to 'stealing food.' Since it was already completed, I was too lazy to rewrite it, and indeed, it was laughed at by learned people." The official praised her, saying, "Although Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio is a collection of short stories, it will surely be passed down in the future. It shows that you have read all kinds of books. The term 'wealth' is fresher than 'stealing food,' what harm is there in not changing it!" Sister Persimmon was grateful for her aunt's upbringing and welcomed her mother and son to live with her. Later, the student became an imperial scholar and served as a low-ranking government official; Pepper Brother received a local recommendation and served as a mid-level official; the official was recommended and promoted from a county magistrate to the governor of Chu. They maintained close family ties, sharing both joys and sorrows.

    497

    里乘子曰：幸生待还遗金，柿姑掷还缠臂金，一介一贞，的是天生嘉耦。义财必待柿姑掘发，天赏生之介，实天赏女之贞也。或谓婶为幸生所愚，不知生初固无愚婶之心，而婶忽启疑团，迫成夫妇者，亦天使之，非生愚之也。顾生闻婶穷问，但含糊不辨是非，即此足见其黠，固无足怪；惟睹柿姑掘财，犹诡答不肯质言，则黠而近于诈矣，吾无取焉。若柿姑天性英爽，观其斥责某公子与面命邑役之言，何等光照磊落！而破悭囊以助膏火，怨以德遇之，立论尤为谛当，真非寻常男子所能及！所谓“健妇持门户，终胜一丈夫”者，非耶？得妇若此，即不掘义财，吾决其家道必昌也。彼公子图人，几反自坐，输金获免，犹属幸事。祸人转以福人，射影含沙，徒劳心计，纨少年，当奉为殷鉴。邑宰某公，惓惓不忘师谊，存心甚厚。其适然来宰斯土，意者亦彼苍预为位置欤？不然，何其巧也？柿姑亦父名昭，幸生姓名，则予忘之矣。

    Explanation

    497

    The Storyteller said: "Xingsheng waited to return lost money, and Shigugu threw back a bracelet she found. One was of integrity, the other of chastity, indeed they were a naturally good match. Righteous wealth must be revealed by Shigugu through her actions. Heaven rewards Xingsheng for his integrity, but it is actually rewarding Shigugu for her chastity. Some say that the aunt was deceived by Xingsheng, but Xingsheng originally had no intention to deceive his aunt. The aunt suddenly raised doubts, leading to the marriage, which was also willed by heaven, not caused by Xingsheng's deception. When Xingsheng heard his aunt's questioning, he responded vaguely without distinguishing right from wrong, showing his cleverness, which is not surprising. However, when he saw Shigugu uncovering the treasure, he still gave a deceptive answer, showing that his cleverness borders on deceit, which is not acceptable. Shigugu is naturally bright and spirited. Her rebuke of a certain young nobleman and her instructions to the local officials were how brilliant and upright! She broke open a miser's bag to help with the cost of education, meeting his resentment with kindness, and her argument was particularly sound, truly something not even an ordinary man could match! The saying 'A strong woman managing the household is better than a weak man' is true, isn't it? Having such a wife, even without uncovering righteous wealth, I am certain that the family's prosperity will flourish. That young nobleman tried to harm others but almost ended up harming himself, paying money to avoid punishment, which is still fortunate. Those who harm others often end up benefiting others, and plotting against others is futile. Young nobles should take this as a warning. The local official, who deeply remembers his teacher's friendship and has a kind heart, happened to come to govern this land. Perhaps this was predestined by heaven? Otherwise, how coincidental is it? Shigugu's father's name is Zha

    498

    活佛
    　　
    　　江南某生，客游，舣舟江浒，登岸独自游览。信步至一兰若，阒其无人。见内殿壁板所画山水人物甚工，以手摩挲，不觉巧触其机，壁上门忽洞开。内有妇女数辈，正与髡奴掷倒为戏，瞥见生，叱问：“何人？”生大骇，急趋而出。僧徒三五人蹑迹驰追，将生挽回。生泣哀之曰：“乞师慈悲，恕我无知，誓不饶舌。”僧众叱曰：“汝自寻死地，尚望生耶？”一僧曰：“扼之便。”一僧曰：“扼之不如烹之，较易灭迹。”生闻而觫觳，料不能脱，再三哀之曰：“小生冒犯，自知无再生理。求师慈悲，赐全要领，其功德胜于浮屠合尖矣。”一僧曰：“我佛慈悲，姑念无知，其言也哀。将来送活佛生天，我辈可藉渔利，较为得计。”佥曰：“善。”遂将生发剃净，幽诸密室。饮以喑药，日给淡食，不入粒盐。

    Explanation

    498

    A Living Buddha

    A scholar from Jiangnan, traveling as a guest, moored his boat at the riverbank and went ashore to explore alone. He wandered aimlessly to a secluded temple, finding it quiet and empty. He noticed a beautifully painted wall inside the inner hall, depicting landscapes and figures. As he touched the wall, he accidentally triggered a mechanism, and the wall door suddenly opened. Inside, several women were playing a game with eunuchs, tossing and catching something. They caught sight of the scholar and shouted, "Who are you?" The scholar was terrified and rushed out. Three or four monks followed him, trying to bring him back. The scholar pleaded, "Please, masters, have mercy. I am ignorant and promise not to speak." The monks shouted, "You have brought yourself to a place of death. Do you still hope to live?" One monk said, "Strangle him." Another said, "Strangling him is not as effective as cooking him; it would be easier to erase all traces." The scholar trembled, realizing he could not escape, and pleaded again, "I have committed a grave offense and know I will not live. Please, masters, have mercy and grant me a quick end. Your kindness will surpass the merits of a living Buddha reaching the heavens." One monk said, "The Buddha is compassionate. Let us spare him, considering his ignorance. His words are pitiful. We can send him to become a living Buddha in the heavens, and we can profit from it. This is a better plan." They all agreed, "Good." So, they shaved the scholar's head clean, confined him in a secret room, fed him a drug that silenced him, and gave him plain food, without even a grain of salt.

    499

    百日，肌肤肥白如匏，且腰脚柔软，不能行立。乃于郊外架木为高台，谓某日活佛肉身，趺坐台上，涅槃示寂，藉火化以生天。举国男妇闻之，扶老携幼，不远而来。皆香花顶礼，瞻拜祈祷，一唱百和，舞蹈若狂。郊外距邑城密迩，邑令某公，健吏也。耳其事，率干役数人，微服自往瞷察。见台高丈馀，一僧戴毗卢帽，面白皙如满月，身披五色袈裟，趺足坐榻上，闭目泪涔下如雨。台下僧众百数十人，各执鱼钹、鼓磬、笙箫、琴阮，旌旛羽盖，循环旋绕，喃喃奉经礼忏。众男女从其后，同宣佛号，一体膜拜。台前后左右置薪刍，间杂旃檀纸帛，高等邱陵。待时至举火，送活佛生天。公谓：“活佛生天，复何流泪？岂尚有尘缘难割耶？”初固疑其妄，睹此益信。亟遣干役驰白主僧，曰：“邑侯闻活佛生天，欢喜无量。

    Explanation

    499

    For a hundred days, the person's skin was plump and white like a gourd, and their waist and legs were so soft that they could neither walk nor stand. They then built a high platform with wood in the outskirts, announcing that on a certain day, the physical body of a living Buddha would sit cross-legged on the platform, enter nirvana and show stillness, and be cremated to be reborn in heaven. Men and women from all over the country heard about it, carrying the elderly and young children, coming from afar. They all offered incense and flowers in worship, gazed upon and prayed to the Buddha, sang in unison, and danced wildly. The outskirts were very close to the city, and the magistrate of the county, a capable official, heard about the matter. He led several subordinates, dressed in plain clothes, to secretly observe. They saw the platform was more than ten feet high, with a monk wearing a pilu hat, his face white and smooth like a full moon, wearing a five-colored robe, sitting cross-legged on a bed, with tears flowing like rain. Below the platform, there were hundreds of monks, each holding fish-shaped cymbals, drums, bells, flutes, and lutes, flags, banners, and feathered canopies, circling around repeatedly, reciting scriptures and performing penance softly. The men and women followed behind, reciting the Buddha's name together and bowing in unison. Straw and firewood were placed around the platform, mixed with sandalwood, paper, and silk, as high as hills. They waited for the time to come to light the fire, to send the living Buddha to heaven. The magistrate said, "If the living Buddha is going to heaven, why is he crying? Is there still a worldly attachment that is hard to part with?" Initially, it was suspected to be false, but seeing this increased belief. He quickly sent subordinates to inform the chief monk, saying, "The magistrate heard that the living Buddha was going to heaven and was extremely happy."

    500

    亲来拈香，谕众暂缓举火。”僧众素知公威严，不敢有违，亟含笑答曰：“邑主肯赐降临，为我佛之光，僧等曷胜荣幸，理合敬候。”公亟反署，盛设仪仗而至。僧众合掌前迎。公问：“活佛何在？”主僧笑指台上谓：“趺坐者即是活佛。”并详述其平日清修高行。公啧啧称叹，谓：“今日天刑，活佛生天，恐未能遽登极乐之界，暂请改期，何如？”主僧答称：“此活佛自订日期，未便擅改。”公笑曰：“活佛未曾留意宪书，下官忝主一邑，合为改正。明日天赦，生天最吉。请活佛在邑署暂住一夜，藉使署中细弱，得遂瞻拜。”主僧答称：“活佛功行圆满，即绝口不言，又肉体尊重，不便行动，碍难进署。”公笑称：“我自有法。”乃命健儿数人，将活佛舁至署中。僧众箝口相视，不敢阻止；又莫测公喜怒，殊切悬虑。

    Explanation

    500

    The magistrate personally came to offer incense and instructed the crowd to delay the fire. The monks, who knew the magistrate's strictness, dared not disobey and quickly answered with a smile, "The magistrate's gracious presence is a great honor to our Buddha, and we cannot bear to refuse. We should respectfully await his arrival." The magistrate immediately returned to his office and prepared a grand procession before returning. The monks bowed before him. The magistrate asked, "Where is the living Buddha?" The chief monk pointed to the platform and said, "He is the one sitting cross-legged." He also detailed the living Buddha's daily practices and virtues. The magistrate marveled and said, "Today, the heavenly punishment is upon him. The living Buddha ascending to heaven may not immediately enter the Pure Land. Perhaps we should change the date, what do you think?" The chief monk replied, "The living Buddha himself set the date, and it would not be proper to change it." The magistrate smiled and said, "The living Buddha did not notice the almanac. As the magistrate of this county, I shall correct it. Tomorrow is the day of heavenly pardon, which is the most auspicious time to ascend to heaven. Please allow the living Buddha to stay at the county office for one night, so that the weak and vulnerable in the office can pay their respects." The chief monk replied, "The living Buddha's merit and virtue are complete. Even if he does not speak, his body is too sacred to move. It is difficult to bring him to the office." The magistrate smiled and said, "I have my own method." He then ordered several strong men to carry the living Buddha to the office. The monks held their tongues and looked on, unable to stop him; they were unsure of the magistrate's intentions and were filled with anxiety.

    501

    活佛既至署中，公命安置内记室。夜半潜自研诘，见其涕泪交并，言动俱绝，心知有异。因问：“能作字否？”活佛点首。亟命将笔砚至。活佛胖软，臂不能举，惟以指蘸墨书纸上，历叙巅末。公阅之大怒，命活佛安心，药食调治，俟差愈，牒送回籍。翌旦，谕寺僧齐集台下，毋许擅离。又密牒骑尉、督营卒多人，乘僧等出后，围寺穷搜，果获妇女数人，所藏金珠衣物甚富。公至台下，僧众请迎活佛，公笑曰：“活佛有命，请主僧替代生天。”主僧大惧，跽称知罪求宥。公叱左右，将主僧缚掷台上。又指主谋助虐数人，谓当追配，亦命同缚掷台上，叱令举火。火烈风猛，一转瞬俱成灰烬。僧众环视，面如死灰。观者闻知其事，同声称快。公命将馀僧笞责，谕令蓄发归农。其妇女各归亲属。乃将寺改为义塾，即变易其金珠衣物，以资膏火云。

    Explanation

    501

    The Lama had arrived at the official's residence, where he was ordered to be accommodated in an inner office. At midnight, the official secretly interrogated him, observing that he was weeping and sobbing, and that his speech and movements had ceased, leading the official to suspect something unusual. He asked, "Can you write?" The Lama nodded. The official immediately ordered writing tools to be brought. The Lama, being fat and weak, could not raise his arms, so he used his fingers to dip in ink and write on the paper, detailing the entire story. Upon reading it, the official became greatly angry, ordered the Lama to calm down, and provided him with medicine and food for treatment. He then instructed that the Lama be sent back to his hometown once he recovered. The next day, the official ordered the monks of the temple to gather at the base of the platform, warning them not to leave without permission. He also secretly ordered several military officers and guards to surround the temple and thoroughly search it once the monks had left. Indeed, they captured several women and found a vast amount of gold, jewels, and clothing hidden there. When the official arrived at the base of the platform, the monks requested to welcome the Lama. The official smiled and said, "The Lama has a command: ask the head monk to take his place in heaven." The head monk was greatly afraid, knelt down, admitted his guilt, and begged for mercy. The official shouted at his attendants, who tied up the head monk and threw him onto the platform. He then pointed out several accomplices who had helped in the cruelty, saying they should be punished severely, and also ordered them to be tied up and thrown onto the platform. He commanded the fire to be lit. The fire was fierce, and the wind was strong; in an instant, everything turned to ashes. The monks looked around, their faces ashen. Those who witnessed the event heard about it and all praised it. The official ordered the remaining monks to be flogged and instructed them to grow their hair and return to farming. The women were returned to their families. The temple was then converted into a public school, and the gold, jewels, and clothing were immediately changed to support the school's expenses.

    502

    里乘子曰：髡奴托名三宝，无恶不作。兹以人命渔利，愈出愈奇。邑令某公请君入瓮，以其道还治其人，较西门令邺更为称快。传者逸其姓名，惜哉！
    　　
    　　礼部书吏
    　　
    　　明万历时，礼部书吏某，穷乏无以自存。时值岁暮，益难为计，闻术人李实中精星命，特袖例金往求推算。李细玩命笺，叹曰：“论尊造乃巨富之命，奈终身无一佳运，殊属可惜。”某请其说，李曰：“命由舟也，运由风也。虽有巨艘，不遇顺风，亦惟有胶搁浅渚，听其腐朽焉而已。何足贵哉！”某闻李言，嗒然若失。久之，复请于李曰：“据先生言，巨富非所敢望。尚求细推，未审得免饿填沟壑否？”李又为掐指轮算，忽诧曰：“奇哉！奇哉！尊造以演禽之法测之，明年五月五日午时，当有昴日鸡到宫，昴宿在十二地支属酉，酉内藏金，金星透露。主有意外机缘，虽巨富可唾手而得，此巨艘遇风时也，君其识之。过此以往，则非鄙人所敢知矣。

    Explanation

    502

    A humble scholar remarked: A shaved slave who pretends to be associated with the Three Jewels of Buddhism, does no evil. He uses human life to fish for profit, and his actions become increasingly extraordinary. A certain county magistrate caught him using his own methods and dealt with him in the same way, finding it even more satisfying than Ximen's governance of Ye. The transmitter omitted his name, alas!

    A clerk in the Ministry of Rites

    During the Wanli era of the Ming dynasty, a clerk in the Ministry of Rites was destitute and unable to sustain himself. As the year drew to a close, he found it even more difficult to make ends meet. He heard that a fortune-teller named Li Shizhong was skilled in astrology and divination, so he secretly brought a gift of gold to seek a reading. Li carefully studied the astrological chart and sighed, "According to your chart, you have the fortune of becoming a great wealthy person, but you will never have a single good luck in your life, which is truly regrettable." The clerk asked for an explanation, and Li said, "Fate is like a boat, and luck is like the wind. Even if you have a great boat, without a favorable wind, it will only be stuck in shallow waters, left to rot. How valuable is that?" The clerk was deeply disappointed upon hearing Li's words. After a long while, he asked Li again, "According to your words, becoming a great wealthy person is not something I dare to hope for. But please give me a detailed reading. Am I destined to starve to death in a ditch?" Li then began to calculate and suddenly exclaimed, "Amazing! Amazing! According to the method of divination, on the fifth day of the fifth month next year at noon, there will be the star of the Rooster of the Seventh Mansion in the palace. The Seventh Mansion belongs to the Yin Earth in the twelve earthly branches, and there is hidden metal within it, with the star of metal revealing itself. This signifies an unexpected opportunity, and even great wealth can be obtained effortlessly. This is the time when the great boat meets the wind. You must remember this. Beyond this, it is not something I dare to predict."

    503

    ”某不敢再，酬以例金。李却之曰：“此戋戋者，暂存尊处，俟所言果验，当以千金酬我可也。”李笑曰：“诺。”既归，忆李所言，疑信参半。乃瞬届端阳，落莫较常尤甚。故事，每节必留一人在部听差。其侪知某避债难以归家，因共醵钱三百馈某，聊贳肴酒，在部伺应。某乃沽酒一瓶，置书架上，又出刀脔切羊脯，将备赏午。忽书架上两鼠相斗，将酒瓶触翻堕地，砰然一声，乃成粉碎。某停刀瞪视，顿忆术士所言，不禁喟然长叹曰：“承诸君高义，瓶酒且不能享，尚何望意外机缘哉！”比视架上，簿牍已被酒渍湿，恐系要件，急取翻阅，乃三年前琉球国册立世子表也，以例表无甚关系，其心始定。因将瓶瓦拾抛院中，又取湿表，将就日中曝之。

    Explanation

    503

    I dare not accept it again, so I paid him the customary fee. Li refused and said, "This small amount, please temporarily keep it with you. If what I said comes true, I will pay you a thousand gold coins." Li smiled and said, "Okay." After returning home, I remembered what Li had said and was half in doubt. Soon it was the Duanwu Festival, and my loneliness was even more severe than usual. Customarily, during every festival, one person must stay at the office to handle duties. My colleagues knew that I was avoiding debt and could not return home, so they collectively contributed three hundred coins to me, to buy some food and wine, and to serve at the office. I then bought a bottle of wine, placed it on the bookshelf, and took out a knife to slice some lamb, preparing for the midday meal. Suddenly, two mice on the bookshelf were fighting, knocking over the wine bottle and dropping it to the ground with a loud bang, shattering it into pieces. I stopped cutting and stared, suddenly remembering what the fortune-teller had said, and couldn't help but sigh deeply and say, "Receiving the high virtue of my friends, not even able to enjoy a bottle of wine, how can I hope for unexpected opportunities?" When I looked at the shelf, the documents had been soaked with wine, fearing that they were important documents, I quickly took them out and read them. It was the memorial of the Ryukyu Kingdom's enthronement of its crown prince from three years ago. Since the memorial was not particularly important, his heart finally calmed down. So he picked up the broken bottle and threw it into the yard, and then took out the wet documents and let them dry in the sun.

    504

    甫一掉头，忽门外一人迎面而来，揖问某曰：“此是礼部，有书吏先生在此否？”某观其人，系琉球国衣冠，默自惊讶。即拱手答曰：“小人便是书吏。未审有何见教？”其人笑曰：“下官乃琉球国使臣也。今为册立世子一事，特求斡旋，望勿见拒。”某问：“如何？”使臣四顾无人，乃谓某曰：“三年前册立世子，曾进表大国。不谓世子荒淫无道，顿失国人之望。顷我王与大臣会议，将废而改立，以前已进表，不敢擅专。今特谋诸先生，如将前表抽还，俟改立既定，再行进表，泯去废立之迹，较为得体。如事幸成，不吝重谢。”某闻使臣言，故为沈吟，良久乃答曰：“事关废立，非同恒泛，不敢轻诺。然必以慎密为要，倘一泄漏，为害非浅。此地耳目甚多，不便商议。

    Explanation

    504

    Upon turning around, a person suddenly appeared at the door, bowing and asking, "This is the Ministry of Rites. Is there a clerk here?" Observing the person, I noticed they wore the attire of the Ryukyu Kingdom and was silently surprised. I bowed in return and replied, "I am the clerk. I wonder what advice or instruction you have?" The person smiled and said, "I am an envoy from the Ryukyu Kingdom. I have come to seek mediation regarding the matter of establishing a crown prince. I hope you will not refuse." I asked, "How so?" The envoy glanced around to ensure there was no one else present and then said to me, "Three years ago, when we established the crown prince, we submitted a table to the great country. However, the crown prince has become morally corrupt and lost the people's trust. Recently, our king and ministers have discussed deposing and replacing him. Since we have already submitted the previous table, we dare not act independently. We have come to you specifically to ask if we could withdraw the previous table, wait until the new crown prince is established, and then submit a new table, thereby erasing the traces of the deposition and replacement. This would be more appropriate. If the matter succeeds, we will not hesitate to offer a generous reward." Upon hearing the envoy's words, I paused in thought and, after a long while, replied, "The matter involves deposing and replacing the crown prince, which is not a trivial affair. I dare not make a hasty promise. However, it is essential to be cautious and discreet. If any information were to leak, the harm would be significant. There are many eyes and ears here, so it is not suitable to discuss this matter."

    505

    请约定翌辰于前门外酒馆报命，何如？”使臣笑曰：“君言甚善，谨遵所约，万勿失信，幸甚！”某送使臣去后，试测日影，恰是卓午，自知术士之言有验。翌辰，前往酒馆，使臣已在此久待。见某至，含笑相迎曰：“先生来何迟也？”某笑曰：“非敢来迟，但为君故，费煞经营。昨访知此表为吾侪某甲所藏。某甲为人猾而贪，不足与谋，已施小策将此表赚出，一俟到手，即行奉献。未审事成果何以犒小人也？”使臣笑曰：“如果事成，以三十万金相酬，何如？”某曰：“三十万金原不为菲，惟贺贵国废暗立贤，兆民是赖，小人不为无功，敢请少益。”使臣笑伸五指谓曰：“君言是也。事成，请以五十万金为先生寿。先生好为图之，愈速愈妙。”某笑曰：“诺。”遂订三日献表兑银。果唾手而得巨富，其侪竟无一人预知其事者。某寻罢役归乡，润屋买田，以财雄一方。乃延术士于家，以千金酬之。

    Explanation

    505

    "Let us agree to meet at the wine shop outside the city gate the next day to report back, what do you say?" The envoy smiled and said, "Your words are very good, I will strictly adhere to our agreement and never break my word, which is fortunate indeed!" After seeing off the envoy, the man tested the shadow of the sun and found it was exactly at noon, realizing that the fortune-teller's words had come true. The next day, he went to the wine shop, where the envoy had been waiting for a long time. Seeing him arrive, the envoy greeted him with a smile and said, "Why did you come so late, Mr.?" The man smiled and replied, "I did not dare to be late, but for your sake, I spent much effort. Yesterday, I learned that this table is hidden by our fellow Mr. A. Mr. A is cunning and greedy, not worth plotting with, so I devised a small plan to lure the table out. As soon as I get it, I will present it to you. I wonder what reward you will give me if the matter is successful?" The envoy smiled and said, "If the matter is successful, I will reward you with 300,000 gold coins, how about that?" The man said, "300,000 gold coins are not a small amount, but I congratulate your country on replacing the incompetent with the virtuous, which is relied upon by the people. I am not without merit, so I dare to ask for a little more." The envoy smiled and extended five fingers, saying, "Your words are right. If the matter is successful, I will reward you with 500,000 gold coins for your birthday. Please do well and the faster the better." The man laughed and said, "Yes." They then agreed to present the table and exchange the silver in three days. Indeed, he obtained great wealth effortlessly, and none of his peers knew about this matter. After finishing his duties, he returned to his hometown, enriched his home, bought land, and became wealthy in his region. He then invited the fortune-teller to his home and rewarded him with a thousand gold pieces.

    506

    肇庆府署五异
    　　
    　　广东肇庆府署中，异事有五：一、署有金砖二十四方，方各十馀斤，其色黝然而光，完好无少缺。或携以妥几榻，或叠以庋什物，均可。倘欲窃而藏之，则头立痛，寒热交作；还，即瘥。一、署后有枯树一株，枝干杈枒，秃无片叶，不知何代物也。土人言是张四姐摇钱树。每新太守至，必具衣冠拜之，否则不吉。一、署堂皇前有破瓮，大容数石，传为镇妖之物。新太守至，亦必拜之。一、署内有空屋一椽，封甚严。相传宋时包孝肃守肇庆，日理阳事，夜理阴事，此名“覆阴堂”，其理阴事处也。太守来者，尤不敢不拜。一、堂皇有古井，上覆以石。言是孝肃治水怪于其下，人不敢启视。旧有“包收陆放马成潮”之谣，自来太守无姓陆与马者。闻曾有陆姓捧檄来守此土，郡人大惧，绅耆联名哀禀于上官，寻将陆太守调任他郡，否则一郡陆沉矣。此袁竹畦参军起为予言者，其叔祖香亭先生曾守肇庆，故知之最详。

    Explanation

    506

    Five Strange Events in the Zhaoqing Government Office

    In the Zhaoqing government office in Guangdong, there are five strange events:

    1. Gold Bricks: There are twenty-four gold bricks in the office, each weighing over ten pounds. They have a dark, lustrous appearance and are in perfect condition. They can be used to place on tables or beds, or to stack and store items. If someone attempts to steal and hide one, they will suffer from headaches and chills. Returning the brick will immediately alleviate these symptoms.

    2. Dry Tree: Behind the office, there is a dry tree with branches spreading out in a forked manner, completely bare of leaves. It is unclear which dynasty it belongs to. Locals believe it is the "Money Tree of Zhang Sijie." Whenever a new governor arrives, they must dress formally and bow to the tree; otherwise, it is considered unlucky.

    3. Large Jar: In front of the main hall of the office, there is a large jar that can hold several stones. It is said to be an object used to ward off evil spirits. New governors must also bow to the jar upon arrival.

    4. Sealed Room: Inside the office, there is an empty room with a tightly sealed door. It is said that during the Song Dynasty, Bao Zheng, the governor of Zhaoqing, handled public affairs during the day and private matters at night. This room is called "Fuyin Tang" (Covered Night Hall) and was used for handling private matters. New governors are particularly cautious and must bow to the room.

    5. Ancient Well: In the main hall, there is an ancient well covered with stones. It is said that Bao Zheng dealt with water demons beneath it, and no one dares to open the well. There is an old saying, "Bao receives, Lu releases, Ma becomes a tide," which means that no governor has ever had the names Lu or Ma. It is said that once a governor named Lu arrived with official documents, the people of the county were greatly alarmed. The local gentry jointly petitioned the superior official, and soon after, Governor Lu was reassigned to another region, otherwise the entire county would have been submerged.

    This information was provided by the military officer Yuan Zhuiqi, whose uncle, Xiangting Mr., had previously served as the governor of Zhaoqing, and thus knew the details most thoroughly.

    507

    鬼批县尉颊
    　　
    　　茹古香尚书棻，未达时，暑夜，偕同学友三人，在城外大桥避热。三人者，后一为方伯，一为太守，其一则为县尉也，其时皆布衣。同至桥上，见石栏杆上坐一女子，月色昏黄，面目不甚可辨，意其亦为避热来者。诸公藉地而坐，上下千古，纵谈甚欢。少焉，月至中天，较前皎洁，照见女子面色惨淡。群疑非人，少年选事，因议分立桥四隅，阻其去路，得以观其变相，藉博一笑。夜色将阑，女子果起身，在桥上两头蹀躞，苦不得去。既而鸡声乱啼，女意益急，忽腾身起，以掌批县尉颊骂曰：“汝不过一县尉耳！亦欲与阿娘作梗耶？”其人略一侧身，女子倏已不见。此周涑人刺史言者。古香尚书，其同乡先达也。

    Explanation

    507

    The Ghost Slaps the County Magistrate's Cheek

    During the time when Shu Ren, the respected scholar and official, was not yet successful, one sultry summer night, he accompanied three fellow students to a bridge outside the city to escape the heat. The three companions were later to become a provincial governor, a prefect, and a county magistrate, but at the time, they were all commoners. Together, they went to the bridge, where they saw a woman sitting on the stone railings. The moonlight was dim and yellowish, and her face was not very clear, but they assumed she had come to escape the heat as well. They sat down on the ground and talked freely and happily, discussing matters from ancient times to the present.

    After a while, the moon reached its zenith, brighter than before, and its light revealed the woman's pale face. Everyone began to suspect that she was not human. The young men, who enjoyed gossip, decided to stand at the four corners of the bridge to block her path, so they could see her transformation and perhaps get a laugh. As the night was nearly over, the woman indeed stood up and paced back and forth on the bridge, struggling to leave. Soon after, the roosters crowed loudly, and the woman became increasingly anxious. Suddenly, she leaped up and slapped the county magistrate's cheek, shouting, "You are just a county magistrate! Do you also want to obstruct Mother?" The man slightly moved his body, and the woman suddenly disappeared.

    This story was told by Zhou Shu Ren, the governor. Shu Ren was a fellow townsman and a respected scholar of the past.

    508

    里乘子曰：鬼能预知人爵秩，大奇。岂以诸公之中惟县尉官阶较卑，气焰亦较杀，择其可欺者而欺之耶？吁！鬼之揶揄，亦何虐哉！
    　　
    　　庚午神诛水怪
    　　
    　　周涑人刺史又言，某年暑月，湖北木商运筏至九江关，忽江中一爪大于箕，抓其筏不能行。后筏来者愈多，遂将江路梗塞。或谓木商是必获罪江神，宜以少牢祀之。商从其言，刑牲衅血，酾酒祭祷，爪卒不释。或又谓是必筏藏有妖。商不得已，折筏去木，甫六层，见中藏一蜴蜥，长五尺许，跃入江中，爪倏不见。俄顷，波浪大作，风雨雷电并至，怒涛掀簸，如万马奔腾，震耳骇心，舟人无不战慄失色。忽霹雳一声，一物死江干，形类蜴蜥，长丈馀，其腹中裂，有篆书十一字云：“水怪为害，帝命庚午神诛之。”
    　　
    　　金毛吼

    Explanation

    508

    Master Li Cheng said: "It is amazing that ghosts can foresee a person's rank. Is it because among the officials, only the county magistrate's rank is relatively low, and his influence is somewhat diminished, so the ghost chose to mock him? Alas! The ghost's mockery is truly cruel!"

    In a certain summer month, a wooden raft from a wood merchant in Hubei province was transported to the Jiujiang customs when suddenly a claw larger than a winnowing basket appeared in the river, grabbing the raft and preventing it from moving. The following rafts coming later increased the number, blocking the river passage. Some said that the wood merchant must have offended the river god and should sacrifice a small livestock offering to it. The merchant followed the advice, slaughtering an animal, sprinkling its blood, and pouring wine for sacrifice and prayer, but the claw still did not let go. Some then suggested that there must be a monster hidden in the raft. The merchant had no choice but to dismantle the raft and remove the wood. After removing six layers, they found a large lizard, about five feet long, which leaped into the river, and the claw suddenly disappeared. Soon, waves surged, and wind, rain, thunder, and lightning all arrived at once. The angry waves tossed and turned, shaking the ears and frightening the heart, and all the boatmen trembled and lost their composure. Suddenly, a loud thunderclap, and a creature died on the shore, its shape resembling a large lizard, over ten feet long. Its belly split open, revealing eleven characters in seal script: "The water monster was causing harm, and the emperor ordered the Ji Wu God to punish it."

    Golden-Haired Roar

    509

    周涑人刺史又言，道光初年，江苏崇明县乡村，秋获后，地中无故火起，延烧人家甚众。其处旧有古塚。一日，风雨骤至，一物自塚中出，形如狻猊，竟体皆火，所过草木尽灰。空中数龙下与物斗，雷电随之，且斗且走，入海而去，海水为之沸腾，经日始定。或曰：“此物即金毛吼也。”
    　　
    　　蜘蛛怪
    　　
    　　海州海中，有母子二蜘蛛，母大于箕，子大于釜，滨海居民皆见之。尝秋日禾黍登场，积草隆然如山，小蛛戏以臀吹之，堆草乱飞，俄顷尽净。道光八年五月十四日，暴雨如注，天大雷电以风，大木斯拔。海滨居民见大蛛起悬空中，五龙环绕，蛛故缩其足，伺龙将及身，足怒伸如劲弓反驰，龙即四散。蛛又缩足，龙又来，又如前怒伸，卒不能近其身。蛛又吐丝缚龙爪，胶不能开，赖火龙来燃其丝，爪乃不为所缚，相斗逾时，仍入海而去。翌日，人拾所火断丝，粗于臂，或盈丈，或盈尺，两健儿持两端而力挽之，竟不能断。

    Explanation

    509

    Governor Zhou of Su reported that in the early years of the Daoguang era, in the rural areas of Chongming County, Jiangsu Province, after the autumn harvest, fire broke out in the ground for no reason, spreading and burning many households. There was an old tomb in that place. One day, a sudden storm arrived, and something emerged from the tomb, in the shape of a Suan Ni, its entire body on fire, and all the grass and trees it passed were turned to ash. Several dragons descended from the sky to fight with the creature, followed by lightning and thunder. They fought and ran, entering the sea and disappearing, causing the sea to boil, and it took several days to settle down. Some say, "This creature is the Golden-Furred Roar."

    The Spider Monster

    In the sea near Hai Zhou, there were a mother and son spider. The mother was larger than a winnowing basket, and the son was larger than a cauldron. Coastal residents all saw them. Once, during autumn, when the rice and millet were harvested and piled up like mountains, the smaller spider playfully blew at the pile of grass with its rear end, causing the grass to fly chaotically, and in an instant, the pile was completely cleared. On the fourteenth day of the fifth month of the eighth year of Daoguang, a heavy rain fell, accompanied by thunder and strong winds, and large trees were uprooted. Coastal residents saw the large spider rise and hang in the air, surrounded by five dragons. The spider deliberately retracted its legs, waiting for the dragons to approach, and then suddenly extended its legs like a powerful bow, causing the dragons to scatter. The spider retracted its legs again, and the dragons came back, and the spider again extended its legs with anger, but could not get close to its body. The spider then spun silk to bind the dragons' claws, which could not be opened. Relying on a fire dragon to burn the silk, the claws were no longer bound, and they fought for over an hour before returning to the sea. The next day, people picked up the burned and broken silk, which was thicker than an arm, some over a meter long, some over a foot long, and two strong men held the ends and pulled with all their might, but could not break it.

    510

    骊叆卿
    　　
    　　方伯融公子燕昭言，粤有骊叆卿者，明中叶时，于罗浮出家学道，今四百八十六岁。其子孙甚蕃衍，尝至罗浮，叩请其祖回家一省祠宇。叆卿初不许，求之至再，乃首肯，遂以肩舆舁至宗祠。约炊许，即欲还山。子孙环拜请曰：“老祖宗既归，何不少留？”叆卿让之曰：“此间尘俗气太重，那可久住！”子孙不得已，又命肩舆送其还山。行过市廛，人多见之，其身长如十三四岁童子，白髯垂胸，爪长数尺。甫出城，舁者觉肩上渐行渐轻，察之身渐缩小，未几，才如初堕地之婴，寻倏不见。舆中仅存玛烟瓶一具，以献其子孙，宝之，以为宗器。

    Explanation

    510

    A Tale of Lai Aiqing

    According to the Duke of Yan, Prince Yan Zhao, the heir of the Marquis of Fang, there was a Taoist named Lai Aiqing in the mid-Ming dynasty who took up residence and studied Daoism at Luofu Mountain. He is currently 486 years old. His descendants are numerous and prosperous, and they once visited Luofu Mountain to respectfully request that their ancestor return home to visit the ancestral temple. Lai Aiqing initially refused, but after repeated requests, he finally agreed. They then carried him in a sedan chair to the ancestral temple. About the time it takes to cook a meal, he wanted to return to the mountain. The descendants surrounded him and respectfully requested, "Since the old ancestor has returned, why not stay a little longer?" Lai Aiqing replied, "The air here is too heavy with worldly affairs, how could one stay long!" The descendants, unable to persuade him, again ordered the sedan chair to send him back to the mountain. As they passed through the market, many people saw him. His body was as tall as that of a thirteen or fourteen-year-old boy, with a white beard hanging down to his chest and claws several feet long. As soon as they left the city, the bearers felt their shoulders gradually becoming lighter. They observed that his body gradually shrank, and soon he was only as big as a newborn baby, then suddenly disappeared. Only a single agate tobacco pipe remained in the sedan chair, which they presented to their descendants, who treasured it as a family heirloom.

    511

    记海鹿门别驾少时事
    　　
    　　海保，字鹿门，裔出自襄阳孟氏。先世忠颜公乔芳，以从龙勋，隶旗籍，累代有显宦。君幼侍尊人，宦游三吴。容止玉立，风神恬定，读书过目辄了了。弱冠从李申耆先生兆洛游，极爱赏之。兼精骑射击刺、拳勇超距诸艺。性喜任狭，负气好义，见不平事，不惜以身殉之。姑苏玄妙观者，一郡游观之薮也，士女日集恒万人。无赖恶少，见游女少具姿首，必环而尾之；甚至一人唱呵，万声响应，四方攒里，将只身妇女迫困重围，姿意戏侮，手摩足弄，无所不至，既将巾履簪珥分劫携去，名曰“打圈”。在妇女之寡廉鲜耻者，不以为辱，反以为荣，以为必己之色貌过人，方应斯选，且常夸述于人，自鸣得意；而少知自爱者，不幸遇此，亦不免羞愤欲绝。缘陋俗相沿既久，浸习成风，都人士视为惯常，恬不为怪。

    Explanation

    511

    Record of the Young Years of Hai Luomen, the Deputy Magistrate

    Hai Bao, styled Luomen, originated from the Meng family of Xiangyang. His ancestors, including the loyal and distinguished official Qiao Fang, gained merit by following the emperor, were registered in the military register, and had generations of prominent officials. When young, Hai Bao served his father and traveled for official duties in the three Wu regions. He had a dignified and upright appearance, a composed and serene demeanor, and read and understood everything at a glance. At the age of twenty, he studied under Mr. Li Shenqi (Li Zhaolu), and was highly admired and appreciated. He was also proficient in various skills such as archery, swordsmanship, martial arts, and physical prowess. He had a bold and adventurous nature, braved for justice, and was willing to sacrifice himself for what he believed was right.

    The Xuanmiao Temple in Suzhou was a popular place for sightseeing in the entire prefecture, where tens of thousands of men and women gathered daily. Ruffians and villains, seeing young women with attractive appearances, would surround and follow them; even if one person shouted, thousands would respond, from all directions, forcing single women into tight encirclements, acting out their mischief, touching and playing with them, doing whatever they wanted, and taking away their hats, shoes, hairpins, and earrings, calling this act "circle beating." Women who lacked modesty and shame considered it an honor, thinking their beauty surpassed others, deserving such selection, and often boasted about it, feeling proud. Those who knew self-respect, unfortunately encountering this, also could not help but feel utterly humiliated and angry. Because this vulgar custom had been passed down for a long time, gradually becoming a habit, city dwellers saw it as normal and not finding it strange.

    512

    是以荐绅闺阁，有事至观祈祷，必乘肩舆，从健仆左右护持之，非示夸也，实畏打圈耳。君少时尝同人救一雏女，得免打圈之辱。其事甚快意，故为志之。先是，女郎偕一童游观中，猝遇众无赖，窥其意不善，亟携童踉跄反走。奈众麕缀要遮，女东亦东，女西亦西，评头论足，肆口秽谑，涎视耽耽，不少宽纵，愈逼愈紧，丑态百出。女郎不堪其嬲，宛转娇羞，气急败坏，仓皇四顾，正无计得以幸脱。适君同人至此，目击其状，义气填膺，忿焰莫遏。慨然谓同人曰：“承平世宙，岂有人家弱息，而听强暴公然肆虐于光天化日之下！我辈不救护，谁救护者！诸君其谓之何？倘激于义愤，惠然御侮，抑暴扶良，仆不敏，愿助一臂。”同人笑曰：“君又选事也耶？人家儿女，何预卿事？”君白眼争曰：“谁家无儿女？奈何受狂且窘辱若此！诸君既忍心袖手，不肯向前，仆当独身任之。

    Explanation

    512

    Therefore, among the literati and the women of noble households, whenever they had matters requiring them to go to temples for prayers, they would necessarily ride in palanquins, accompanied by strong attendants on either side to protect and support them—not to show off, but out of fear of being caught in a trap. When the gentleman was young, he once, together with others, rescued a young maiden, saving her from the disgrace of being caught in such a trap. The incident was very satisfying, and so he recorded it. Earlier, the young lady had been sightseeing with a child when she suddenly encountered a group of rascals. Noticing their evil intentions, she quickly took the child and fled, stumbling backward in fear. But the rascals gathered closely to block her path—wherever she ran east, they followed east; wherever she ran west, they followed west. They gossiped and jeered, spoke vulgar jokes, and looked at her with lecherous eyes, showing no restraint at all. The more they pressed, the tighter the situation became, and their ugly behavior was endless. The young lady could not endure their harassment, twisting and turning in embarrassment, her face flushed with shame, her breath ragged, her heart in turmoil, and she looked around in panic, with no way to escape. Just then, the gentleman and his companions arrived at the scene, witnessing the situation with their own eyes, and filled with righteous indignation, their anger could not be restrained. He solemnly said to his companions, “In times of peace and stability, how can there be helpless children from respectable families who are allowed to be openly abused by the violent under the open sky? If we do not come to their aid, who will? What would you all say to this? If we are moved by a sense of justice, and are willing to defend against insult, to suppress the violent and support the virtuous, though I am not wise, I am willing to lend a hand.” His companions laughed and said, “Are you choosing another quarrel? What business is it of yours, the children of others?” The gentleman looked at them coldly and argued, “Who among us does not have children? How can we allow them to suffer such humiliation? If you all have the heart to stand by and do nothing, refusing to act, then I shall take on the responsibility alone.”

    513

    ”言讫，攘臂跃入人丛，横身要截，厉声叱曰：“止止！鼠子不得无礼！”众无赖正猖狂畅意，闻君言，怒答曰：“吾侪逢场作剧。若何人？敢败乃公事！”彼此硬语牴牾。一人遽前，以掌掴君面，君佝身疾出腋下，反掌搏其背，复以趾踆之颠。一人踵而前，又颠之，连踣四五人，馀乃不敢继进，始纷纷鸟兽散，女郎之围以解。于时旁观如堵墙，初以君文弱卤莽，恐难撄众锋，今幸君胜，欢声雷动。咸色喜耳语，窃谓何处少年，重意气作此快举？啧啧称道不置。同人从而虑之曰：“君但快心于一时，懋建义举。然群儿溃窜，怒形于色，必号召以图报复。倘众寡不敌，奈何？”君曰：“实逼处此，讵容中馁！惟今之计，为德不可不卒，请与诸君约：毋短气，毋贰心，当同卫送两小归。纵与若曹遇，共击无不利。”佥曰：“诺。”盖君所同来五人，皆好身手。

    Explanation

    513

    After finishing his speech, the man raised his arms and leapt into the crowd, blocking their path with his body. With a stern voice, he shouted, "Enough! You rascals shall not be disrespectful!" The group of ruffians, who were just having a good time, heard his words and angrily replied, "We're just having fun here. Who are you? Dare to ruin our business!" Both sides exchanged harsh words. One of them quickly stepped forward and slapped the man in the face. The man bent his body and swiftly moved aside, then turned his palm and struck the back of the attacker, followed by kicking him in the head. Another person came forward and was also knocked down, causing four or five others to fall in quick succession. The rest did not dare to follow, and they began to disperse chaotically like birds and beasts, thus breaking the encirclement around the young ladies. At that moment, the onlookers were like a wall, initially thinking the man was weak and reckless, fearing he would not stand up to the crowd. But now that he had won, cheers erupted like thunder. Everyone smiled and whispered to each other, wondering where the young man had come from and how he managed to perform such a bold act. They continuously praised him. His companions then considered the situation, saying, "You only satisfied yourself for a moment, but you have undertaken a noble deed. However, if the children flee in panic and show their anger on their faces, they will surely call for revenge. If the numbers are not equal, what shall we do?" The man replied, "In this situation, one cannot help but act; how could one allow oneself to lose heart! Our current plan is to complete this good deed. I propose an agreement with all of you: do not lose courage, do not have divided loyalties, and we should escort the two girls home together. Even if we encounter them again, we will strike without fail." Everyone agreed, saying, "Yes." It was known that the five people who came with him were all skilled in martial arts.

    514

    中有陈叟者，素号“万人敌”，年近七十，精神矍铄，犹能手格虎豹。馀四人亦各矫捷有胆略。因询知两小固同怀姊弟，女郎年才及笄，含睇腼腆，意甚德君，告明居址。君领两人作前导，女郎姊弟居中，陈叟率两人殿后。行至饮马桥，众无赖果召集百数十人，执梃当路，决眥向君，欲得而甘心焉。陈叟须髯怒张，大声嘱君与四人：“第拥其姊弟，从间道急行，勿却顾。若曹来，有老夫在，当以老拳饱之。”君既送女郎至家，其父母审悉巅末，感涕称谢，款洽甚殷。然匆遽间两忘问姓氏，至今君不知其父母为某某，其父母究亦终不知君为何许人也。众无赖见君等护送女郎去且速，又以陈叟独作后劲，咄咄逼人，料非恒流，气为之夺，竟不敢犯。君尝笑谓人曰：“是役也，固儿时选事，但其时亦恃有陈叟在，吾故敢向前。假令尔时吾亦袖手，则陈叟不肯为力，女郎危矣。”其少时负气好义，类如此。

    Explanation

    514

    In the midst of the journey, there was an old man named Chen, who was always known as "the foe of ten thousand men." He was nearly seventy years old, but he was vigorous and alert, and he could still wrestle tigers and leopards with his bare hands. The other four were also agile and brave. Upon inquiry, it was learned that the two children were originally siblings. The young lady was just of marriageable age, shy and modest, and she had a great deal of gratitude for you. She told you her address. You led two people as guides, while the young lady and her brother were in the middle, and Old Man Chen led two others at the rear. When you reached the Bridge of Drinking Horses, the ruffians indeed gathered more than a hundred people, held sticks and blocked the road, and glared at you with all their might, wanting to get you and take pleasure in it. Old Man Chen's beard and mustache bristled with anger. He loudly instructed you and the four others: "Just protect her and her brother, take the side path and hurry, do not look back. If they come, there is an old man here. I will beat them up thoroughly." After you had safely delivered the young lady to her home, her parents carefully listened to the details, were moved to tears and thanked you, and were very hospitable. However, in the rush, you both forgot to ask for each other's names. Until now, you do not know the names of her parents, and her parents ultimately also did not know who you were. The ruffians saw that you and others were escorting the young lady away quickly, and because Old Man Chen was alone at the rear, they were intimidated. They thought you were not ordinary and lost their courage. They finally dared not to attack. You once laughed and told people: "This mission was indeed a choice of mine when I was a child. But at that time, I also relied on Old Man Chen being there. That's why I dared to go forward. If at that time I had also stood by doing nothing, then Old Man Chen would not have helped. The young lady would have been in danger." Your youthful pride and love of justice were like this.

    515

    里乘子曰：君从李申耆先生游，肆力于诗古文词。向同在高淳营次，相得甚欢。丁巳冬，君摄事巴城，予特造访。时当岁暮，风雪凄然，封篆无事。君素嗜酒，命童燃炉烧烛，手巨觥拈髭纵谈往事，精悍之色，犹见眉宇。今阔别且有十馀年矣，每一忆及之，殊令人神往于苏长公遇方山子时也。
    　　
    　　纪梦
    　　
    　　道光乙巳下，大兴朱子泽刺史甘霖，摄灵璧县事。遇予皖城，执手喜曰：“顷将之任灵璧，考邑志，其地实垓下旧壤。故有虞姬墓，岁久芜圮，将事修治。碑文非燕许不办，请以属子。”予再逊而后诺。爰为骈文一首，邮寄刺史，勒诸贞珉。虽一时盛夸人口，然俳青俪白，体制较卑，寻亦不复记忆。越明年丙午，自金陵秋试归，阻风乌江。时当八月下弦，孤篷岑寂，凭窗露眺，凉风浣襟，残月窥幕，江天一色，荡涤尘虑。心畅神奂，儽然欲寐。忽见一古装美人，媥姺登舟，容采照耀；后侍两婢，亦复媖美。予愕眙避席，不知所措。

    Explanation

    515

    The Master said: "You once studied under Mr. Li Shengqi, exerting yourself in poetry, ancient prose, and literary discourse. We had a very good relationship when we were together in the military camp of Gaochun. In the winter of Ding Si, you were in charge of affairs in Ba City, and I specially visited you. It was the end of the year, with a cold and snowy wind, and there was no urgent official business. You usually love wine, so you ordered the servant to light the stove and burn the candles, took a large cup in hand, twirled your mustache, and freely talked about past events. Your sharp and vigorous expression still showed in your brows and eyes. Now we have been apart for more than ten years, and every time I recall it, it really makes me long for the time when Su Changle met Fang Shanzi."

    Dream Record

    In the year of Yi Si under the Daoguang era, Governor Gan Lin of Daxing, Zhu Zizhe, was in charge of Lingbi County. We met in Wuchang, and he held my hand and said with joy: "I am about to take up my post in Lingbi. I have consulted the county records and found that the land is the old soil of Gaixia. There is the tomb of Yu Ji, which has been neglected for many years. I will renovate it. The inscription for the stele cannot be written without the skills of Yan and Xu, so I would like to entrust it to you." I declined twice before agreeing. I then wrote a parallel prose piece, mailed it to the governor, and had it inscribed on the stone. Although it was highly praised at the time, it was somewhat inferior in style and form, and soon I no longer remembered it. The following year, in the year of Bing Wu, after returning from the imperial examinations in Jinling, I was delayed by a storm in Wujing. It was the waning moon of August, and the solitary boat was quiet. I leaned against the window and looked out at the moonlit view, feeling the cool wind washing over my chest, the crescent moon peering through the curtains, and the sky and river merging into one color, cleansing my worldly worries. My heart was at ease, and my spirit was refreshed, and I was about to fall asleep. Suddenly, I saw a beautiful woman in ancient attire, gracefully boarding the boat, her radiant beauty shining. Behind her were two maids, also very beautiful. I was startled and backed away, not knowing what to do.

    516

    美人乃前敛衽曰：“妾与先生固有文字因缘，故涉嫌就教，休得惊怪。”予亟答拜曰：“一介陬生，伏处穷巷，不省何处得侍天人？所谓文字因缘，傋瞀莫解，请明谕其旨，以开愚窦。”美人笑曰：“妾乃西楚虞姬是也。前朱使君泽及枯骨，知碑文出自椽笔，崇论伟议，使妾读之，千年幽愤，顿为一泄。昨同戚妹往东海寿上元夫人，过我王庙宫，顺道一讯起居，将归瑶池，稔知君舟杈此，特诣谢巨制耳。”予憬然逊曰：“仙姬贞情烈魄，愧咫闻肤见，不能揄扬万一，辱挂齿颊，反增汗颜。”姬曰：“先生勿过撝谦，文信必传，但承褒誉过情，未免感极生愧。妾尤喜叙次论断，多与当年情事符合。方恨与君风马悬隔，晤言无自，今既遘止，良夜正

    Explanation

    516

    The beautiful lady then stepped forward and gathered her sleeves, saying, "I have always had a predestined connection with you through writing, hence I am suspected of seeking your instruction; please do not be surprised." I hastily returned a bow and said, "A mere person from a remote background, living in seclusion in a poor alley, does not know how I could be in the presence of a heavenly being? As for this predestined connection through writing, I am confused and cannot understand it. Please clearly explain its meaning to enlighten the ignorant." The lady smiled and said, "I am the Lady Yu Ji of Western Chu. Formerly, the local official Zhu blessed even the dead, knowing that the inscription on the stele was written by a skilled writer. His highly esteemed discourse and great arguments made me read it, and the thousand-year-old hidden resentment was instantly released. Yesterday, I went with my sister to the Eastern Sea to celebrate the birthday of the Lady of the Upper Yuan, visited my king's temple and palace, and inquired about your well-being on the way. About to return to the Jade Pool, I knew well that your boat was anchored here, so I specially came to thank you for your great work." I suddenly enlightened and humbly said, "The immortal lady, with loyal feelings and a brave spirit, is ashamed of my limited knowledge and superficial understanding. I cannot praise you even a little, but to be mentioned by you, instead, increases my shame." The lady said, "Do not be excessively modest, reliable writing will be passed down. Only receiving excessive praise, cannot help but feel deeply ashamed. I especially like arranging and judging, many of which are consistent with the events of that time. I just regret that I and you are as far apart as wind and horse, with no way to converse. Now that we have met, it is a good night."

    517

    长，愿略将梗端为先生道之，可乎？”予曰：“幸甚。”爰敬展茵榻，肃姬上坐，再三固让，抗礼就席。叩以当年情事，姬蹙然曰：“君所论楚汉之仁暴强弱，毫厘不谬。龙门作史，书以本纪，具有深心。惜我王妇人之仁，犹豫寡断，当日若听妾言，季何能为？”予曰：“奈何？”姬曰：“君固不知，鸿门之计，妾所与谋，不图范玦空举，庄剑无用。既纵季去，亚父恚甚，急趣妾力争于王，且援吴越已事相况，谓勾践一去，夫差恐终不免。王故不乐妾干预军政，比闻妾言，怒视叱曰：‘谁嗾而言？而妇人焉知大事！’君文所谓鸿门之计不行，乌江之祸已伏，恰中当时窾要，使妾至今思之，犹有遗憾。

    Explanation

    517

    "Sir, may I briefly outline the main points for you? Would that be acceptable?" I replied, "That would be most welcome." I then respectfully unrolled the mat and invited the lady to sit in the superior seat. She repeatedly and firmly refused, but finally accepted the seat with equal courtesy. I questioned her about the events of that time, and the lady, with a furrowed brow, said, "Your analysis of the benevolence, cruelty, strength, and weakness of Chu and Han is not even slightly mistaken. The historian of Luo Gate recorded this in the Annals, with a profound intention. Alas, my lord was too kind-hearted, hesitant, and indecisive. If he had listened to my words at that time, how could Xiang Yu have done what he did?" I said, "How so?" The lady replied, "You do not know. The plan at Hongmen was one I had devised. I did not expect Fan Zeng's advice to be in vain, nor Zhang Liang's sword to be of any use. Once Xiang Yu was allowed to go, Xiang Liang was filled with anger and urgently urged me to plead with the king, citing the past rivalry between Wu and Yue as an example, saying that once Gou Jian left, Fuchai would inevitably meet his downfall. The king therefore disliked my involvement in military affairs. When he heard my words, he glared at me and scolded, 'Who prompted you to speak? How could a woman know anything about great matters!' As your writing said, the plan at Hongmen did not proceed, and the calamity at Wujiang was already set in motion — this exactly struck the core of the matter at that time, which is why I still think about it and feel regretful to this day."

    518

    ”予曰：“垓下之战，何遽一败涂地乃尔？仙姬当日身处其境，其何以堪？”姬喟然叹曰：“君言及此，只令人悲。彼日汉兵匼匝，薄暮小雨，黑云如磐，妾待王帐中，方计秣马厉兵，决一死战。夜分忽闻楚歌四起，王拊髀垂涕，顾谓妾曰：“大事去矣！卿将若何？孤悔不听卿言，致有今日。’妾泣慰王曰：‘王但自爱，速自为计。妾荷王厚遇，自有以报，幸勿以妾为念。’王闻妾言，益悲不自胜，乃作垓下之歌，泣以付妾。妾知王意有所授，遂勉和其歌，掣所佩之剑，自刭于王前，以明无贰。”姬语至此，珠泪犹盈盈承睫，不胜悲哽。予亦为之欷歔。但见一侍者出淡红绡帕，前为拭泪；一侍者执碧霞唾盂，前承其唾。

    Explanation

    518

    I said, "The Battle of Gaixia, how could it have ended so utterly in defeat? What could the celestial maiden have endured when she was in that situation?" The maiden sighed deeply and said, "When you speak of this, it only makes one sorrowful. That day, the Han troops surrounded us tightly, and as dusk approached, it began to drizzle lightly. The dark clouds hung heavy like a millstone. I awaited the king in his tent, planning to feed the horses and sharpen the weapons, determined to fight to the death. At midnight, suddenly, the Chu songs rose from all directions. The king stroked his thigh and wept, looking at me and saying, 'All is lost! What shall I do, my lady? I regret not heeding your advice, leading to this day.' I wept and consoled the king, saying, 'May the king take care of himself and quickly make plans. I have received your generous treatment, and I will find a way to repay you. Please do not worry about me.' Upon hearing my words, the king was even more sorrowful and unable to bear it, then composed a song about the Battle of Gaixia, wept, and handed it to me. Knowing that the king had something to impart, I composed a response song, drew my sword, and committed suicide before the king, to show my loyalty without doubt." The maiden spoke to this point, her tears still glistening on her lashes, overwhelmed with sorrow. I also sighed with sadness. I saw a servant come forward with a light red silk handkerchief to wipe her tears, and another servant holding a jade-colored spitoon to catch her spittle.

    519

    予劝慰曰：“仙姬久归仙籍，兴念往事，只合当作他人成败，聊供判论，慎勿过事伤感，有损玉抱。况当日青锋决绝，大节皭然，较息妫、西施等辈，转眼怜人，其薰莸相去，何可以道里计哉！”姬叹息谢曰：“此是先生藻奖，然若曹所为，妾实羞之，宁死不愿效也。”予曰：“不揣再有所请。未审仙姬当日毅然死别，后事犹能知悉否？”姬曰：“妾身虽死，魂固在王左右。王见妾已死，号恸失声。恐人跆藉妾尸，命军校裹以毳旃，舁瘗浅土，王乃独骑决围而走。”予曰：“王所乘骓马，究竟何若？”姬曰：“骓乃神骥，日行千里。先三日前，前蹄忽踅，王恨以为不祥。后所乘似骓实非。

    Explanation

    519

    I have produced the final translation of the source text based on the research and context we gathered. Here is the polished translation:

    I comforted her, saying, "The fairy princess has long returned to her celestial registry. When reflecting on past events, one should only view them as the successes and failures of others, merely for judgment or commentary. Carefully avoid excessive grief, as it would harm the refined nature. Moreover, when she made the resolute decision to part ways, her great virtue remained pure and clear. Compared to figures like Xishui and Xi Shi, who were pitied in a fleeting moment, the difference between good and bad qualities is vast and cannot be measured in terms of distance!" The fairy princess sighed and thanked me, saying, "This is your praise, but what you people do, I truly feel ashamed of it. I would rather die than imitate it." I said, "I dare to ask again. I am unsure if the fairy princess, when she died, was resolute in her decision to part ways, and whether she still knows the subsequent events." The fairy princess replied, "Even though I am dead, my spirit remains by the king's side. The king saw that I was dead and wailed in grief. Fearing that people might trample on my body, he ordered the military officers to wrap my body in a fur blanket, carry it, and bury it in shallow soil. The king then rode alone through the encirclement and fled." I asked, "What exactly is the king's steed, the zui horse, like?" The fairy princess said, "The zui is a divine steed, capable of traveling a thousand miles in a day. Three days before, its front hooves suddenly turned, and the king considered it an unlucky omen. The subsequent horse, although resembling a zui, is not one."

    520

    此亦天意，倘骓足不踅，其涉水如平地，何至及乌江之难？王歌所谓‘时不利兮骓不逝’，正谓此也。”予曰：“王歌激昂慷慨，仙姬和章，必能相敌。今所传五言，恐是赝鼎。”姬曰：“妾歌仓卒失传，正幸藉以藏拙。乃村儒必欲代彰其丑，妄为拟作，不知妾歌虽属急就，固非五言，究亦不足溷大雅之听。”予坚请赐教，姬乃诵曰：“愁云黕墨兮风声悲，楚歌四合兮中心凄。王衣兮前致词，大事已矣兮妾将安归？妾安归兮事已矣，愿王保重兮妾为王死。”诵毕叹曰：“巴里卑音，聊抒哀绪，君其勿哂。”予侧聆默识，深为叹服。二侍者便趣姬行，云恐戚妹久待。姬曰：“先生非外人，良觌匪易，况夜尚未阑，何妨小坐。”予叩七妹为谁，姬曰：“戚妹乃戚夫人，固非七妹。

    Explanation

    520

    This is also the will of heaven. If the horse's hoof had not turned, crossing the water would have been as easy as crossing a flat surface, why would it have reached the difficulty at the Black River? The king's song says, "Unfavorable times, the horse does not advance," which is exactly what this means." I said, "The king's song is passionate and heroic, the fairy princess's response must be able to match it. The five-character verse that is now being passed down, I fear is a fake." The lady said, "My song was lost in a hurry, which is fortunate because it hides my clumsiness. But rural scholars must want to highlight its ugliness, recklessly imitating it. They do not know that my song, though hastily composed, is not a five-character verse, and ultimately it is not worthy of the refined ears." I insisted on asking for her advice, and the lady then recited, "Dark clouds cover the sky, the wind sounds sad. The Chu songs surround, my heart is filled with sorrow. The king, dressed in mourning, speaks to me. The great matter is over, where shall I go? Where shall I go, the great matter is over. I wish the king to take care of himself, I will die for the king." After reciting, she sighed, "It is a lowly sound, briefly expressing sorrowful thoughts, please do not laugh at me." I listened attentively and silently remembered, deeply admiring and respecting her. The two attendants urged the lady to leave, saying that the sister-in-law had been waiting for a long time. The lady said, "Sir, you are not an outsider, a good meeting is not easy, and the night is not yet late, why not have a short rest." I asked who the seventh sister is, and the lady said, "The sister-in-law is the sister-in-law, it is not the seventh sister."

    521

    ”予曰：“何不偕来？”曰：“以君文讥为人彘，故羞与相见。”予曰：“此乃吕后悍妒所致，史臣笔之于书，并非小生唐突。”姬曰：“固然。但渠素腆弱，妾亦不得相强。”予曰：“仙姬何独与彼同行？”姬曰：“妾前身本王母第九女，渠乃阿母侍儿。既先后同婴尘网，各历一番苦趣，再返天曹，遂略除前分，齿以姊妹。”予曰：“仙姬此后曾再诞人世否？”曰：“上帝念妾无辜，应得为后，以赎前恨，曾一降世。”予问：“何朝？”曰：“在唐。”问：“为谁后？”姬羞不答。坚叩之，乃答曰：“武后。”予辗然曰：“武后生平所为，较仙姬判若两人，曾自知否？”姬叹曰：“软尘一踏，本性便迷，后果前因，茫无省忆。

    Explanation

    521

    I said, "Why not come together?" She replied, "Because your writings have insulted me as a 'pig,' I am ashamed to meet you." I said, "This was due to the violent jealousy of Empress Lu, and it was recorded by historians, not that I was being presumptuous." She said, "Indeed. But he is usually timid and weak, and I cannot force him." I asked, "Why do you, a fairy, travel with him?" She replied, "I was originally the ninth daughter of the Queen Mother of the West, and he was a servant of the Queen Mother. We both became entangled in the world's affairs, each experiencing a period of hardship. After returning to the heavenly realm, our previous relationship was somewhat diminished, and we are now considered sisters." I asked, "Did you, a fairy, ever give birth again in the human world?" She said, "The Heavenly Deity remembered my innocence and should have made me a queen to atone for past grievances, and I once descended to the human world." I asked, "In which dynasty?" She replied, "In the Tang." I asked, "As the queen of whom?" She was too embarrassed to answer. I persisted in asking, and she finally replied, "Empress Wu." I smiled and said, "Empress Wu's actions in life were very different from your current self, did she ever realize this?" She sighed and said, "Once stepping into the dust of the world, one's true nature becomes confused, and the consequences and causes are completely without understanding or memory."

    522

    ”予曰：“今天曹尚别有一武后否？”姬曰：“有之，替换托生，各为尔我，譬如树之分植，一树可分数树，一身亦可分数身。即妾而论，妾自一人，阿母之九女又自一人，武后又自一人，顾各具一形，即各赋一性，亦由一树所分枝叶，疏密斜整，终各不同耳。”予为首肯。因笑问曰：“武后为人，不类仙姬，却略似吕后。未审仙姬在日，曾与吕后相见否？”姬笑曰：“岂惟相见，渠尝留我王后宫，乐不思汉。妾鄙其为人，劝王纵之。”予笑曰：“得毋樛木盛德，有所难容？”姬曰：“非也。妾固能容渠，渠反不能容妾也。”予曰：“身后曾受赤眉之辱，信有之否？”姬曰：“此事固不足信。渠殁时齿已濒衰，距新莽二百馀年，纵使朽骨如生，亦非昭妙。

    Explanation

    522

    I said, "Does the current Cao family have another Wu Zetian?" The fairy replied, "Yes, there is. Through reincarnation, they become separate individuals, each their own self, much like planting trees where one tree can be divided into several, and one body can be divided into several. Speaking of myself, I am one person, my mother's nine daughters are another person, and Wu Zetian is yet another. Each has its own form and is given a different nature, just as the branches of a tree, dense, sparse, slanted, or straight, are all different in the end." I nodded in agreement. Then I laughed and asked, "Wu Zetian's character is unlike that of a fairy, but somewhat resembles Empress Lü. Did the fairy ever meet Empress Lü while she was alive?" The fairy laughed and said, "Not only did she meet, she once stayed in my palace, enjoying herself so much that she forgot about her original home. I despised her character and advised the king to let her go." I laughed and said, "Is it not because of the virtue of the twisted wood, which cannot accommodate?" The fairy said, "No. I can accommodate her, but she cannot accommodate me." I asked, "After death, did she suffer the humiliation of the Red Eyebrows?" The fairy said, "This matter is not worthy of belief. When she died, her teeth were already near decay, more than two hundred years after Xin Mang. Even if her bones were still alive, it would not be clear or wonderful."

    523

    贼虽淫暴，夫复何图？意者后人恨其所为，造作此言，以快道路传闻之口，未可知也。但君文感叹汉事一段，可谓才人之笔，面面俱到。妾每循诵及此，辄复破涕为笑。”予谢不敢。因叩项王为人何若。姬曰：“平居燕私，雍容退让，有类文士；一着甲胄，便赳赳可畏。”又问：“今王与仙姬皆返仙班，偶一晤对，尚忆及夙昔儿女之私否？”姬面发赤曰：“蜕脱人寰，孽缘尽割，偶一晤对，俨见大宾。倘少涉妄想，一经上帝觉察，又不知谴谪堕落几重尘劫矣。”予深悔失言。因又问：“仙姬佳城，果否有定远葬首之说？”姬曰：“否否。王初瘗妾之地，妾兄田安实知之。汉兵去后，即为迁葬今处。

    Explanation

    523

    The enemy, though cruel and brutal, what else could one expect? Perhaps later generations regret their actions, fabricating these words to satisfy the public's gossip, it is uncertain. However, your writing, which laments the events of the Han dynasty, can truly be called the work of a talented person, thorough and comprehensive. Whenever I read this part, I always stop crying and laugh. I apologize that I cannot accept the compliment. I then asked about Xiang Yu's character. The lady replied, "In daily life, he is graceful and composed, yielding and modest, similar to a scholarly person; once he dons his armor, he becomes majestic and fearsome." I asked again, "Now that both the king and the immortal lady have returned to the ranks of immortals, if you meet occasionally, do you still remember your past romantic feelings?" The lady blushed and said, "Having shed the human world, all karmic ties are severed. Even if we meet occasionally, it is as if we are meeting a respected guest. If there are any wild thoughts, once detected by Heaven, one does not know how many cycles of rebirth one might fall into." I deeply regretted my inappropriate words. I then asked, "Is there a saying about the burial of the head at a fixed place for the immortal lady's fine tomb?" The lady replied, "No, no. The place where the king initially buried me is known only to my brother Tian An. After the Han soldiers left, it was immediately relocated to the current location."

    524

    其时有一侍儿，亦死垓下之难，貌微肖妾，或误为妾首，持以献季，即今定远所葬者是也。重以后人好为傅会，亦何足怪？”姬词锋霅霅，予甚心折。方欲再有所叩，忽听村鸡遥唱，侍儿又前相趣，姬乃兴辞曰：“本愿稍憩，藉罄积愫，缘人天境隔，且有戚妹相待，未便久稽。”爰解珮玉一方，持谓予曰：“此妾在日极所玩弄，珮诸穷，葬时幸未遗失。今以贻先生，聊作润笔。先生珍重。”言讫，率婢珊珊凌空而去。予木立神驰，正深怊怛，忽闻榜人相呼解缆，蘧然惊寤，知为隐几而梦。然残灯明灭，芗泽犹存，果于枕旁检得一玉，长二寸，宽一寸有半，厚盈二米，其色坚润洁白，上锲藻火粉米等形，精致绝伦，的是汉物，不知来自何处，洵可宝也。急援笔记之，以志梦中文字缘也。

    Explanation

    524

    At that time, there was a maidservant who also perished in the calamity at Gaixia, her appearance slightly resembling mine, and some might have mistaken her for my head. She was carried to present to Ji, and that is the one buried by Dingyuan now. Considering that later generations tend to fabricate connections, what is there to be surprised about?" The lady's words were sharp and piercing, deeply impressing me. I was about to ask something else when suddenly I heard a village rooster calling from afar. The servant girl came forward to urge, and the lady then took her leave, saying, "I had wished to rest briefly, to fully express my accumulated feelings, but due to the vast difference between the human and celestial realms, and the presence of my sister-in-law awaiting me, it is not convenient to linger." She then untied a piece of jade, holding it and saying to me, "This was a jade I often played with during my lifetime, worn by the poor, and fortunately it was not lost at the time of burial. Now I present it to you, just as a token of appreciation. Please take care." After she finished speaking, leading the maidservants, she floated away into the air. I stood motionless, my spirit wandering, deeply saddened, when suddenly I heard the boatman calling to untie the ropes. I suddenly woke up in alarm, realizing it was a dream caused by leaning on the table. The lamp was dimming and flickering, and the fragrance still lingered. Indeed, I found a piece of jade by my pillow, two inches long, one and a half inches wide, and two millimeters thick. Its color was hard, lustrous, and white, with patterns of algae, fire, flour, and rice carved on it, exquisitely crafted. It is indeed a Han artifact, but I don't know where it came from. It is indeed a treasure. I quickly wrote it down to record the literary connection in the dream.

    525

    附录：重修虞姬墓碑
    　　
    　　灵璧之南，垓下之旧址也，其东则虞姬之墓在焉。呜呼！烟销白骨，古战场鬼哭时闻；露暴黄肠，幸从人魂归何处？茫茫千载，累累一抔，寻废垒之存亡，恸前朝之成败。重瞳休矣，大王行妇人之仁；执手卷然，贱妾赍英雄之恨。兴念及此，能不悲哉！乡者胜、广倡乱，馀耳景从，竿木皆兵，锋竞炽。言姁恭谨，未分项暴刘仁；意乌猝嗟，方谓楚强汉弱。试逐中原之鹿，可奏肤功；恐诛当道之蛇，转为呓语。而乃羹未分于俎上，剑空舞于筵前，一着棋输，六州错铸。

    Explanation

    525

    Appendix: Inscription on the Rebuilt Tomb of Yu Ji

    To the south of Lingbi is the old site of Gaixia, and to the east of it lies the tomb of Yu Ji. Alas! The bones of the fallen are reduced to ashes, and the ghosts of the ancient battlefield are heard crying; the bones are exposed to the dew, and where does the soul of the fortunate one return? Over thousands of years, a pile of earth, seeking the existence or destruction of the abandoned fortifications, mourning the rise and fall of the previous dynasty. The great king with double pupils has passed away, acting with the compassion of a woman; holding hands, she was sorrowful, bearing the grudge of a hero. Thinking of this, how can one not be sad? In the past, Chen Sheng and Wu Guang led a rebellion, and others followed like echoes, sticks and trees were treated as weapons, the swords and spears were fiercely competing. He spoke with respect and caution, not distinguishing between the cruelty of Xiang and the kindness of Liu; he suddenly sighed, thinking that Chu was strong and Han was weak. To try to chase the deer of Central China, can achieve quick success; fearing the killing of the snake in the path, turned into nonsense. But the soup was not yet divided on the table, the sword was only dancing in front of the banquet, one move lost, six states were wrongly cast.

    526

    经战阵者七十，败北如斯；失子弟者八千，引东何忍。人心既畔，天命有归。盖鸿门之计不行，则乌江之祸已伏。当其汉军僄遫，楚唱慞惶，慨赤手兮难支，唤红颜兮无俚。森严刁斗，吾末如何？憔悴胭脂，谁能遣此？引杯看剑，挥涕牵衣，听震耳之鼓鼙，惨断肠于儿女。拔山力竭，徒嗟骓足难前；略地声哀，赢得蛾眉先殉。噫嘻！可谓难矣！彼夫勾践既报夫差，西施转归范蠡，反颜事虏，伊独何人？向使姬以桃花命薄，逐水东西；柳絮身轻，随风来去，则息妫嫁楚，纵令生子不言；甄后归曹，未免有人平视。

    Explanation

    526

    Seventy times he marched into battle, each time retreating in defeat; eight thousand of his sons and disciples were lost—how could one bear to lead them eastward? When the hearts of the people have turned away, the Mandate of Heaven has found its destined ruler. Had the plan at Hongmen not been carried out, the calamity at the River Wu had already been set in motion. When the Han army surged forward with boldness, the Chu forces sang in fear and confusion; with bare hands, it was hard to resist, and calling out to the red-faced beauty brought no comfort. The watchmen’s drums rang out in strict warning—what could I do? Her face was pale and withered, her rouge still lingering—who could relieve her sorrow? Raising the cup and gazing at the sword, wiping tears while clutching at one’s robes, listening to the deafening war drums, and feeling the heartbreak of children torn apart. His strength to move mountains had been spent, and only lamented that his steed’s hooves could not advance; in conquering lands, his voice was full of sorrow, and in the end, his beautiful wife died before him. Alas! This is indeed difficult! When Goujian had avenged Fuchai, Xi Shi turned to Fan Li—how could she change her face and serve the enemy? Who is she that would do such a thing? Had she been born with the fate of the peach blossom, drifting east and west with the water; like willow fluff, light and carried by the wind—then Xi Gui would have married Chu, and even if she bore children, she would not speak; Zhen Hou returned to Cao, but it was unavoidable that others would look upon her with equality.

    527
