    而乃饮刃计决，匪石心坚，拼一死所以报恩，庶千秋斯无遗憾。原情略迹，在天可配英皇；国破家亡，入地不同褒妲。君子谓：“姬贞而有操，烈而不污。”谅哉！嗟乎！金刀运尽，玉匣尸寒，王业同霸气俱销，尺地与一民安在！戚呼人彘，生罹熏耳之灾；吕号野鸡，死受赤眉之辱。以视姬之就义凛凛，伸志昭昭，完大节于生前，留清名于殁后者，其得失为如何耶？或谓定远之南，亦有姬墓，彼葬其首，此葬其身。花歌草舞，傅会有之；头岱腹嵩，荒唐颇甚。间尝考其图史，按其山川，知仓皇遇敌之时，正宛转捐生之处。

    Explanation

    527

    But she resolved to die by the sword, with a heart as unyielding as stone, sacrificing her life to repay a favor, hoping that for a thousand autumns there would be no regrets. Examining the original intent rather than the details, in heaven she could be matched with the heroic kings; with the nation destroyed and the family lost, her death was not the same as being praised like Bao and Daji. The gentleman said, "Ji was steadfast and had integrity, loyal and not tainted." Indeed! Alas! The golden blade's fortune is exhausted, the jade box is cold, the imperial cause and the aura of power both fade away, where is a foot of land and a single subject? The lament of being treated as a pig, suffering the calamity of being burned alive; Lü is called a wild chicken, dying and receiving the humiliation of the Red Eyebrows. To see Ji's act of righteousness with awe, her aspirations are clear and bright, completing great virtues in life, leaving a clear name after death — what is the gain or loss? Some say that south of Dingyuan, there is also a tomb of Ji, that place buries her head, this place buries her body. Flowers sing, grass dances, there is a connection; head on Tai, belly on Song, quite absurd. I have occasionally examined the maps and histories, investigating the mountains and rivers, knowing that in the moment of encountering the enemy in panic, it was also the place where she gave up her life in a graceful manner.

    528

    金钿委地，指故壤之未湮；紫玉成烟，信佳城之不远。窃恐星霜屡易，瓦砾交丛，石发既滋，溪毛莫荐耳。今令尹大兴朱公，凭吊芳徽，主持韵事。披榛扫径，伐石坚茔，酹旨酒以招魂，征新诗而表烈，不使阿环罗袜获见人间，庶几玉奴金钗永藏地下。江山无恙，风月自佳，茂草遍锄，野花如绣。香埋净土，青冢则怨异明妃；墨洒新碑，黄绢则词惭幼妇。舒伯鲁郎中焘评云：“偶效南朝徐庾体，娓娓可诵。至赞姬好处，原是平心之论，虞兮有知，当感泣地下，为君一作楚舞也。”王研云学博宝仁评云：“笔意大似陈伽陵，而排偶之中，畅发议论，又伽陵所不及。”

    Explanation

    528

    Golden diadems scattered on the ground, pointing to the ancient soil that has not yet been buried; purple jade turning into smoke, confirming the nearness of the good tomb. I fear that the stars and frost have changed repeatedly, tiles and rubble intermingled, stone moss has already grown, and stream grass cannot be offered. Now, the Lord Magistrate Zhu Gong, who is greatly esteemed, mourns the fragrant traces and takes charge of elegant affairs. He clears thorns and sweeps the path, carves stone and strengthens the tomb, pours wine to summon the soul, and gathers new poems to commemorate the heroic. He does not allow Ahuan's embroidered socks to be seen in the human world, hoping that the jade slave and golden hairpin are forever buried underground. The mountains and rivers are unharmed, the wind and moon are naturally beautiful, lush grass is everywhere cleared, and wildflowers are like embroidery. Fragrance is buried in the pure earth, the green tomb is filled with the resentment of the Mingfei; ink is sprinkled on the new stele, the yellow silk is filled with words that make the young wife ashamed. Shu Bero Lu Langzhong Tao commented, "Occasionally imitating the style of the Southern Dynasties' Xu and Yu, it can be recited smoothly and fluently. As for praising Ji's merits, it is a fair and balanced opinion. If Yu knew, he would feel moved and weep in the underworld, and perform a dance of Chu for you." Wang Yanyun Xue Bo Bao Ren commented, "The spirit is similar to Chen Galing, and in the midst of parallel couplets, he freely expresses his views, which is something Galing could not do."

    529

    里乘卷八
    　　
    　　小卫玠
    　　
    　　山右卫生，世家子也。儿时颖悟过人，温其如玉。十三岁应童子试，学使者命题面试，赏其文，以为神童，拔冠一军。且谓其学校官曰：“此小卫玠也，异日当清贵，可善视之。”由是小卫玠之名，噪于一黉。遐迩皆倾慕之，愿妻以女。生益自负，择配殊不肯草草。顾幼失恃怙，而家甚寒，年将弱冠，逑好尚虚。生攻苦弥笃，自以为青紫唾手可得，但有千钟粟，何患无颜如玉也。邑郦翁者，家称素封。有女珊柯，年及笄，美而慧，女红之馀，酷嗜翰墨，翁与人酬应书札，类皆倩女代笔。翁益爱怜之，尝夸谓戚党曰：“我家有埽眉才子，若开闺阁科，何患不状元及第耶？”坐是，遴选东床，颇难如愿。一日，珊柯与嫂五台礼佛归，途中适与生遇。珊柯秋波频睨，情殊惓惓。嫂窥其意，悄谓之曰：“小姑知此人乎？此即乡里所称小卫玠者是也。渠与家兄为社友，过从甚密，故识之。小姑如有意，当使兄为执柯。

    Explanation

    529

    Volume Eight of Li Cheng

    　　The Little Wei Jie

    　　Wei Sheng from Shanxi is a scion of a distinguished family. As a child, he was extraordinarily intelligent and insightful, gentle and refined like jade. At the age of thirteen, he took the imperial examination for young scholars. The provincial examiner set questions and conducted an oral examination, praising his writing, calling him a prodigy, and declaring him the best among all. He also told the school officials, "This is the Little Wei Jie, who will surely achieve a high and honorable position in the future. Treat him well." Thus, the name Little Wei Jie became well-known throughout the school. People far and near were all admiring him, wishing to marry their daughters to him. Wei Sheng became even more self-assured and was very particular about choosing a spouse. However, he had lost his parents at a young age, and his family was very poor. By the time he was approaching adulthood, his prospects for marriage were still uncertain. He studied even more diligently, believing that high-ranking positions were easily attainable, and with wealth, there was no need to worry about finding a beautiful and virtuous wife. In the county, there was a man named Li, whose family was known for their wealth. He had a daughter named Shan Ke, who was of marriageable age, beautiful and intelligent. In her spare time from her needlework, she had a deep love for writing. Her father often wrote letters to others, which were usually written by her. Her father loved and pitied her even more, and once boasted to his relatives, "My daughter is a talented woman, if there were a civil service examination for women, she would surely be the top scorer." Because of this, it was very difficult to find a suitable match for her. One day, Shan Ke returned from worshipping at the Wuta Temple with her sister-in-law. On the way, she happened to meet Wei Sheng. Shan Ke frequently glanced at him with her eyes, showing a deep affection. Her sister-in-law noticed her feelings and quietly told her, "Do you know this person, little sister? He is the Little Wei Jie, the one the villagers call. He is a close friend of my brother, and they have a very close relationship, so he knows him. If you are interested, I will ask my brother to act as a matchmaker."

    530

    ”珊柯红晕于颊，笑而不答。既归，思恋綦切，饮食俱废。嫂固与珊柯善，不时省问，珊柯喟然低谓嫂曰：“我亦不解何故。他日归后，魂魄若失，似此恹恹，恐非佳兆也。”嫂戏慰之曰：“小姑得毋为小卫玠乎？果尔，得谐伉俪，的是嘉耦。当即风示渠，央吾兄来，请命于翁，无不谐也。但其人才丰境啬，家徒壁立，与相如等，未审小姑患贫不？”珊柯叹曰：“实告嫂氏，妹筹之已熟，命好，贫亦可富；不则富亦可贫。富贵在天，有命存焉，何患贫之与有。惟嫂氏图之。”嫂笑曰：“如此，易矣。小姑请自保重，不三日，必有以报命。”珊柯大喜，厥疾顿瘳。里有某公子者，父官粤东太守，卒于任，宦囊充牣，公子扶榇归，服阕。年甫十八，适聘妻某氏病卒，耳珊柯名，倩媒求婚于翁。翁慕公子门第多财，遽喜诺之。嫂闻之，知难挽回，乃备告珊柯，且婉劝之曰：“非嫂方命，奈翁已许公子，虽智如良平，亦难为计。

    Explanation

    530

    Sanchao's cheeks flushed with a rosy hue, and she smiled without responding. After returning home, she longed intensely, stopping eating and drinking altogether. Her sister-in-law, who was close to Sanchao, visited her frequently. Sanchao sighed and whispered to her sister-in-law, "I don't understand why. After returning home, it's as if my soul has left my body, and I feel so listless. It's probably not a good omen." Her sister-in-law playfully comforted her, saying, "Isn't it you, little sister, who looks like the beautiful Wang Rong? If so, finding a good match would be a perfect pair. I'll let him know, and I'll ask my brother to come and request permission from your father-in-law. There's no doubt it will work out. But his talent is great, but his circumstances are meager, and his family is poor, like Sima Xiangru. I wonder if you mind being poor?" Sanchao sighed and said, "Let me tell you, sister-in-law. I've thought it through. If my fate is good, poverty can turn into wealth; otherwise, wealth can turn into poverty. Wealth and honor are determined by heaven, and there is a destiny involved. Why worry about poverty? Just leave it to you to figure it out." Her sister-in-law laughed and said, "If that's the case, it's easy. Little sister, please take care of yourself. Within three days, I'll have something to report back." Sanchao was greatly pleased, and her illness suddenly disappeared. In the neighborhood, there was a certain young man whose father was the governor of Guangdong and died in office, leaving his treasury full of wealth. The young man brought his father's coffin back home and completed the mourning period. He was just eighteen years old when his fiancée, a certain lady, died of illness. Hearing of Sanchao's name, he asked a matchmaker to propose marriage to her father. The father admired the young man's family status and wealth and quickly agreed. Upon hearing this, her sister-in-law knew it was difficult to change, so she fully informed Sanchao and gently advised her, "It's not my fault. Your father-in-law has already promised the young man. Even if we were as wise as Zhang Liang and Han Xin, it would be difficult to devise a plan."

    531

    闻公子少年才貌，亦不减小卫玠，况门第家道，有过之而无不及。可见婚姻大事，自有天定，人力断不能与造化角胜。似此天作之合，未尝非福，小姑亦何必甲彼乙此，沾沾然成心不化哉！”珊柯闻之，无可如何，只得惟父命是听。亡何，吉期已届，公子亲迎，演剧宴宾，备极华侈。夜分客散，入房更衣，复出自私，突有人掩出其后，以刀洞其胸而殪之。其人疾入房，吹灭灯烛，知珊柯坐床上，入帏遽抱求欢。珊柯以为公子，骇问：“君何为者，如此卤莽？”其人低答曰“我、我非公子也，乃小、小卫玠也。感、感汝意，特、特来谢。”珊柯惊曰：“公子行且至矣！宜速去，毋使两有不便。”其人曰：“公、公子我、我已刃之矣，可请放心。”珊柯骇曰：“汝言真耶？”曰：“那、那敢相诳。”珊柯顿足失声大哭曰：“汝累我矣！奈何！”其人知不可犯，又恐人至，急摸取珊柯髻上簪，出房拔关窜去。

    Explanation

    531

    Hearing that the young gentleman is both talented and handsome, no less than the young Wei Jie, and considering his family background and household affairs, which surpass all others, it is clear that marriage is a major affair, determined by heaven. Human effort cannot compete with the power of nature. Such a match made in heaven is not a bad thing; why does the young wife have to compare this one to that one, being so fixated and unyielding? Shan Ke, upon hearing this, could do nothing but obey her father's command. Soon after, the auspicious date arrived, and the young gentleman personally came to fetch her, entertaining guests with performances and feasts, all in great splendor. At midnight, the guests departed, and she went into the room to change clothes. She came out again from her private quarters, and suddenly someone came up behind her, stabbing her in the chest and killing her. The person quickly entered the room and blew out the lamps. Knowing that Shan Ke was sitting on the bed, he rushed in and tried to embrace her. Shan Ke, thinking it was the young gentleman, was shocked and asked, "Who are you? Why are you so reckless?" The person whispered, "I, I am not the young gentleman. I am the small, small Wei Jie. I am grateful for your intentions, so I specially came to thank you." Shan Ke was surprised and said, "The young gentleman will be here soon! You should leave quickly, so as not to cause any inconvenience." The person replied, "The young gentleman, I have already killed him. Please rest assured." Shan Ke was horrified and asked, "Is what you say true?" The person denied, "How could I lie to you?" Shan Ke stamped her foot and burst into tears, saying, "You have ruined me! What shall I do?" Knowing he could not force her, and fearing that others would arrive, he quickly grabbed Shan Ke's hairpin from her hair, unlocked the door, and fled.

    532

    比婢媪等闻珊柯哭声，各秉烛来问讯，见珊柯披发汗喘，惨无人色，争前问故。珊柯具告所以，众大惊，急出觅公子，果见洞胸，僵仆地下，血流遍地。须臾，合宅男妇毕集，公子两弟未及成童，抚尸大哭。平明，具状诉于邑宰。词内并牵连珊柯，保无知情。邑宰即率伍伯来验，时当暮春，见公子身着短袷，俯仆地上，果系出其不意，被刺身死。乃拘生与珊柯，分别研讯。珊柯哭称素与生漫不相识，实不知情。

    Explanation

    532

    The maidservants heard Shan Ke's weeping and each, holding a candle, came to inquire. They saw Shan Ke with her hair loose, sweating, and panting, pale and ghastly. Rushing forward, they asked the reason. Shan Ke fully explained the cause, and the crowd was greatly alarmed, hurrying out to look for the young master. They indeed saw a chest pierced, lying stiff and fallen on the ground, with blood flowing all over the ground. Soon, all the men and women of the household gathered. The young master's two younger brothers, not yet of age, wept over his body. At dawn, they prepared a complaint and reported it to the county magistrate. The complaint also implicated Shan Ke, guaranteeing that she was unaware. The county magistrate immediately led the guards to inspect. It was late spring, and they saw the young master, wearing a short jacket, lying face down on the ground. Indeed, it was unexpected, and he was killed by a stab wound. Then they arrested the perpetrator and Shan Ke, interrogating them separately. Shan Ke cried, claiming she had never known the perpetrator, truly unaware of the matter.

    533

    及至讯生，生素未登公堂，骤见有司厉声叱诘，仓猝莫知所对，情殊惶恐。邑宰信以为真，遽请褫革巾衿，备加榜虐。生不堪酷刑，遂致承服。爰书既订，直延颈以待秋决。珊柯虽不知情，以事出有因，亦不能遽释出狱。会按察某公，由牧令起家，陈臬晋省。虑囚及生，颇疑其冤，思为平反，而苦不得法。夜梦一人，持铜镜一枚掷地，碎其半而存其半，歌曰：“铜镜如月，半明即灭。先缺后圆，先圆不缺。”寤后，寻绎梦兆，味后二语，忽有所触。乃授计狱吏，净除一室，备设床帏衾枕，纵生与珊柯聚处其中，以察其情形以告。

    Explanation

    533

    When the prisoner was interrogated, he had never before appeared in court and was suddenly faced with the officials' stern rebuke and questioning. In a panic, not knowing how to respond, he was extremely frightened. The county magistrate believed it to be true and immediately requested the removal of his scholar's cap and gown, subjecting him to severe beating. The prisoner could not bear the harsh punishment and thus confessed. The official record was completed, and he passively waited for execution in autumn. Shan Ke, though innocent, was involved in the matter, and because there was a reason behind the matter, the magistrate could not immediately release him from prison. Coincidentally, Governor [Name], who rose from the position of magistrate and was appointed governor of the province, came across the prisoner when reviewing prisoners. He suspected the prisoner was innocent and intended to clear his name, but struggled to find a way to do so. That night, he dreamed of a person who held a copper mirror and threw it to the ground, breaking it into two halves, one of which remained intact. The person sang, "The copper mirror is like the moon, half bright and half dark. First broken, then whole; first whole, never broken." After waking, he pondered the meaning of the dream, focusing on the last two lines, and suddenly had an idea. He then instructed the jailer to clean a room and prepare a bed with sheets and pillows, allowing the prisoner and Shan Ke to stay together in the room to observe their situation and report back.

    534

    狱吏如命，并具肴酒，延二人至而告之曰：“公子一案，业已定谳。怜汝二人实一双嘉耦，生离死别，近在指日，特具不腆，聊与稍叙决绝，勿却为幸。”二人俱再三固辞，狱吏笑曰：“我不过一片哀矜之心，并无他意，请勿疑虑，决不使长官知之。”言讫，钥扉径去。初，五台途中之遇，珊柯因嫂知生，而生固不曾留意于珊柯也。自公子狱起，生以为珊柯素无仇隙，意外横遭诬陷，殊深抱恨；至珊柯虽有意于生，自遭公子之变，颇恨生凶暴，前念顿灰。兹被钥于一室，彼此相视，未免心动。珊柯见生温文尔雅，似非杀人之人；即或如此，亦因爱我起见，我幸免祸，而彼指日即正典刑，究竟与我毫无所染，徒丧厥生，情殊可悯，一种怜生之心，不觉时形于色。生虽尝耳珊柯艳声，未曾见面，即狱起，偶与对质，更不敢公然平视，今近在咫尺，细意领略，果信名不虚传。

    Explanation

    534

    The prison official followed orders, prepared a feast of dishes and wine, and invited the two people, telling them, "The case of the young master has already been decided. I pity you two as a pair of excellent partners, facing a separation of the living from the dead, which is imminent. I have specially prepared a modest offering to briefly discuss our parting. Do not refuse, it is fortunate." The two people repeatedly refused firmly. The prison official smiled and said, "I have only a heart full of compassion, with no other intention. Please do not be concerned, and I will definitely not let the superior know." After speaking, he locked the door and left.

    Initially, their encounter on the Wutai Mountain road, Shan Ke learned about the young master through her sister-in-law, but the young master had never paid attention to Shan Ke. Since the young master's case arose, the young master thought that Shan Ke had no grudge against him and was unexpectedly falsely accused, deeply regretting it. As for Shan Ke, although she had feelings for the young master, after encountering the young master's misfortune, she greatly resented his cruelty, and her previous thoughts were instantly extinguished. Now, locked in a room together, they looked at each other, and could not help but feel moved. Shan Ke saw that the young master was gentle and refined, seeming not to be a person who kills. Even if he were, it would be because of his love for her. She was lucky to avoid disaster, while he would be executed in a few days, and ultimately, he was not involved in any way, only losing his life, the situation was particularly pitiable. A kind of sympathy for the young master often showed in her expressions. Although the young master had heard of Shan Ke's beauty before, he had never met her. Even when the case arose, they were occasionally confronted, and he dared not look at her openly. Now, close at hand, he carefully appreciated her, and indeed, her reputation was well-deserved.

    535

    因忆狱吏之言，死期伊迩，与其徒坐虚名，不如暂图实乐，纵正典刑，死亦无憾。乃强颜向前，揖珊柯而叹曰：“小生与娘子平日并无仇隙，一旦横遭诬陷，果何故耶？”珊柯腼腆久之，叹曰：“君所作事，君自知之。妾纵不无怜君之心，然杀人者抵，有国法在，于妾何尤也！”生叹曰：“卿今日尚以为杀人者真小生耶？以卿视小生，力乏缚鸡，岂能杀人者耶？卿既苦口相坐，百喙难解。

    Explanation

    535

    Recalling the words of the prison official, I realize that the time of my death is near. Rather than sitting idly on a false reputation, I might as well seek real joy, even if I am subjected to proper punishment; there would be no regret in death. Thus, I forced a smile and faced forward, bowing to Shan Ke and saying, "We have never had any enmity, yet I have suddenly and unjustly been accused. What is the reason for this?" Shan Ke was embarrassed for a long time before sighing, "You know what you have done. Even though I may have some sympathy for you, the law demands that the killer be punished, and what does that have to do with me?" I sighed, "Do you still believe that the killer is truly me? If you look at me, I am too weak to even tie up a chicken, how could I possibly be the killer? You speak harshly and sit in judgment, but a hundred mouths cannot explain this."

    536

    但枉被虚名，心实不甘！卿如慈悲，俾得一亲肌肤，死亦瞑目矣！”言讫，便拉珊柯求欢。珊柯闻生所言，心甚凄然，雅不欲拒，解衣并寝，曲尽绸缪。事已，珊柯问曰：“始君口吃，而狐臭之气刺鼻，今何不尔也？”生笑曰：“小生向无此疾。卿何所见而云然也？”珊柯因述曩日公子被害后，其人灭烛入帏，所闻实系如此。“然则果非君耶？”生叹曰：“事已至此，想是夙冤。今蒙卿见怜，复何尤怨！”珊柯又将五台归后，如何抱恙，如何与嫂同谋，历历为生具述一过。生不胜感激，叹曰：“小生缪承错爱，若非娘子述及，至死不知。

    Explanation

    536

    But I am unjustly burdened with a false reputation, and my heart truly resents it! If you show compassion, allow me to touch your skin once, and even if I die, I will be at peace!” Having said this, he pulled Shan Ke and sought her favor. Shan Ke heard what he said and was very sorrowful in her heart, naturally not wishing to refuse, so she undressed and lay down together with him, showing the utmost affection and care. After the deed was done, Shan Ke asked, “At first, you stuttered, and the fox stench was offensive, why is it not so now?” The man laughed and said, “I have never had this ailment. What did you see to say such a thing?” Shan Ke then recounted the events of the past when the young master was harmed, and the person extinguished the candle and entered the curtain, what he heard was indeed like that. “Then, was it not you?” The man sighed and said, “The matter has come to this, it must be an ancient grudge. Now that you have shown me mercy, what more grievances or resentments can there be!” Shan Ke then told the man in detail about her return from Wutai, how she fell ill, and how she conspired with her sister-in-law. The man was overwhelmed with gratitude and sighed, “I have mistakenly received your affection, if not for your lady mentioning it, I would never have known.”

    537

    然此中消息，得毋漏泄，俾奸人乘机假冒，致嫁祸于小生乎？”珊柯叹曰：“闺房秘语，人何由知？信如君言，果系含冤。然爰书既订，料难平反。君如屈死，妾誓相从九原，必不独生也！”二人喁喁絮语，狱吏潜听甚晰，一一转达于公。公笑曰：“得之矣。”亟密召郦翁至，问曰：“汝家侮甬来往人等，有口吃而狐臭者乎？”翁沉思久之，对曰：“平日来往人等，惟衣工金二朋者如此。”公曰：“是矣。”亟飞签拘金至。

    Explanation

    537

    "But will this secret not be leaked, allowing wicked people to take advantage and impersonate, leading to the blame being placed on me?" Shan Ke sighed and said, "Secret words from the inner chambers, how can people know? If indeed it is a case of injustice, as you say. But the official record is already made, it is likely to be difficult to overturn. If you die unjustly, I swear to follow you to the netherworld, I will definitely not live alone!" The two of them talked intimately, and the jailer listened secretly and clearly, and relayed everything to the official. The official smiled and said, "I have it." Immediately, he secretly summoned Lishi to come and asked, "Do you have anyone who comes and goes in your household with a stutter and a fox-like odor?" After a long time of thought, Lishi replied, "Those who usually come and go, only the tailor Jin Erpeng is like that." The official said, "Yes." Quickly, he issued a warrant to arrest Jin.

    538

    公见其气象猛鸷，料非良善，据案叱曰：“汝杀某公子，嫁名卫生，何也？”金固口吃，闻公言，顿惊失色，口中喃喃，犹欲强辩。公叱左右搜其身，果得质券一纸，赎取验之，即珊柯当日髻上簪也。公笑拈以示金曰：“赃物已得，汝犹欲辩耶？”叱令痛笞之，果吐其实。先是，金幼从师学为衣工，在翁家制衣。比长，所业甚精，翁家男妇衣，大半皆金手制。及珊柯长成，所需衣裙，非金制不着。金尝闻珊柯美，恨未一见。珊柯偶往省妗氏，金窃窥之，不禁狂喜。
    以珊柯衣非己制不着，谬幸于己有缘，时萌妄想。有某媪者，向在翁家服役，固与金有私。前珊柯与嫂所谋卫生一事，不料媪属垣有耳，闻之，戏述于金。金久欲图珊柯，正苦无当，闻媪言，陡生恶计：乘公子亲迎，潜入其宅，拚为孤注一掷，计杀公子，假冒生名，当可遂愿；即不然，嫁祸于生，己亦可脱然无累。至是，尽吐其实。以金坐抵，而生冤以白。

    Explanation

    538

    The official saw the fierce and menacing demeanor of the man and suspected that he was not a good person. Sitting behind his desk, he shouted, "You killed a certain nobleman and pretended to be Wei Sheng, why did you do that?" The man named Jin, who had a speech impediment, heard the official's words and suddenly turned pale with shock. He mumbled, still trying to defend himself. The official ordered his attendants to search the man's body and indeed found a bond or contract. He redeemed and examined it, and it turned out to be the hairpin that Sanchi had in her hair at the time. The official smiled, picked it up, and showed it to Jin, saying, "The evidence has been found. Do you still want to defend yourself?" He then ordered Jin to be severely beaten, and the man finally confessed the truth.

    Previously, Jin had learned to be a tailor when he was young and worked in Old Master's family, making clothes for the men and women. By the time he grew up, his work became very skilled, and most of the clothes in Old Master's family were made by Jin. When Sanchi grew up, the clothes she needed were only worn if made by Jin. Jin had heard that Sanchi was beautiful and regretted not having seen her. One day, Sanchi went to visit her aunt, and Jin secretly watched her, unable to help but be overjoyed.

    Because Sanchi only wore clothes made by Jin, he mistakenly hoped that he had a connection with her and often had wild thoughts. There was a certain old woman who used to serve in Old Master's family and had a private relationship with Jin. She overheard what Sanchi and her sister-in-law planned about pretending to be Wei Sheng and told Jin about it. Jin had long wanted to get close to Sanchi but was frustrated because he had no way to do so. After hearing the old woman's words, he suddenly came up with a wicked plan: taking advantage of the occasion when the nobleman was being escorted, he would sneak into his house, risk everything, and plan to kill the nobleman and pretend to be him, which would fulfill his wish. If not, he would shift the blame onto the nobleman and escape without any consequences. By this point, he finally confessed everything. Jin was punished, and the nobleman's innocence was cleared.

    539

    公念生无罪，几陷大辟，诃责邑宰及承讯各官，使为媒，以珊柯妻生；并罚醵资助奁，资生膏火，以赎其愆。闻者无不啧啧称颂。公始悟梦兆铜镜掷碎其半者，仅存二金字也；歌词“铜镜如月，半明即灭”者，月合半明，为朋字，统合之，金二朋即灭也；后二语，谓生与珊柯当为夫妻，先有缺陷，而后团圆；然必先暂使团圆，而后乃无缺陷，故曰“先缺后圆，先圆不缺”也。公初授计狱吏，固是揣度结句而有所触，不谓果以此而获真犯也。生出狱后，喜妻珊柯，又得助醵赀，益发奋下帷，寻联捷。入词馆，秩跻清要。士林传为美谈，足征学使品藻不谬。

    Explanation

    539

    The official considered the person to be innocent and almost sentenced him to death. He rebuked the local magistrate and the officials involved in the case, arranging for Shan Ke to marry the person. He also imposed penalties, raised funds, and helped with the dowry and living expenses to atone for the person's fault. Everyone who heard about it praised it enthusiastically. The official finally understood the dream omen of the bronze mirror being thrown and broken in half, with only two golden characters remaining. The song lyrics said, "the bronze mirror is like the moon, half bright and then extinguished." The moon being half bright formed the character for "friendship," and when combined, two golden characters of friendship would be extinguished. The last two phrases said that the person and Shan Ke should be husband and wife, but there was a defect at first and then they would be reunited. However, they must be temporarily reunited first, and then there will be no defects. Therefore it is said, "first there is a defect, then it is complete, first it is complete, and there is no defect." The official was initially assigned to a prison official and indeed speculated on the concluding phrase and had a realization, not expecting that indeed, for this reason, the real culprit was caught. The person, after being released from prison, was happy with his wife Shan Ke and also received help in raising funds. Even more diligent in studying, he soon achieved success. He entered the literary bureau and advanced to a high and important position. The scholarly community passed it down as a beautiful story, sufficient to prove that the provincial examiner's evaluation was not mistaken.

    540

    里乘子曰：听讼折狱，谈何容易！圣门七十子之贤，独许仲氏，其难可知也。小卫玠之狱，邑宰颟顸锻炼，遽订爰书，使非按察某公设法平反，几何不沉冤莫白。可见天下断无难折之狱。如事关人命，尤不可草草听断；至万分疑难，亦心审慎迟回，澄思渺虑，务求水落石出，不使稍留馀憾。否则，生杀自由，视人命如草菅，漫不加意，彼苍昭昭，难保不无冥报也。某公精诚所格，见于寤寐，授计狱吏，神妙莫测，可以为法。所谓“思之思之，鬼神通之”。观此，足证古人“田中走，东门草”之说不虚也。

    Explanation

    540

    Li Chengzi said: Adjudicating cases is no easy task! Among the seventy disciples of the Sage, only Zeng Shen was praised, which shows how difficult it is. In the case of Wei Jie, the local magistrate acted foolishly and hastily sealed the case record, leading to a deep injustice that remained unexposed. If not for the intervention of a high-ranking official who devised a plan to rectify the situation, how could the truth have been revealed? It is evident that there are no cases that cannot be resolved. When it comes to matters of life and death, one must not make hasty judgments; even in the most difficult and complex cases, one must exercise caution and careful deliberation, striving to uncover the truth so that no lingering regrets remain. Otherwise, treating life and death as one pleases, regarding human life as if it were grass, and showing no concern, the heavens above are clear, and one cannot be sure of avoiding retribution in the afterlife. The official’s sincere heart was so powerful that it was evident even in his dreams, as he provided instructions to the prison officials that were so ingenious and mysterious as to be beyond comprehension, serving as a model for others. As the saying goes, “Think deeply, think deeply, and the spirits will help you.” This demonstrates that the ancient saying, “Running in the fields, grass grows in the east gate,” is indeed true.

    541

    婉姑
    　　
    　　前明世庙时，浙江绍兴某甲，少游京师，学为银工。心性慧黠，所制务出新式，极臻奇巧，一时长安良匠，佥逊谢不逮。以故都中戚畹勋贵及一切仕族，凡闺阁钗饰，非出某手不贵。缘此出入显贵之门，累资数万。甲有妹名婉姑，素所钟爱，年已及笄，姿首妍丽。幼字同里某乙，以贫故，不能至京亲迎；甲又以事繁不得送归，时以为虑。曾有中表弟某孝廉，公车北上，依甲为居停。试毕下第，将归，甲置酒祖饯。数巡后，甲以朱柈盛朱提一函列几上，前再拜致词曰：“仆有心事，思之数年，未得其人，今幸得吾弟，此愿可了。吾弟少年豪俊，且系至诚君子，倘荷允诺，乃敢毕其词。”孝廉见甲情词恳切，答曰：“我尔骨戚，如力所能任，自当如命，义何敢辞。

    Explanation

    541

    Wan Gu
    During the reign of Emperor Jiajing in the Ming Dynasty, a young man from Shaoxing, Zhejiang, traveled to the capital to learn silversmithing. He was intelligent and clever, always striving for novelty in his creations, reaching the highest level of exquisite craftsmanship. All the skilled craftsmen in Chang'an humbly admitted they could not match him. Because of this, he frequented the homes of the wealthy and powerful, accumulating wealth in the tens of thousands. The young man had a sister named Wan Gu, whom he deeply loved. She had reached the age of marriageable maturity and was beautiful. She was engaged in childhood to someone from the same village, but due to poverty, he could not come to the capital for the wedding. Due to busy affairs, the young man could not send her back, and people at the time were concerned about it.

    Once, a cousin who passed the imperial examinations and traveled north by official carriage stayed with him as a guest. After the exams, he failed to pass and was about to return home, so the young man held a farewell banquet. After several rounds of drinking, the young man placed a red wooden tray with a box of silver on the table and bowed twice before speaking. "I have something on my mind that I have thought about for several years, but did not find the right person. Now I am fortunate to have my younger brother, and this wish can be fulfilled. My younger brother is a young, heroic figure and a sincere gentleman. If you agree to it, then I dare to fully express my thoughts," he said. The cousin, seeing the young man's earnestness, replied, "We are blood relatives, and if I can bear the responsibility, I will certainly follow your command. How could I dare to refuse such a request?"

    542

    ”甲遂以婉姑相托，谓己不能躬送，“今吾弟南旋，敢请挈带归里，就便为之完姻，曷胜感幸！谨具戋戋，聊助资斧，蕲勿以不腆见却。”孝廉感其情亲谊厚，遂毅然允诺。既抵浙，孝廉即留婉姑在家小住数日，涓吉送其于归。某乙惟有老母，婉姑既嫁至乙家，翌日晨兴，见乙与其母皆为人所杀，骈死厨下。大骇喊呼，邻舍毕至，觇验猜疑，互相惊诧，因共鸣官讯究。明府某公，少年科甲，素以精刻自负。勘验毕，先后拘婉姑并孝廉至，廉得同路回籍情事，乃拍案作色厉声曰：“此案不待问，固已瞭如指掌矣！以怨女旷夫，同行数千里，且皆少年美好，旁无一人，谓一路彼此防闲，历数月之久，能始终作鲁男子，吾不信也！”命虔婆验婉姑，果非处女。某公更以自神，益得意曰：“何如？吾言固不谬也。”遽命以严刑相加，惨掠倍至。二人不任箠楚，只得诬服。狱具，论以大辟。

    Explanation

    542

    Wan Gu was then entrusted to me, as I could not personally see her off. Now my younger brother is returning south, and I boldly ask you to take her back to my hometown, and to finalize the marriage while you are at it. How could I not feel deeply grateful! I humbly offer a small gift, to slightly help with expenses, and I hope you will not reject this modest offering. The scholar was moved by his deep affection and friendship, and resolvedly agreed. After arriving in Zhejiang, the scholar immediately kept Wan Gu at his home for a few days, and chose a favorable day to send her back.

    Wan Gu's husband, Mr. Yi, only had an elderly mother. After Wan Gu was married to Mr. Yi's family, the next morning when she woke up, she saw that Mr. Yi and his mother had both been killed by someone, dying together in the kitchen. She was greatly shocked and shouted, and the neighbors all came, investigating and guessing, and were mutually surprised. They then reported to the authorities for investigation.

    The magistrate, a young scholar who had passed the imperial exams, was always proud of his keen insight and judgment. After the examination, he summoned Wan Gu and the scholar to the court. Learning about the details of their journey together, he banged the table, turned red with anger, and spoke sternly: "This case does not need to be questioned; it is already clear as day! With a woman without a husband and a man without a wife, traveling together for thousands of miles, and both being young and beautiful, with no one else around, how could they maintain their chastity for several months, traveling together? I do not believe it!" He ordered a matron to examine Wan Gu, and indeed, she was not a virgin. The magistrate became even more confident and said, "How is it? My words were not wrong, were they?" He immediately ordered severe punishment, and the two suffered even more cruel torture. Unable to bear the beatings, they were forced to falsely confess. The case was concluded, and they were sentenced to death.

    543

    时人亦同声称明府之神，且姗骂婉姑同孝廉人面兽心，有负某甲之托，死不为枉。某甲在京闻之，骇异懊恨，亦以二人之非人类，罪有应得。既又念婉姑自幼相依十馀年，向以礼自守，言笑跬步，不稍苟且；即孝廉为人，亦少年纯谨，边幅甚修，何遽作此蔑礼犯法之事？以此沉吟，疑信不能自决。缘离乡多年，暂将店务倩人督理，自旋展墓，藉侦访此事迹耗。甲故京师名匠，北道大店商贾多与往来。日者至一典店中，正与主人谈次，忽见店伙持金钏一股来，请于主人曰：“此钏制法精巧，因质价太昂，不敢自主，特请命以定去留。”某甲从旁见钏，大惊，泣谓主人曰：“此乃小人女弟于归时赠嫁之物，今幸无意见之，则死者之冤可白矣！”乃具为主人道其原委，请将质钏之人用计留禁。自诣辖邑，鸣鼓上状，饬役拘质钏者至，一讯而服。

    Explanation

    543

    People also praised the magistrate's wisdom, but they mocked and cursed the magistrate and his niece, saying they had the appearance of humans but the heart of beasts, and had betrayed the trust of "某甲," even if they were to die. "某甲," upon hearing this news in the capital city, was shocked, angry, and regretful, and believed that the magistrate and his niece, who were not human, deserved their punishment. However, he also remembered that his younger sister had been his companion for over ten years, and she had always maintained propriety, speaking and laughing with decorum, without any impropriety; the magistrate, too, had been a young man of purity and diligence, with a well-mannered demeanor. How could he suddenly engage in such disrespectful and illegal actions? Thus, "某甲" pondered deeply, unable to decide whether to believe or doubt. Since he had been away from his hometown for many years, he temporarily entrusted the management of his shop to someone else and returned to visit the tomb, using this opportunity to investigate the details of the matter. "某甲" was a renowned craftsman in the capital city, and many merchants and traders from the north frequently interacted with him. One day, he went to a pawnshop and was talking with the owner when suddenly a pawnshop employee came in with a part of a gold bracelet and asked the owner for permission. "某甲," seeing the bracelet, was greatly surprised and wept, saying to the owner, "This is a bracelet that my younger sister gave to me when she married. Now that I have seen it, the dead person's冤 can be cleared!" He then explained the entire story to the pawnshop owner and requested that the person who pawned the bracelet be detained. He then went to the county office and reported the matter publicly, and the authorities arrested the person who pawned the bracelet, and after interrogation, the person confessed.

    544

    先是，某甲以某乙家寒，恐妹嫁去难以治生，遂广制金钏数事，约计千金，以作妆奁之资。质钏人本京师剧贼，探知此事，沿途尾婉姑、孝廉之后，直至浙江。于归日，乙家以贫故，合卺成礼后，诸亲便各自辞归。贼乘人众时，预伏厨下，乙母至厨料检什物，贼暗中突出，以刃挥而殪之；乙闻扑击声，自往烛之，贼又突出刃之，遂将乙衣履更换，秉烛进房。婉姑新至，不辨真伪。就寝后，贼以言婉姑云：“闻汝兄赠嫁有金钏数事，制法精巧，何不出以相示？”婉姑以为己夫也者，乃尽将所有出而献之。贼大喜，佯为称赞不已，又与同寝。天明，瞷婉姑睡熟，尽携所有而遁。贼之所供如此。邑令以状上大府，移知浙省，并以入告。世庙震怒，除贼寸磔外，命将该邑令———即素以精刻自负之某明府，处决论抵。承讯在事各官，自督抚以次，均严加议罚。又特旨婉姑给予旌表建坊；孝廉子给荫入监读书。恩法兼施，存殁均感。然则折狱者慎勿以精刻自负矣！

    Explanation

    544

    Previously, a man named Jia, whose sister was to marry into a poor family, feared that she would find it difficult to support herself after the marriage. Therefore, he manufactured several gold bracelets, valued at about a thousand gold pieces, to serve as part of her dowry. The pawnbroker, who was actually a notorious thief from the capital, discovered this and followed the couple, including their namesake, all the way to Zhejiang Province. On the day of the wedding, due to their poverty, the couple performed the traditional rites of marriage, and after the ceremony, the relatives all left. The thief, taking advantage of the crowded moment, hid in the kitchen beforehand. When the mother of person Yi went into the kitchen to check on the preparations, the thief suddenly emerged from the darkness and struck her down with a blade. Hearing the commotion, person Yi went to investigate and was also attacked by the thief, who then took off Yi’s clothes and shoes, replaced them with his own, and entered the bedroom with a candle. The newly arrived woman, unaware of the deception, did not realize the truth. After they went to bed, the thief spoke to her, saying, "I heard that your brother gave you several gold bracelets, crafted with great skill. Why don’t you show them to me?" Mistaking him for her husband, she revealed all the bracelets to him. The thief was delighted and pretended to praise her continuously, then shared the bed with her. At dawn, when he saw that she was asleep, he took all the bracelets and fled. This is what the thief confessed. The county magistrate reported the incident to the provincial government, which in turn informed the imperial court. The emperor was greatly angered and ordered the thief to be executed by dismemberment. He also ordered the execution of the county magistrate, who had been proud of his meticulous and severe administration. All officials involved in the case, from the governor down, were severely punished. Additionally, a special decree was issued to honor the woman by building a memorial archway, and the son of the virtuous and talented person Yi was granted an imperial favor to enter the imperial academy and study. Both mercy and law were applied, and both the living and the dead felt grateful. Therefore, those who judge cases should be careful not to be overly proud of their meticulousness and severity.

    545

    里乘子曰：予尝谓折狱有三不可：一不可忽，二不可动气，三不可执己见。忽，则曲直是非未尽分明，便已潦草结案，倘有不实不尽，不惟有害于人，兼亦不利于己。动气，则一坐公堂，如归仇寇，不问情由，横加鞭扑，如系罪有应得，固不为过；假使波及无辜，问心亦复何忍？在乡曲良民，平日无事，见官已多恐惧骇汗，况有事拘质公堂，一见官长怒威相加，纵有十分冤情，亦觫觳不敢上达。有司更复执以己见，则箠楚之下，何求不得？虽逞一时之威福，差自快意，而鱼肉苍生，凿伤元气，恐一旦权移势夺，兴尽悲来，作业既多，报施亦复不少。某明府少年科甲，素以精刻自负，遇此大狱，遽命以严刑惨掠，诬服具狱，所谓三不可者，某明府兼而有之。厥后，世庙震怒，罚令论抵，此真罪有应得，夫复何怨？吾愿世之为民父母者，倘遇大狱，皆当以此为鉴。

    Explanation

    545

    The author of Li Cheng Zi says: I have often said that in adjudicating a case, there are three things one must not do: one must not be careless, two must not lose one's temper, and three must not insist on one's own opinion. If one is careless, the right and wrong, the true and false, will not be fully clarified, and the case will be wrapped up carelessly; if there are any inaccuracies or omissions, it not only harms others but also harms oneself. If one loses one's temper, then sitting on the bench in the courtroom is like facing an enemy, without asking the reasons, brutally beating the accused. If the accused is indeed guilty, it is not wrong; but if the innocent are affected, how can one bear it in their conscience? For ordinary, law-abiding people in the countryside, they already fear and sweat when they see officials, and when they are summoned to the courtroom, if they see the official's anger and power, even if they have a great injustice, they will tremble and dare not speak up. If the officials then insist on their own opinions, then under the threat of torture, what cannot be obtained? Though one may indulge in temporary power and pleasure, feeling somewhat satisfied, treating the common people like fish and meat, damaging the vital energy of the people, one fears that when power shifts and circumstances change, joy will turn to sorrow, and when one has committed many wrongdoings, the retribution will be great. A certain magistrate, who passed the imperial examinations in his youth, always boasted of his sharpness, when faced with this major case, immediately ordered severe and brutal punishment, causing the accused to falsely confess and wrap up the case. The three things that should not be done, this magistrate did all of them. Later, the emperor was furious and ordered him to be executed, this was truly a punishment that was deserved, what more can one complain about? I wish for all those who govern the people, if they encounter major cases, they should all take this as a warning.

    546

    某氏子
    　　
    　　某氏子，频年出外贸易。家惟一母一妻，母老而且盲，赖妇贤孝，藉针黹以供甘旨，晨昏定省，不敢或亏。姑妇二人，相依为命。他日，某氏子归，母喜，命妇烹鸡食之。中夜，某氏子暴亡。邻里以为异，鸣之官。验之，果是中毒。邑令疑妇有私，倍加榜掠，妇不胜其苦，遂诬服。问：“奸夫为谁？”妇本无事，况所识素无多人，仓卒间遽以十郎对，十郎者，某氏子在服之弟也。初，某氏子出门时，嘱十郎时为省母，藉代支理家政。十郎年少诚谨，以受某氏子之托，时至其家，经理甚周，母与妇甚德之。今妇迫于严刑，不得已以十郎塞责，令签拘十郎至。十郎见妇，泣曰：“嫂氏云何？”妇亦泣曰：“叔叔，奴……”语未毕，已哽咽不能成声。令见其情状，拍案叱之曰：“奸夫淫妇，在公堂之上，犹不知耻，而靦然人面，相对嘤喔，作儿女丑态耶！”乃不容十郎置辩，横加鞭楚，死而复苏者数次。十郎无奈，亦遂诬服。

    Explanation

    546

    A Man from a Certain Family

    A man from a certain family frequently went out for trade. His family consisted only of a mother and a wife. The mother was old and blind, and they relied on the wife's virtue and filial piety. She supported the family through needlework to provide delicious food, visiting her mother in the morning and evening, and never neglecting her duties. The mother and wife relied on each other for survival. One day, the man from a certain family returned, and the mother was happy, ordering the wife to cook chicken for him. In the middle of the night, the man from a certain family suddenly died. The neighbors thought it strange and reported it to the authorities. Upon examination, it was found that he had been poisoned. The county magistrate suspected the wife of having a lover and subjected her to severe beatings. The wife could not bear the torture and confessed falsely. When asked, "Who is the lover?" the wife, who had no wrongdoing, and knowing few people, hastily replied "Shi Lang." Shi Lang was the younger brother of the man from a certain family who was in mourning. Initially, when the man from a certain family left home, he instructed Shi Lang to visit his mother and help manage household affairs. Shi Lang, being young and sincere, accepted the man from a certain family's trust, often visited his home, managed things thoroughly, and was greatly appreciated by the mother and wife. Now, under the severe torture, the wife had no choice but to blame Shi Lang to cover up, and the magistrate ordered him to be brought in. When Shi Lang saw the wife, he cried, "What happened, Sister-in-law?" The wife also cried, "Uncle, I..." She couldn't finish her sentence, choked up, and couldn't speak. The magistrate, seeing their emotional state, slammed his desk and shouted, "The adulterer and the adulteress, even in the courtroom, still do not know shame, and dare to show their faces, whispering to each other, acting like children in disgraceful ways!" He did not allow Shi Lang to defend himself and subjected him to brutal beatings, causing him to die and revive several times. Shi Lang, having no choice, also confessed falsely.

    547

    狱具，论辟，行刑有日矣。巡抚某公者，公明仁恕之大君子人也。虑囚至此，心甚疑之，以问幕宾。会幕宾方与其徒围棋，正专心致志，不遑旁骛，乃漫应曰：“此狱已具，属吏不知费几许推敲，料亦无所冤曲。公又何必故意驳诘，致滋多事耶？”某公乃不复平反。妇与十郎遂均坐大辟矣。是夜，漏三下，幕宾将就寝，忽闻门外剥啄声甚厉，审是妇女声音，怪暮夜何得有妇女来此，叱令速去。闻门外厉声答曰：“而不开门，我岂不能入耶？”歘见一女从门罅入，披发喋血，怒视幕宾，戟手指而詈之曰：“而以布衣为军门上宾，不过粗识得几个之无，便谓精熟申韩，诓骗居停，坐享厚俸，以人命为草菅，毫不详慎。昨妾此案，中丞方欲平反，不耻虚怀下问，倘能迎机襄赞，或得一线生机；而但以围棋故，支吾漫应，以致妾等冤情不能昭雪。妾死固不足惜，惟弑夫恶名，心实不甘。妾已请于帝，许向而索命矣！”言讫，便欲向前扑攫。

    Explanation

    547

    The case was fully prepared, and the verdict was pronounced, with the day of execution set. The governor, a great gentleman of fairness, benevolence, and leniency, was reviewing the prisoners when he came across this case. He was deeply suspicious and asked his guest advisor. Just then, the advisor was engrossed in a game of Go with his disciples, too absorbed to be distracted. He vaguely and evasively responded, "This case has already been thoroughly investigated. The subordinates have spent considerable effort, and I doubt there is any injustice. Why should you deliberately raise objections and create unnecessary trouble?" The governor did not further review the case. The woman and her husband, Ten Lang, were both sentenced to death.

    That night, at the third watch, the advisor was about to go to bed when he suddenly heard a loud knocking at the door. He realized it was a woman's voice and wondered why a woman would be here at such a late hour. He shouted, "Go away quickly!" A loud voice from outside replied, "If you don't open the door, I can still get in!" Suddenly, a woman with disheveled hair and blood-stained face appeared from the crack in the door. She glared at the advisor and pointed at him with her finger, cursing, "You, a commoner, are a guest of the military governor. You know only a few trivial things and pretend to be an expert in the laws of Shang Yang and Han Fei. You deceive your host, enjoy a generous salary, and treat human life as worthless, showing no care. Yesterday, when my case was being reviewed, the governor was about to overturn the verdict and humbly sought advice. If you had acted in accordance with the situation and assisted, there might have been a chance of survival. But because you were engrossed in your game of Go, you vaguely and evasively responded, so my case could not be cleared. My death is not worth lamenting, but I am unwilling to bear the stigma of killing my husband. I have already petitioned the emperor, who has granted me permission to demand your life!" With that, she lunged forward to attack.

    548

    幕宾骇汗如雨，急长跪请于妇曰：“某罪诚应死，但离家年久，尚有八旬老母，能容回家一诀别不？”妇应曰：“念而孝心，姑宽贷一月。而宜速归，迟则无及矣！”言讫，恨恨而去。某见妇去，毛发竖立。诘旦，谒居停，具以实告，治任驰归，匝月果卒。中丞某公，闻而骇异，乃改装易服，诣某氏子家，见妪备审崖末。闻瞽妪泣且詈曰：“客尔何知，吾儿之惨死，不知其由；惜有司昏愦，不加详察，诬我贤妇，坐以大辟，伤哉冤也！”公佯问：“何谓也？”妪曰：“客固不知，老妇与彼，名虽姑妇，恩逾母女。终朝厮守，坐卧不离，何由有私？乃有司刑逼诬服。闻巡抚某公，公明仁恕，狱上，万一希冀或得平反。不谓亦一体瞉霿，诬正典刑，沉冤莫白。惜老妇残年向尽，又以目废，不能上叩九阍，一为申雪耳！”公又问：“十郎为谁？”妪曰：“彼乃老妇之犹子。吾儿出门时，以老妇及家政相托，少年诚谨，德反成仇。

    Explanation

    548

    The guest attendant was terrified and sweating profusely, hurriedly knelt down and pleaded with the woman, saying, "My crime truly deserves death, but I have been away from home for a long time, and I still have an eighty-year-old mother. Can I be allowed to return home to bid her farewell?" The woman replied, "Considering his filial piety, I will temporarily grant him a month. He should return quickly; otherwise, it will be too late!" After saying this, she left angrily. The guest saw the woman leave and his hair stood on end. The next morning, he visited the host and told him everything. He prepared his belongings and hurried back home. A month later, she indeed passed away. The Governor-General heard about this and was surprised. He then changed his clothes and went to the house of the man's family. He saw the old woman and carefully examined the details. He heard the blind old woman crying and cursing, saying, "What do you know, guest? My son's tragic death, you don't understand the reason; I pity the officials who are incompetent and did not conduct a thorough investigation, falsely accusing my virtuous wife and sentencing her to death. Alas, it is a great injustice!" The official pretended to ask, "What do you mean?" The old woman said, "You certainly don't know, old woman. Although our relationship is that of a grandmother and daughter-in-law, our affection surpasses that of a mother and daughter. We stayed together all day, sitting and lying down without parting, how could there be any private affair? It was the officials who forced us to confess under torture. I heard that Governor-General Mr. Public is just and merciful. If the case is brought to him, there is a chance of a possible reversal. I did not expect him to be equally blind, falsely accusing and sentencing her to death, leaving the injustice unresolved. Alas, my old age is nearly over, and I am also blind, unable to ascend to the nine gates to seek a single vindication!" The official asked again, "Who is Shí Láng?" The old woman said, "He is my adopted grandson. When my son left, he entrusted me and the household affairs to me. The young man was sincere and cautious, but his virtue turned into enmity."

    549

    想业由前世，夫复何说！”公不胜叹息。既诘得食鸡一事，便托腹肌，出钱命代市一鸡，倩人烹好，即置于向日子所具食之处，乃一葡萄架下。公留心黠察，见热气上薰，少选，架上一丝下缒，直入碗中，非竭目力不见。公知有异，取一脔饲犬，犬毙。乃谓妪曰：“尔妇之冤，我能代申，尔姑待之可也。”妪不解所谓，但合手称谢而已。公将熟鸡裹以旋署，檄邑令及承讯在事各官至，以实告之，众喏喏相视，若不深信。公随命呼一犬至，饲以鸡一脔，果立毙，众始服罪。命人往搜架上，得一蝎，长四寸许，盖所缒之丝即是物也。公乃以实入告，自请议处，邑令以诬拟罪论抵，馀各议罚有差。又请以贤孝旌某氏妇，以义士旌十郎，各建坊以慰冤魂；妪着地方有司伏恤，以终馀年。制曰：“可。”此狱幸赖中丞之贤，卒得暴白。向使幕宾因居停来问，一经诘驳，断不致令贤妇义士抱屈九泉。观其暮夜叩门索命一节，洵属快事。可不慎欤！可不慎欤！

    Explanation

    549

    A Matter of Justice and Vengeance

    "Destiny is shaped by actions of the past, what more can be said?" The official could not help but sigh. After investigating the matter of eating a chicken, he entrusted the task to his stomach, spent money to have another chicken bought, asked someone to cook it well, and placed it in the spot where food was prepared the previous day, under a grapevine. He carefully observed, noticing the steam rising, and soon, a thread descended from the vine, directly into the bowl, barely visible to the naked eye. Knowing something was amiss, he took a piece and fed it to a dog, which died. He then told the old woman, "Your wife's injustice, I can help to redress. You may wait for it." The old woman did not understand what he meant but clasped her hands and thanked him. The official wrapped the cooked chicken and sent it to the office, summoning the county magistrate and other officials involved in the case. He told them the truth, and the crowd nodded and looked at each other, though they did not fully believe. He then ordered a dog to be brought and fed a piece of the chicken, which indeed caused the dog to die immediately, and the crowd finally admitted their guilt. He ordered someone to search the grapevine, finding a scorpion about four inches long, which was indeed the thread that had been lowered. He then reported the truth to the emperor, volunteering to be punished. The county magistrate proposed a charge of slander and suggested a punishment, while the others were each given different penalties. He also requested that the virtuous and filial wife of a certain family be honored, that the righteous man Shilang be honored, and that monuments be built to comfort the spirits of the wronged. The old woman should be cared for by the local officials until the end of her life. The emperor said, "Agreed." This case was fortunate to have the virtue of the vice-premier, and the truth was finally revealed. If the guest had come to ask because of lodging, once questioned and refuted, it would not have allowed the virtuous wife and righteous man to suffer injustice in the afterlife. Considering the part where he knocked on the door at night demanding a life, indeed it was a pleasing matter. How careful one must be!

    550

    里乘子曰：某氏子之狱，巡抚某公疑问幕宾，向使幕宾闻公之言，细意重究，未尝不可覆盆得雪；乃贪恋手谈，漫应了事，致使含冤地下者暮夜前来索命。彼鬼所责之语，句句中的，幕宾虽有百喙，不能置辩。鬼念其孝心，宽贷一月，得以归正首邱，诚为侥幸之至。窃惟吾儒读书读律，出膺民社，折狱一事，切不可自恃精明，稍事疏忽。先贤曾子有言：“如得其情，则哀矜而勿喜。”是得其情者尚当哀矜，况未必尽得其情者乎！《书》曰：“罪疑惟轻，功疑惟重。”又云：“与其杀不辜，宁失不经。”此数语与曾子之言，实可互相发明。凡司刑名者，皆当时存方寸，则子惠元元，造福无量矣！

    Explanation

    550

    Li Cheng Zi said: In the case of a certain individual, the provincial governor had doubts and consulted his retainer. If the retainer had carefully considered the governor's words and thoroughly investigated the matter, he might have been able to clear the sky from under an overturned bowl; instead, he indulged in chess and carelessly dismissed the case, causing the wrongfully imprisoned soul to come at night to demand his life. The ghost's accusations were logically sound, and though the retainer had many mouths to speak with, he could not defend himself. The ghost, moved by his filial piety, granted him a month's reprieve, allowing him to return to his roots, which was truly a stroke of luck. I reflect that Confucian scholars who study the classics and laws and are entrusted with governing the people must be extremely cautious in judging cases, not relying on their own intelligence and avoiding any carelessness. The sage Zengzi said, "If one understands the truth, then one should feel compassion and pity without rejoicing." Even those who understand the truth should feel compassion; how much more so for those who may not fully understand it! The Book of History says, "In cases of doubt, err on the side of leniency in punishment and generosity in reward." It also says, "It is better to err in favor of the innocent than to follow an unjust law." These words from the Book of History and Zengzi's teachings truly complement each other. All those who administer criminal justice must maintain a clear and just mind, thereby benefiting the common people and bringing immeasurable good fortune.

    551

    媚芗
    　　
    　　巢县富室某翁，五十无子，惟一女，名媚芗，美而慧。年十四，犹延师教之读书，诸子百家，过目辄了了。翁夫妇皆钟爱之，凡泉刀出纳，皆归经理。每晚读罢，纪纲仆鱼贯而入，屏息立左右白事毕，先后呈簿请会计，女耳听目察，口咨手画，无毫厘差谬。众咸敬畏，不敢稍欺。翁尝曰：“古云：生女虽逊男，慰情良胜无。若我媚芗，正恐男子中亦未易多觏也。”初，邻舍某秀才，有子曰恂生，长女一岁。髫龄即与女同学，齿相若，情亦极相亲，丰姿颖悟，亦甚相埒。见者皆叹为天然嘉耦，翁夫妇亦以为然，但以贫故，未之许也。

    Explanation

    551

    In the wealthy household of a certain Mr. from Chaoyang County, who was fifty years old and had no sons, there was only one daughter named Meixiang. She was beautiful and intelligent. At fourteen years old, she still invited teachers to teach her to read, and she understood various schools of thought and classics upon a glance. Her parents treasured her deeply, and all financial transactions were managed by her. Every evening after reading, the servants stood breathlessly on both sides to report matters, and they presented accounts for review in sequence. Meixiang listened with her ears, observed with her eyes, consulted with her mouth, and drew with her hand, with no slightest error. The servants feared and respected her, dared not deceive slightly. The father once said, "From ancient times, having a daughter is inferior to having a son, but comforting the heart is far better than nothing. If my Meixiang, I fear it would be hard to find many men who are as good." Initially, a neighbor, a certain scholar, had a son named Xunsheng, who was a year older than Meixiang. At the age of childhood, he began studying with the girl, and their ages were similar, and their feelings were extremely close. The neighbor's son was graceful and quick-witted, and he was very similar to Meixiang. Those who saw them both sighed that they were a naturally good match, and the parents also agreed, but due to poverty, he was not yet betrothed.

    552

    一日，师饭后来塾，见女被人杀死地上，大惊，急呼翁夫妇至。验之，女下体衣褫褪私处，伤痕狼藉，血流至踝，审是强奸迫杀。翁夫妇睹此情状，恸彻于心。疑必恂生所为，遂指名讼之官，并贿求必以恂生议抵而甘心焉。邑令某公，健吏也。既受翁贿，又默自因事揣情，亦不甚径庭，遂将恂生严刑敲楚，迫勒具供，以逼奸致杀，加等坐抵。师以约束不严，失察酿祸，议流二千里。某秀才仅此一子，虽心知其冤，以家贫无力营救，况素性懦葸，但坐视其诬受大辟而已。师故邑名诸生，流至闽省，士林中怜其非罪，又仰其文名，故多乐与之游。

    Explanation

    552

    One day, after finishing his meal, the teacher went to the school and saw a woman lying on the ground, killed by someone. He was greatly shocked and hurriedly called the woman’s husband and wife to come. Upon examination, the woman’s underclothes had been pulled down to her private parts, her body bore chaotic wounds, and blood had flowed down to her ankles. It was determined to be a case of rape followed by murder. Upon witnessing this scene, the husband and wife were overwhelmed with grief. Suspecting it must have been done by Xunsheng, they named him specifically and took the matter to the authorities, bribing them to ensure that Xunsheng would be sentenced to death so that they could be satisfied. The county magistrate, a certain official known for his capability, having accepted the old man’s bribe, and also silently reasoning based on the circumstances, did not deviate much from their position. He therefore subjected Xunsheng to severe punishment and forced him to confess, sentencing him to death for causing a murder through rape, with an added degree of severity. The teacher was blamed for lax supervision, failing to notice the danger, and was sentenced to banishment for two thousand li. The scholar had only this one son; though he knew he was innocent, he was too poor to rescue him. Moreover, he was naturally timid and cowardly, and could only sit by and watch as his son suffered the death penalty. The teacher had once been a famous scholar from the county, banished to Fujian province; in the scholarly community, he was pitied for his innocence and admired for his literary reputation, so many were glad to associate with him.

    553

    会尚书某公予告在籍，欲延名师，以课其公子，选阅多人，都不当意。或以师荐，尚书命取平日所为制艺读之，大为赞赏。聘币相延，极称契合。师适需理发，命呼整容匠至。甫入户，瞥见师，踉跄返走。师甚诧异，以其面熟，猝难省忆，以叩公子。云：“比来待诏，乃师乡人，不知何以见师返走。”师闻为乡人，顿觉省悟，乃瞿然曰：“是矣，陷我于此者，殆即此贼也。”盖此匠本与富翁同里，幼即出入其家。彼日亦因将为师理发来塾，见惟媚芗在此作书，更无他人，不禁顿起淫心，强拉求欢。撑拒间恐女声喊，恰有一切书刀，遂按而刭之，怀刀而奔。又恐狱终及己，乃逃亡。

    Explanation

    553

    Just as the high-ranking official was on leave but still registered, he wished to invite a renowned teacher to educate his son. He examined many people, but none were satisfactory. Someone recommended a teacher, and the official ordered his works to be read. He was greatly impressed. The teacher was invited with gifts and honorariums, and they were extremely compatible. The teacher just needed a haircut, so he ordered a barber to come. As soon as the barber entered the door, he caught a glimpse of the teacher and stumbled back in retreat. The teacher was very surprised, as he looked familiar, but suddenly found it difficult to recall. He asked the son, who replied, "Recently, he was waiting for an imperial summons, and is a fellow villager of the teacher. I do not know why the teacher retreated." The teacher, upon hearing that he was from the same village, suddenly realized and exclaimed, "Indeed, the one who has trapped me here is probably this very villain." It turned out that the barber was originally from the same village as the wealthy man and would come and go from his house from a young age. That day, he came to the school to give the teacher a haircut, saw only Mèixiāng there writing, and no one else. He could not help but suddenly develop lustful thoughts and forcibly pulled to have sexual relations. While struggling, fearing the girl would scream, he just happened to have a writing knife, so he pressed down and slit her throat, hid the knife, and fled. Also fearing that the case would eventually involve him, he fled.

    554

    转徙至闽，不图忽与师遇，深虑行踪败露，故心虚情怯，猝见师，急欲回避。师初固料不及此，今见其形迹可疑，因忆此狱初兴时，彼便无故亡去，安知非畏罪而遁？爰以情告尚书，拘送有司，果一械尽吐其实。闽抚以师无罪，具文咨准开复。并将整容匠槛解回籍，讯质定罪。此狱已越六七年，某秀才正以恸子卧病，忽师归，述知此事，大喜，沉疴顿减。谋与师联名上控，务求昭雪。时前邑令已荐擢郡守，奉准公文，情知错误，又闻师与秀才将谋上控，恐不利于己，乃倩人关说，以重资啖二人，求罢控。初固不许，既念死者不能复生，又贪重资，遂不复控。惟将整容匠瘐死狱中。

    Explanation

    554

    The person migrated to Fujian, never expecting to suddenly encounter the teacher. Deeply worried that his movements would be exposed, he felt timid and fearful. Upon seeing the teacher unexpectedly, he urgently wanted to avoid him. Initially, the teacher had not anticipated this, but now, observing his suspicious behavior, he recalled that when this case first began, the person had suddenly disappeared without cause. How could it not be that he fled out of fear of guilt? Therefore, the teacher reported the situation to the Minister of Justice, who detained and sent the person to the authorities. Under interrogation, he fully confessed the truth. The governor of Fujian declared the teacher innocent and prepared a formal document to request and obtain reinstatement. The embalmer was ordered to be shackled and sent back to his hometown, where he was questioned and sentenced. This case had lasted for six or seven years. A certain scholar was grieving over his son and was ill. Suddenly, the teacher returned, and upon hearing of the matter, the scholar was greatly pleased, and his severe illness quickly diminished. He planned to join the teacher in filing a joint appeal, determined to seek a clear exoneration. At the time, the former magistrate, who had been promoted to governor, received official documents confirming the error. Knowing that the teacher and the scholar were planning to file an appeal, he feared it would be detrimental to himself and hired someone to intercede, offering a large sum of money to the two people to ask them to drop the appeal. Initially, they refused, but then, thinking that the dead could not be brought back to life, and coveting the large sum of money, they agreed to drop the appeal. Only the embalmer died in prison.

    555

    里乘子曰：向闻此县令由翰林出膺民社，素号健吏，此狱以贿故，不肯详察，锻炼坐抵，致使无辜儒童含冤地下，可谓憯极。然天道之巧，其师议流，不于他处而在闽省，俾与真犯相遇，事越六七年，卒使覆盆得雪，诚属快事。惟师与某秀才，但贪重资，不肯联名上控，不然县令虽擢郡守，焉能逃罪哉！

    Explanation

    555

    The traveler from the village said: I previously heard that this county magistrate was promoted from the Hanlin Academy to govern the people and was always known as an energetic official. However, this case was decided due to bribery; he refused to investigate thoroughly and fabricated evidence to convict the innocent scholar child, causing him to suffer injustice in the underworld. This can be called extremely cruel. Yet, the ingenious workings of heaven intervened; the teacher decided to exile the real culprit, not to another place but to Fujian province, where he met the true perpetrator. After six to seven years, the bowl of injustice was finally cleared, truly a joyful matter. Only the teacher and a certain scholar were greedy for a large sum of money and refused to file a joint appeal. Otherwise, even if the county magistrate was promoted to governor, how could he escape punishment?

    556

    清苑县某氏女
    　　
    　　直隶清苑县，有兄弟伯仲析爨而居者。仲无行，遗产荡尽，赖伯友爱，时赒济之。伯年逾五旬，仅一子，弱冠娶某氏女，逑好甚敦。会仲妻以急逋，诣伯求助。伯子暮从市中归，腹苦饥，某氏女方晚炊，伯子遽索蔬饭食之，七窍血暴涌，逾时顿卒。某氏女惶恐失措，奔告伯夫妇，相持恸哭，几不欲生。仲妻瞪目旁哭，厉声曰：“伯年半百，仅此一脉，今无辜暴死，某氏女不能辞其咎矣！若置不究，其如死者何！予夫妇忝期服亲，决不稍贷也！”归诉于仲，遂偕伯夫妇共鸣之官，直控某氏女毒死亲夫。官使伍伯验之，确系服毒致死，乃严刑榜讯。

    Explanation

    556

    A Woman from a Certain Family in Qingyuan County

    In Qingyuan County, Zhili, there were brothers who lived separately in different households after dividing their property. The younger brother, lacking virtue, had squandered all his inherited wealth. Relying on the elder brother's affection, he was helped from time to time. The elder brother, over fifty years old, had only one son who, when young, married a woman from a certain family. Their relationship was very close. One day, the younger brother's wife, burdened by urgent debts, went to the elder brother for help. The elder brother's son returned home from the market in the evening, extremely hungry. The woman from the certain family was just starting to cook dinner. The elder brother's son immediately asked for vegetables and rice to eat. Suddenly, blood gushed out from all seven orifices, and he died suddenly after a short while. The woman from the certain family panicked and was at a loss, rushing to tell the elder brother and his wife. They held each other and cried bitterly, almost unable to bear living. The younger brother's wife stared with wide eyes and cried by the side, shouting loudly, "The elder brother is over fifty years old, with only this one descendant. Now he died suddenly without cause. The woman from the certain family cannot escape blame! If we do not investigate, what will become of the dead? My husband and I are related by marriage and are close relatives; we will not show any leniency!" She returned to tell the younger brother, and then together with the elder brother and his wife, they went to the authorities, directly accusing the woman from the certain family of poisoning her husband-in-law. The official sent a constable to examine the case, and it was confirmed that he died from poisoning. Then, severe punishment and questioning under torture were applied.

    557

    某氏女荏弱，不能耐五木，遂诬服因奸起见，并指某甲为奸夫。某甲故中表亲，读书虽未成，而素行不苟。拘至，畏刑，亦直承不敢置辨。爰书已定，将付大辟矣。适讷近堂制军移节总督直隶，虑囚至此案，疑其不实，欲平反之。明府某公者，健吏也，听讼为一时之冠。因公晋省谒制军，讷公告以此狱，命为平反。明府以案逾三年之久，业经多官研讯，又系大辟，不敢承鞫。讷公怒让之曰：“君久膺民社，素著循声，若不昭晰此案，是视人命如草菅。似此畏难规避，吾将以白简劾之，勿谓老夫无情也！”明府不得已，细核历年案牍，几厚盈尺。观其屡断屡翻，其中情词实有可疑，爰命拘集在案诸人，隔别细研。

    Explanation

    557

    A woman from a certain family was frail and could not endure the five kinds of torture, so she falsely confessed that adultery was the motive and pointed to a certain person A as the adulterer. Person A was a cousin by marriage, had not finished his studies, but had always been meticulous in his conduct. When he was brought in, fearing punishment, he also simply confessed and dared not dispute. The official record had already been finalized, and he was about to be sentenced to death. Coincidentally, the recently appointed governor-general of Zhili Province, who was also a military commander, arrived and examined the case of prisoners. He doubted the truthfulness of the case and wanted to overturn it. The magistrate Mr. X was a capable official, the best in hearing cases at the time. He visited the governor-general on official business, and the governor-general informed him about this case, ordering him to overturn it. The magistrate, however, thought that the case had been ongoing for over three years, had already been thoroughly investigated by many officials, and involved the death penalty, so he dared not take on the case. The governor-general scolded him, saying, "You have long held public office and have always been known for your integrity. If you do not clarify this case, it would be treating human life as worthless as weeds. If you shirk responsibility out of fear, I will impeach you with a formal accusation. Do not think that I am heartless!" The magistrate had no choice but to carefully review the case files from previous years, which were nearly a foot thick. He observed that the case had been repeatedly decided and overturned, and that the statements within were indeed suspicious. Thus, he ordered the summoning of all those involved in the case and separately questioned them in detail.

    558

    见某氏女与某甲均辞气温和，不类杀人者，益信其冤。又讯伯夫妇，佥谓某氏女入门后，事舅姑甚孝，夫妇亦曾无诟谇，且未见某甲往来，是非有无，不敢臆断。讯至仲妻，则厉声哭骂，谓贱婢以奸斩伯氏之嗣，不付大辟，不足以服死者云云，词色悍暴，殊非良善。明府谛审久之，忽拍案叱谓仲妻曰：“下毒者非他人，即汝是也！汝毒犹子，奈何诬某氏女乎？”仲妻失色，极口叫屈。明府怒命严刑拷之，不肯遽服。时方冬月，自日昳讯至漏三下，犹未吐实。夜半，忽阴风惨栗，屋瓦飞鸣，满堂灯烛幽绿如萤，众见一大蜈蚣，长二尺许，蜿蜒由户外驰入仲妻裈中。

    Explanation

    558

    Seeing the woman from a certain family and the man named "甲" both with gentle speech, they did not seem like killers, and thus their innocence was increasingly believed. Further questioning the elder brother and his wife revealed that the woman from a certain family, after marrying into the family, served her in-laws with great filial piety, and the couple had no quarrels or criticisms, nor had they seen the man named "甲" around. As to whether it happened or not, they dared not speculate. When questioned about the second wife, she shouted and cried while cursing, saying that the lowly woman killed the heir of her elder brother through adultery, and not executing her was not enough to satisfy the dead. Her words and expressions were fierce and violent, far from being good and virtuous. The magistrate carefully examined the case for a long time, then suddenly slapped the table and shouted at the second wife, saying, "The poisoner is not someone else, it is you! You poisoned your stepson, how could you slander the woman from a certain family?" The second wife turned pale and loudly cried out her innocence. The magistrate, angered, ordered severe punishment and torture, but she refused to confess. It was mid-winter, and from dusk until the third watch, she still did not reveal the truth. At midnight, suddenly a cold wind blew, causing the tiles to fly and make a sound, and the entire room was filled with dim, green light like that of fireflies. Everyone saw a large centipede, about two feet long, slithering from outside and rushing into the middle of the second wife's skirt.

    559

    群诧为异事，急命褫裈搜之，不得。明府知为冤鬼作祟，益命加刑。仲妻知不可掩，乃尽吐其实。盖仲妻久欲吞伯产，每至伯家，必怀砒少许，伺隙投之，恰值某氏女晚饮，瞷其无人，遂潜下焉。固欲酖伯一家，及伯子以饥先服毙命，遂乘机嫁祸某氏女，亦计之至得也。至此吐实，始共颂制军虑囚之神，且叹明府不愧为老吏断狱。或请于明府曰：“公何以知为仲妻也？”明府笑曰：“是不难，要在听讼者留心察言观色耳。彼姑舅且不敢臆断，而仲妻竟敢直声其罪，令人可疑；况其日适至伯家，更露破绽。姑试恐喝，不谓竟成信谳也。”爰以仲妻抵罪；又以某甲无辜株连，遂以某氏女妻之，并嗣为伯后。

    Explanation

    559

    The crowd was astonished by the unusual event and quickly ordered to strip and search him, but they found nothing. The local official knew it was the ghost of an unjustly wronged person causing trouble and ordered to increase the punishment. Zhong's wife, knowing she could not hide her guilt, finally confessed everything. It turned out that Zhong's wife had long wanted to take over Bo's property. Every time she went to Bo's house, she always carried a small amount of arsenic and waited for an opportunity to poison it. Just happened to coincide with a woman from a certain family having a late drink, when she saw no one there, she then secretly poisoned it. She intended to poison the entire family of Bo, and her son died first from hunger, then took the opportunity to frame a woman from a certain family. Until she finally confessed, they began to praise the governor's divine insight in dealing with prisoners and praised the local official for not being ashamed to be an experienced judge. Someone asked the local official, "How did you know it was Zhong's wife?" The local official smiled and said, "It is not difficult, it lies in the careful observation of words and expressions by the judge. Even the husband and wife dared not guess, but Zhong's wife dared to openly confess her crime, which made people suspicious, especially since she happened to be at Bo's house that day, which revealed more inconsistencies. Let's try to scare her, who would have thought it would lead to a credible verdict." Zhong's wife was punished, and someone named A was also wrongfully implicated. So the woman from a certain family was married to him and became the heir of Bo.

    560

    里乘子曰：此狱据舅姑供称，某氏女入门后，事舅姑甚孝，夫妇亦曾无诟谇，且未见某甲往来，是非有无，不敢臆断，而讯及仲妻，则厉声哭骂，竟敢促付大辟，揣情察理，原有破绽可寻，惜粗心人不能细意领会。一经健吏听断，便难掩饰。“察言观色”四字，的是听讼要诀。吾愿为民父母者，凡遇折狱，皆当以明府某公为法；然非制军，则明府虽有才亦不肯用，尤愿为大府者，虑囚当以制军为法也。

    Explanation

    560

    The commentator said: In this case, the court's judgment is based on the testimony of the in-laws, who reported that a woman from a certain family, after entering the household, was very filial to her in-laws, and the couple had no quarrels. There was also no evidence of a particular person's involvement. Whether the allegations are true or false, it is not possible to make a definitive judgment. However, when the wife was questioned, she cried and cursed loudly, and her actions were so extreme that they warranted the death penalty. By carefully considering the circumstances and reasoning, there were flaws and inconsistencies that could be identified. Unfortunately, those who are careless cannot appreciate the nuances. Once a skilled official hears the case, it becomes difficult to conceal any attempts at deception. The phrase "察言观色" (observing words and expressions) is indeed the key to effective judicial proceedings. I wish all officials, when dealing with cases, would follow the example of Magistrate Mr. X; however, without the support of a higher authority, even a talented magistrate would not be utilized. I also wish that higher officials, when considering prisoners, would follow the example of Commander Z.

    561

    杜有美
    　　
    　　太原诸生杜坚，字子密。世席厚资，藏书甚富。壮年生一子，命名有美，字小甫。杜有妹，嫁同里诸生卢某，家亦小阜，有女名慧娟，与有美同月生。杜妻郑，与妹极相得，以故，妹时归宁，小儿女常易乳而哺，相爱各不啻己出。既齿日长，容貌都美，情亦日亲。年已十三，俱未婚配。凡为有美执柯者，郑意在慧娟，悉却之；试商之妹，亦首肯，归以告卢。卢素迂拘，以有内戚嫌，殊不以为可。妹让之曰：“腐儒，何太不脱头巾气！只许我杜家人嫁汝，不准汝卢家人嫁还我家耶？况我侄殊不恶，亦未必有玷汝女！我业已许之矣。生女当由母作主，勿预父事，汝休得过问！”卢大怒曰：“汝何太不通道理？古云：女子三从，在家从父，出嫁从夫，夫死从子。我一息尚在，不惟慧娟当从父命，即汝亦当从夫命。

    Explanation

    561

    A Woman Named Youmei

    In Taiyuan, there was a scholar named Du Jian, whose courtesy name was Zimi. He inherited substantial wealth and had a vast collection of books. In his prime years, he had a son whom he named Youmei and gave the courtesy name Xiaofu. Du Jian had a younger sister who married a local scholar named Lu, whose family was also modestly prosperous. They had a daughter named Huijuan, who was born in the same month as Youmei. Du Jian's wife, Zheng, got along very well with her sister, so the sister would often visit home, and the two young children would sometimes exchange milk and be nursed by each other, loving each other as if they were their own children. As they grew older, both were beautiful in appearance, and their affection for each other grew stronger. By the age of thirteen, neither had been married.

    Whenever someone proposed a match for Youmei, Zheng intended for him to marry Huijuan and turned down all other proposals. She then discussed this with her sister, who also agreed. Upon returning home, Zheng informed Lu about her decision. Lu, who was traditionally conservative and cautious, objected strongly due to the familial relationship, feeling it was inappropriate. His sister rebuked him, saying, "You are such a rigid scholar! Why are you so bound by convention? Do you only allow our family to marry into yours, but not the other way around? Besides, my nephew is not bad, and he is unlikely to dishonor your daughter! I have already agreed to the match. When it comes to arranging a marriage for a daughter, the mother should make the decision, not the father. You should not interfere!" Lu became furious and retorted, "How unreasonable of you! As it is said in ancient times: women must follow three rules. In the home, they must obey their father; after marriage, they must obey their husband; and after their husband's death, they must obey their son. As long as I am alive, not only should Huijuan follow her father's wishes, but you should also follow your husband's wishes."

    562

    今我不肯缔婿，汝焉敢擅专耶！”夫妻反唇相稽，几至反目。初，杜妹归宁，每携慧娟与俱，自议婚后，卢不许慧娟再往。有美偶来省视，亦不许慧娟相见，中表从此路人矣。有美与慧娟，一经睽隔，思慕綦殷。荏苒倏逾两年，情窦俱开，益难为情，而两地相思，只可自愉，各不能掬心相示。有美无可奈何，倩画师写《太真献镜图》，自题一诗云：“狡狯温郎绝世才，风流不厌自为媒。三生幸遇金闺彦，一笑亲陈玉镜台。”将画与诗，贿某媪密付慧娟求题，以察其意。慧娟正苦思有美，不知可否同心，见画与诗，喜感交并，爰拈笔端楷题一绝于轴末，以答其意。曰：“两地相思两不知，玉台一献当红丝。老奴伎俩何难料？请待良宵却扇时。”题毕，仍倩某媪携归有美。有美见诗狂喜，朝夕焚香披诵，如获异宝。由是两心遥遥相印，各以嫁娶自誓。惟苦不得遂，不觉恹恹俱病，医药罔效。

    Explanation

    562

    "Today, I refuse to agree to this marriage alliance, how dare you act on your own!" The couple retorted to each other, almost coming to blows. At first, Du Mei would return to her parents' home, always bringing Hui Juan with her. After discussing the marriage, Lu forbade Hui Juan from visiting again. When You Mei occasionally came to visit, Lu also prevented Hui Juan from seeing him, and thus, the relationship between cousins became as distant as strangers. You Mei and Hui Juan, once separated, deeply longed for each other. Time passed swiftly, and two years went by; their feelings blossomed, making it even more difficult to express their emotions. While longing for each other from afar, they could only find comfort in themselves, unable to share their true feelings openly. You Mei, feeling helpless, commissioned a painter to create a work titled "The True Consort Offering the Mirror" and wrote a poem: "Witty and charming Wen, a talent of the world, flows with grace and serves as his own matchmaker. Three lifetimes of fortune to meet this distinguished young man from a noble family, a smile to present the jade mirror stand." He gave the painting and poem to a servant, asking her to secretly deliver them to Hui Juan for her to write a response, in order to gauge her feelings. Hui Juan, deeply missing You Mei and unsure if they could be together, saw the painting and poem and felt a mix of joy and emotion. She then picked up a brush and wrote a poem in neat calligraphy at the end of the scroll to respond to his intentions. It read: "Longing for each other across two places, unaware of each other's feelings, the jade mirror stand is offered, symbolizing the red thread. The tricks of the old servant are easily predictable; please wait for the good night when the fan is removed." After completing the poem, she still had the servant carry it back to You Mei. Upon seeing the poem, You Mei was overjoyed, burning incense and reciting it day and night, as if he had gained a precious treasure. Thus, their hearts were connected from afar, and they both vowed to marry each other. However, they were frustrated by their inability to fulfill their vows, and soon, they both fell ill, with no medicine proving effective.

    563

    杜夫妻与妹皆知两人致病之由，筹有以破卢之执。妹曰：“腐儒，外固而内葸，非以计劫之不可。兄嫂可无虑，我自有以报命。”杜夫妻大喜拜谢，并求速为玉成，妹诺之。卢固钟爱慧娟，见其病甚绵惙，实深愁忧。会杜妹归，卢问有美病状如何，妹怒答曰：“殆不起矣，何劳汝问！”卢叹曰：“我正虑慧儿疾革，不谓汝侄亦尔！”妹白眼相视，诋之曰：“一双好儿女，皆为汝杀却！夫复何说！”卢惊问：“何谓也？”妹唾其面曰：“老物尚梦梦耶！若自幼哺同乳，寝同席；比长，相亲相爱，此人情也。既知亲爱，则必欲偕伉俪，以图永好，此尤人情之常也。圣人制礼，尚缘人情，偏汝老物，拘执成见，竟不曲体人情耶？自汝拒婚后，若即俱病，病且日剧，殆皆不起。我实不忍仰息见若俱死，请先自刭于君前，泉下当撮合若仍成嘉耦，以偿夙愿。

    Explanation

    563

    The couple from the Du family and their sister all knew the cause of the illness, and they planned to break through Lu's stubbornness. The sister said, "This old scholar, outwardly strong but inwardly timid, cannot be overcome without using strategy or force. You can have no worries, brother and sister-in-law; I have my own way to fulfill the duty." The couple from the Du family were greatly pleased and thanked her, and they also requested a quick resolution, to which the sister agreed. Lu had a strong affection for Hui Juan, seeing her illness as being very severe and weak, and he was deeply sorrowful and worried. When the sister from the Du family returned, Lu asked about the condition of the beautiful (referring to Hui Juan). The sister angrily responded, "She is likely to die, so why bother asking!" Lu sighed, "I was concerned about Hui Juan's worsening illness, not expecting that your nephew would be the same!" The sister looked at him with contempt and scolded, "A pair of good children, all killed by you! What more can be said!" Lu was startled and asked, "What do you mean?" The sister spat in his face and said, "This old man is still in a state of confusion! If they were breastfed together, slept in the same bed from a young age, and grew up loving each other dearly, this is human nature. Knowing love, then wanting to marry and seek eternal happiness together, this is especially a common human sentiment. Sages established rituals based on human nature, but you, this old man, stubbornly hold onto your own views and do not understand human nature. Since you refused the marriage, both of them fell ill, and the illness became increasingly severe, and they were likely to die. I cannot bear seeing them both die, so I will kill myself in front of you. In the afterlife, they would still be joined as a good couple, fulfilling an old wish."

    564

    老物又将奈何耶！”言毕，袖出匕首，长尺有咫，莹然如雪，掷置案头，铮铮有声。指谓卢曰：“我两人结缡廿载，请从此别！”卢瞪视良久，曰：“卿且少安毋躁，容再商议。”曰：“我志已决，复何商议！”曰：“然则允若婚配，卿可不死否？”杜妹笑曰：“果尔，若疾当已，我又何必求死？”卢曰：“如卿所言。卿重母党，尝自誉其侄；我本俗人，不喜白衣女婿。必俟汝侄读书成名，方准亲迎，可乎？”杜妹笑曰：“有何不可？吾侄喜如所愿，必能刻志用功，又何患不成名耶？”卢曰：“若是，汝可归告兄嫂，速遣冰人来，当遵前命。”杜妹乃袖刀而藏。翌日，归告兄嫂，大喜，遂委禽焉。两小闻之，疾俱若失。有美果下帷攻苦，岁试，补博士弟子员；越岁，食饩秋闱，一战捷于乡。杜妹闻报，喜曰：“若今可以亲迎矣。”卢犹欲待礼闱后

    Explanation

    564

    "What will you do with this old thing?" she exclaimed, then drew a dagger from her sleeve, one chi and a zhi long, shining like snow, and tossed it onto the table, where it rang out clearly. Pointing to Lu, she said, "We have been married for twenty years; let us part ways now!" Lu stared at her for a long time before saying, "Please calm down and be patient; let us discuss this further." She replied, "My mind is made up; what more is there to discuss?" He asked, "Then if you agree to the marriage, will you not have to die?" Du Mei laughed and said, "If that is true, your illness should be cured, and why would I seek death?" Lu said, "As you say. You value your maternal relatives and often praise your nephew; I am a commoner and do not like a white-collar son-in-law. I will only agree to the marriage if your nephew studies hard and becomes successful. Is that acceptable?" Du Mei laughed and said, "What is there to object to? My nephew will be happy to do as you wish and will surely study diligently. What reason is there for him not to succeed?" Lu said, "If that is the case, you may return and inform your brother and sister-in-law to quickly send the matchmaker. I will follow the previous agreement." Du Mei then hid the dagger. The next day, she returned and informed her brother and sister-in-law, who were very pleased and proceeded to send the gift of poultry. The two children heard this and became very sad. Youmei diligently studied, passed the annual examination, and became a student of the Imperial Academy. The following year, he passed the provincial examination in the autumn and won in the first attempt. Du Mei heard the news and was delighted, saying, "Now your nephew can be formally married!" Lu still wanted to wait until after the metropolitan examination.

    565

    再议，杜妹抵之曰：“汝始终不免固执！科名迟早难定，吾侄乡荐已属侥幸，人生能得几回侥幸？青春几何，何忍令其孤负耶！”卢不得已，准其亲迎。杜夫妻大喜，遂涓吉为两人毕姻。同里有周生、韦生者，皆名诸生。以杜藏书多，皆幞被下榻其家，与有美同窗肄业，意气相得。周生是科亦登副车，情谊益洽。吉期当九月下旬，杪秋天气，已凉未寒。

    Explanation

    565

    After reconsideration, Du's younger sister replied to him, saying, "You are always stubborn! Whether one passes the imperial examinations early or late is uncertain. My nephew has already passed the provincial examinations by chance, and how many times can one be fortunate in life? Youth is fleeting; how can we let it go to waste?" Lu had no choice but to agree to the arranged marriage. The couple from the Du family were very happy and chose a good day to complete the marriage.
