When they flowed downstream and scattered, secretly occupying Jinling, for more than ten years, along the river region, people had no safe homes, and the slaughter of innocent lives was beyond count.

    692

    试观其猖獗无忌，形同枭獍，不真令人发指哉！有自粤西来者，言众贼实无伎俩，即韦逆而论，以巡检下僚督众往捕，如缚鸡犬，其易可知。似此巡检胆识亦殊过人，而大吏闻之，不赏其功，反怪多事，时事如此，真可浩叹！故当其金田倡乱，都人士惧其被累，亦曾密白邑令，邑令曾密白郡守，郡守曾密白司道巡抚，皆一一瞪目相视，默无一语，大都意存讳盗，粉饰偷安；方且以持重自矜，决不肯轻举妄动，激生事端，致挂吏议。噫嘻！为虺不摧，为蛇奈何！忍之须臾，而孰知后日之祸如此之烈哉！予虎口馀生，备尝艰苦，间关万里，侥幸生还。回忆十馀年来目击流离琐尾情形，时复伤心酸鼻。而推原其始，当日之为封疆大吏者，虽百身亦岂能赎其罪哉！

    Explanation

    692

    Observe how they act with impunity and without restraint, their behavior being like that of a fierce bird of prey and a mythical beast, truly does it not make one's hair stand on end? There was someone who came from the west of Guangdong, saying that the bandits actually have no skill. Even speaking of Wei the rebel, if a low-ranking official in charge of local security were to lead the capture, it would be like tying up chickens and dogs—how easy it is to see. Such a low-ranking official's courage and insight are far beyond the ordinary, yet the high-ranking officials, upon hearing this, did not reward his achievements but instead blamed him for causing trouble. The situation is like this, truly it is a matter for deep sighs! Therefore, when the rebellion began in Jintian, the people of the capital city feared being implicated and secretly reported to the county magistrate. The county magistrate then secretly reported to the prefectural governor, who in turn secretly reported to the provincial officials. All of them stared at each other in silence, none uttering a word, mostly intent on concealing the truth and pretending to be at peace; they prided themselves on being cautious and prudent, refusing to act rashly or recklessly, provoking trouble, and bringing themselves under official scrutiny. Alas! If not crushed when it is still a viper, what can be done when it becomes a snake? To endure for a moment, and who could have known that the calamity of the future would be so severe? I survived by the skin of my teeth, enduring great hardships, traveling a thousand miles through difficulties, fortunately returning alive. Recalling the scenes of wandering and suffering I witnessed over the past ten years, I often felt heartache and shed tears. Tracing back to the beginning, those high-ranking officials in charge of regional administration, even if one had a hundred lives, could they atone for their sins?

    693

    书秦小罗
    　　
    　　初粤寇陷金陵，伪东王杨贼秀清就何绅宦住宅改造伪王府，穷极奢侈。又制广大榻，可容十人，每夕轮选所掠妇女九人伴寝，淫媟无度。妇女之含羞赍忿、抑郁以死者踵相椄也。一日，杨贼夜宴，庖人进面一簋，时贼已中酒，不能食；以食侍者，七窍噀血，立毙。贼惊且怒，以人之谋鸩己也。尽出所有妇女不下千人，发伪官研讯。伪官承贼意旨，将次第严加榜掠。忽一女年可十六七，柳眉蕴怒，杏靥含嗔，袅娜而前，娇声喝曰：“谋鸩杀杨贼者，我秦小罗也！不预他人事。”伪官诘之，曰：“自贼陷城，不幸门惨遭屠戮，我被掳在此，所以暂缓须臾无死者，原欲乘间手刃贼首，以泄家国恨。

    Explanation

    693

    The Book of Qin Xiaoluo

    At the outset, the Taiping rebels captured Nanjing, and the false East King, Yang Xiuqing, transformed the residence of a certain official into a false royal palace, which was extremely extravagant. He also constructed a large bed that could accommodate ten people, and every night he selected nine of the captured women to accompany him in bed, engaging in lewd and unrestrained behavior. The women, bearing shame and anger, died of depression one after another. One day, during a night banquet, a cook served a bowl of food. At the time, Yang Xiuqing was already drunk and unable to eat; he fed the food to an attendant, who spat blood from all seven orifices and died instantly. Yang Xiuqing was frightened and angry, suspecting that someone had plotted to poison him. He then brought out all the women, no less than a thousand, and sent false officials to investigate. The officials followed his intentions and began to severely beat them in sequence. Suddenly, a young woman, about sixteen or seventeen years old, with willow-like eyebrows concealing anger and apricot cheeks holding a frown, gracefully approached and shouted in a soft voice, "The one who plotted to poison and kill Yang Xiuqing is I, Qin Xiaoluo! I am not involved in others' affairs." The false officials questioned her, and she said, "Since the rebels captured the city, my family was unfortunately massacred. I was captured here, and the reason I delayed my death for a moment was to seize an opportunity to kill the rebel leader, to avenge my family and country."

    694

    奈苦无兵械，又不得当，只藏有砒屑少许，为他日自尽地，适见面心动，因潜下之，将图一快意，虽粉骨亦甘心焉。今计不成，数也！然观贼所为必膺天诛，特时未至耳！我死固不足惜，独怪尔曹俨然须眉男子，孰非食毛践土，竟腆颜事贼，为之爪牙，受其豕畜而犬嗾之，一旦天兵云集，丑类尽歼，漏网难期，噬脐莫及！而尔曹误入迷途，不知早为自新，智反出我女子下矣！不亦羞乎？”伪官闻言，俯首愧汗，箝口无语。会杨贼伪旨叠次敦逼问状，伪官不敢讳，遂以实告，贼怒，命寸磔以警众。小罗引颈受刃，全无惧色，贼党见者多窃窃称叹。越日，伪官竟惭悔自经以殉。小罗，名敷，金陵人。伪官姓名里居无可考。

    Explanation

    694

    Nai ku wu bing xie, you bu de dang, zhi cang you pi xie shao xu, wei ta ri zi jin di, shi jian xin dong, yin qian xia zhi, jiang tu yi kuai yi, sui fen gu yi gan xin yan. Jin ji bu cheng, shu ye! Ran guan zei suo wei bi ying tian zhu, te shi wei zhi er! Wo si gu bu zu xi, du guai er cao yan ran xu mei nan zi, shu fei shi mao jian tu, jing tian yan shi zei, wei zhi zhua ya, shou qi shi chu er quan sou zhi, yi dan tian bing yun ji, chou lei jin jian, lou wang nan qi, shi qi mo ji! Er er cao wu ru mi tu, bu zhi zao wei zi xin, zhi fan chu wo nü zi xia yi! Bu yi xiu hu?" Wei guan wen yan, fu shou kui han, qian kou wu yu. Hui Yang zei wei zhi die ci dun bi wen zhuang, wei guan bu gan wei, sui yi shi gao, zei nu, ming cun zhe yi jing zhong. Xiao Luo yin jing shou ren, quan wu ju se, zei dang jian zhe duo qie qie cheng tan. Yue ri, wei guan jing can hui zi jing yi xun. Xiao Luo, ming fu, Jing Ling ren. Wei guan xing ming li ju wu ke kao.

    695

    里乘子曰：方粤寇蔓延，两江总督某以封疆大吏奉命九江防剿，迟迟其行，卒乃掩耳盗铃，开门揖盗，长江上下纵横袤亘千馀里，城郭闾阎悉罹烽燹，彼虽一死，不能蔽辜。试绳其初，心岂有涓埃计及于报国哉！小罗，一女子耳，犹知以泄家国恨为重，同一死也，小罗之死重如泰山矣！君子于是悲小罗有杀贼之心，无杀贼之力；而又叹两江总督有杀贼之力，无杀贼之心。天下事何其相左也！观小罗所言能使伪官惭悔自经，而凡天下之为须眉男子者，能不闻风兴起乎！

    Explanation

    695

    Li Chengzi said: When the Canton rebels were spreading, a certain governor-general of the Two Rivers, as a high-ranking official, was ordered to defend and suppress the rebels in Jiujiang, yet he delayed his departure. In the end, he acted like one who covers his ears while stealing a bell and opened the door to welcome robbers. The Yangtze River, stretching over a thousand miles from north to south, saw all cities, walls, and neighborhoods suffer the devastation of war. Though he died, he could not escape his guilt. If we examine his initial intentions, could his heart have had even the slightest thought of serving the country? Xiaoluo, merely a woman, still understood that avenging the sorrow of the nation was most important. Though it is the same death, Xiaoluo’s death was as weighty as Mount Tai! The virtuous man thus laments that Xiaoluo had the will to kill the rebels but lacked the power, and laments further that the governor-general of the Two Rivers had the power to kill the rebels but lacked the will. How opposite are the affairs of the world! Observing Xiaoluo’s words, one could make the false officials feel ashamed and remorseful, even to the point of taking their own lives. How could any man in the world not rise up upon hearing of this?

    696

    书傅善祥
    　　
    　　傅善祥者，亦金陵女子之授伪职为女丞相者也。容华媚冶，资性儇黠。幼读书，粗知大义，诗赋文词颇能畅晓。年十六，嫁同邑王生。生故寒素，为人朴钝，齿逾弱冠，犹艰于一衿。傅自叹所适不淑，每庸奴其夫。生心虽嗛之，而口不敢言。尝应童子试，被黜归，傅适在门，他顾若无睹，生恚诟之。傅诮让之曰：“囨，休矣！为丈夫者，年二十许尚不能博一衿，犹负气吓婆子，复何为者！可惜妾非丈夫耳！假若妾易钗而冠，莫道一衿，即掇取科第亦易易也。囨，休矣！作此丑态谁耐看耶！”生闻言，益恨恨，由此，夫妻积不相能。既而粤寇陷金陵，生亡去不知所之；傅被掳在伪东王府。杨贼既悦其色，傅又自衒其才，贼大加宠任，官以女丞相，凡伪文往复，皆归司掌。

    Explanation

    696

    Book of Fu Shanxiang

    Fu Shanxiang, a woman from Nanjing who was appointed to the position of Female Chancellor in the usurper regime, was beautiful and charming, with a clever and cunning nature. She read books from a young age and had a basic understanding of principles. She was quite proficient in poetry, prose, and literary expression. At the age of sixteen, she married Wang, a man from her hometown. Wang was poor and simple, lacking in intelligence and refinement, and even beyond the age of twenty, he still struggled to pass the imperial examinations. Fu lamented that she had married poorly and often treated her husband as a common laborer. Although Wang resented this, he dared not speak up. Once, when Wang took the children's examination and was rejected, Fu was at the gate and ignored him as if she hadn't seen him. Wang was angry and scolded her. Fu rebuked him, saying, "Fool! At your age, you still can't pass the examination, yet you still have the arrogance to intimidate a woman. What are you good for? Fortunately, I am not a man! If I were a man, passing the examination would be easy, let alone getting a degree. Fool! Who would want to see such a disgraceful act!" Wang, upon hearing this, became even more resentful, and thus, their relationship deteriorated. Later, when the Taiping rebels captured Nanjing, Wang fled and was never seen again. Fu was captured and taken to the East Palace of the usurper regime. Yang Xiuqing, the East King, was pleased with her appearance, and Fu also boasted of her talents. The rebel leader greatly favored her, appointing her as Female Chancellor and entrusting her with managing all official documents.

    697

    杨贼与傅尝同饮，酒酣，戏抚傅背作骄态曰：“孤以匹夫起市井中，提三尺剑从粤西顺流而下，所过郡县，闻风奔溃。今雄踞江南，贵为东王，偶一声呼，全军听命。后宫美人充下陈者以千计，大丈夫处世如斯，亦足矣！以卿视孤亦可谓一世之雄也耶？”傅时亦微醺，恃宠忘形，不觉吃吃笑答曰：“囨，休矣！尔不过粤西一小贼，幸逢劫数，乘间窃发，才窜得江南一隅地。内受制于天王，外受困于官兵，而犹自足骄人，复何为者！可惜妾非丈夫耳！假使妾易钗而冠，莫道江南，即纵横天下，亦易易也！囨，休矣！作此丑态谁耐看耶！”贼初以傅必迎合其词而加谀焉，今闻所言，殊出意外，乃勃然变色，怒叱曰：“贱婢！安敢侮孤耶！”立命幽诸空室，迟明加诛不贷。诘旦，伪执法者请行刑，杨贼悔之，而赧于反齿，漫应曰：“贱婢侮孤，罪固不赦，但系女官，不敢自专，是不得不请命于天王。

    Explanation

    697

    Yang Xiuqing, the rebel leader, once shared a drinking session with Fu, his concubine. When the wine was flowing, he playfully patted her back in a proud manner and said, "I rose from the common people, carrying a three-foot sword, advancing from western Guangxi downstream. The counties and prefectures I passed through fled at the sound of my approach. Now I dominate the south and am honored as the Eastern King. A single command from me, and the entire army obeys. There are thousands of beautiful women in my harem. A man living in this way is also sufficient! Do you think I can be considered a hero of his age?" At the time, Fu was also slightly drunk, feeling favored and forgetting her place, and she laughed and replied, "Stop it! You are just a small rebel from western Guangxi. By chance, you seized the opportunity to rise up and managed to occupy a corner of the south. Internally, you are controlled by the Heavenly King, and externally, you are constrained by the soldiers, yet you still boast and show off. What else is there to do? It's a pity that I am not a man! If I were to change from a hairpin to a hat, not only would I dominate the south, but I could also roam freely across the world! Stop it! Who could bear to see such a shameful act?" Initially, Yang Xiuqing thought Fu would flatter him, but hearing her words, he was surprised and became furious, his face turning red with anger. He shouted, "You lowly woman! How dare you insult me?" He immediately ordered her to be confined in an empty room and decreed that she would be punished without mercy the next morning. The next day, the pretend law enforcer requested to carry out the execution. Yang Xiuqing regretted his decision but was ashamed to retract his words. He carelessly responded, "The lowly woman insulted me, and her crime is indeed unforgivable. However, since she is a female official, I cannot act alone. Therefore, I must seek the Heavenly King's command."

    698

    ”遂具状上于伪天王。洪贼秀全以事无大小，皆杨处分，此细故，何独请命？沉思良久，笑曰：“我知之矣。”爰降伪诏曰：“按状，女丞相傅醉侮东王，大不敬，罪应立决；但念其颇耆微劳，且属酒后失言，姑恩从末减。着荷校，七次礼拜而宥释之，仍许复官办公自赎。”贼中故奉左道，七日礼拜一次，七次，盖四十有九日云。杨贼接伪诏大喜，即命将傅荷校于中庭。傅亦深有怨悔，因取杨贼旧所贻金条脱一双，解自所着红罗诃黎子裹好，遣左右赍还杨贼，并附以书曰：殿左女丞相臣妾傅善祥诚惶诚恐，死罪死罪，上书东王殿下。窃以臣妾遭逢高厚，福薄灾生，醉后失检，出言无状，冒犯于王，主赐臣妾死，臣妾何敢爱其生？今乃曲施矜全，不加诛戮，仅予荷检，聊示薄罚。

    Explanation

    698

    Then, he prepared a written report and submitted it to the false Heavenly King. Hong Xiuquan, upon receiving the report, reflected that all matters, regardless of their size, were handled by Yang Xiuqing. He pondered, "This is a minor matter, why should you seek my permission?" After deep contemplation, he smiled and said, "I understand now." He then issued a false imperial decree stating: "According to the report, the female chancellor Fu, while drunk, disrespected the East King, committing a grave act of disrespect. The crime should be punished immediately; however, considering her modest service and that it was a slip of the tongue after drinking, I will show leniency and reduce the punishment. She shall wear a yoke, and after seven times of worship, she will be forgiven and released, still allowed to resume her official duties to redeem herself." In the rebel camp, they followed the left path, worshipping once every seven days. Seven times amounts to forty-nine days. Yang Xiuqing, upon receiving the false imperial decree, was greatly pleased and immediately ordered Fu to wear the yoke in the courtyard. Fu also deeply regretted and felt resentment, and she took out a pair of gold bracelets that Yang had given in the past, unwrapped them from the red silk cloth she was wearing, and sent someone to deliver them back to Yang, attaching a letter that read: "I, the female chancellor of the left palace, Fu Shanyang, am deeply fearful and trembling, and commit a grave offense, writing to the East King. I humbly submit that I have encountered great favor, but due to my thin fortune, misfortune has occurred. After drinking, I lost my composure and spoke improperly, offending the king. If the king grants me death, how dare I cherish my life? Now you show leniency, do not punish me, and only order me to wear the yoke, showing a slight punishment."

    699

    臣妾再生，实王所赐，犬马有知，能无感激！惟是臣妾自获罪以来，五中忧惧，神思惝怳，如失魂魄；藐兹孱弱，断难久存。蒲柳之质，虽见宥于风霜；蝼蚁之命，究莫保于旦暮。在臣妾猥以女流，忝叨异数，平章巾帼，宅揆绮罗，荣幸已极，死复何憾？但念未列妃嫔之队，而宠逾粉黛三千，特膺宰辅之权，而报乏涓埃万一。怅对玉葭，难倚托于今世；愿求环草，当衔结于来生。金条脱一双，王所嘉赉，临死遣使赍缴，裹以自着红罗诃黎子，亵服不恭，藉寓亲爱。倘王异日垂念微劳，见物如见臣妾可也。伏惟起居，万福珍摄千金。不尽欲言，祈赐省览焉。杨贼循诵来书，摩弄二物，心大不忍，急踊屣出，见傅荷校垂泪，如虫网幽花，轻盈带雨，益觉楚楚可怜。遂不终日，命劈其校，遑待七次礼拜乎！傅既脱校，将屈膝谢杨贼，掖而止之，仍以金条脱为纳两腕，携手而归。自是宠爱较前有加。

    Explanation

    699

    Regenerated as a servant, truly a gift from the King. If a dog or horse had knowledge, they could not help but feel grateful! Since I have been disgraced, I have been filled with five internal fears, my spirit wandering and confused, as if losing my soul; how frail and weak, definitely cannot last long. A body like a willow or reed, even though spared from the wind and frost; a life like that of an ant or a grasshopper, ultimately cannot be protected for even a day or night. Unworthy of such special treatment, I have managed and advised women, overseen and managed beautiful garments, honor has reached its peak, what regret can there be in death? But thinking about not being part of the imperial concubines, yet favored more than the three thousand beauties, especially granted the authority of a chancellor, yet lacking even a tiny fraction of what is owed. Wistfully facing jade reeds, difficult to rely on in this world; wishing to seek a ring of grass, to carry it as a bond in the next life. A pair of golden bracelets, a gift from the King, sent just before death with an envoy to return, wrapped in a red silk robe, casual attire showing disrespect, to express love and affection. If the King ever remembers my small efforts, seeing the object as if seeing me. May the King's daily activities, blessed with a thousandfold protection. Unable to express all that I wish to say, I hope the King will take the time to read it.

    Yang the traitor reads the letter, handling the two items, feeling very reluctant, rushing out in haste. Seeing Fu carrying the yoke and crying, like a spider caught in a flower, light and graceful with rain, even more pitiable. Without finishing the day, ordering to break the yoke, without waiting for seven times to bow! Fu has been released from the yoke, about to kneel and thank Yang the traitor, holding back and stopping him, still using the golden bracelets to bind the two wrists, holding hands and returning. From then on, the favor was even greater.

    700

    里乘子曰：善祥始以恃才而反目于夫，继以炫才而辱身于贼。甚矣！女子之不可有才也！吾独怪与秦小罗生同里闬，乃一则以图贼而甘蹈于死，一则以媚贼而苦求其生，迹其两人行事，一薰一莸，真判若天渊矣！试为顾名思义，小罗固不难继美于罗敷；若善祥者，则无善可称，不祥莫大焉！

    Explanation

    700

    Lichengzi said: Shanxiang, at first, relied on her talent and turned against her husband, and later, she boasted of her talent and disgraced herself in the presence of bandits. How terrible! It is indeed terrible for women to have talent! I find it particularly strange that she lives in the same neighborhood as Qin Xiaoluosheng. One plotted against the bandits and willingly faced death, while the other flattered the bandits and desperately sought to survive. Tracing the actions of these two women, one is virtuous and the other is wicked, truly as different as heaven and earth! Let us consider the meaning of their names: Qin Xiaoluosheng certainly did not have difficulty following in the footsteps of Luo Fu; as for Shanxiang, there is nothing good to say about her, and the greatest misfortune could not be greater.

    701

    易容之
    　　
    　　粤东有豪杰之士易容之者，少孤贫。十二岁时，为驻防汉军刘氏家牧马，性廉介，除岁入佣工钱三五千文，一介不取。刘家甚爱之。弱冠，忽不辞而去。刘使人踪迹之，竟不可得。越年馀，刘仆过委巷，见易于此设地市，罗列无多长物，生涯亦殊冷落。仆笑问曰：“子胡为者？主人遇汝不薄，胡为不辞而去也？”易但笑而不答。仆又曰：“自汝去后，主人使人遍求之不得，思汝殊甚。我观汝生涯亦殊冷落，盍偕我归，以塞主人之思，可乎？”易摇首曰：“我志非人所能测也。”仆请其说。易日：“始吾在主人家，衣食温饱，非不安逸；即思人生在世，当自立门户，寄人宇下，究非长策。吾岁入佣工钱历年积得四十馀千，小权子母，当不至冻饿。

    Explanation

    701

    In the eastern region of Guangdong, there was a distinguished individual named Yi Rongzhi. He was young and orphaned, living in poverty. At the age of twelve, he was hired by the Liu family, a military unit stationed in the area, to herd horses. He was honest and upright, and besides his agreed annual wage of three to five thousand coins, he did not take even a single coin. The Liu family loved him very much. When he reached the age of twenty, he suddenly left without saying goodbye. The Liu family sent someone to track him down, but they could not find him. After more than a year, a servant of the Liu family passed through a narrow alley and saw Yi Rongzhi setting up a market stall, displaying few valuable items, and his livelihood was quite bleak. The servant smiled and asked, "What are you doing here? The master treated you well, why did you leave without saying goodbye?" Yi Rongzhi just smiled and did not answer. The servant then said, "Since you left, the master has sent people to search for you everywhere but could not find you. He misses you very much. I see that your livelihood is quite bleak. Why don't you come back with me to satisfy the master's longing?" Yi Rongzhi shook his head and said, "My intentions are beyond human understanding." The servant asked for an explanation. Yi Rongzhi said, "When I was with the master, I had enough food and clothing, and it was not uncomfortable. But I thought that in life, one should be self-reliant and not depend on others, as that is not a long-term strategy. I have saved enough money over the years to avoid freezing and starving."

    702

    吾志既决，意辞主人必慰留不令遽去，不如不辞之为得也。吾在此设地市一年有馀，日出而出，日入而入，优哉游哉，意甚适也。而谓我肯再偕汝归乎！主人遇我厚，我非恝然忘情者。烦君为我谢主人，图报有日，请勿以小人为念。”仆太息而别。归语主人，知易志已决，难以挽回，亦遂置之。刘仆去后，易虑刘自来相嬲，迁避他巷，仍设地市如初。忽有夷贾过此，相易良久，便拱手诘问氏族，易怪其唐突，姑曼应之。夷贾谓易日：“予幼至中华，爱读麻衣相书，颇深窥其秘，相君之面，富不可言，乃局促作此生活，货与人殊不相称，奈何？”易益怪其言不伦，冷笑对曰：“我所有资本只可办此货，谚所谓‘量布为衣，量米为炊’是也。何劳见笑！”夷贾曰：“我非敢笑君也。

    Explanation

    702

    My resolve is already made, and I expected the master would surely comfort and retain me, not allowing me to leave immediately; it would be better not to say goodbye. I have been setting up a market stall here for more than a year, going out when the sun rises and returning when it sets, leisurely and content, my mind is very at ease. And you think I would willingly go back with you? The master treated me kindly, and I am not one who can be indifferent or forgetful of feelings. Please convey my thanks to the master, there will be a time for repayment, please do not consider me as insignificant. The servant sighed and parted, returning and told the master, knowing that Yi's resolve was already made, difficult to change, and then left it aside. After Liu the servant left, Yi moved to another alley, fearing Liu would come to bother, still setting up the market stall as before. Suddenly an exotic merchant passed by here, looking at each other for a long time, then bowed and asked about the family name. Yi found it rude and temporarily responded calmly. The exotic merchant said to Yi, "I came to China as a child, loved to read the Ma Yi physiognomy book, deeply understood its secrets, judging by your face, you are extremely wealthy, yet you live such a confined life, the goods you sell do not match your appearance, what to do?" Yi found his words unreasonable and coldly replied, "My capital is only enough to handle this merchandise, as the saying goes, 'measure cloth for clothing, measure rice for cooking.' What need is there to laugh at me!" The exotic merchant said, "I am not daring to laugh at you."

    703

    我在某处开张洋行，洋货颇多，君如不见弃，可在我行中拣选数种置此出售，不亦可乎？”易谢曰：“承君盛意，非敢见拒。我就资办货，货虽贱，固是己物，出售与否，可以自便。尊货皆珍贵之品，如不能出售，徒骇人目；且万一误令损坏，何以偿君？君虽有盛意，不敢从命。”夷贾叹曰：“君真至诚大君子也！然观君相，断非长贫贱者，君好为之，珍重！”叮咛而去。易思贾所言，以为戏己，自念茕茕一身，并无戚党依傍提携，何由致富？亡何，夷贾又来，易厌其絮聒，不甚为礼。夷贾笑谓易曰：“君尚忆吾言否？”易冷笑颔之。夷贾径前执易袂曰：“君勿见哂，我相人穷通，百不失一，年来默相粤中可致巨富者，惟君一人。吾合与君有缘，他日别君归，胸中竟刻不能忘，今特来与君熟商，君肯从我言乎？”易怃然请闻。

    Explanation

    703

    In one place, I opened a foreign trading house, which had a considerable amount of foreign goods. If you do not reject me, you could select several items from my store to sell here, would it not be possible?" Yi thanked him, saying, "I appreciate your kind intentions, but I dare not refuse. I will provide capital for the goods, and although they may be cheap, they are still my property. Whether to sell them or not, I can decide for myself. Your goods are all valuable items. If they cannot be sold, they will only shock people; and if by chance they are damaged, how would I compensate you? Although you have good intentions, I dare not follow your orders." The foreign merchant sighed and said, "You are truly a sincere and virtuous gentleman! However, looking at your appearance, you will certainly not remain poor and lowly for long. You should pursue this, take care!" He then left after giving some advice. Yi thought the merchant's words were just playing with him, and he reflected that he was alone with no relatives or close associates to support him, so how could he become wealthy? Not long after, the foreign merchant came again. Yi was tired of his incessant talking and did not treat him with much respect. The foreign merchant smiled and asked Yi, "Do you still remember what I said?" Yi coldly nodded. The foreign merchant stepped forward and took Yi's sleeve, saying, "Do not laugh at me; I can judge a person's fortune or misfortune by their appearance, and I have never been wrong. In recent years, I have secretly observed in Guangdong and found that only you can become extremely wealthy. I believe we have a special connection. When I leave and return home, I could not help but remember you in my heart. Today, I have come specifically to discuss this with you. Will you follow my advice?" Yi felt disappointed and asked to hear more.

    704

    夷贾曰：“君不肯携吾行货物来此，足见君至诚，我亦不敢相强。但吾所开之行，门前隙地甚属宽敞，敢请君迁至行前开设地市，就近将吾行货物酌检代为出售，所售货物只归我资本，如有利息，一概归君，可乎？”易见其言谆恳，似非戏己者，沉思久之，乃拱手谢曰：“屡承下顾，深感垂爱。既辱相招，敢不如命。”夷贾大喜，订日使人为易迁至其地。初，粤中洋行共十有三家，此行实居其首。易既迁其地，每代售货物，即归其资，不稍短少。夷贾喜其至诚，情日益亲。不五六年，易获利息已数十馀万金，即存寄行中。顾深藏若虚，虽获巨资，外人多不知者。一日，夷贾忽具柬招饮，肆筵设席，并无别客。饮酣，夷贾笑谓易曰：“君知我招饮之意乎？”易笑谢曰：“窃深讶君客气，正欲请命。”夷贾叹曰：“以君至诚，故敢以心腹相告。我族祚薄丁单，自高曾而下，仅得四人。

    Explanation

    704

    The foreign merchant said, "Sir, if you are unwilling to bring my goods here, it clearly shows your sincerity, and I dare not force you. However, the space in front of my shop is quite spacious. May I ask you to move there and open a market? You can select and sell my goods on your behalf. The goods sold will only belong to my capital, and if there is any profit, it will all belong to you. Is that acceptable?" Yi found the merchant's words sincere and not meant to deceive him, so he thought for a long time and then bowed his hands and thanked, "I have often received your favor and deeply appreciate your kindness. Having been honored by your invitation, I dare not disobey your command." The foreign merchant was very pleased and agreed on a day to have people move Yi there. Initially, there were thirteen foreign trading houses in Guangdong, and this trading house was indeed the first among them. After Yi moved there, every time he sold goods on behalf of the merchant, he would return the capital, not shortchanging at all. The foreign merchant was delighted with Yi's sincerity, and their relationship grew closer over time. Within five or six years, Yi had earned tens of thousands of gold in profits, which he kept hidden, and few outsiders knew about it. One day, the foreign merchant suddenly sent an invitation to drink, laid out a feast, and there were no other guests. After drinking deeply, the foreign merchant smiled and said to Yi, "Do you know why I invited you to drink?" Yi replied, "I secretly deeply admire your politeness and wanted to ask you." The foreign merchant sighed, "Because of your sincerity, I dare to tell you my innermost thoughts. My family's fortune is thin, and the number of descendants is small. From the high and distant ancestors, there are only four people."

    705

    只我有二子，其余三人为我伯叔行，齿皆逾六旬，景迫桑榆，各拥厚资，苦无嗣息。频年叠接家书，促我遄归，委以后事。自我至中国贸易，今将三十年，已一世矣。所获利约计三千万金有奇，屡欲赋归，以行务无人可托，故迟迟未决。今幸得君，敢烦作替，未知君能许我乎？”易蹙然对曰：“年来谬承厚爱，视同骨肉，如能效力，断不敢辞。窃思行务非同细事，自顾年少材轻，深恐难肩重任。虽君有命，不敢与闻。”夷贾曰：“我筹之烂熟，目前克肩此任，舍君更无其人。且与君约：我此次归去，重来与否不能预定，如三年不来，君即为此行主人，所有货物资本，概以相赠，一切君自主持，无庸迟回疑虑。”命纪纲将各款底簿交易，并召各款司事人等齐至席前。

    Explanation

    705

    Only I have two sons, while the other three are my older brothers and uncles, all over sixty years old. With the sunset of their lives approaching, each possesses substantial wealth but suffers from the lack of heirs. Over the years, I have received frequent family letters urging me to return home quickly and entrust me with the after matters. Since I came to China for trade, it has been nearly thirty years, already a lifetime. The profit I have gained is approximately three million and some gold. I have often wanted to return home, but because there is no one to entrust the business to, I have delayed the decision. Now fortunately, I have you. Dare I trouble you to take my place? Do you know if you can grant me this?” Yi responded with a worried expression: “In recent years, I have misleadingly received great affection, treating me as flesh and blood. If I can be of service, I certainly would not decline. Secretly, I think that business affairs are not trivial matters. Looking at myself, young and lacking ability, I deeply fear being unable to bear heavy responsibilities. Even if you command, I would not dare to participate.” The foreign merchant said: “I have thought it through thoroughly. Currently, I can shoulder this responsibility, and without you, there is no one else. And I make an agreement with you: this time I will return, but whether I come back again cannot be predetermined. If I do not come back within three years, you will be the owner of this business. All goods and capital will be given to you. You will take full charge of everything, no need to hesitate or doubt.” He ordered the steward to bring the account books and transaction records and summoned all the staff involved in the business to gather before the table.

    706

    夷贾大声谓众曰：“我已择日归去，行务已托易君料理。易君即汝曹之主，勤者赏，惰者罚，如敢慢侮，即行黜退，定不宽贷！”言毕，命众当筵拜易为主人，众果交口诺诺，罗拜在地。易仓卒不能置词，避席答拜，踧不安。夷贾遽牵易袂，按令就坐，笑曰：“君休矣。若曹礼所当拜，何多让也？”易见夷贾所言真实不虚，遂亦不复固辞。夷贾归后，瞬逾三年，众料其不来，遂共奉易为行主人。先是，行中岁有十馀艘，各载重资分至各国采买货物，历年以来，共计尚有二十馀艘去无音耗，佥谓飓风漂没，不复为念。自易接事，三年内次第齐归，合计行中所存货物，通共不下二千万金，生业日益蕃茂。易年已二十有六，尚矜居无耦，执柯者日趾相错，易概拒绝之。佥力劝当以似续为计，易笑曰：“诸君言皆珠玉，顾仆不娶则已，娶则以多为贵。

    Explanation

    706

    The foreign merchant spoke loudly to the assembly, saying, "I have already chosen a day to return, and the affairs of the business have been entrusted to Master Yi to manage. Master Yi is now your master: the diligent shall be rewarded, the lazy shall be punished. If anyone dares to be disrespectful or contemptuous, they will be immediately dismissed, and I shall certainly not show leniency!" After speaking, he ordered the assembly to kneel and pay homage to Yi as their new master. The crowd indeed responded with unanimous assent, prostrating themselves in a circle on the ground. Yi, taken aback and unable to respond, left his seat to bow in return, feeling awkward and uneasy. The foreign merchant hastily grasped Yi's sleeve and pressed him back into his seat, smiling and saying, "You may rest now. If the etiquette demands that you kneel, why are you hesitating so much?" Seeing that the foreign merchant's words were true and not exaggerated, Yi no longer insisted on declining the position. After the foreign merchant returned home, in less than three years, the others had expected him not to return, and so they collectively honored Yi as the master of the business. Previously, the enterprise had operated a fleet of more than ten ships each year, which carried heavy capital to various countries to purchase goods. Over the years, more than twenty ships had gone out without returning, and all believed they had been lost to typhoons, no longer giving them a second thought. But since Yi took charge, within three years, all the ships returned one after another. Calculating the total value of goods stored within the enterprise, it amounted to no less than twenty million taels of silver, and the business grew increasingly prosperous. Yi was now twenty-six years old, yet still remained unmarried, proud of his single status. Matchmakers came in a daily stream, but Yi rejected them all. The others strongly advised him to consider marriage for the continuation of the family line. Yi smiled and said, "All your words are as precious as jade and pearls, but I will either not marry at all, or if I do, I shall seek a wife of high quality."

    707

    仆自念出身微贱，大家世族难以系援，小家子又非仆所愿。就今别创一格，如合仆意，聘资虽万馀金不惜也。”佥问：“何如？”易曰：“所聘必须年甫及笄之处女，德言工貌俱备，然尤以能识字为要；如不识字，虽备四德，无取也。如诸君不以仆言为妄，即请代为物色，当图厚谢。”佥应曰：“诺。”于是，三年之内，先后共聘及笄女郎四十人，齿序姊妹，不论嫡庶。女郎果皆识字，奁次各置字典一部，六部律例一部。易本不识多字，自是即以女郎为师，每日轮次当夕，必先令其教字若干，口诵律例一条，而后就寝。易年至四十，凡字典之字及各部律例，皆能畅晓大义。且喜诸女郎俱各生育，共举男子八十馀人，女子五十馀人。览揆之日，易大合乐，召客为寿。

    Explanation

    707

    I reflect on my humble origins, finding it difficult to seek support from prominent families, and small families do not align with my wishes. Now, I will create a new category: if it suits my intentions, I would not hesitate to spend tens of thousands of gold as a dowry. They asked, "What do you think?" I replied, "The women to be hired must be young girls just reaching the age of marriage, possessing virtue, speech, skills, and appearance, but knowing how to read and write is especially important; if they cannot read, even if they possess the four virtues, they are not suitable. If you gentlemen do not consider my words as empty, please help me find them, and I will offer a generous reward." They agreed, saying, "Yes." Thus, within three years, I hired forty young girls of marriageable age, treating them as sisters regardless of whether they were born in or out of wedlock. The girls indeed knew how to read, and each of their dowries included a dictionary and a copy of the legal codes of the six ministries. I, who did not know many characters, took the girls as my teachers, and each day, in turn, they taught me several characters and recited a law code passage before we went to bed. By the time I was forty, I understood the meanings of all the characters in the dictionary and the legal codes of the six ministries. Moreover, I am pleased that all the girls have given birth, raising more than eighty boys and over fifty girls. On my birthday, I held a grand musical performance and invited guests to celebrate my longevity.

    708

    席罢，易揖谓众客曰：“仆犬马齿甫及四十，何敢言寿？今不过特藉贱辰，招诸君痛饮，兼以话别耳。”佥问：“将何之？”易曰：“《礼》云：‘四十曰强而仕。’仆俨然丈夫，正当强仕。况时方多事，倘但偷安，不力图报效，录录浮沉，是与草木何异？仆决意罢贾而仕，行将入都就铨矣。”四座闻之，无不唯唯称叹。翌日，易果具牍上制军，告退行务。计所积尚千馀万金，乃卜筑四十所，分置诸姬，各领所生子女相依以居。子各给白金五万两，女半之，交其母置产生息，以为教养、婚嫁之资。馀尚有数百万金，概施作善事，并分赠友朋。自所存不上十万金，纳资郡守，孑身入都，铨得楚南常德府太守。一麾之任，会粤寇下宰，易战御有方，斩馘无算。卒以贼众我寡，咸丰七年，以粮尽援绝殉难毕命。督抚上其事，恤典甚厚，并予以云骑尉世袭罔替。呜呼！如易君者，真可谓豪杰之士矣！

    Explanation

    708

    After the banquet, Yi bowed to the assembled guests and said, “My dog’s and horse’s teeth have just reached forty — how dare I speak of longevity? Today I am merely taking advantage of this humble occasion to invite all of you to drink heartily, and also to say our goodbyes.” All asked, “Where are you going?” Yi replied, “The Rites says, ‘At forty, one is strong and should take up official duties.’ I am now a full-grown man, and this is precisely the time to enter public service. Moreover, the times are fraught with troubles. If I were to merely seek comfort in ease, not strive to serve the state, and simply drift along without purpose, what would that be but no different from a plant or tree? I have resolved to abandon commerce and take up official service, and I shall soon go to the capital to take the examination.” Upon hearing this, all the guests nodded in agreement and praised him. The next day, Yi indeed prepared a formal document and submitted it to the commander-in-chief, resigning from his commercial affairs. Calculating the amount he had accumulated, it still exceeded ten million taels of silver. He then built forty residences, distributing them among his concubines, with each living with the children born to her, supporting one another. Each son was given fifty thousand taels of silver, and the daughters received half that amount, to be given to their mothers to invest and generate interest, as funds for their upbringing, education, and marriage. The remainder, still amounting to several million taels, he donated entirely for charitable causes and distributed among his friends. What he kept for himself did not exceed one hundred thousand taels, which he paid as a fee to the county magistrate, and then set off alone to the capital. He was appointed as the magistrate of the Chunan Changde Prefecture. Upon assuming his post, he encountered the Canton rebels descending upon the area. Yi fought with skill and strategy, slaying countless enemies. In the end, however, due to the enemy’s overwhelming numbers and our forces being few, in the seventh year of the Xianfeng era, he perished in the face of starvation and lack of reinforcements. The provincial governors and administrators reported his deeds, and the posthumous honors bestowed upon him were very generous, including the hereditary title of Yunqiwei, which could be passed down without interruption. Alas! A man like Yi was truly a hero and a man of outstanding character.

    709

    里乘子曰：易君自谓“我志非人所能测”，迹其生平行事，是真令人不测也。观其娶妻以多为贵，尤以识字为要，奁次置书两部，十馀年皆能畅晓大义，是以妻为师，娶妻别创一格，从师更别创一格，可谓妙人妙想。尤喜所娶之女，各有生育，即酌给教养、婚嫁之资，令子女依母而居，安排既定，丝毫无所系念。所馀巨资一旦挥霍尽净，孑身入官，慨然不惜捐躯就义。光争日月，大节皭然，谓之豪杰之士，不亦宜乎！易君不忘故主之德，得志后，以女妻刘馨石观察之子小馨太守。小馨与吾友徐公可司马为儿女姻娅。公可尝道易君事甚详，且属为作传，姑笔其大略如此。

    Explanation

    709

    Yi Jun said: "My intentions are beyond what others can measure." Tracing his life and deeds, it is indeed true that his actions were unpredictable. He valued having many wives, especially those who could read, and placed two sets of books in the bridal chamber. After many years, all his wives could understand the main ideas of the books. He treated his wives as teachers, establishing a new standard in marriage, and also created a new pattern in the way he learned from them. He was truly a clever and original person. He was particularly pleased that each of his wives had children, and he provided funds for their education and marriage, arranging for them to live with their mothers. Once everything was arranged, he had no further concerns. He spent all his remaining wealth and went to the government office alone, ready to sacrifice himself for a cause. He shone brightly like the sun and moon, with a clear and noble character. To call him a hero is fitting. Yi Jun did not forget the kindness of his former master, and after achieving success, he married his daughter to Xiao Xinyuan, the son of Liu Xingshi, an observer. Xiao Xinyuan is related by marriage to my friend Xu Gongke, a military officer. Xu Gongke spoke in great detail about Yi Jun's life and asked me to write a biography. Here is a brief summary of his life.

    710

    江西刘某
    　　
    　　江西刘某，世家子也。家富饶而性刚直，喜蓄舞女歌童，豪放不检。壮岁以团练功荐擢监司，益盛气自负。遇里党是非曲直，片言立断，罔敢不遵。乡人以为武断，皆严惮侧目，而无可如何也。里有甲与乙者，家皆素封。甲女端姑与乙子长郎同年生，襁褓中即缔婚好。长郎性极慧，十一二岁头角峥嵘，居然能文。以通家故时至甲家，甲夫妇绝爱怜之。端姑发才覆额，眉目如画，两小相值亦不回避。虽无所知识，而亲慕殊切，见者佥称佳耦焉。无何，粤寇下窜，甲闻警，预挚家室辇资远避，乙一家被掳陷贼中。贼平，甲归，探乙无音耗，意已流亡。而于是端姑年且十七矣，貌益娟好，女红之暇且粗识字，晓大义。甲谓妻曰：“乙家殆填沟壑矣。端儿齿日长，死守非计，不如择人改字为善。

    Explanation

    710

    Liu of Jiangxi

    Liu of Jiangxi was a son of a prominent family. His household was prosperous, and his nature was upright and unbending; he enjoyed keeping female dancers and male singers, and lived a bold and unrestrained life. In his prime, he was recommended and promoted to the position of supervisor due to his military service and merits, and he grew even more arrogant and self-important. When matters of right and wrong arose in the local community, he would make a decision with a single word, and none dared to disobey. The villagers regarded him as arbitrary and authoritarian, and all feared and looked upon him with suspicion, yet they could do nothing about it.

    In the village there were two families, one called Jia and the other Yi, both of whom were wealthy. Jia’s daughter, Duan Gu, and Yi’s son, Chang Lang, were born in the same year, and their marriage was arranged while they were still infants. Chang Lang was exceptionally bright; by the age of eleven or twelve, he was already showing remarkable promise and could write with ease. Because their families were related, he often visited Jia’s home, and Jia’s husband and wife deeply loved and cherished him. Duan Gu’s hair had just begun to cover her forehead, her eyebrows and eyes were like painted portraits, and when the two children met, they did not shy away from each other. Though they had no knowledge of the world, their affection for one another was intense and sincere, and those who saw them all praised them as an ideal couple.

    Not long after, rebels from the south began to flee southward, and Jia, upon hearing the alarm, prepared in advance to take his family and move his wealth far away to escape. The entire Yi family, however, was captured and fell into the hands of the rebels. When the rebels were finally pacified, Jia returned home and inquired about the Yi family, but found no news of them, and assumed they had either perished or fled. At this time, Duan Gu was nearly seventeen years old, and her beauty had grown even more striking. In her leisure time, when not engaged in needlework, she could read a little and understood the principles of right and wrong. Jia said to his wife, “The Yi family is likely to have perished. Duan Er is growing older by the day, and it is not a good plan to keep her waiting. It would be better to choose another husband and remarry her for the sake of her future.”

    711

    ”妻曰：“固然，长郎吾甚爱怜之，万一归来，奈何？请待三年，端儿年二十，如尚无音耗，改字未晚。”先是，乙陷贼中，夫妇相继殁，惟长郎独存。贼爱其能文，而恐其逸，使多贼逻守之。我兵克复金陵，歼其魁，长郎乘间得脱。转徙乞食还乡里，以庐舍灰烬，怅无所归。闻甲乱后出资营良田腴产，较前倍殷富，念平日与父至契，且系妇翁，暂往相依，当无不纳。既如甲家，甲闻大惊，欲拒勿纳。妻让之曰：“君休矣。端儿字长郎，里党无不知者。不来则已，郎来欲与之绝，如人言何？”甲不得已，躧屣出。长郎见甲，伏地而泣。甲佯喜，携长郎手起，谓曰：“始老夫遍探郎音耗不可得，夫妻望眼欲穿，虑郎不克生还矣。郎归大好，殊慰老夫望。顾汝父母何若也？”长郎又泣告所以。

    Explanation

    711

    The wife said, "Indeed, I greatly love and pity the eldest son. If he returns, what shall we do? Please wait for three years. When Duan'er turns twenty, if there is still no news, it's not too late to change the name." Previously, B was captured by the rebels, and the husband and wife died in succession, leaving only the eldest son alive. The rebels loved his ability to write but feared he might escape, so they assigned many rebels to guard him. Our troops recaptured Jinling and killed their leader. The eldest son took the opportunity to escape, wandering and begging his way back to his hometown, with his house reduced to ashes, feeling desolate and having no place to return to. He heard that after the chaos, A invested in good land and fertile property, which was twice as prosperous as before. Thinking of his close relationship with his father and being related to his mother-in-law, he temporarily went to rely on A, expecting to be accepted. Upon arriving at A's house, A was greatly surprised and wanted to refuse to accept him. His wife scolded him, saying, "You should stop. Duan'er has been betrothed to the eldest son, and everyone in the village knows about it. If he doesn't come back, that's fine. If he comes back, you want to break off the engagement, how will people talk about it?" A had no choice and reluctantly went out. The eldest son saw A, fell to the ground and wept. A pretended to be happy, took the eldest son's hand and stood him up, saying, "Originally, I searched everywhere for news of the eldest son but could not find any. My wife and I were on the verge of despair, fearing he would not survive. It's very good that the eldest son has returned, it greatly comforts my hopes. What about your parents?" The eldest son wept and told the reason.

    712

    甲叹曰：“然则郎今无家可归矣！虽然，婿犹子也，即长居吾家，何害？但我女素爱好，业与汝为妇，汝若无长进，恐小妮子骄傲，不甘作窭人妻。闻汝幼慧能文，今与汝约，从此闭户理旧业，倘得上进，完姻不较光彩耶？”长郎起立对曰：“此儿志也。丈人教训甚善，敢不如命。”由是，长郎遂在甲家读书，攻习勤苦。甲藉课读为名，相待极严，不少假以辞色，而心实憎恶，欲潜图之，别觅快婿，殊苦无当。会刘某丧耦，选择继配。甲素慕刘势，闻之大喜，爰自诣刘，谓有女端姑，年甫及笄，才貌双绝，愿为执箕帚。刘固素艳端姑名，侦知已字，不萌妄想。闻甲言，诧曰：“久耳令媛名，已知幼字有人，何得相戏？”甲正色曰：“焉敢相戏？小女固幼字某乙之子长郎，闻在贼中已曾娶妻。

    Explanation

    712

    甲叹曰：“然则郎今无家可归矣！虽然，婿犹子也，即长居吾家，何害？但我女素爱好，业与汝为妇，汝若无长进，恐小妮子骄傲，不甘作窭人妻。闻汝幼慧能文，今与汝约，从此闭户理旧业，倘得上进，完姻不较光彩耶？”长郎起立对曰：“此儿志也。丈人教训甚善，敢不如命。”由是，长郎遂在甲家读书，攻习勤苦。甲藉课读为名，相待极严，不少假以辞色，而心实憎恶，欲潜图之，别觅快婿，殊苦无当。会刘某丧耦，选择继配。甲素慕刘势，闻之大喜，爰自诣刘，谓有女端姑，年甫及笄，才貌双绝，愿为执箕帚。刘固素艳端姑名，侦知已字，不萌妄想。闻甲言，诧曰：“久耳令媛名，已知幼字有人，何得相戏？”甲正色曰：“焉敢相戏？小女固幼字某乙之子长郎，闻在贼中已曾娶妻。

    甲叹息道：“那么你现在无家可归了！虽然如此，女婿就像是儿子一样，即使长期住在我们家，又有什么妨害呢？但我的女儿一向爱好，已经与你结为夫妻，如果你没有进步，恐怕她会骄傲，不愿意做贫穷人的妻子。听说你从小就聪明能文，现在与你约定，从此以后闭门读书，如果能够有所进步，完成婚姻岂不是更加光彩吗？”长郎站起来回答说：“这是我的志向。岳父的教诲非常正确，我怎敢不遵从命令。”于是，长郎就在甲家读书，刻苦攻读。甲以课读为名，对待他非常严格，不给他任何好脸色，而心中却实在厌恶他，想暗中图谋，另找一个理想的女婿，但实在找不到合适的人选。恰逢刘某失去了配偶，选择继配。甲一向羡慕刘某的权势，听到这个消息非常高兴，于是亲自去见刘某，说：“我有一个女儿端姑，刚刚及笄，才貌双全，愿意为执箕帚。”刘某本就一向仰慕端姑的名声，得知她已经许配，不再有非分之想。听到甲的话，惊讶地说：“我早就听说过令爱的名字，知道她已经许配给某乙的儿子长郎，怎么可以开玩笑？”甲正色道：“怎么敢开玩笑？我的女儿确实已经许配给某乙的儿子长郎，听说他在贼中已经娶过妻子。

    713

    他日倘踪迹而至，焉置吾女？即不然，渠贫无立锥地，亦不愿眼见爱女终从乞丐。今与渠熟商，已写有退婚书。以渠先人交情，暂且留住，翁婿之义则已绝矣！君请勿虑。”刘大喜，乃欢笑订盟而别。翌日，刘遣冰纳采委禽，备极丰腆，涓吉亲迎，为期甚速。甲固有河东之俱，欲藉刘横暴以制服其妻。既订盟，归以告妻，妻怒不答。及将届吉期，又与妻谋曰：“端儿于归在迩，留长郎在，终是未断葛藤。吾今夜决意手刃之，以杜后患。”妻闻言，戟手唾面骂曰：“以若所为，天良丧尽，狗彘不食其余。皇天有知，决不汝宥！”甲曰：“古称六州铁不能铸一大错，我今未尝不悔。刘某横暴，卿所知也，倘有反覆，彼甘心耶？今我所谓势同骑虎，卿纵不忍故婿，独不为夫计乎？”妻长叹，以袖掩面，恨恨而卧。初，端姑以父憎婿贫，心常不快。适窃闻父言，始知将己改字刘某，又将不利于长郎，大骇，亟怀钗钏珠宝数事，趁漏初下，掩至书斋。

    Explanation

    713

    If one day they trace their steps and arrive, what will become of my daughter? Even if not so, he is so poor that he has no place to stand a cone, and I also do not wish to see my beloved daughter end up as a beggar. Now after thorough discussion with him, we have written a letter of divorce. Because of our friendship with his ancestors, we are allowing him to stay temporarily, but the relationship between father-in-law and son-in-law is now severed! Please do not worry," Liu was greatly pleased and they happily sealed the alliance before parting. The next day, Liu sent an ice messenger to present gifts and accept the betrothal offerings, with great abundance and richness, and chose a favorable date for the wedding, which was scheduled very quickly. Jia indeed has a connection to Hedong and wants to use Liu's violent nature to control his wife. After sealing the alliance, he returned home to tell his wife, but she angrily refused to respond. When the wedding date was approaching, he again plotted with his wife saying, "Duan's wedding is near. Leaving Changlang here will never sever the ties. I have decided tonight to kill him to eliminate future troubles." Upon hearing this, the wife raised her fist and spat in his face, cursing him, "Because of what you have done, your conscience is completely lost; even dogs and pigs would not eat your leftovers. If heaven knows, it will certainly not forgive you!" Jia said, "As the saying goes, six states' iron cannot forge one great mistake. I have never regretted this. Liu's violent nature is something you know well. If there is any change, will he be satisfied? Now I am in a situation like riding a tiger. Even if you cannot bear to harm your former son-in-law, don't you think about your husband's situation?" The wife sighed deeply, covered her face with her sleeve, and lay down in anger. Initially, Duan's mother was unhappy because her father disliked her poor son-in-law. She just overheard her father's words and realized that she would be given to Liu and that it would be harmful to Changlang. She was greatly alarmed, quickly took several pieces of jewelry, and sneaked into the study when it was still dark.

    714

    长郎正挑灯观书，蓦举首见之，大惊，叱曰：“暮夜卿何至此？与卿名为夫妇，尚未成礼，卿父视我犹子，我若无礼，是禽兽也！瓜李之嫌，不可不慎，卿宜速退！”端姑叹曰：“噫！妾岂淫奔者耶！君祸且不测，宜速为计，勿迂守头巾气也。”乃以顷所闻备告之。长郎闻之失色，长跪求救。端姑曰：“事急矣！计惟从郎偕亡。由后门出，再作别议。”长郎曰：“善。”如言相将扶持而出。筹商所往，端姑沉吟曰：“妾父有妹早寡，与妾姑侄之义颇相得。往投之，无不纳。”长郎叩其居址，端姑约略记其大概，揣度形势，彳亍前行。昧爽，见前树林中有村庄，端姑喜曰：“是矣。”径往投之。乃村庄非他，即所改字刘某家也。时刘喜聘端姑，平生愿足。是日早起，正督工匠垩壁涂墙，准备亲迎，以壮观瞻。忽见少男弱女，踉跄犯露晓行，形迹可疑。要至室中，研悉巅末，大惊。见二人暮夜奔驰，气急败坏。

    Explanation

    714

    The young master was holding a lamp and reading a book when, suddenly raising his head, he saw her. Greatly surprised, he shouted, "Why are you here at night? Although we are named husband and wife, the rites have not been completed. Your father regards me as his son; if I were to act improperly, I would be no better than a beast! The suspicion of the Gua-Li incident must be taken seriously; you should leave quickly!" Duanggu sighed, "Ah! Am I a woman who runs away with a man? Your misfortune is unpredictable; you should act quickly and not stubbornly cling to formalities." She then fully informed him of what she had just heard. The young master was shocked and lost color, kneeling long to beg for help. Duanggu said, "The situation is urgent! The only plan is to die with you. We will leave through the back door and discuss other matters later." The young master agreed, "Very well." As planned, they supported each other and left. Considering where to go, Duanggu pondered, "My father has an early widowed sister, with whom I share a close relationship as if we were aunt and niece. If we go to her, she will surely welcome us." The young master inquired about her address, and Duanggu vaguely remembered the general location. Assessing the situation, they walked slowly and hesitantly. At dawn, they saw a village in the trees ahead, and Duanggu exclaimed, "That's it." They went directly to the village. It was none other than the village belonging to Liu Xi, whose name had been changed. At the time, Liu Xi was about to marry Duanggu, fulfilling his lifelong wish. That day, he had risen early to oversee workers whitewashing walls and preparing for the wedding, to make a grand spectacle. Suddenly, he saw two young people stumbling and hurriedly walking in the morning dew, their behavior suspicious. He brought them into the room and thoroughly investigated the details, greatly surprised. Seeing the two rushing at night, their breaths ragged and disheveled, he was deeply alarmed.

    715

    以袖拂面谛视之，女固娟好，男亦婉娈，大喜，慰令毋恐，遂并纳而藏之。遍戒众人，秘勿妄语。众窥刘意，誓不敢泄。又窃叹二人何其命蹇，虎口方离，旋投陷井，自贻伊戚，夫复何尤？盖甲妹与刘某家同在甲家东偏，相距各十馀里。出门即是歧路，本应下达，反趋上游，以致舛错。其夜，甲见责于妻，自知理绌，以妻恨恨而卧，伺其熟睡，漏已三下，乃袖刃疾至书斋，索长郎不得，大惊；反至女室，亦阒无人，大怒。计女与长郎偕亡，必投妹家。急呼客作七八人起，同至妹家叩门。妹问：“何人？”甲曰：“兄音妹不知也耶？”妹曰：“夜阑，孀妇独处，即兄来亦不纳。”彼此硬语牴牾，甲益信逋逃所在，命众破扉入。妹怒问：“兄欲何为？”甲曰：“汝藏人在室，谓兄不知也耶？盍自献出，免我搜索。”妹力辨其无，而色殊沮丧。甲詧其状益信，率众遍搜不得。既见妹房一柜甚大，命妹启钥，妹遽以身横眠其上，执不肯启。甲谓众曰：“必在内无疑矣。

    Explanation

    715

    Wiping the face with a sleeve and looking carefully at them, the girl was indeed graceful and beautiful, and the boy was also charming and handsome. Greatly delighted, he comforted them not to be afraid and then accepted both and hid them. He warned everyone to keep the matter secret and not to speak falsely. The crowd understood Liu's intention and swore not to leak it. They also secretly sighed at how unfortunate the two individuals' fate was, having just escaped from the tiger's mouth and immediately falling into a trap. They reflected on the self-inflicted sorrow and wondered what blame there could be. It is said that Jia's sister and Liu's family were both located on the eastern side of Jia's house, each more than ten miles apart. As soon as they went out, they encountered a forked road. They should have descended, but instead went upstream, leading to a mistake. That night, Jia was scolded by his wife and realized his lack of reason. Because his wife was angry and resentful and lying down, he waited until she was deeply asleep, and when the water clock had struck three times, he hurriedly went to the study with a knife hidden in his sleeve. He searched for the eldest son but could not find him, and was greatly alarmed. Returning to the girl's room, he found it also empty and no one there, and was greatly angry. Calculating that the girl and the eldest son had gone together and must have gone to his sister's house, he hurriedly called seven or eight hired workers to get up and went together to his sister's house, knocking on the door. His sister asked, "Who is it?" Jia said, "Is it not my brother's voice?" His sister said, "It is late at night, and I am a widow alone. Even if it were you, I would not let you in." Each other's firm words were contradictory. Jia became even more convinced of the fugitives' location and ordered the crowd to break the door. His sister angrily asked, "What do you want?" Jia said, "You are hiding people in the room, do you think I don't know? Why don't you come out yourself, to avoid my search?" His sister strongly denied it, but her expression was very dejected. Observing her expression, Jia became even more convinced and led the crowd to search everywhere but found nothing. After seeing his sister's room had a very large cabinet, he ordered her to unlock it. His sister quickly lay across the top of the cabinet and refused to open it. Jia said to the crowd, "They must be inside without a doubt."

    716

    ”自力挽妹起，叱众舁柜。妹无奈何，且泣且咒，瞪目干怒，坐视舁柜而去。比至家，天甫平明。急斧柜钥验之，则一僧踡卧其中，死焉。大惊，踌蹰良久，乃市棺殓好。使人讣报刘某，谓端姑福薄，不幸猝病以殀。刘故错愕，对使叹曰：“寒家方拟亲迎，姑何疾之暴也？烦归报而主人，吾义当临吊。”使者唯唯以退。越日，刘果备礼往吊，哀悼不胜。甲叹曰：“小女无福奉事君子，亦寒家之不幸也！”刘太息曰：“自是鄙人福薄，殃及令嫒。然义重夫妇，未视含殓，心实欿然。敢请开棺，一觌其面，以慰素愿。”甲曰：“已经盖棺，君之高义，敢闻命矣，敢辞。”刘坚执其说，甲辞益力，且愿尽返聘物。刘怒曰：“汝语言颟顸，其中显有别情，得勿将女改适他人，故以空棺塞责耶！”甲以刘言直抉其隐，倍觉惶惧，只好再三哀告。刘作色曰：“是儿殊狡狯，不屑与辨，是非赴诉有司不可！”拂袖径去。越日，具词控官。

    Explanation

    716

    From the moment he pulled his sister himself, he scolded the crowd to carry the cabinet. The sister, helpless, cried and cursed, glaring with angry eyes, sitting and watching as the cabinet was carried away. By the time they arrived home, it was just at dawn. Hurriedly, he broke open the cabinet lock to inspect it, and found one monk crouching and lying inside, who had died there. He was greatly surprised and hesitated for a long time before buying a coffin and properly burying him. He sent someone to inform Mr. D, saying that the young lady lacked good fortune and unfortunately suddenly fell ill and died young. Mr. D was therefore startled and said sadly to the messenger, "My humble family was planning to come and fetch the bride. What sudden illness is this? Please return and report to the master. I ought to come to pay respects." The messenger nodded in agreement and then left. The next day, Mr. D indeed prepared the necessary rites and went to pay respects, unable to bear the mourning. Mr. A said sadly, "My daughter lacks the good fortune to serve a gentleman, also an unfortunate event for my humble family!" Mr. D sighed, "I lack good fortune, affecting negatively your daughter. However, the importance of husband and wife, not yet seen the burial rites, heart indeed disappointed. Dare request to open the coffin, once see her face, to comfort my long-held wish." Mr. A said, "Already closed the coffin, your noble righteousness, dare obey the command, dare refuse." Mr. D firmly insisted on his argument, and Mr. A refused even more forcefully, and was willing to completely return the dowry items. Mr. D became angry, "Your words are vague, there must be another reason, did you not remarry your daughter to someone else, so as to cover up with an empty coffin?" Mr. A, with Mr. D's words directly exposing his hidden motives, felt even more frightened and could only repeatedly plead. Mr. D, with a stern expression, said, "This child is very cunning, I do not wish to argue, it is not possible to not report to the officials!" He swept his sleeves and left. The next day, he prepared a petition to sue the official.

    717

    官素耳刘名，遂拘甲舁棺，当堂启验，乃是一僧。大惊，命伍伯验之，谓系惊恐气促自毙。官叱榜甲讯究，甲称得之孀妹柜中。又命拘其妹，已羞忿雉经以殉矣。其事可知，姑置勿论。然刘终求官讯甲，其女究竟何在。甲没奈何，谓偕人逃亡。官饬限严捕。逾限，刘具词敦促。甲自涉讼事，家业已耗去大半，乃托人关说，愿贿刘三千金，求罢讼。刘故不许，再益二千金，始勉首肯。而甲已荡然无馀矣。刘乃具词诉官，历陈甲之所为，且谓长郎、端姑现均收留在家，怜其遇蹇无依，已除室备奁，择日为合卺。又怜无以自存，即以甲所贿五千金助妆，俾资食用，云云。官阅词，不禁肃然起敬，击节称赞。谓：“向闻刘横暴，武断一乡；似此处分，斟酌尽善，是侠而近于道者也，岂横暴武断者所能为耶！即方古之义士，何多让焉！”长郎、端姑感刘恩，以父事之，刘亦视如骨肉，两家往来无间。后甲夫妻穷无所之，长郎、端姑迎归同居，不念旧恶矣。

    Explanation

    717

    The magistrate, upon hearing the name Liu, then arrested Jia and carried the coffin into the hall to open and examine it. It turned out to be a monk. Greatly surprised, he ordered the constables to examine it and concluded that it was self-inflicted due to fright and shortness of breath. The magistrate ordered Jia to be interrogated thoroughly, and Jia claimed he got it from his widowed sister-in-law's cabinet. He then ordered her to be arrested, but she had already hanged herself in shame and anger. The matter is clear, so it will be set aside for now. However, Liu insisted on having Jia interrogated, wanting to know where his daughter was. Jia had no choice but to say she had fled with someone. The magistrate ordered a strict deadline for capturing her. After the deadline passed, Liu submitted a petition urging action. Jia, involved in the lawsuit, had already spent most of his family wealth. He then asked someone to mediate, offering to bribe Liu with 3,000 gold pieces to drop the case. Liu refused, but when Jia offered an additional 2,000 gold pieces, Liu reluctantly agreed. But Jia had already spent everything. Liu then submitted a petition to the magistrate, detailing Jia's actions, and said that his son and daughter were now living with him. He felt sorry for their misfortune and lack of support, so he prepared a dowry and arranged a wedding date. He also felt sorry for their inability to support themselves, so he used the 5,000 gold pieces Jia had offered as a dowry to help them live. The magistrate read the petition and could not help but stand up in respect, clapping his hands and praising Liu. He said, "I have heard that Liu is violent and domineering in his village; such a decision, carefully considered, is truly just and noble, something that a violent and domineering person could never do! Even ancient righteous men would not compare to this!" The son and daughter were grateful to Liu and treated him like a father; Liu also regarded them as family, and the two families visited each other without any barriers. Later, when Jia and his wife were destitute and had nowhere to go, the son and daughter invited them to live with them, no longer holding any grudges.

    718

    里乘子曰：长郎、端姑天生嘉耦，迹其所历之境，多出人意料之外。当夫仓皇出奔，离虎口而投陷井，刘拂面谛视，喜其娟好婉娈，纳而藏之。似此居心叵测，不惟当日见者叹其命蹇，即后世闻者亦无不同有此叹也。乃诸事昭晰，刘具诉始末，至此水落石出，始知刘平日刚直，非同横暴，又不惟当日阅词之官起敬称叹，即后世闻者亦无不起敬称叹也。而其中甲妹一节尤奇。向使二人径投甲妹，必难免于祸；幸误投刘，蹈凶反吉，足见天道之巧不可思议也。夫刚直，美德也；横暴，戾气也，相类而实相反。大抵刘遇是非曲直，片言而断，正其刚直之性，不稍假借，岂横暴乎？乡人严惮，罔敢不遵，遂谓为武断为之侧目，过矣！吾以为刘之刚直如张僧繇所画之龙，而官之一言之褒，谓侠而近于道者，是为龙点睛也。斯人而在，执鞭所欣慕焉，谈者传其姓而忘其名，惜哉！

    Explanation

    718

    Master Li Cheng said: Longlang and Duangü were naturally a virtuous pair, and the path of their experiences often exceeded people's expectations. When they fled in a hurry, escaping from one danger only to fall into another, Liu gently touched her face and looked closely, admiring her grace and beauty, and took her and hid her. Such ulterior motives, not only did those who saw it on that day lament her bad luck, but even those who heard of it in later generations also shared the same lament. However, when all the facts became clear, Liu recounted the beginning and end of the story, and thus the truth came to light. It was then known that Liu was upright and honest in his daily life, not tyrannical. Not only did the officials who read the case on that day show respect and admiration, but even those who heard of it in later generations also showed respect and admiration. Among these, the part about Jia's younger sister was particularly unusual. If the two had gone directly to Jia's younger sister, they would have inevitably encountered misfortune; fortunately, they mistakenly went to Liu, and instead of falling into danger, they found safety, clearly showing the intricate and inscrutable workings of heaven. Uprightness is a virtue; tyranny is malevolent energy. They may seem similar but are actually opposite. Generally, Liu made decisions on right and wrong with a single word, acting on his upright nature without showing leniency. How could he be tyrannical? The local people were fearful and dared not disobey, so they called him arbitrary and looked askance at him, which was a mistake! I believe that Liu's uprightness is like the dragon painted by Zhang Sengyao, and the official's praise, calling him a hero who is close to the way, is the final stroke that completes the dragon. If such a person were to be around, one would be delighted to hold the whip and follow him. Those who talk about him only remember his surname and forget his given name, truly a pity!

    719

    富贾某
    　　
    　　楚南某甲，懋迁汉口。粤寇猝至，携妻遁匿乡村。妻某氏有殊色，逑好甚笃。以匆遽出走，未带多资，寇退，甲无以为生，遂投缳焉。妻闻，趋往解救，扶卧榻上，逾时始苏。妻泣问：“君何为者？倘万一不救，将焉置妾！”甲叹曰：“我此举正为卿也。男子不能自立，饿死，分也。累卿同苦，心殊不安。况卿尚少艾，可作他图，但愿异日不忘故人，岁时以一盂麦饭临风遥为招魂，感且不朽矣！区区之私，实系如此，不则厮守俱死，无益也！”妻曰：“君何计之左也！与其君弃妾而逝，曷若少缓须臾，将妾鬻去？妾身得所，君亦可藉资以营生计。

    Explanation

    719

    A Wealthy Merchant

    In the south of Chu, there was a man named Jia who engaged in trade in Hankou. When the Cantonese rebels suddenly arrived, he fled with his wife to the countryside. His wife, whose name was Ji, had exceptional beauty, and their affection was very deep. Fleeing in haste, they did not bring much wealth. After the rebels retreated, Jia had no means of living and hanged himself. Upon hearing this, Ji hurried to save him, helping him lie on the bed. After some time, he regained consciousness. Ji wept and asked, "What are you doing? If you were not saved, what would become of me?" Jia sighed and said, "This action is for your sake. A man cannot support himself; dying of hunger is one's fate. To burden you with suffering, I feel very uneasy. You are still young and beautiful; you can make other plans. I only hope that one day you will not forget your old friend, and at certain times, you will offer a bowl of millet porridge to the wind to summon my spirit. That would be touching and immortal! My humble request is truly like this; otherwise, we would both die together, which would be pointless!" Ji replied, "How misguided your plan is! Rather than you abandoning me and leaving, why not wait a moment? I would find a place, and you could also use the money to support your livelihood."

    720

    若此，不较两全耶？”甲曰：“固然，我一息尚存，实不忍出此。”妻曰：“妾自欲之，于君何尤！”展转议定，倩媒将某氏改嫁汉口富贾某。初，某贾世贩煤炭，家拥巨资。母、妻、幼子俱被贼掳。楚境肃清，复发窖金重理旧业。嘱媒为觅继配，适娶某氏。入门后，微睨新人年貌，意颇慊。比将就寝，见某氏背烛而坐，啜泣甚哀。贾窃异之，因问曰：“娘子何哀之深？得弗憎我蠢耶？”某氏哽咽起立对曰：“妾自伤命薄，焉敢憎君？顾妾自事旧夫，结褵以来，未尝反目，今忽舍而他适，数年举案，一旦分钗，未审旧夫此时何以为情？妾对新特而思旧姻，哀从中来，实有出于不能已者，惟君怜而恕之，幸甚！”贾惊曰：“娘子固自有夫耶？”曰：“然。

    Explanation

    720

    If we proceed in this manner, would it not be a compromise that achieves both?" Person A replied, "Indeed, as long as I still draw breath, I truly cannot bring myself to do this." His wife said, "I wish for it myself; what fault do I have with you?" After much discussion and deliberation, they asked a matchmaker to arrange for a certain woman to remarry a wealthy merchant from Hankou. Initially, this merchant had been engaged in the business of coal trading for generations and owned vast wealth. His mother, wife, and young son were all captured by bandits. When the bandit situation in Chu (Hubei) was cleared up, he dug out his hidden wealth and resumed his old business. He instructed the matchmaker to find him a new wife, and married a woman named [her surname]. Upon entering the household, he glanced at the new wife's age and appearance, feeling somewhat satisfied. As he was about to go to bed, he saw the new wife sitting with her back to the candle, crying softly with great sadness. The merchant was surprised by this and asked, "Why are you so deeply sad? Are you not resenting my foolishness?" The woman stood up, choked back tears, and replied, "I lament my own thin fate; how dare I resent you? Considering that I have been with my previous husband since we were married, we have never had a quarrel. Now suddenly leaving me to marry someone else, the many years of sharing meals, now suddenly separated, I wonder what my former husband is feeling now. I think of my old marriage while being with my new husband, sorrow comes from within, it is truly something I cannot help. Only you can pity and forgive me, I would be very grateful!" The merchant was startled and asked, "You already have a husband?" She replied, "Yes."

    721

    ”曰：“媒人误我！媒人误我！微娘子言，几陷我于不义矣！”遂慰令勿哀，诘朝仍送归旧夫，价值百缗并以奉贶。某氏闻之，收泪叩谢。贾召厨媪来伴某氏，自秉烛独宿外室。天明，令媒召某甲至，告以意，嘱携妻归。甲感泣投地。贾询知甲向善贸易，又赠钱二百千缗，使权子母，以资食用。甲夫妻喜出非望，再三拜谢而归。某氏谓甲曰：“妾闻受恩必报。难得某贾如此高义，君当努力，必谋有以酬之。”甲点首曰：“卿言是也。”终岁，小有经营，获利倍蓰。某氏谓当分半利往酬贾，甲如言往。贾喜曰：“子真可谓善生财者也。惜资本太少，愿再益，假子二千缗，庶稍足以展子之志。此戋戋半利，请仍携归，子好为之。俟岁星一周，当听君好音也。”甲拜别贾，遂挈资如长沙。

    Explanation

    721

    He said, "The matchmaker has deceived me! If it were not for the lady's words, I would have been caught in a situation of injustice!" He then consoled her, telling her not to grieve, and promised to send her back to her former husband the next day, along with a gift of a hundred strings of cash. Upon hearing this, the woman wiped away her tears and bowed to thank him. The merchant summoned a kitchen maid to accompany the woman and lit a candle to sleep alone in an outer room. The next morning, he instructed the matchmaker to call Mr. Jia, explaining the situation and asking him to take his wife back. Mr. Jia was moved to tears and fell to the ground in gratitude. The merchant learned that Mr. Jia was skilled in trade and, in addition, gave him two thousand strings of cash to engage in usury, to support their living expenses. Mr. Jia and his wife were overjoyed beyond expectation and bowed repeatedly before returning home. The woman told Mr. Jia, "I have heard that one must repay kindness. It is rare for someone like this merchant to show such high virtue. You should work hard and surely find a way to repay him." Mr. Jia nodded and said, "You are right." By the end of the year, with a little effort in business, he made a profit several times over. The woman suggested that they should share half of the profit to repay the merchant, and Mr. Jia followed her advice. The merchant was pleased and said, "You are truly a master at generating wealth. Unfortunately, your capital is too small. I wish to lend you another two thousand strings of cash so that you can slightly expand your ambitions. This small amount of half the profit, please take it back and use it well. Wait until the year has passed, and I will hear good news from you." Mr. Jia bowed to bid farewell to the merchant and then took the capital to Changsha.

    722

    以米价廉可获重息，爰买巨舟载米。将行，会楚兵援皖，甲舟遂为群卒所据。比至武昌，甲哀求偏将，愿出资买舟易换。偏将见甲舟宽大，叱去群卒，自为坐船，甲无如何，只合听之。偏将甚喜甲之为人，抵皖，寻拔皖城。各营需粮甚殷，偏将代甲分售，获利无算。贼所掳两湖妇女甚多，甲求偏将，以百余缗谋得一妇人，年可三十许，且有一媪一童为伴，将归以报贾。既旋汉口，备奁择日，使妻送妇至贾家。方下舆，妇与贾觌面，不禁相持恸哭失声。妇人非他，即贾被掳之妻也。甲妻闻之，大喜称贺。贾问妻，知老母、幼子俱无恙，益喜。其母与子非他，即妇人为伴之一媪一童也。贾亟命舆迎母与子归，一家团聚，悲喜并集，乃刑牲演剧，以报天佑。自是贾与甲缔交，竟成通家，往来无间焉。

    Explanation

    722

    Because the price of rice was cheap, it could yield high interest, so he bought a large ship to load rice. Just as he was about to depart, he encountered the Chu army coming to aid Anhui, and his ship was then seized by a group of soldiers. By the time he reached Wuchang, Jia, in desperation, asked a subordinate general to buy the ship and exchange it. The subordinate general, seeing Jia's ship was spacious, ordered the soldiers to leave and took it as his own. Jia had no choice but to accept it. The subordinate general was very pleased with Jia's character and, upon reaching Anhui, soon captured the city. The various camps were in great need of food, so the subordinate general sold the rice on Jia's behalf and made a huge profit. Many women from the Two Lakes region were captured by the rebels. Jia asked the subordinate general to buy one for him with over a hundred strings of cash. The woman was about thirty years old and had an elderly woman and a child accompanying her. He intended to bring her back to repay Jia. After returning to Hankou, he prepared a dowry, chose a date, and sent his wife to deliver the woman to Jia's home. As the woman stepped down from the carriage, she and Jia saw each other face to face and could not help but embrace and weep uncontrollably. The woman was none other than the wife Jia had been captured with. Jia's wife heard about it and was very happy and congratulated him. Jia asked his wife and learned that his elderly mother and young son were both safe. He was even more delighted. His mother and son were none other than the elderly woman and child who accompanied the woman. Jia immediately ordered a carriage to bring his mother and son back. The family was reunited, and joy and sorrow mingled. They sacrificed animals and staged a play to thank heaven for their good fortune. From then on, Jia and the subordinate general became close friends and eventually became like one family, visiting each other frequently.

    723

    里乘子曰：贾还甲之妻，甲亦还贾之妻，不惟还其妻，且还其母、其子，贾之所获利益多矣。盖贾不忍使人骨肉分离，天亦断不忍使贾骨肉分离，此中因缘凑合，造物煞费安排，倘有毫厘之失，即不堪设想矣！凡百君子，可不勉哉！

    Explanation

    723

    The Master Li Cheng said: Jia returns Jia's wife, and Jia also returns Jia's wife. Not only does he return his wife, but also his mother and son. Jia has gained much benefit. Because Jia could not bear to see his family separated, Heaven also could not bear to see Jia's family separated. The coincidences and arrangements in this matter are truly intricate, and the creator has spent much effort in arranging them. If there were even the slightest mistake, it would be unimaginable. All gentlemen should strive to do so.

    724

    中州某生
    　　
    　　中州某生，年少，美俊而才，精音律，兼娴武艺。弱冠游蜀，有戚某官重庆司马，招生为司书记。蜀俗：春日乡村报赛，类精选梨园演《三国》《水浒》《西游》等小说，全部累月，笙歌不绝，士女云集，举国若狂。生尝于剧场见一女郎，年才及笄，淡妆靓服，不假修饰，而风神楚楚，诚绝色也。生意夺神驰，几难自主。每日演剧，必预使仆侦探，女郎到场，自即驰往，遥而觑之，立不移步。久之，女即意似有所觉。生间以目相挑，女郎或亦以目相答，生益惑之。浃旬以后，两情益亲。生密访之，知女名蕊霞，父曾官游击，仅此一女，钟爱特甚。幼延师课读，诗词歌赋，粗能脱稿。游击固嗜音律，亦使女习之，筝琶尤所擅长。游击殁后，宦橐萧条，蕊霞随母乡居。屡有人问名，母选择多不遂意。

    Explanation

    724

    A Young Man from Zhongzhou

    A young man from Zhongzhou, handsome, talented, and skilled in music and martial arts, traveled to Sichuan in his early twenties. A relative named Qi held the position of military governor of Chongqing and hired him as a secretary. In Sichuan, it was customary for rural areas to hold spring festivals, during which selected troupes would perform plays based on famous novels such as Romance of the Three Kingdoms, Water Margin, and Journey to the West. These performances lasted for months, with continuous music and singing, attracting crowds of men and women from all over the country, creating a frenzy of excitement.

    One day, the young man saw a young lady at the theater. She was just coming of age, dressed simply yet elegantly, with no need for elaborate adornment. Her graceful demeanor and beauty were truly extraordinary. The young man was so captivated that he almost lost control of himself. Every day, he would send a servant to keep watch and, whenever the young lady arrived, he would rush to the theater, standing at a distance to gaze at her without moving. After some time, the young lady seemed to sense his presence. Occasionally, the young man would try to catch her eye, and she would sometimes respond with a glance. This only deepened his fascination. After ten days, their feelings grew closer. The young man secretly inquired about her and learned that her name was Rui Xia. Her father had been a military officer, and she was his only daughter, whom he had loved dearly. From a young age, she had been taught by a tutor and could write poems, lyrics, songs, and essays, though not perfectly. Her father, who was fond of music, also taught her, particularly in playing the zither and lute. After her father's death, they fell into financial hardship, and Rui Xia moved to the countryside with her mother. Many suitors had asked for her hand, but her mother's choices were often unsatisfactory.

    725

    生访之既确，演剧既毕，犹恋不忍舍。日必一过女郎之门，无论见与不见，其心始慊；否则，忽忽若有所失。他日又过其门，门外固有蔷薇一架，蕊霞适撷花簪髻，骤见生，嫣然含睇，红涨于颊。生瞷无人，托言行路苦渴，长揖求饮。蕊霞颔之，便呼婢取茶，生称娖向前，径诘氏族，女郎含笑具告之，亦转诘生氏族，彼此酬对，殷如熟识。须臾，婢捧茶至，生饮毕兴辞，蕊霞笑曰：“母往省舅氏，家无人，不敢久留。妾知君为风雅士，母归，妾当相告。君得暇第至此，母爱才，必不见拒也。”生闻之喜出望外，诺诺而别。既归，忆女所言，以为有情，辗转沉思，彻夜不能交睫。翌日饭后，具冠服，执贽往拜其母。母果出见，年四十以来，不失大家风范。生谓：“昨路过贵宅，蒙女公子赐饮解渴，

    Explanation

    725

    Sheng visited and confirmed, and after the play had ended, he still loved and could not bear to part. Every day, he must pass by the young lady's door, whether he sees her or not, his heart is only satisfied; otherwise, he feels restless as if something is missing. One day, he passed by her door again, and outside the door, there was indeed a trellis of roses. Rui Xia was just picking flowers to adorn her hair. She suddenly saw Sheng, looked at him charmingly, and her cheeks turned red. Sheng, seeing no one, pretended to be thirsty on the road and bowed to ask for a drink. Rui Xia nodded in agreement and called a maid to fetch tea. Sheng respectfully stepped forward and directly asked about her family. The young lady smiled and told him in detail, also asking about his family. They exchanged words, as if they are already familiar with each other. In a moment, the maid brought the tea. Sheng drank and then rose to take his leave. Rui Xia smiled and said, "My mother has gone to visit her brother-in-law, and there is no one at home, so I cannot keep you long. I know you are a man of culture; when my mother returns, I will tell her. If you have free time, please come here. My mother loves talent and will not refuse you." Sheng hears this and is overjoyed, nodding and taking his leave. After returning, he recalls what the young lady said and thinks she has feelings for him, turning over in thought all night, unable to sleep. The next day after breakfast, he dresses formally and carries a gift to pay respects to her mother. Her mother indeed comes out to see him, aged forty or more, and still maintains the grace of a noble lady. Sheng says, "Yesterday, I passed by your noble residence and was given a drink to quench my thirst."

    726

    感且不朽。”母言：“老妇昨适外出，致多简亵。归闻小女道及郎君风雅，如不嫌蜗居湫溢，得暇第过我少坐，亦可藉辉蓬荜。”生唯唯谦让。自是靡日不往。蕊霞有舅，为邑诸生，时来省妹。他日适与生遇，彼此倾谈，殊属投契。尝密告其母曰：“妹日为甥择配，某生尚无偶，眼见佳婿，何可错过？”母谓：“妹非无此意。以生为异乡人，不肯令女远嫁，故迟疑不决也。”舅谓：“择婿但求快意，何必以远嫁为嫌。”母曰：“儿女大事，何可草草！兄言固是，容徐商之。”亡何，粤寇下窜，各道征兵孔急。

    Explanation

    726

    The young man was deeply moved and felt a sense of gratitude that would endure forever. The mother said, "I, an old woman, just went out yesterday and was not properly dressed. Upon returning, I heard my daughter speak highly of your elegance and refinement. If you do not find our humble home too shabby, please come by for a brief visit when you have time. It would also bring honor to my humble abode." The young man nodded politely and modestly declined. From then on, he visited without fail every day. Rui Xia had an uncle who was a scholar in the county, and he would often come to visit his sister. One day, he happened to meet the young man, and they engaged in a deep conversation, finding a strong rapport. The uncle once privately told his mother, "My sister has been looking for a suitable match for her nephew. This young man is still unmarried, and he seems to be a fine groom. How can we miss such an opportunity?" The mother replied, "My sister is not without this intention. However, since the young man is from a different place, she is reluctant to let her daughter marry far away, so she is hesitating and unsure." The uncle said, "When choosing a son-in-law, one should only seek what brings pleasure. Why should we object to a distant marriage?" The mother said, "Marriage is a major matter for children. How can we take it lightly! What you say is true, but we must consider it carefully." Not long after, the Cantonese bandits descended, and there was an urgent call for troops across various regions.

    727

    蜀中某提督奉调援楚，生有志投笔，慨然投营，愿为效命，提督许之，行有日矣。舅又促其母曰：“闻生戒途有日，甥婚如不早订，后悔无及。”母沉思久之，乃令风示生，倩冰委禽。生闻狂喜，既委禽，即择吉毕烟。两情嬿婉，惟就道伊迩，未免眷恋难别。启行前一日，母设筵祖饯，席罢，蕊霞呼婢将秦筝至，叹谓生曰：“君久欲闻妾歌，非吝不肯，缘优伶伎俩，究非闺媛所宜习。今君将远别，旧作有《闺中五更词》，强为一奏，即当赠行。何如？”生笑曰：“久愿闻佳奏赐教，幸甚！”蕊霞乃卷袖搊弦，曼声歌曰：一更房栊黯夕阴，双飞倦鸟归投林。

    Explanation

    727

    A certain military commander in Sichuan was summoned to aid Chu. The protagonist had a desire to leave his civilian career and join the military, so he boldly joined the camp, wishing to serve with dedication. The commander agreed, and the departure date was set. The protagonist's uncle then urged his mother, saying, "I hear that the protagonist is preparing to depart soon. If his marriage is not arranged early, it will be too late to regret it." The mother thought deeply for a long time, then instructed someone to inform the protagonist and arrange a marriage proposal. The protagonist was overjoyed upon hearing the news. After the proposal was made, he immediately chose a favorable date to complete the wedding ceremonies. The two were deeply affectionate, but as the departure date approached, they could not help but feel reluctant to part. On the day before his departure, the mother held a farewell banquet. After the banquet, Rui Xia called a maid to bring the Qin zither, and she sighed to the protagonist, saying, "You have long wished to hear my singing, but I have not been unwilling. However, the skills of entertainers are not suitable for a woman of the inner chambers. Now that you are about to depart far away, I have an old composition of mine, 'The Five Watches of the Night in the Boudoir.' I will force myself to play it and offer it as a farewell gift. How about it?" The protagonist smiled and said, "I have long wished to hear your beautiful performance and receive your guidance. How fortunate!" Rui Xia then rolled up her sleeves, plucked the strings, and sang melodiously: "In the first watch, the room is dim with evening shadows, and weary birds fly back to their nests in pairs."

    728

    罗衣斜亸翠袖薄，灯开红豆明照心。鼓沸沸去，一更既，我所思兮远莫致。颦眉默卷水晶帘，帘波浸透珍珠泪。吁嗟一更兮更鼓敲，蝴蝶同梦鸳鸯交。二更嫦娥扶影立，窃药缘何背后羿。天上人间一片心，细语花阴绣鞋湿。柝琅琅，二更长，我所思兮渺难望。辛荑开尽芙蓉落，孤负流苏白玉床。吁嗟二更兮更柝缓，柔肠九曲铁石软。三更睡鸭嘘龙涎，情如望岁夜如年。薰笼斜倚裙带结，侍儿熏被催早眠。漏淅淅，三更疾，我所思兮不可必。屡卜金钱占绣鞋，慈悲烂诵波罗蜜。吁嗟三更兮更漏深，愁城厌破芙蓉衾。

    Explanation

    728

    A woman's robe hangs loosely, her thin green sleeves draped elegantly. The light of a red bean illuminates her heart, casting a soft glow. The drums beat loudly, signaling the first watch of the night. What I think of is far beyond my reach. She frowns, silently rolling up the crystal curtain, her tears soaking through its folds. Alas, as the first watch passes, the drum strikes, and in her dream, a butterfly meets a mandarin duck. In the second watch, Chang'e stands in her shadow, her heart heavy with the regret of stealing the elixir from Houyi. A shared heart bridges heaven and earth, her soft whispers in the shade of flowers dampen her embroidered shoes. The wooden clapper rings, the second watch stretches endlessly, and what I think of is difficult to see. The magnolia has withered, the lotus has fallen, and the luxurious bed lies neglected. Alas, as the second watch drags on, her heart is as soft as iron. In the third watch, the sleeping duck exhales dragon's breath, her longing stretches endlessly, the night as long as a year. She leans against the perfumed cage, her skirt tied, as the servant burns incense on the quilt, urging her to sleep. The water clock drips, the third watch passes swiftly, and what I think of is uncertain. She repeatedly divines with coins and embroidered shoes, reciting the Heart Sutra with devotion. Alas, as the third watch deepens, her heartbreak shatters the lotus-patterned quilt.

    729

    四更梦君远迎接，霞帔珠冠五云叠。见说贵为执金吾，黄金缠腰不买妾。筹绵绵，四更连，我所思兮各一天。南山木石有时尽，恨海波澜何日填？吁嗟四更兮更筹快，铜山难了相思债。五更更筹如水流，罗袜纤瘦冰双钩。惺忪薄息鬟堕枕，金鸡唤人起梳头。香冉冉，五更转，我所思兮行当返。富贵归来尚青，愿花长寿月常满。吁嗟五更兮更香灰，合欢预錾黄金杯。歌毕，生赞曰：“促弦曼歌，疾徐中节，不同俗韵。而词旨哀感顽艳，直合文姬《十八拍》、平子《四愁》、子美《七哀》为一手，可称绝唱。小生方寸已乱，容有日凯旋，定当勉效郢和。”既生从征，屡年无耗。

    Explanation

    729

    At the fourth watch, I dreamt of you coming to meet me from afar, adorned with a ceremonial robe and a headdress of five clouds. It is said that you are a high-ranking official, the Chief of the Gold Guard, and even with a golden belt, you do not buy a concubine. The time flows continuously, and the fourth watch continues, each day we think of each other in our own realm. The stones of the southern mountain will eventually wear away, but when will the turbulent waves of our regret be filled? Alas, the fourth watch, the time passes quickly, and the copper mountains cannot repay the debt of longing. At the fifth watch, the time flows like water, and my delicate, slender feet rest on ice. I wake up with a slight breath, my hair falling from the pillow, and the rooster calls me to rise and comb my hair. The fragrance rises, and the fifth watch turns, and I think of you, who will soon return. May the green bamboo of prosperity remain, and may the flowers live long and the moon be always full. Alas, the fifth watch, the incense burns out, and the golden cup is pre-carved with a symbol of harmony. After the song ends, I praise you, saying, "Your rapid and graceful singing, with a balanced mix of speed and slowness, is unlike the common style. Your words and meaning are deeply moving and beautiful, truly combining the essence of Cai Wenji's 'Eighteen Beats,' Zhang Heng's 'Four Sorrows,' and Du Fu's 'Seven Sorrows' into one, which can be called a masterpiece. My heart is already in turmoil, and I will surely strive to harmonize with your music when I return victorious." Since I went on campaign, there has been no news for many years.

    730

    蜀有虎而冠者，素艳蕊霞名，欲谋夺之，乃造蜚语，谓生久没于阵，兼以重金啖母，愿娶蕊霞为继室。母试商之蕊霞，笑答曰：“儿幼从父读相人书，稔知生无凶折相，母请毋虑。”未几金陵克复，江路无梗。蜀中从征归者纷纷，或谓生已得官另娶。母闻，又以告，蕊霞亦斥其妄。乃决意自往寻生，央母与舅相送。沿流而下，舟次鸠江。邻舟有官航，月夜张宴，饮酣，忽闻琵琶之声，有客大声高歌，蕊霞顿触夙好，倾耳侧听。歌毕，蕊霞笑谓母与舅曰：“邻舟歌者，必是某生。”母与舅殊不深信。诘且，使人探之，果然。

    Explanation

    730

    In the region of Shu, there was a person named Rui Xia, who was as beautiful as a tiger wearing a crown. Someone wished to take her away and fabricated rumors, claiming that the man she loved had been missing in battle for a long time and offering a large sum of money to her mother, wishing to marry Rui Xia as a second wife. Rui Xia's mother tried to ask her about it, and she smiled and replied, "I was young when I followed my father to read books on physiognomy, and I know that the man does not have a bad fate. Please do not worry, mother." Not long after, Jinling was recaptured, and the river route became unobstructed.
